tool_name: CNC Milling — Cutting Force, Specific Cutting Energy & Spindle Power

inputs.name: Cutting Speed (V_c)
inputs.note: From Sandvik C-2920 Table 1 or tool manufacturer data for workpiece/tool material combination
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Feed per Tooth (f_z)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Axial Depth of Cut (a_p)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Radial Depth of Cut (a_e)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Number of Cutter Teeth (z)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Cutter Diameter (D_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Specific Cutting Force at 1 mm² Chip Area (k_c1)
inputs.note: Sandvik C-2920: Steel 0.2C → k_c1=1900; Stainless 316L → 2700; Ti6Al4V → 1700; Al7075 → 700 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 3002-4:2017 — Specific cutting force, material-dependent: ±8% (Sandvik Coromant)

inputs.name: Chip Thickness Exponent (m_c)
inputs.note: Sandvik: steel = 0.22–0.27; cast iron = 0.26; aluminium = 0.25
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 3002-4:2017 — Empirical Taylor exponent: ±5% (Type B, material-specific)

formulas: n_rpm = Vc_m_min * 1000 / (Math.PI * D_mm)   // [rpm] Spindle Speed (n) Reference: n = V_c × 1000 / (π × D_c) — Sandvik C-2920 §4.1
Vf_mm_min = fz_mm * z_teeth * n_rpm   // [mm/min] Table Feed Rate (V_f) Reference: V_f = f_z × z × n — ISO 3685:1993 §3.2
hm_mm = fz_mm * Math.sqrt(ae_mm / D_mm)   // [mm] Mean Chip Thickness (h_m) Reference: h_m = f_z × √(a_e/D_c) — Sandvik C-2920 §4.3; valid for a_e/D_c ≤ 0.5
kc_mpa = kc1_mpa / Math.pow(hm_mm, mc_exponent)   // [MPa] Specific Cutting Force at Operating Conditions (k_c) Reference: k_c = k_c1 / h_m^m_c — Kienzle force model; Sandvik C-2920 §4.4
Fc_N = kc_mpa * ap_mm * hm_mm   // [N] Mean Tangential Cutting Force (F_c) Reference: F_c = k_c × a_p × h_m — Kienzle; CIRP Annals Vol.57 §2.1
Pc_kw = Fc_N * Vc_m_min / 60000   // [kW] Net Spindle Power (P_c) Reference: P_c = F_c × V_c / 60000 — Sandvik C-2920 §4.5; add 25% for spindle efficiency

V1.error_msg: Radial depth a_e cannot exceed cutter diameter D_c — reduce a_e or select larger cutter

V2.error_msg: Spindle power demand > 50 kW: verify machine spindle rating; add 25% mechanical loss for machine requirement

V3.error_msg: Mean chip thickness < 0.01 mm: rubbing regime — built-up edge and tool wear risk; increase f_z or a_e

smart_warnings.message: a_e/D_c > 70%: full or near-full engagement — tool deflection and chatter risk severe; reduce radial depth to ≤ 50% and use finishing pass strategy
Specific cutting force > 3500 MPa: hard material or worn tool — verify k_c1 input and check for built-up edge; consider coated carbide grade
Spindle speed > 20,000 rpm: HSM regime — verify spindle bearing rating, tool balance (ISO 1940 G2.5), and coolant pressure capacity
Table feed > 5000 mm/min: high-feed machining — verify CNC controller acceleration and servo drive current limits
Deep axial cut with many teeth: high chip volume — verify chip evacuation; coolant-through-tool strongly recommended

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: ae_mm > D_mm
fmea.effect: Radial depth a_e cannot exceed cutter diameter D_c — reduce a_e or select larger cutter
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pc_kw > 50
fmea.effect: Spindle power demand > 50 kW: verify machine spindle rating; add 25% mechanical loss for machine requirement
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: hm_mm < 0.01
fmea.effect: Mean chip thickness < 0.01 mm: rubbing regime — built-up edge and tool wear risk; increase f_z or a_e
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: a_e/D_c > 70%: full or near-full engagement — tool deflection and chatter risk severe; reduce radial depth to ≤ 50% and use finishing pass strategy
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ae_mm / D_mm > 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Specific cutting force > 3500 MPa: hard material or worn tool — verify k_c1 input and check for built-up edge; consider coated carbide grade
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: kc_mpa > 3500

fmea.failureMode: Spindle speed > 20,000 rpm: HSM regime — verify spindle bearing rating, tool balance (ISO 1940 G2.5), and coolant pressure capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm > 20000

fmea.failureMode: Table feed > 5000 mm/min: high-feed machining — verify CNC controller acceleration and servo drive current limits
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vf_mm_min > 5000

fmea.failureMode: Deep axial cut with many teeth: high chip volume — verify chip evacuation; coolant-through-tool strongly recommended
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ap_mm > D_mm * 1.5 && z_teeth > 4

tool_name: CNC Turning — Cutting Force, MRR, Surface Roughness & Tool Life Estimation

inputs.name: Cutting Speed (V_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Feed Rate (f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Depth of Cut (a_p)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Specific Cutting Force (k_c1) at h=1 mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 3002-4:2017 — Specific cutting force, material-dependent: ±8% (Sandvik Coromant)

inputs.name: Insert Nose Radius (r_ε)
inputs.note: ISO 1832 insert designation — 4th character; R = 0.4 → standard; R = 0.8 → medium
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Taylor Tool Life Constant (C) at Reference Conditions
inputs.note: From Sandvik TMMS or manufacturer data; steel P15 grade: C ≈ 180–250 m/min at n=0.25
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Taylor Exponent (n)
inputs.note: Carbide turning steel: n=0.20–0.30; ceramic: n=0.40–0.50; CBN hardened steel: n=0.45
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: MRR_cm3_min = Vc_m_min * f_mm_rev * ap_mm / 10   // [cm³/min] Material Removal Rate (MRR) Reference: MRR = V_c × f × a_p / 10 — Sandvik C-2920 §5.2

kc_mpa = kc1_mpa / Math.pow(f_mm_rev, 0.25)   // [MPa] Specific Cutting Force at Operating Conditions (k_c) Reference: k_c = k_c1 / f^0.25 — Kienzle model; turning chip thickness ≈ f for orthogonal cut
Fc_N = kc_mpa * f_mm_rev * ap_mm   // [N] Tangential Cutting Force (F_c) Reference: F_c = k_c × f × a_p — ISO 3685:1993
Pc_kw = Fc_N * Vc_m_min / 60000   // [kW] Net Cutting Power (P_c) Reference: P_c = F_c × V_c / 60000

Ra_um = 1000 * Math.pow(f_mm_rev, 2) / (32 * re_mm)   // [µm] Theoretical Surface Roughness (Ra) — Nose Radius Model Reference: Ra ≈ f²/(32·r_ε) × 1000 — ISO 4287; valid for ideal geometry, add 25% for real conditions
T_min = Math.pow(Taylor_C / Vc_m_min, 1 / Taylor_n)   // [min] Tool Life at Operating Cutting Speed (T) — Taylor Reference: Taylor's equation: T = (C/V_c)^(1/n) — ISO 3685:1993 Annex A

V1.error_msg: Tool life < 5 min: rapid tool wear regime — reduce V_c by 20% or select higher-grade insert coating (PVD AlTiN, CVD Al₂O₃)

V2.error_msg: Theoretical Ra > 6.3 µm: rough finish — reduce feed rate or increase nose radius for finish passes

V3.error_msg: Cutting power > 80 kW: verify lathe spindle motor rating; excessive power causes thermal distortion in workpiece

smart_warnings.message: Tool life < 10 min: uneconomical — optimise V_c using Taylor equation; target T = 15–20 min for productive insert change interval
High feed with low Ra requirement conflict — theoretical roughness model predicts Ra > specification; reduce f or apply wiper insert geometry
Low speed + high k_c: built-up edge risk on sticky materials — increase V_c above BUE threshold or apply coated grade with sharp edge prep
MRR > 500 cm³/min: heavy roughing — verify workpiece fixture rigidity and coolant pressure; chip control critical at high MRR
Deep cut with fine feed: check radial force F_p — deep cuts generate large passive forces that deflect thin workpieces; verify L/D ratio ≤ 4

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_min < 5
fmea.effect: Tool life < 5 min: rapid tool wear regime — reduce V_c by 20% or select higher-grade insert coating (PVD AlTiN, CVD Al₂O₃)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Ra_um > 6.3
fmea.effect: Theoretical Ra > 6.3 µm: rough finish — reduce feed rate or increase nose radius for finish passes
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pc_kw > 80
fmea.effect: Cutting power > 80 kW: verify lathe spindle motor rating; excessive power causes thermal distortion in workpiece
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Tool life < 10 min: uneconomical — optimise V_c using Taylor equation; target T = 15–20 min for productive insert change interval
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_min < 10

fmea.failureMode: High feed with low Ra requirement conflict — theoretical roughness model predicts Ra > specification; reduce f or apply wiper insert geometry
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: f_mm_rev > 1.0 && Ra_um < 3.2

fmea.failureMode: Low speed + high k_c: built-up edge risk on sticky materials — increase V_c above BUE threshold or apply coated grade with sharp edge prep
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vc_m_min < 50 && kc_mpa > 3000

fmea.failureMode: MRR > 500 cm³/min: heavy roughing — verify workpiece fixture rigidity and coolant pressure; chip control critical at high MRR
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: MRR_cm3_min > 500

fmea.failureMode: Deep cut with fine feed: check radial force F_p — deep cuts generate large passive forces that deflect thin workpieces; verify L/D ratio ≤ 4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ap_mm > 10 && f_mm_rev < 0.2

tool_name: OEE & TPM Analysis — Availability, Performance, Quality & Six Big Loss Breakdown

inputs.name: Planned Production Time (PPT)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Unplanned Downtime — Breakdowns & Failures (D_u)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Planned Downtime — Setups & Adjustments (D_s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Ideal (Theoretical) Cycle Time per Part (t_ideal)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total Parts Produced (including rejects) (N_total)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Rejected / Defective Parts (N_reject)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Rework Parts (counted in total, N_rework)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: OT_h = planned_production_time_h - downtime_unplanned_h - downtime_setup_h; A = OT_h / planned_production_time_h   // [h, dimensionless] Operating Time & Availability (A) Reference: ISO 22400-2:2014 §4.3.3 — Availability = Operating Time / Planned Production Time
P = (total_parts_produced * ideal_cycle_time_s / 3600) / OT_h   // [dimensionless] Performance Rate (P) Reference: ISO 22400-2 §4.3.4 — Performance = (Total Pieces × Ideal CT) / Operating Time

good_parts = total_parts_produced - rejected_parts - rework_parts; Q = good_parts / total_parts_produced   // [dimensionless, parts] Quality Rate (Q) Reference: ISO 22400-2 §4.3.5 — Quality = Good Parts / Total Parts; rework excluded from good count
OEE = A * P * Q   // [dimensionless] OEE Score Reference: OEE = A × P × Q — JIPM TPM standard; World Class benchmark: OEE ≥ 85%

WC_OEE = 0.85; actual_good = good_parts; wc_good = Math.round(total_parts_produced * WC_OEE / OEE); parts_lost = wc_good - actual_good   // [parts] Throughput Loss (Good Parts Lost vs World-Class) Reference: Gap to World Class benchmark (85% OEE per JIPM) — production loss quantification

calendar_hours = 24 * Math.ceil(planned_production_time_h / 8); utilisation = planned_production_time_h / calendar_hours; TEEP = OEE * utilisation   // [dimensionless] TEEP (Total Effective Equipment Performance) Reference: TEEP = OEE × Utilisation — SEMI E10-0304; full-time calendar basis

V1.error_msg: Total downtime exceeds planned production time — check hours input

V2.error_msg: Defect + rework count exceeds total production — verify part counts

V3.error_msg: Performance rate > 105%: physically implausible — verify ideal cycle time; it may be set too conservative

smart_warnings.message: OEE < 65%: below minimum acceptable threshold — initiate full Six Big Loss analysis; priority losses: breakdowns (Loss 1) and setup time (Loss 2)
Availability < 80%: unplanned downtime dominant loss — implement Planned Maintenance (PM Step 3) and predictive maintenance programme
Quality rate < 95%: >5% defect/rework — conduct root cause analysis per 8D; check tooling wear schedule and incoming material certification
Performance < 85%: speed loss and minor stoppages — install cycle time monitoring; target micro-stoppage elimination through Autonomous Maintenance (AM Step 2)
TEEP < 50%: significant unused capacity — evaluate shift extension or weekend running before CAPEX investment per JIPM TPM §6

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: downtime_unplanned_h + downtime_setup_h > planned_production_time_h
fmea.effect: Total downtime exceeds planned production time — check hours input
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rejected_parts + rework_parts > total_parts_produced
fmea.effect: Defect + rework count exceeds total production — verify part counts
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: P > 1.05
fmea.effect: Performance rate > 105%: physically implausible — verify ideal cycle time; it may be set too conservative
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: OEE < 65%: below minimum acceptable threshold — initiate full Six Big Loss analysis; priority losses: breakdowns (Loss 1) and setup time (Loss 2)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OEE < 0.65

fmea.failureMode: Availability < 80%: unplanned downtime dominant loss — implement Planned Maintenance (PM Step 3) and predictive maintenance programme
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A < 0.80

fmea.failureMode: Quality rate < 95%: >5% defect/rework — conduct root cause analysis per 8D; check tooling wear schedule and incoming material certification
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q < 0.95

fmea.failureMode: Performance < 85%: speed loss and minor stoppages — install cycle time monitoring; target micro-stoppage elimination through Autonomous Maintenance (AM Step 2)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P < 0.85

fmea.failureMode: TEEP < 50%: significant unused capacity — evaluate shift extension or weekend running before CAPEX investment per JIPM TPM §6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: TEEP < 0.50

tool_name: VDI 2230 Bolted Joint — Tightening Torque, Preload Loss & Fatigue Safety Factor

inputs.name: Bolt Nominal Diameter × Pitch (ISO metric)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bolt Property Class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Thread & Under-Head Friction Coefficient (μ_total)
inputs.note: VDI 2230 Table A5: dry steel = 0.14–0.18; MoS₂ = 0.06–0.10; zinc-phosphate = 0.09–0.14
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Bolt Utilisation at Assembly (% of proof load)
inputs.note: VDI 2230 §5.3.1: controlled torque tightening → 90%; torque-angle → 95–100%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Expected Embedding / Relaxation Loss (% of initial preload)
inputs.note: VDI 2230 Table 5: machined surfaces = 5–10%; rough cast = 15–25%; plastic inserter = 20%
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: External Axial Working Load per Bolt (F_A)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Load Introduction Factor (Φ)
inputs.note: VDI 2230 §4.2: concentric bolt through long joint → Φ≈0.1; direct shear plane → Φ≈0.5
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

formulas: const A_s = {M6:20.1, M8:36.6, M10:58.0, M12:84.3, M16:157, M20:245, M24:353, M30:561}[bolt_size]; const Rp02 = {"8.8":640, "10.9":900, "12.9":1080}[property_class]; F_proof_kn = A_s * Rp02 / 1000   // [kN, mm²] Bolt Stress Area & Proof Load Reference: ISO 898-1:2013 — proof load = A_s × Rp0.2
FM_kn = F_proof_kn * utilisation_pct / 100   // [kN] Assembly Preload (F_M) Reference: VDI 2230 §5.3.1 — F_M = R_p0.2 × A_s × ν_A; ν_A = utilisation factor

const d = parseFloat(bolt_size.replace('M','')); const p = {M6:1.0,M8:1.25,M10:1.5,M12:1.75,M16:2.0,M20:2.5,M24:3.0,M30:3.5}[bolt_size]; MA_Nm = FM_kn * 1000 * (p/(2*Math.PI) + mu_friction * d * 0.583 + mu_friction * d * 0.5) / 1000   // [N·m] Tightening Torque (M_A) — Approximate Reference: VDI 2230 Eq.(167) — M_A = F_M(p/2π + μ_G·d_2·0.58 + μ_K·D_Km/2)

FVmin_kn = FM_kn * (1 - preload_loss_pct / 100) - phi_force_ratio * FA_kn   // [kN] Minimum Clamp Force After Embedding (F_Vmin) Reference: VDI 2230 §5.4.1 — F_Vmin = F_M(1-f_Z) - Φ·F_A; f_Z = embedding factor
SA = FVmin_kn / ((1 - phi_force_ratio) * FA_kn)   // [dimensionless] Interface Separation Safety Factor (S_A) Reference: VDI 2230 §5.4.2 — S_A ≥ 1.2 for reliable joint; S_A < 1.0 = joint separates

dFA_kn = phi_force_ratio * FA_kn; sigma_a_mpa = dFA_kn * 1000 / A_s   // [MPa] Bolt Fatigue Stress Amplitude (σ_a) Reference: VDI 2230 §6 — fatigue amplitude σ_a = Φ·F_A/A_s; compare to σ_ASV from Haigh diagram

V1.error_msg: Joint separation: clamp force exhausted by working load — increase preload (larger bolt, higher class), reduce F_A, or increase bolt count

V2.error_msg: Assembly preload exceeds proof load: bolt will yield on tightening — reduce utilisation to max 95% or select 10.9/12.9 class

V3.error_msg: Separation safety factor < 1.2: marginal joint — VDI 2230 minimum S_A = 1.2 for reliable sealing and fatigue resistance

smart_warnings.message: Bolt fatigue amplitude > 50 MPa: high fatigue loading — check against VDI 2230 Haigh diagram; apply thread-rolling (fatigue limit improvement ~30%) or increase bolt diameter
Very low friction coefficient: self-loosening risk in vibration environments — evaluate NORD-LOCK washers or anaerobic adhesive per VDI 2230 §3.5
12.9 class with low friction: hydrogen embrittlement risk in acidic environments — verify plating and service environment compatibility per ISO 4042
High embedding loss (>15%): rough or soft interface — machine mating surfaces or add hardened washer to reduce embedding; re-torque protocol recommended after 48h
Low utilisation (<70%): bolt is underloaded — susceptible to fatigue and loosening; increase preload per VDI 2230 §5.3 or reduce to smaller bolt diameter

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SA < 1.0
fmea.effect: Joint separation: clamp force exhausted by working load — increase preload (larger bolt, higher class), reduce F_A, or increase bolt count
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: FM_kn > F_proof_kn
fmea.effect: Assembly preload exceeds proof load: bolt will yield on tightening — reduce utilisation to max 95% or select 10.9/12.9 class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SA < 1.2
fmea.effect: Separation safety factor < 1.2: marginal joint — VDI 2230 minimum S_A = 1.2 for reliable sealing and fatigue resistance
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Bolt fatigue amplitude > 50 MPa: high fatigue loading — check against VDI 2230 Haigh diagram; apply thread-rolling (fatigue limit improvement ~30%) or increase bolt diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma_a_mpa > 50

fmea.failureMode: Very low friction coefficient: self-loosening risk in vibration environments — evaluate NORD-LOCK washers or anaerobic adhesive per VDI 2230 §3.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mu_friction < 0.08

fmea.failureMode: 12.9 class with low friction: hydrogen embrittlement risk in acidic environments — verify plating and service environment compatibility per ISO 4042
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: property_class === '12.9' && mu_friction < 0.10

fmea.failureMode: High embedding loss (>15%): rough or soft interface — machine mating surfaces or add hardened washer to reduce embedding; re-torque protocol recommended after 48h
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: preload_loss_pct > 15

fmea.failureMode: Low utilisation (<70%): bolt is underloaded — susceptible to fatigue and loosening; increase preload per VDI 2230 §5.3 or reduce to smaller bolt diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: utilisation_pct < 70

tool_name: WPS Preheat Temperature & PWHT Requirements — Carbon Equivalent & Hydrogen Risk

inputs.name: Carbon Content (C)
inputs.note: From mill certificate; structural steel: 0.10–0.25%; HSLA: 0.05–0.18%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Manganese Content (Mn)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Silicon Content (Si)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Chromium Content (Cr)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Mo + Ni + Cu Combined Content
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Plate / Section Thickness at Joint (t)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Welding Heat Input (HI)
inputs.note: HI = U × I × η / (1000 × v); SMAW η=0.80; GMAW η=0.80; SAW η=0.99
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Diffusible Hydrogen Level (H_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: CE_IIW = C_pct + Mn_pct/6 + (Si_pct + Cr_pct)/24 + Mo_Ni_pct/40   // [dimensionless] Carbon Equivalent — IIW Formula (CE_IIW) Reference: IIW / AWS D1.1-2020 Commentary — CE = C + Mn/6 + (Si+Cr)/24 + Mo/4 + Ni/40 + Cu/13

CET = C_pct + (Mn_pct + Mo_Ni_pct * 0.5 + Cr_pct * 0.1) / 10   // [dimensionless] Carbon Equivalent — CET (EN 1011-2 Annex C) Reference: EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C — CET used for preheat calculation by CET method

T_p_AWS_c = (CE_IIW < 0.40) ? 10 : (CE_IIW < 0.45) ? 66 : (CE_IIW < 0.60) ? 107 : 150   // [°C] Preheat Temperature — AWS D1.1 CE Method (T_p_AWS) Reference: AWS D1.1-2020 Table 3.2 — minimum preheat by CE and thickness category

T_p_EN_c = 697 * CET + 160 * Math.tanh(plate_thickness_mm / 35) + 62 * Math.pow(H_content, 0.35) - 328   // [°C] Preheat Temperature — EN 1011-2 CET Method (T_p_EN) Reference: EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C Eq.(C.3) — T_p = 697×CET + 160×tanh(t/35) + 62×H^0.35 - 328

T_p_governing_c = Math.max(T_p_AWS_c, T_p_EN_c, 10)   // [°C] Governing Minimum Preheat Temperature (T_p_governing) Reference: Conservative: maximum of AWS D1.1 and EN 1011-2 requirements; minimum 10°C per standards

PWHT_required = (CE_IIW > 0.45 && plate_thickness_mm > 32) || (plate_thickness_mm > 38 && property === 'Class D/E') ? 'PWHT REQUIRED per ASME IX / AWS D1.1 §5.8' : 'PWHT not mandatory — verify per design code'   // [text] PWHT Requirement Flag Reference: ASME B31.3-2022 §331.1.1 — P-No.1 PWHT required above 19 mm (¾ in) for pressure piping

V1.error_msg: CE_IIW > 0.65: very high hardenability — cold cracking risk is severe; mandatory H2.5 consumables, preheat ≥ 150°C, and post-heat treatment required

V2.error_msg: Preheat temperature > 250°C: verify interpass temperature control ≤ 300°C — excessive interpass causes heat-affected zone toughness loss per EN 1011-2

V3.error_msg: Very low heat input on hardenable steel: rapid cooling rate — HAZ hardness may exceed 350 HV; increase HI or raise preheat to slow cooling

smart_warnings.message: CE > 0.50 + H_d > 10 ml/100g: hydrogen-assisted cold cracking risk — switch to H5 or H2.5 consumables immediately; minimum 48h inspection delay before NDT
High preheat + high heat input: creep in HAZ possible during multipass welding — implement interpass temperature monitoring ≤ T_p + 100°C
Heavy section with low calculated preheat: heat sink effect underestimated — add 25°C for plates > 50 mm per AWS D1.1 Commentary §C-3.5
CET > 0.45: HSLA or alloy steel with elevated cold cracking susceptibility — document in WPS and PQR; hydrogen bake-out at 200°C × 2h strongly recommended
Standard hydrogen consumable on alloy steel: upgrade to H10 minimum — AWS D1.1 Table 4.5 restricts H15 for CE > 0.40 without mandatory elevated preheat

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CE_IIW > 0.65

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

tool_name: Capital Investment Appraisal — NPV, IRR, Payback & Profitability Index

inputs.name: Initial Capital Investment (CAPEX at t=0)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Net Cash Inflow (Revenue - OPEX)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Project Economic Life (N)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Discount Rate / WACC (r)
inputs.note: WACC = weighted cost of equity + debt; industrial: typically 8–15%; emerging markets: 12–20%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Terminal / Salvage Value at End of Life
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Cash Flow Escalation Rate (inflation adj.)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Effective Corporate Tax Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: CF_after_tax_usd = annual_cash_inflow_usd * (1 - tax_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD/yr] After-Tax Annual Cash Flow Reference: After-tax CF = EBITDA × (1 - t); simplified — excludes depreciation tax shield; add D × t for full model

r = discount_rate_pct / 100; g = annual_escalation_pct / 100; PVA = (Math.abs(g - r) < 0.001) ? CF_after_tax_usd * project_life_yr / (1+r) : CF_after_tax_usd * (1 - Math.pow((1+g)/(1+r), project_life_yr)) / (r - g); PV_salvage = salvage_value_usd / Math.pow(1+r, project_life_yr); NPV_usd = PVA + PV_salvage - initial_investment_usd   // [USD] Net Present Value — Growing Annuity (NPV) Reference: NPV = Σ CF_t/(1+r)^t - CAPEX — ICMA financial appraisal; growing annuity for g≠r

let irr = 0.10; for(let i=0;i<1000;i++){let f=-initial_investment_usd; let df=0; for(let t=1;t<=project_life_yr;t++){let cf=CF_after_tax_usd*Math.pow(1+annual_escalation_pct/100,t-1); f+=cf/Math.pow(1+irr,t); df-=t*cf/Math.pow(1+irr,t+1);} if(t===project_life_yr) f+=salvage_value_usd/Math.pow(1+irr,project_life_yr); let di=f/df; irr-=di; if(Math.abs(di)<1e-8) break;} IRR_pct = irr * 100   // [%] IRR — Newton-Raphson Iterative (MaxIter=1000) Reference: IRR: Newton-Raphson convergence — financial mathematics §3; MaxIter=1000 guard per SectorCalc standard
PBP_yr = initial_investment_usd / CF_after_tax_usd   // [years] Simple Payback Period (PBP) Reference: PBP = CAPEX / annual CF — simple payback; does not account for time value of money

let cumPV = 0; let DPBP = project_life_yr; for(let t=1; t<=project_life_yr; t++){cumPV += CF_after_tax_usd * Math.pow(1 + annual_escalation_pct/100, t-1) / Math.pow(1 + discount_rate_pct/100, t); if(cumPV >= initial_investment_usd){DPBP = t - 1 + (initial_investment_usd - (cumPV - CF_after_tax_usd / Math.pow(1+discount_rate_pct/100, t))) / (CF_after_tax_usd / Math.pow(1+discount_rate_pct/100, t)); break;}} DPBP_yr = DPBP   // [years] Discounted Payback Period (DPBP) Reference: Discounted payback — IAS 36 impairment; cumulative PV reaching CAPEX

PI = (NPV_usd + initial_investment_usd) / initial_investment_usd   // [dimensionless] Profitability Index (PI) Reference: PI = PV of cash inflows / CAPEX; PI > 1.0 → value creating — ISO 15686-5 LCC §5.3

V1.error_msg: Negative NPV: project destroys value at given WACC — evaluate cost reduction, revenue enhancement, or alternative investment

V2.error_msg: IRR < WACC: project return insufficient — do not proceed unless strategic rationale overrides financial return

V3.error_msg: Payback period exceeds project life: investment never recovers in nominal terms — re-examine cost structure or financing

smart_warnings.message: IRR > 50%: unusually high — verify cash flow projections for optimism bias; conduct sensitivity analysis on key revenue assumptions
Discounted payback > 70% of project life: late-life cash flow dependence — project is sensitive to abandonment risk; assess terminal value robustness
Profitability Index 1.0–1.1: marginal value creation — compare alternative uses of capital; opportunity cost may be higher
High escalation assumption (>5%): NPV is sensitive to inflation forecast — run downside at g=0% and upside at g=3% for scenario range
Large salvage value (>30% of CAPEX): NPV materially dependent on terminal residual — verify asset market and contractual exit options before committing

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: NPV_usd < 0
fmea.effect: Negative NPV: project destroys value at given WACC — evaluate cost reduction, revenue enhancement, or alternative investment
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: IRR_pct < discount_rate_pct
fmea.effect: IRR < WACC: project return insufficient — do not proceed unless strategic rationale overrides financial return
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: PBP_yr > project_life_yr
fmea.effect: Payback period exceeds project life: investment never recovers in nominal terms — re-examine cost structure or financing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: IRR > 50%: unusually high — verify cash flow projections for optimism bias; conduct sensitivity analysis on key revenue assumptions
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: IRR_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: Discounted payback > 70% of project life: late-life cash flow dependence — project is sensitive to abandonment risk; assess terminal value robustness
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DPBP_yr > project_life_yr * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Profitability Index 1.0–1.1: marginal value creation — compare alternative uses of capital; opportunity cost may be higher
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: PI < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High escalation assumption (>5%): NPV is sensitive to inflation forecast — run downside at g=0% and upside at g=3% for scenario range
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: annual_escalation_pct > 5

fmea.failureMode: Large salvage value (>30% of CAPEX): NPV materially dependent on terminal residual — verify asset market and contractual exit options before committing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: salvage_value_usd > initial_investment_usd * 0.3

tool_name: Pump NPSH Available, Cavitation Index & System Head Curve Analysis

inputs.name: Atmospheric Pressure at Installation (P_atm)
inputs.note: Sea level = 1.013 bar; altitude 1000m ≈ 0.899 bar; altitude 2000m ≈ 0.795 bar
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Liquid Vapour Pressure at Pumping Temperature (P_v)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Suction Head — Elevation Difference (Z_s) [+above pump, -below]
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Total Suction Line Head Loss (h_L,s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Pump Required NPSH (NPSHr) from Manufacturer Curve
inputs.note: At operating flow point from pump data sheet; API 610 NPSHr = 3% head drop criterion
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Pumped Liquid Density (ρ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Total Static Discharge Head (H_s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total Pipe Friction & Minor Losses at Design Flow (H_f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: NPSHa_m = (P_atm_bar - P_vapour_bar) * 100000 / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81) + Z_s_m - hL_suction_m   // [m] NPSHa — Available Net Positive Suction Head Reference: NPSHa = (P_atm - P_v)/(ρg) + Z_s - h_Ls — HI ANSI/HI 9.6.1:2012 §2.3
MR = NPSHa_m / NPSHr_m   // [dimensionless] NPSH Margin Ratio (MR) Reference: MR = NPSHa/NPSHr — API 610 §6.1.14: minimum MR = 1.1 (10% margin) for standard service; 1.3 for hot/volatile liquids
sigma = NPSHa_m / (static_head_m + pipe_friction_head_m)   // [dimensionless] Cavitation Sigma (σ) Reference: Thoma cavitation number σ = NPSHa / H_total; σ_critical from Thoma curve per ISO 9906
H_sys_m = static_head_m + pipe_friction_head_m   // [m] Total System Head (H_sys) Reference: H_sys = H_static + H_friction — system curve definition; pump operating point = H_sys

Zs_max_m = (P_atm_bar - P_vapour_bar) * 100000 / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81) - NPSHr_m - hL_suction_m - 0.5   // [m] Maximum Suction Lift (Z_s,max) Reference: Z_s,max = (P_atm - P_v)/(ρg) - NPSHr - h_Ls - 0.5 m safety — API 610 §6.1.14

NPSH_margin_m = NPSHa_m - NPSHr_m * 1.1   // [m] NPSH Deficit or Safety Margin Reference: Positive = adequate (API 610 10% margin met); negative = cavitation risk — lower suction lift or upsize suction pipe

V1.error_msg: NPSHa < NPSHr: cavitation will occur — pump will cavitate, causing impeller erosion and vibration; lower pump elevation or reduce suction losses immediately

V2.error_msg: NPSHa ≤ 0: liquid will flash in suction — verify vapour pressure and elevation inputs; flood suction (pump below tank) required

V3.error_msg: NPSH margin < 10% (API 610 minimum): cavitation damage risk — increase suction pipe diameter, reduce suction losses, or lower pump installation elevation

smart_warnings.message: Low NPSH margin on hot/volatile liquid: API 610 requires minimum 1.3× margin for T > 65°C or volatile hydrocarbons — redesign suction system
Suction lift > 8 m: practical centrifugal pump limit in most water applications — consider submersible pump or lower pump installation
Suction losses > 50% of NPSHr: suction pipe undersized — upsize suction pipe by one nominal size or eliminate elbows; target h_Ls < 0.3×NPSHr
Low atmospheric pressure (altitude > 1500 m): NPSHa significantly reduced — altitude correction mandatory per HI 9.6.1 §2.3 for installations above 1500 m ASL
Thoma σ < 0.10: pump operating near cavitation onset on system curve — verify cavitation-free zone on pump curve and consider variable speed operation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: NPSHa_m < NPSHr_m
fmea.effect: NPSHa < NPSHr: cavitation will occur — pump will cavitate, causing impeller erosion and vibration; lower pump elevation or reduce suction losses immediately
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: NPSHa_m < 0
fmea.effect: NPSHa ≤ 0: liquid will flash in suction — verify vapour pressure and elevation inputs; flood suction (pump below tank) required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: MR < 1.1
fmea.effect: NPSH margin < 10% (API 610 minimum): cavitation damage risk — increase suction pipe diameter, reduce suction losses, or lower pump installation elevation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Low NPSH margin on hot/volatile liquid: API 610 requires minimum 1.3× margin for T > 65°C or volatile hydrocarbons — redesign suction system
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: MR < 1.3 && P_vapour_bar > 0.2

fmea.failureMode: Suction lift > 8 m: practical centrifugal pump limit in most water applications — consider submersible pump or lower pump installation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Z_s_m < -8

fmea.failureMode: Suction losses > 50% of NPSHr: suction pipe undersized — upsize suction pipe by one nominal size or eliminate elbows; target h_Ls < 0.3×NPSHr
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: hL_suction_m > NPSHr_m * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Low atmospheric pressure (altitude > 1500 m): NPSHa significantly reduced — altitude correction mandatory per HI 9.6.1 §2.3 for installations above 1500 m ASL
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_atm_bar < 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Thoma σ < 0.10: pump operating near cavitation onset on system curve — verify cavitation-free zone on pump curve and consider variable speed operation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma < 0.10

tool_name: Steel Column Axial Compression — Slenderness, Critical Stress & Euler Buckling Load

inputs.name: Factored Axial Compressive Demand (P_u / N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Effective Column Length (KL)
inputs.note: Effective length = K × L; AISC Commentary C-C2.2: pinned-pinned K=1.0; fixed-pinned K=0.7; fixed-fixed K=0.5
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Minimum Radius of Gyration (r_min)
inputs.note: From section tables; r_y for wide-flange; r_min for angle — typically controls buckling
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Gross Cross-Section Area (A_g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (F_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: End Condition — Effective Length Factor (K)
inputs.note: Used if KL not pre-calculated; L_eff = K × physical length
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: KL_r = L_eff_m * 1000 / r_min_mm   // [dimensionless] Slenderness Ratio (KL/r) Reference: AISC 360-22 E2 — slenderness ratio; limit KL/r ≤ 200 for compression members
E_steel = 200000; Fe_mpa = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E_steel / Math.pow(KL_r, 2)   // [MPa] Elastic Buckling Stress (F_e) Reference: AISC 360-22 E3-4 — F_e = π²E/(KL/r)²; E = 200,000 MPa for steel

lambda_c = Math.sqrt(Fy_mpa / Fe_mpa); Fcr_mpa = (lambda_c <= 1.5) ? Math.pow(0.658, lambda_c * lambda_c) * Fy_mpa : (0.877 / (lambda_c * lambda_c)) * Fy_mpa   // [MPa] Critical Compressive Stress (F_cr) — AISC LRFD Reference: AISC 360-22 E3 — λ_c ≤ 1.5: inelastic; λ_c > 1.5: elastic Euler range
phi_Pn_kn = 0.90 * Fcr_mpa * A_g_mm2 / 1000   // [kN] Nominal Column Capacity (φP_n) Reference: AISC 360-22 §E1 — φ_c = 0.90 (LRFD); P_n = F_cr × A_g
DCR = Pu_kn / phi_Pn_kn   // [dimensionless] Demand-Capacity Ratio (DCR) Reference: AISC 360-22 §H1-1 — DCR ≤ 1.0 required; DCR > 1.0 = structural inadequacy

I_min_mm4 = A_g_mm2 * Math.pow(r_min_mm, 2); Pcr_kn = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * 200000 * I_min_mm4 / Math.pow(L_eff_m * 1000, 2) / 1000   // [kN] Euler Critical Load (P_cr) — Elastic Buckling Reference: Euler: P_cr = π²EI/L_eff² — EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1; reference for elastic buckling check

V1.error_msg: Column overstressed: axial demand exceeds φP_n — increase section area, reduce effective length, or reduce axial load

V2.error_msg: KL/r > 200: exceeds AISC 360 maximum slenderness limit for compression members — add intermediate bracing or select deeper section

V3.error_msg: Elastic buckling stress < 50% of yield: slender column dominated by buckling — verify effective length assumptions and add lateral bracing

smart_warnings.message: DCR > 90%: minimal reserve capacity — no additional loads or load combination factors can be accommodated; redesign before construction
Very slender column (KL/r > 150): highly sensitive to eccentricity and initial imperfections — apply additional amplification per AISC Appendix 8 or second-order analysis
High-strength steel with moderate slenderness: F_cr may be governed by elastic buckling, not yield — verify cost benefit of S460 vs S355 at this slenderness level
Applied load > 80% of Euler critical load: very close to elastic bifurcation point — P-delta effects and geometric imperfections become critical; perform second-order analysis
Cantilever column (K=2.0) over 5 m: very high effective length — verify fixed base connection rotational stiffness; partial fixity significantly increases effective K

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Column overstressed: axial demand exceeds φP_n — increase section area, reduce effective length, or reduce axial load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: KL_r > 200
fmea.effect: KL/r > 200: exceeds AISC 360 maximum slenderness limit for compression members — add intermediate bracing or select deeper section
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Fe_mpa < Fy_mpa * 0.5
fmea.effect: Elastic buckling stress < 50% of yield: slender column dominated by buckling — verify effective length assumptions and add lateral bracing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: DCR > 90%: minimal reserve capacity — no additional loads or load combination factors can be accommodated; redesign before construction
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR > 0.90

fmea.failureMode: Very slender column (KL/r > 150): highly sensitive to eccentricity and initial imperfections — apply additional amplification per AISC Appendix 8 or second-order analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: KL_r > 150

fmea.failureMode: High-strength steel with moderate slenderness: F_cr may be governed by elastic buckling, not yield — verify cost benefit of S460 vs S355 at this slenderness level
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fy_mpa > 355 && KL_r > 80

fmea.failureMode: Applied load > 80% of Euler critical load: very close to elastic bifurcation point — P-delta effects and geometric imperfections become critical; perform second-order analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pu_kn > Pcr_kn * 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Cantilever column (K=2.0) over 5 m: very high effective length — verify fixed base connection rotational stiffness; partial fixity significantly increases effective K
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: end_condition === 2.0 && L_eff_m > 5

tool_name: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness Sizing (ASME VIII / PED)

inputs.name: Design Pressure (P)
inputs.hint: Calculated as 10% above maximum operating pressure
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Inner Radius (Ri)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Vessel Head Geometry
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Material Grade
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Allowable Stress (S)
inputs.hint: Automatically resolved from material selection — ASME Sec. II Part D
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Weld Joint Efficiency (E)
inputs.hint: ASME VIII UW-12 weld joint category
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Weld Joint Efficiency Value (E)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Corrosion Allowance (CA)
inputs.hint: General service: 3mm | Sour service: 6mm | Dry air: 1.5mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Mill Under-tolerance
inputs.hint: ASME SA-6 / SA-20 plate mill tolerance — typically 12.5%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Design Temperature
inputs.hint: Allowable stress depends heavily on operating temperature
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Hydrostatic Test Pressure Factor
inputs.hint: ASME VIII UG-99: minimum 1.3 factor
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

formulas: t_cyl_calc = (design_pressure_mpa * inner_radius_mm) / ((allowable_stress_mpa * joint_efficiency_val) - (0.6 * design_pressure_mpa))   // [mm] Cylindrical shell — ASME VIII UG-27(c)(1)
t_sphere_calc = (design_pressure_mpa * inner_radius_mm) / ((2 * allowable_stress_mpa * joint_efficiency_val) - (0.2 * design_pressure_mpa))   // [mm] Spherical shell — ASME VIII UG-27(d)
t_ellip_calc = (design_pressure_mpa * inner_radius_mm) / ((2 * allowable_stress_mpa * joint_efficiency_val) - (0.2 * design_pressure_mpa))   // [mm] 2:1 Ellipsoidal Head — ASME VIII UG-32(d)
t_hemi_calc = (design_pressure_mpa * inner_radius_mm) / ((2 * allowable_stress_mpa * joint_efficiency_val) - (0.2 * design_pressure_mpa)) * 0.5   // [mm] Hemispherical Head
t_min_nominal = t_cyl_calc + corrosion_allowance_mm   // [mm] Minimum nominal thickness including corrosion
t_ordered = t_min_nominal / (1 - mill_tolerance_pct / 100)   // [mm] Ordered plate thickness (including mill tolerance)

MAWP = (allowable_stress_mpa * joint_efficiency_val * (t_min_nominal - corrosion_allowance_mm)) / (inner_radius_mm + 0.6 * (t_min_nominal - corrosion_allowance_mm))   // [MPa] Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP)
HydroTest_P = hydro_test_factor * design_pressure_mpa   // [MPa] Hydrostatic test pressure — ASME VIII UG-99
Hoop_Stress = (design_pressure_mpa * inner_radius_mm) / (t_min_nominal - corrosion_allowance_mm)   // [MPa] Circumferential hoop stress
Safety_Factor = allowable_stress_mpa / Hoop_Stress   // [—] Actual factor of safety

thin_wall_limit.error_msg: Thin wall formula invalid: P >= 0.385*S*E threshold violated. Thick wall (Lamé) equation must be applied (ASME VIII UG-27 footnote).

pressure_positive.error_msg: Design pressure must be minimum 0.1 MPa. Separate external pressure analysis is required for negative pressure (vacuum) (ASME VIII UG-28).

corrosion_min.error_msg: High temperature + zero corrosion allowance: corrosion cannot be neglected in services >50°C. Check API 571 damage mechanisms.

temperature_material.error_msg: Above 400°C, the allowable stress for SA-516 Gr.70 drops below 138 MPa. Use ASME Sec. II Part D temperature tables.

hydro_test_minimum.error_msg: ASME VIII UG-99: Hydrostatic test factor must be minimum 1.3. This is a regulatory requirement.

smart_warnings.message: Low corrosion allowance: CA < 1.5mm. Minimum 3mm is recommended for general service. Increase to 6mm if process fluid is corrosive (acid, H2S, Cl-). Insufficient CA leads to premature pinholes.
Critical: Hoop stress exceeds 90% of allowable stress. Safety margin is insufficient. Increase wall thickness or select material with higher S value.

Thickness > 50mm: PWHT (Post-Weld Heat Treatment) may become mandatory. Check ASME VIII UCS-56 tables. Evaluate cost and schedule impact of heat treatment.

tool_name: Compressed Air Leakage Energy Loss Audit (Sonic Flow)

inputs.name: System Operating Pressure (gauge)
inputs.hint: Read from system pressure gauge — typical industrial: 6–8 bar(g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Leak Orifice Diameter (d)
inputs.hint: Ultrasonic leak detector reading or estimated. Pinhole: 0.5mm | Fitting: 1–3mm | Open hose: 6–12mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Discharge Coefficient (Cd)
inputs.hint: Sharp-edged orifice: 0.61 | Rounded: 0.72 | Cracked fitting: 0.65
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Compressor FAD Capacity
inputs.hint: Free Air Delivery from nameplate — ISO 1217 Annex C
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Polytropic Index (n)
inputs.hint: Dry screw: 1.35 | Lubricated screw: 1.20 | Piston (adiabatic): 1.40 | Isothermal: 1.00
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Overall Drive Efficiency (ηmech)
inputs.hint: Motor × gearbox × coupling efficiency. Direct drive: 92–95% | Belt: 85–90%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.hint: 1 shift: 2000h | 2 shift: 4000h | 3 shift: 6000h | Continuous: 8400h
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Electricity Tariff
inputs.hint: Industrial tariff including demand charges
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Total Number of Leak Points
inputs.hint: From ultrasonic audit survey report
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Grid CO₂ Emission Factor
inputs.hint: Turkey grid 2023: 0.547 | EU avg: 0.255 | IEA source
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

formulas: T_abs_K = intake_temp_c + 273.15   // [K] Absolute inlet temperature
P_abs_bar = system_pressure_bar_g + 1.01325   // [bar(a)] Absolute system pressure
P_atm_bar = 1.01325   // [bar(a)] Atmospheric reference
Critical_Pressure_Ratio = POWER(2 / (polytropic_index_n + 1), polytropic_index_n / (polytropic_index_n - 1))   // [—] Sonic flow threshold — choked if P_abs/P_atm > 1/CPR
Is_Choked = P_abs_bar / P_atm_bar > (1 / Critical_Pressure_Ratio)   // [bool] True = sonic (choked) flow
Orifice_Area_m2 = 3.14159265 / 4 * POWER(leak_diameter_mm / 1000, 2)   // [m²] Leak orifice area
Sonic_Velocity_m_s = SQRT(polytropic_index_n * 287 * T_abs_K)   // [m/s] Speed of sound in air at inlet
Air_Density_kg_m3 = (P_abs_bar * 100000) / (287 * T_abs_K)   // [kg/m³] Air density at system pressure

Leak_Flow_kg_s = discharge_coefficient_Cd * Orifice_Area_m2 * SQRT(polytropic_index_n * Air_Density_kg_m3 * P_abs_bar * 100000 * POWER(2 / (polytropic_index_n + 1), (polytropic_index_n + 1) / (polytropic_index_n - 1)))   // [kg/s] Mass flow — choked orifice equation
Leak_Flow_m3min_FAD = (Leak_Flow_kg_s * 60) / (Air_Density_kg_m3 * P_abs_bar / P_atm_bar)   // [m³/min FAD] Leak flow at free air conditions
Total_Leak_Flow_m3min = Leak_Flow_m3min_FAD * number_of_leak_points   // [m³/min FAD] Total system leakage
Leak_Pct_of_FAD = (Total_Leak_Flow_m3min / compressor_fad_m3min) * 100   // [%] Leakage as % of compressor output

Specific_Power_kW_m3min = ((polytropic_index_n / (polytropic_index_n - 1)) * P_atm_bar * 100 * (POWER(P_abs_bar / P_atm_bar, (polytropic_index_n - 1) / polytropic_index_n) - 1)) / (mechanical_efficiency_pct / 100)   // [kW/(m³/min)] Specific power — ISO 1217
Wasted_Power_kW = Total_Leak_Flow_m3min * Specific_Power_kW_m3min   // [kW] Power wasted on leaks
Annual_Energy_Loss_kWh = Wasted_Power_kW * annual_operating_hours   // [kWh/year]
Annual_Cost_USD = Annual_Energy_Loss_kWh * electricity_rate_usd_kwh   // [USD/year] Financial loss
Annual_CO2_tonnes = Annual_Energy_Loss_kWh * co2_factor_kg_kwh / 1000   // [tCO₂/year] Carbon footprint of leaks
Repair_ROI_months = 0   // Placeholder — repair cost input needed for full ROI

polytropic_range.error_msg: Polytropic index n must be 1.00–1.40. Isothermal (ideal intercooling): 1.00 | Adiabatic: 1.40. Values outside this range are physically impossible for air compression.

leak_exceeds_fad.error_msg: Total leak flow exceeds compressor FAD. System cannot maintain pressure — compressor runs continuously at full load. Data check required.

pressure_minimum.error_msg: System pressure < 2 bar(g): Below practical compressed air systems range. Check input units — must be gauge pressure (bar(g)), not absolute.

smart_warnings.message: SONIC (CHOKED) FLOW confirmed: At this system pressure, air exits the leak point at the speed of sound. Flow rate is independent of downstream pressure — leakage cannot be reduced by lowering system pressure. Fix is mechanical repair only.

CRITICAL SYSTEM LOSS: Leakage exceeds 30% of compressor output. Industry average is 20–30% in poorly maintained systems. At this level, an additional compressor may be running solely to compensate for losses. Immediate audit and repair program mandatory.
Significant leakage: 10–30% of FAD lost. Well-maintained systems achieve <10%. Schedule leak repair program. Typical payback: 3–12 months.
Annual financial loss exceeds $5,000 USD from this leak source alone. Ultrasonic leak detection survey and systematic repair program will deliver rapid ROI.
Large orifice leak (≥3mm): This is a fitting, joint, or valve failure — not a pinhole. Immediate isolation required. At 6 bar(g), a 3mm orifice leaks ~4 m³/min FAD continuously.
Carbon impact: Leakage responsible for significant Scope 2 CO₂ emissions. Relevant for ISO 14001, ESG reporting, and EU CBAM supply chain exposure.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: polytropic_index_n < 1.0 || polytropic_index_n > 1.4
fmea.effect: Polytropic index n must be 1.00–1.40. Isothermal (ideal intercooling): 1.00 | Adiabatic: 1.40. Values outside this range are physically impossible for air compression.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Total_Leak_Flow_m3min > compressor_fad_m3min
fmea.effect: Total leak flow exceeds compressor FAD. System cannot maintain pressure — compressor runs continuously at full load. Data check required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SONIC (CHOKED) FLOW confirmed: At this system pressure, air exits the leak point at the speed of sound. Flow rate is independent of downstream pressure — leakage cannot be reduced by lowering system pressure. Fix is mechanical repair only.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_abs_bar / P_atm_bar > 1 / Critical_Pressure_Ratio

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL SYSTEM LOSS: Leakage exceeds 30% of compressor output. Industry average is 20–30% in poorly maintained systems. At this level, an additional compressor may be running solely to compensate for losses. Immediate audit and repair program mandatory.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Leak_Pct_of_FAD > 30

fmea.failureMode: Significant leakage: 10–30% of FAD lost. Well-maintained systems achieve <10%. Schedule leak repair program. Typical payback: 3–12 months.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Leak_Pct_of_FAD > 10 && Leak_Pct_of_FAD <= 30

fmea.failureMode: Annual financial loss exceeds $5,000 USD from this leak source alone. Ultrasonic leak detection survey and systematic repair program will deliver rapid ROI.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_Cost_USD > 5000

fmea.failureMode: Large orifice leak (≥3mm): This is a fitting, joint, or valve failure — not a pinhole. Immediate isolation required. At 6 bar(g), a 3mm orifice leaks ~4 m³/min FAD continuously.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: leak_diameter_mm >= 3.0

fmea.failureMode: Carbon impact: Leakage responsible for significant Scope 2 CO₂ emissions. Relevant for ISO 14001, ESG reporting, and EU CBAM supply chain exposure.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_CO2_tonnes > 10

tool_name: Multi-Product Break-Even Analysis and Degree of Operating Leverage (DOL)

inputs.name: Total Annual Fixed Costs (FC)
inputs.hint: Rent, salaries, depreciation, insurance — costs that do not vary with output
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Target Net Profit After Tax
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Corporate Income Tax Rate
inputs.hint: Turkey: 25% | EU average: 21% | US federal: 21% + state
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Product A — Selling Price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Product A — Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Product A — Sales Mix Share
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Product B — Selling Price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Product B — Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Product B — Sales Mix Share
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Actual Total Units Sold (Current Period)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Semi-Variable (Step) Costs
inputs.hint: Costs that step up at volume thresholds — overtime, extra shifts
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: CM_A = product_A_price - product_A_vc   // [USD/unit] Contribution Margin — Product A
CM_B = product_B_price - product_B_vc   // [USD/unit] Contribution Margin — Product B
Mix_A = product_A_mix_pct / 100   // [—] Product A weight
Mix_B = product_B_mix_pct / 100   // [—] Product B weight
Mix_Sum = Mix_A + Mix_B   // [—] Validation: must equal 1.0
WACM = (CM_A * Mix_A + CM_B * Mix_B) / Mix_Sum   // [USD/unit] Weighted Average Contribution Margin
Total_FC_Adjusted = total_fixed_costs_usd + semi_variable_costs_usd   // [USD] Adjusted fixed cost base
BEP_Units = Total_FC_Adjusted / WACM   // [units] Break-Even Point in total units
BEP_Units_A = BEP_Units * Mix_A   // [units] BEP units for Product A
BEP_Units_B = BEP_Units * Mix_B   // [units] BEP units for Product B
BEP_Revenue = BEP_Units_A * product_A_price + BEP_Units_B * product_B_price   // [USD] Break-Even Revenue
Required_Pre_Tax_Profit = target_net_profit_usd / (1 - corporate_tax_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD] Pre-tax profit needed
Target_Volume_Units = (Total_FC_Adjusted + Required_Pre_Tax_Profit) / WACM   // [units] Volume required to hit profit target
Actual_Operating_Income = actual_total_volume * WACM - Total_FC_Adjusted   // [USD] EBIT at actual volume
Actual_Net_Profit = Actual_Operating_Income * (1 - corporate_tax_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD] Net income after tax
Margin_of_Safety_Units = actual_total_volume - BEP_Units   // [units] Safety cushion above BEP
Margin_of_Safety_Pct = (Margin_of_Safety_Units / actual_total_volume) * 100   // [%] MoS as % of actual volume
DOL = Actual_Operating_Income > 0 ? (actual_total_volume * WACM) / Actual_Operating_Income : 0   // [—] Degree of Operating Leverage — sensitivity multiplier

mix_sum_check.error_msg: Product mix percentages must sum to 100% (±2% tolerance). Current sum is invalid — WACM calculation will be distorted.

negative_cm_check.error_msg: Weighted Average Contribution Margin ≤ 0: Variable costs exceed selling prices on a weighted basis. Break-even is mathematically unreachable — this product mix destroys value at every unit sold.

negative_cm_product.error_msg: At least one product has negative contribution margin (price < variable cost). Selling more units increases losses. Price revision or discontinuation must be evaluated immediately.

tax_rate_range.error_msg: Tax rate cannot equal or exceed 100%. Net profit would be zero or negative by definition.

smart_warnings.message: EXTREME OPERATING LEVERAGE (DOL > 5): A 10% revenue decline will reduce operating income by more than 50%. Fixed cost structure is dangerously high relative to volume. Immediate fixed cost reduction or revenue diversification required.
High Operating Leverage (DOL 3–5): Business is highly sensitive to volume fluctuations. In an economic downturn, profitability will erode rapidly. Stress-test at -15% and -30% volume scenarios.

CRITICAL: Margin of Safety < 10%. Actual sales are within 10% of break-even. Any demand reduction, price pressure, or cost increase could push the business into loss. Pricing, cost structure, or volume must be addressed immediately.
Low Margin of Safety (10–25%): Business has limited buffer above break-even. Acceptable for stable industries; concerning for cyclical manufacturing or export-dependent operations.

Contribution Margin Ratio < 20% on one or more products: Very thin margin structure. Small cost increases (raw materials, energy) will immediately eliminate profitability. Price elasticity analysis required.
Break-even revenue exceeds $10M: High absolute break-even level. Ensure sales pipeline and contracted revenue cover this threshold before capacity expansion.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Mix_Sum < 0.98 || Mix_Sum > 1.02
fmea.effect: Product mix percentages must sum to 100% (±2% tolerance). Current sum is invalid — WACM calculation will be distorted.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: WACM <= 0
fmea.effect: Weighted Average Contribution Margin ≤ 0: Variable costs exceed selling prices on a weighted basis. Break-even is mathematically unreachable — this product mix destroys value at every unit sold.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CM_A < 0 || CM_B < 0
fmea.effect: At least one product has negative contribution margin (price < variable cost). Selling more units increases losses. Price revision or discontinuation must be evaluated immediately.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: corporate_tax_rate_pct >= 100
fmea.effect: Tax rate cannot equal or exceed 100%. Net profit would be zero or negative by definition.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME OPERATING LEVERAGE (DOL > 5): A 10% revenue decline will reduce operating income by more than 50%. Fixed cost structure is dangerously high relative to volume. Immediate fixed cost reduction or revenue diversification required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DOL > 5

fmea.failureMode: High Operating Leverage (DOL 3–5): Business is highly sensitive to volume fluctuations. In an economic downturn, profitability will erode rapidly. Stress-test at -15% and -30% volume scenarios.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DOL > 3 && DOL <= 5

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL: Margin of Safety < 10%. Actual sales are within 10% of break-even. Any demand reduction, price pressure, or cost increase could push the business into loss. Pricing, cost structure, or volume must be addressed immediately.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Margin_of_Safety_Pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: Low Margin of Safety (10–25%): Business has limited buffer above break-even. Acceptable for stable industries; concerning for cyclical manufacturing or export-dependent operations.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Margin_of_Safety_Pct >= 10 && Margin_of_Safety_Pct < 25

fmea.failureMode: Contribution Margin Ratio < 20% on one or more products: Very thin margin structure. Small cost increases (raw materials, energy) will immediately eliminate profitability. Price elasticity analysis required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CM_A / product_A_price < 0.20 || CM_B / product_B_price < 0.20

fmea.failureMode: Break-even revenue exceeds $10M: High absolute break-even level. Ensure sales pipeline and contracted revenue cover this threshold before capacity expansion.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BEP_Revenue > 10000000

tool_name: Mass Concrete Volume, Thermal Crack Risk & Pour Cost (ACI 207)

inputs.name: Slab Net Volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Foundation / Footing Net Volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Column / Wall Net Volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Reinforcement Volume Ratio
inputs.hint: Lightly reinforced: 1% | Standard: 2–3% | Heavily reinforced: 4–5%
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Placement Waste Allowance
inputs.hint: Standard: 5% | Complex formwork: 7–10% | Pumped slabs: 3–5%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Cement Content per m³
inputs.hint: Lean concrete: 150–200 | Standard C25: 280–320 | High strength C45: 380–450
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Cement Type / Heat Class
options: CEM III/B (GGBS ≥66%) — Very low heat: factor 0.55
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Cement Heat Generation Factor
inputs.hint: From cement type selection: CEM I=1.00 | CEM III/B=0.55
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Ambient Air Temperature at Pour
inputs.hint: Use forecast temperature for the pour date and first 72 hours

inputs.name: Ready-Mix Concrete Unit Price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Concrete Pump + Labour Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Minimum Section Thickness
inputs.hint: ACI 207.1R: Mass concrete defined as thickness ≥ 900mm (36 in)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

formulas: Gross_Volume_m3 = vol_slab_m3 + vol_footing_m3 + vol_column_m3   // [m³] Total geometric volume
Rebar_Displacement_m3 = Gross_Volume_m3 * (rebar_volume_pct / 100)   // [m³] Volume displaced by reinforcement
Net_Concrete_m3 = Gross_Volume_m3 - Rebar_Displacement_m3   // [m³] Net concrete volume required
Order_Volume_m3 = Net_Concrete_m3 * (1 + pour_waste_pct / 100)   // [m³] Order quantity incl. waste
Total_Material_Cost_USD = Order_Volume_m3 * concrete_unit_cost_usd   // [USD] Concrete supply cost
Total_Pour_Cost_USD = Total_Material_Cost_USD + pump_mobilization_usd   // [USD] Total placement cost
Cost_per_Net_m3_USD = Total_Pour_Cost_USD / Net_Concrete_m3   // [USD/m³] All-in unit cost
Heat_of_Hydration_kJ_kg = 350 * cement_heat_factor   // [kJ/kg] Cement heat release — ACI 207.2R Table 3.1
Adiabatic_Temp_Rise_C = (cement_content_kg_m3 * Heat_of_Hydration_kJ_kg) / (2400 * 0.92)   // [°C] Max adiabatic temperature rise | ACI 207.1R
Peak_Core_Temp_C = ambient_temp_c + Adiabatic_Temp_Rise_C   // [°C] Estimated peak core temperature
Surface_Temp_C = ambient_temp_c + 5   // [°C] Approximate surface temperature (simplified — no insulation)
Delta_T_Core_Surface = Peak_Core_Temp_C - Surface_Temp_C   // [°C] Thermal gradient — crack driver
Concrete_Weight_tonnes = Net_Concrete_m3 * 2.4   // [tonnes] Structural weight (reinforced concrete density)

total_volume_positive.error_msg: Total concrete volume is zero. Enter at least one non-zero volume (slab, footing, or column).

cement_content_range.error_msg: Cement content < 150 kg/m³: Below minimum for structural concrete workability and strength. ACI 318 minimum for structural applications: 250 kg/m³.

mass_concrete_threshold.error_msg: MASS CONCRETE CONDITIONS: Section ≥ 900mm with high-heat cement (CEM I/II). ACI 207.1R mass concrete protocols mandatory. Thermal control plan required before pouring.

smart_warnings.message: THERMAL CRACKING RISK CONFIRMED: Core-to-surface ΔT exceeds 20°C threshold. Restrained thermal shrinkage will generate tensile stresses exceeding concrete tensile strength. Through-cracking expected. Mandatory mitigations: (1) Use CEM III/B or pozzolanic cement, (2) Pre-cool mix water or aggregate, (3) Limit pour lifts to 600mm, (4) Apply thermal insulation blanket to retain surface heat.

SEVERE THERMAL CRACKING — ΔT > 25°C: At this differential, macro-cracks of 0.2–0.5mm width are near-certain. Structure integrity and watertightness compromised. Redesign concrete mix with low-heat cement and thermal monitoring sensors (embedded thermocouples) required.

HOT WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient temperature > 32°C. Concrete may set prematurely. ACI 305R requirements: (1) Fresh concrete temperature ≤ 35°C at point of discharge, (2) Use chilled water or ice substitution, (3) Pour at night, (4) Increase slump via plasticizer — not water.

COLD WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient < 5°C. Hydration will stall or freeze. ACI 306R: Fresh concrete must be maintained at ≥ 10°C for 7 days minimum. Insulated formwork, heated enclosure, or anti-freeze admixtures required.

DELAYED ETTRINGITE FORMATION (DEF) RISK: Peak core temperature > 70°C. Ettringite phase destroyed during curing and reforms later causing expansive cracking. Use water-reducing admixtures and low-C₃A cements. This risk is irreversible once temperature is exceeded.

Waste allowance < 3%: Likely underestimated for construction site conditions. Insufficient waste factor leads to under-ordering, schedule delays, and cold joint formation. Minimum 5% recommended for poured-in-place work.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Gross_Volume_m3 <= 0
fmea.effect: Total concrete volume is zero. Enter at least one non-zero volume (slab, footing, or column).
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cement_content_kg_m3 < 150
fmea.effect: Cement content < 150 kg/m³: Below minimum for structural concrete workability and strength. ACI 318 minimum for structural applications: 250 kg/m³.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: min_section_thickness_mm >= 900 && cement_heat_factor >= 0.85
fmea.effect: MASS CONCRETE CONDITIONS: Section ≥ 900mm with high-heat cement (CEM I/II). ACI 207.1R mass concrete protocols mandatory. Thermal control plan required before pouring.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: THERMAL CRACKING RISK CONFIRMED: Core-to-surface ΔT exceeds 20°C threshold. Restrained thermal shrinkage will generate tensile stresses exceeding concrete tensile strength. Through-cracking expected. Mandatory mitigations: (1) Use CEM III/B or pozzolanic cement, (2) Pre-cool mix water or aggregate, (3) Limit pour lifts to 600mm, (4) Apply thermal insulation blanket to retain surface heat.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Delta_T_Core_Surface > 20

fmea.failureMode: SEVERE THERMAL CRACKING — ΔT > 25°C: At this differential, macro-cracks of 0.2–0.5mm width are near-certain. Structure integrity and watertightness compromised. Redesign concrete mix with low-heat cement and thermal monitoring sensors (embedded thermocouples) required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Delta_T_Core_Surface > 25

fmea.failureMode: HOT WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient temperature > 32°C. Concrete may set prematurely. ACI 305R requirements: (1) Fresh concrete temperature ≤ 35°C at point of discharge, (2) Use chilled water or ice substitution, (3) Pour at night, (4) Increase slump via plasticizer — not water.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c > 32

fmea.failureMode: COLD WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient < 5°C. Hydration will stall or freeze. ACI 306R: Fresh concrete must be maintained at ≥ 10°C for 7 days minimum. Insulated formwork, heated enclosure, or anti-freeze admixtures required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c < 5

fmea.failureMode: DELAYED ETTRINGITE FORMATION (DEF) RISK: Peak core temperature > 70°C. Ettringite phase destroyed during curing and reforms later causing expansive cracking. Use water-reducing admixtures and low-C₃A cements. This risk is irreversible once temperature is exceeded.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Peak_Core_Temp_C > 70

fmea.failureMode: Waste allowance < 3%: Likely underestimated for construction site conditions. Insufficient waste factor leads to under-ordering, schedule delays, and cold joint formation. Minimum 5% recommended for poured-in-place work.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pour_waste_pct < 3

tool_name: Calibration Drift Analysis & ISO 17025 Conformity Decision Risk (TUR / Guard Band)

inputs.name: Current Calibration Error (found error)
inputs.hint: Signed error: indicated value minus reference standard value. Can be negative.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Previous Calibration Error
inputs.hint: Error from the immediately preceding calibration certificate
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Days in Service Between Calibrations
inputs.hint: Actual elapsed days between calibration dates on certificates
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Planned Next Calibration Interval
inputs.hint: Your planned calibration frequency going forward
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Process Specification Tolerance (±T)
inputs.hint: Half-range of the bilateral tolerance band: |USL - LSL| / 2
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Reference Standard Expanded Uncertainty (U, k=2)
inputs.hint: From calibration laboratory certificate — U at 95% confidence (k=2)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Ambient Temperature Variation During Use (ΔT)
inputs.hint: Temperature range in the measurement environment (max − min)

inputs.name: Instrument Temperature Coefficient
inputs.hint: From manufacturer datasheet. Sensitivity to ambient temperature change.

inputs.name: Instrument Display Resolution
inputs.hint: Smallest graduation or digit. Contributes ±resolution/2 to uncertainty.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Guard Band Decision Rule
options: ILAC G8 Rule — w = U (full guard band)
inputs.default: ILAC G8 Rule — w = U (full guard band)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Guard Band Factor (w / U)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

formulas: Absolute_Drift = ABS(current_error - previous_error)   // [unit] Total drift between calibrations
Drift_Rate_per_Day = Absolute_Drift / days_in_service   // [unit/day] Linear drift rate
Predicted_Drift_Next_Interval = Drift_Rate_per_Day * planned_next_interval_days   // [unit] Predicted drift at next calibration
U_drift = Predicted_Drift_Next_Interval / SQRT(3)   // [unit] Standard uncertainty from drift (uniform distribution)
U_env = temp_coefficient * ambient_temp_variation_c / SQRT(3)   // [unit] Standard uncertainty from temperature effect
U_ref = reference_uncertainty_U / 2   // [unit] Standard uncertainty from reference (k=2)
U_res = resolution / (2 * SQRT(3))   // [unit] Standard uncertainty from resolution
U_combined = SQRT(POWER(U_ref, 2) + POWER(U_drift, 2) + POWER(U_env, 2) + POWER(U_res, 2))   // [unit] Combined standard uncertainty — GUM RSS method
U_expanded = U_combined * 2   // [unit] Expanded uncertainty (k=2, ~95% coverage)
TUR = process_tolerance / U_expanded   // [—] Test Uncertainty Ratio — ILAC G8 / ANSI Z540.3
Guard_Band_w = U_expanded * guard_band_factor   // [unit] Guard band width
Acceptance_Limit = process_tolerance - Guard_Band_w   // [unit] Effective acceptance limit (A2LA / ILAC)
Instrument_In_Tolerance = ABS(current_error) <= process_tolerance   // [bool] Simple pass/fail (no guard band)
Instrument_Conforms_Guarded = ABS(current_error) <= Acceptance_Limit   // [bool] Guarded conformity decision
Calibration_Ratio_CR = U_expanded / process_tolerance   // [—] CR = 1/TUR — should be < 0.25 (4:1 rule)

days_minimum.error_msg: Days in service must be ≥ 1. Division by zero in drift rate calculation.

tolerance_positive.error_msg: Process tolerance cannot be zero or negative. Enter the half-range value (USL−LSL)/2.

uncertainty_vs_tolerance.error_msg: CRITICAL: Expanded measurement uncertainty exceeds the process tolerance. Conformity decisions are statistically meaningless — the instrument cannot determine if the process is in-spec. Lab recertification or instrument replacement required.

smart_warnings.message: CRITICAL DECISION RISK — TUR < 4:1: The 4:1 minimum Test Uncertainty Ratio is not met. Probability of false accept (in-tolerance equipment measuring out-of-spec parts as conforming) is statistically significant. ISO 17025 cl.7.8.6 requires this risk to be disclosed in the conformity statement.
EXTREME RISK — TUR < 2:1: False accept probability may exceed 5–15% depending on distribution. Guard band must be applied and expanded. Product liability exposure is severe. Internal audit required.

DRIFT EXCEEDS 50% OF TOLERANCE before next calibration is due. At the current drift rate, instrument will be out of tolerance before planned recalibration. Shorten calibration interval to maximum half of current interval.
High drift rate detected: Instrument drifts more than 1% of tolerance per 90 days. Current calibration interval may be too long. Review against measurement criticality and reduce interval.

smart_warnings.source: ILAC G8 Guard Band — Conditional Pass
