smart_warnings.message: CONDITIONAL CONFORMITY: Instrument is within raw tolerance but fails guarded acceptance limit. Under ILAC G8, this is a NON-CONFORM result when guard banding is applied. Measurements made since last calibration may contain false accepts.

OUT-OF-TOLERANCE FOUND: Instrument exceeded calibration specification. Mandatory action: (1) Quarantine all measurements made since last in-tolerance calibration, (2) Risk assessment of affected product/decisions, (3) Root cause analysis, (4) Customer notification if product was released.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: U_expanded > process_tolerance
fmea.effect: CRITICAL: Expanded measurement uncertainty exceeds the process tolerance. Conformity decisions are statistically meaningless — the instrument cannot determine if the process is in-spec. Lab recertification or instrument replacement required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL DECISION RISK — TUR < 4:1: The 4:1 minimum Test Uncertainty Ratio is not met. Probability of false accept (in-tolerance equipment measuring out-of-spec parts as conforming) is statistically significant. ISO 17025 cl.7.8.6 requires this risk to be disclosed in the conformity statement.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: TUR < 4

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME RISK — TUR < 2:1: False accept probability may exceed 5–15% depending on distribution. Guard band must be applied and expanded. Product liability exposure is severe. Internal audit required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: TUR < 2

fmea.failureMode: DRIFT EXCEEDS 50% OF TOLERANCE before next calibration is due. At the current drift rate, instrument will be out of tolerance before planned recalibration. Shorten calibration interval to maximum half of current interval.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Predicted_Drift_Next_Interval > process_tolerance * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: High drift rate detected: Instrument drifts more than 1% of tolerance per 90 days. Current calibration interval may be too long. Review against measurement criticality and reduce interval.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Drift_Rate_per_Day > ABS(process_tolerance) / 90

fmea.failureMode: CONDITIONAL CONFORMITY: Instrument is within raw tolerance but fails guarded acceptance limit. Under ILAC G8, this is a NON-CONFORM result when guard banding is applied. Measurements made since last calibration may contain false accepts.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ABS(current_error) > Acceptance_Limit && ABS(current_error) <= process_tolerance
fmea.control_measure: ILAC G8 Guard Band — Conditional Pass

fmea.failureMode: OUT-OF-TOLERANCE FOUND: Instrument exceeded calibration specification. Mandatory action: (1) Quarantine all measurements made since last in-tolerance calibration, (2) Risk assessment of affected product/decisions, (3) Root cause analysis, (4) Customer notification if product was released.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ABS(current_error) > process_tolerance

tool_name: CBAM Financial Exposure Calculator (EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism)
category: Environmental Engineering & Corporate Finance / GHG Compliance

inputs.name: CBAM-Covered Product Sector
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Total Annual Production Volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Volume Exported to EU (CBAM-liable)
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Scope 1 — Fuel Consumption (all fuels combined)
inputs.hint: Natural gas: multiply m³ × 0.0377 GJ/m³ | LPG: × 0.0474 | Coal: × 0.0264 per kg
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Scope 1 — Weighted Fuel Emission Factor
inputs.hint: Natural gas: 0.0561 | Coal: 0.0963 | LPG: 0.0630 | Fuel oil: 0.0779 — IPCC AR6
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Scope 1 — Direct Process Emissions (non-combustion)
inputs.hint: Steel: BF/BOF CO₂ from ore reduction | Cement: calcination CO₂ | Fertiliser: N₂O
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Scope 2 — Electricity Consumption
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Scope 2 — Grid Electricity Emission Factor
inputs.hint: Turkey 2023: 0.547 | Poland: 0.773 | Germany: 0.390 | France: 0.057 | IEA 2023
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: EU ETS Carbon Price (current)
inputs.hint: Check: ember-climate.org/data/carbon-price-viewer | 2024 avg: €60–70/t
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: EU Sector Free Allocation Benchmark
inputs.hint: Steel (hot-rolled): 1.328 | Cement clinker: 0.766 | Aluminium: 1.514 | Ammonia: 1.600 — EU Reg. 2021/447
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Carbon Tax / ETS Already Paid in Origin Country
inputs.hint: Turkey: no carbon tax (0) | UK ETS: market price | Other: national carbon price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: CBAM Phasing Factor (transitional)
inputs.hint: Full liability from 2026: 1.0 | CBAM fully operational after 2034 free allocation phase-out
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

formulas: Scope1_Combustion_tCO2e = scope1_fuel_consumption_gj * scope1_fuel_emission_factor   // [tCO₂e/year] Scope 1 combustion emissions — GHG Protocol
Scope2_tCO2e = scope2_electricity_kwh * scope2_grid_factor_kg_kwh / 1000   // [tCO₂e/year] Scope 2 location-based
Total_GHG_tCO2e = Scope1_Combustion_tCO2e + scope1_process_emissions_tco2e + Scope2_tCO2e   // [tCO₂e/year] Total Scope 1+2
Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne = Total_GHG_tCO2e / annual_production_tonnes   // [tCO₂e/t product] Carbon intensity
Excess_Emissions_rate = MAX(0, Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne - eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne)   // [tCO₂e/t] Excess above EU benchmark
Total_Excess_tCO2e = Excess_Emissions_rate * eu_export_volume_tonnes   // [tCO₂e/year] Total CBAM-liable excess
Gross_CBAM_Liability_EUR = Total_Excess_tCO2e * eu_ets_price_eur_tco2   // [EUR/year] Gross CBAM certificate cost
Local_Carbon_Already_Paid_EUR = Total_Excess_tCO2e * local_carbon_tax_eur_tco2   // [EUR/year] Deductible local carbon payments
Net_CBAM_Cost_EUR = MAX(0, (Gross_CBAM_Liability_EUR - Local_Carbon_Already_Paid_EUR) * cbam_phase_factor)   // [EUR/year] Net CBAM financial exposure
CBAM_Per_Tonne_Exported = eu_export_volume_tonnes > 0 ? Net_CBAM_Cost_EUR / eu_export_volume_tonnes : 0   // [EUR/tonne] CBAM surcharge per exported tonne
Carbon_Intensity_vs_Benchmark_Pct = (Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne / eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne) * 100   // [%] Performance vs EU benchmark (100% = at benchmark)

export_exceeds_production.error_msg: EU export volume cannot exceed total production volume. Data input error.

production_positive.error_msg: Annual production volume must be positive — division by zero in carbon intensity calculation.

benchmark_positive.error_msg: EU sector benchmark must be positive. Check EU Regulation 2021/447 for your product category benchmark value.

smart_warnings.message: CBAM EXPOSURE CONFIRMED: Your carbon intensity exceeds the EU sector benchmark. CBAM certificates must be purchased for every tonne exported to the EU above this threshold. This is a legal obligation from 2026 onwards. Decarbonisation investment or EU market exit must be evaluated.

MATERIAL FINANCIAL EXPOSURE: Annual CBAM liability exceeds €100,000. This represents a significant margin erosion risk for EU-destined exports. Quantify in CAPEX planning for decarbonisation. EU customers will increasingly demand lower-carbon supply chains.

smart_warnings.source: EU Green Deal Supply Chain Requirements
smart_warnings.message: EXTREME CARBON INTENSITY: Your product emits 50%+ more CO₂ than the EU benchmark. At current EU ETS trajectory (projected €100+/t by 2030), export competitiveness will be critically impaired. Immediate industrial decarbonisation roadmap required.

High-carbon electricity grid (>0.5 kgCO₂/kWh): Scope 2 emissions are a major CBAM driver. Corporate PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) with renewable energy can significantly reduce embedded carbon and improve CBAM position. Model the impact using market-based Scope 2 method.

No local carbon tax offset applicable. Countries with carbon pricing (UK, Switzerland, Korea) can deduct equivalent carbon costs from CBAM liability. Monitoring Turkey's carbon market development is recommended.

More than 50% of production exported to EU: High CBAM concentration risk. Any regulatory tightening of EU benchmarks or ETS price spike will have disproportionate financial impact. Diversify export markets or accelerate low-carbon transition.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: eu_export_volume_tonnes > annual_production_tonnes
fmea.effect: EU export volume cannot exceed total production volume. Data input error.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne <= 0
fmea.effect: EU sector benchmark must be positive. Check EU Regulation 2021/447 for your product category benchmark value.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CBAM EXPOSURE CONFIRMED: Your carbon intensity exceeds the EU sector benchmark. CBAM certificates must be purchased for every tonne exported to the EU above this threshold. This is a legal obligation from 2026 onwards. Decarbonisation investment or EU market exit must be evaluated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne > eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne

fmea.failureMode: MATERIAL FINANCIAL EXPOSURE: Annual CBAM liability exceeds €100,000. This represents a significant margin erosion risk for EU-destined exports. Quantify in CAPEX planning for decarbonisation. EU customers will increasingly demand lower-carbon supply chains.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Net_CBAM_Cost_EUR > 100000

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME CARBON INTENSITY: Your product emits 50%+ more CO₂ than the EU benchmark. At current EU ETS trajectory (projected €100+/t by 2030), export competitiveness will be critically impaired. Immediate industrial decarbonisation roadmap required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Carbon_Intensity_vs_Benchmark_Pct > 150
fmea.control_measure: EU Green Deal Supply Chain Requirements

fmea.failureMode: High-carbon electricity grid (>0.5 kgCO₂/kWh): Scope 2 emissions are a major CBAM driver. Corporate PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) with renewable energy can significantly reduce embedded carbon and improve CBAM position. Model the impact using market-based Scope 2 method.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scope2_grid_factor_kg_kwh > 0.5

fmea.failureMode: No local carbon tax offset applicable. Countries with carbon pricing (UK, Switzerland, Korea) can deduct equivalent carbon costs from CBAM liability. Monitoring Turkey's carbon market development is recommended.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: local_carbon_tax_eur_tco2 === 0

fmea.failureMode: More than 50% of production exported to EU: High CBAM concentration risk. Any regulatory tightening of EU benchmarks or ETS price spike will have disproportionate financial impact. Diversify export markets or accelerate low-carbon transition.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eu_export_volume_tonnes / annual_production_tonnes > 0.5

tool_name: Machining Chatter Stability, Surface Roughness & Tool Deflection Analysis

inputs.name: Cutting Speed (Vc)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Tool Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Feed per Tooth (fz)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Number of Cutting Edges (z)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tool Nose Radius (rε)
inputs.hint: From insert catalogue — required for Ra calculation (ISO 3002)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Axial Depth of Cut (ap)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Radial Depth of Cut (ae)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Tool Overhang Length (L)
inputs.hint: Distance from spindle nose to tool tip — critical for deflection
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Drawing Ra Specification (target)
inputs.hint: From engineering drawing: Ra 0.8 / Ra 1.6 / Ra 3.2 — ISO 1302
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Machine / Fixture Rigidity Class
options: HIGH — Heavy machining centre, short toolholder, vise
MEDIUM — Standard VMC, standard extension, milling fixture
LOW — Long overhang, thin wall part, weak fixture, worn spindle
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Rigidity Dynamic Factor (Kd)
inputs.hint: HIGH=1.0 | MEDIUM=1.4–1.8 | LOW=2.0–3.5 — amplifies Rz by chatter
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Toolholder / Shank Material
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Shank Elastic Modulus (E)
inputs.hint: Carbide: 580,000 | Steel: 210,000 | Heavy metal: 650,000
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Elastic modulus (steel): CoV=0.03

inputs.name: Estimated Tangential Cutting Force (Fc)
inputs.hint: From PRO_043 output or Kienzle calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: n_rpm = (1000 * cutting_speed_vc) / (3.14159 * tool_diameter_mm)   // [RPM] Spindle speed
Vf_feed_rate = feed_per_tooth_fz * num_teeth_z * n_rpm   // [mm/min] Table feed rate
Rz_theoretical = (POWER(feed_per_tooth_fz, 2) / (8 * nose_radius_mm)) * 1000   // [μm] Theoretical peak-to-valley roughness — ISO 3002
Rz_actual = Rz_theoretical * rigidity_factor   // [μm] Actual Rz incl. chatter amplification
Ra_approx = Rz_actual / 4.5   // [μm] Approximate Ra (end milling, k=4.5) — ISO 3002
L_D_ratio = overhang_length_mm / tool_diameter_mm   // [—] Overhang-to-diameter ratio — chatter risk indicator
I_moment = (3.14159 * POWER(tool_diameter_mm, 4)) / 64   // [mm⁴] Second moment of area — circular cross section
Tool_Deflection_mm = (cutting_force_fc_n * POWER(overhang_length_mm, 3)) / (3 * elastic_modulus_mpa * I_moment)   // [mm] Static tool tip deflection — cantilever beam model
Tooth_Passing_Freq_Hz = (n_rpm * num_teeth_z) / 60   // [Hz] Tooth passing frequency
Chatter_Risk_Index = L_D_ratio * rigidity_factor   // [—] Composite chatter risk — higher = worse
Dimensional_Error_um = Tool_Deflection_mm * 1000   // [μm] Dimensional error from deflection
Safe_fz_for_Ra = SQRT(8 * nose_radius_mm * ra_specification_um / 1000 / rigidity_factor)   // [mm/tooth] Max fz to achieve Ra spec

nose_radius_minimum.error_msg: Nose radius (rε) cannot be zero — division by zero in Ra calculation. Minimum: 0.01mm. Check insert catalogue.

ae_exceeds_diameter.error_msg: Radial depth (ae) exceeds tool diameter — geometrically impossible. ae must be ≤ D.

ap_exceeds_limit.error_msg: Axial depth (ap) exceeds 80% of tool diameter. Catastrophic tool breakage risk. Reduce ap.

rigidity_factor_range.error_msg: Dynamic rigidity factor (Kd) cannot be less than 1.0. Ideal (chatter-free) conditions = 1.0.

smart_warnings.message: SURFACE FINISH VIOLATION: Predicted Ra exceeds drawing specification. Reduce fz or use a wiper insert (larger rε). Safe fz for spec compliance is shown below.

CHATTER RISK HIGH — L/D > 4: At this overhang ratio, regenerative chatter is likely. Symptoms: harmonic surface marks, tool noise, premature wear. Mitigations: (1) Use anti-vibration toolholder, (2) Reduce ap by 30–40%, (3) Change spindle speed ±10% to shift from resonance zone.
DIMENSIONAL INACCURACY: Tool tip deflection exceeds 0.05mm (50μm). Workpiece dimensional tolerance will be violated. Reduce overhang, increase tool diameter, or reduce cutting force.
Elevated tool deflection (20–50μm): Monitor dimensional output. For tight tolerances (IT6 or better), this deflection will cause out-of-spec parts. Consider shorter toolholder or reduced Fc.
Tooth passing frequency > 1000 Hz: May excite spindle bearing natural frequencies. If chatter occurs, shift RPM by ±5–10% or change number of flutes to detune from structural resonance.

COMBINED HIGH-RISK: Weak fixture + long overhang simultaneously. Predicted Ra and deflection values are unreliable — actual results may be significantly worse. Mandatory: reduce overhang to L/D ≤ 3 before proceeding.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: radial_depth_ae > tool_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Radial depth (ae) exceeds tool diameter — geometrically impossible. ae must be ≤ D.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: axial_depth_ap > tool_diameter_mm * 0.8
fmea.effect: Axial depth (ap) exceeds 80% of tool diameter. Catastrophic tool breakage risk. Reduce ap.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rigidity_factor < 1.0
fmea.effect: Dynamic rigidity factor (Kd) cannot be less than 1.0. Ideal (chatter-free) conditions = 1.0.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SURFACE FINISH VIOLATION: Predicted Ra exceeds drawing specification. Reduce fz or use a wiper insert (larger rε). Safe fz for spec compliance is shown below.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_approx > ra_specification_um

fmea.failureMode: CHATTER RISK HIGH — L/D > 4: At this overhang ratio, regenerative chatter is likely. Symptoms: harmonic surface marks, tool noise, premature wear. Mitigations: (1) Use anti-vibration toolholder, (2) Reduce ap by 30–40%, (3) Change spindle speed ±10% to shift from resonance zone.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_D_ratio > 4

fmea.failureMode: DIMENSIONAL INACCURACY: Tool tip deflection exceeds 0.05mm (50μm). Workpiece dimensional tolerance will be violated. Reduce overhang, increase tool diameter, or reduce cutting force.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tool_Deflection_mm > 0.05

fmea.failureMode: Elevated tool deflection (20–50μm): Monitor dimensional output. For tight tolerances (IT6 or better), this deflection will cause out-of-spec parts. Consider shorter toolholder or reduced Fc.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tool_Deflection_mm > 0.02 && Tool_Deflection_mm <= 0.05

fmea.failureMode: Tooth passing frequency > 1000 Hz: May excite spindle bearing natural frequencies. If chatter occurs, shift RPM by ±5–10% or change number of flutes to detune from structural resonance.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tooth_Passing_Freq_Hz > 1000

fmea.failureMode: COMBINED HIGH-RISK: Weak fixture + long overhang simultaneously. Predicted Ra and deflection values are unreliable — actual results may be significantly worse. Mandatory: reduce overhang to L/D ≤ 3 before proceeding.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rigidity_factor > 2.0 && L_D_ratio > 3

tool_name: Bolted Joint Tightening Torque, Preload & Von Mises Utilisation (VDI 2230)

inputs.name: Bolt Property Class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bolt Proof / Yield Strength (Rp0.2)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Yield/ultimate strength (steel): CoV=0.07

inputs.name: Nominal Bolt Diameter (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Thread Pitch (P)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Thread Friction Coefficient (μG)
inputs.hint: Dry steel: 0.14–0.18 | Lightly oiled: 0.10–0.12 | MoS2 lubricated: 0.04–0.08 | Zinc-plated dry: 0.12–0.18
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Bearing Face Friction Coefficient (μK)
inputs.hint: Same surface condition as thread friction — typically equal to μG
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Effective Bearing Diameter (dw)
inputs.hint: Hex bolt: dw ≈ 1.5×d | Socket head: dw ≈ 1.4×d | Washer used: washer OD
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Target Preload Utilisation of Yield
inputs.hint: VDI 2230 standard: 90% for torque-controlled tightening | 100% for yield-controlled (TTY)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Tightening Factor (αA) — Scatter
inputs.hint: VDI 2230 Table A7 — accounts for preload scatter
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tightening Factor Numerical Value (αA)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Joint Loading Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: d2_pitch_diam = nominal_diameter_mm - (0.649519 * thread_pitch_mm)   // [mm] Pitch diameter — ISO 724
d3_minor_diam = nominal_diameter_mm - (1.226869 * thread_pitch_mm)   // [mm] Minor (root) diameter — ISO 724
A_stress = (3.14159 / 4) * POWER((d2_pitch_diam + d3_minor_diam) / 2, 2)   // [mm²] Stress area — ISO 898-1
F_preload_max = (preload_utilisation_pct / 100) * yield_strength_mpa * A_stress   // [N] Maximum assembly preload
F_preload_min = F_preload_max / alpha_val   // [N] Minimum preload after scatter — VDI 2230
lead_angle_rad = 3.14159 * thread_pitch_mm / (3.14159 * d2_pitch_diam)   // [rad] Thread helix angle
thread_flank_angle_rad = (30 * 3.14159) / 180   // [rad] ISO metric half-angle = 30°
friction_thread_eff = friction_thread_mu_g / (3.14159 * POWER(POWER(1, 0.5 - POWER(lead_angle_rad, 0.5)), 0.5))   // simplified effective thread friction
T_thread = F_preload_max * (d2_pitch_diam / 2) * ((thread_pitch_mm / (3.14159 * d2_pitch_diam)) + friction_thread_mu_g / 0.866)   // [N·mm] Thread torque component
T_bearing = F_preload_max * friction_bearing_mu_k * (bearing_diameter_dw_mm / 2)   // [N·mm] Bearing face torque component
T_tightening_Nmm = T_thread + T_bearing   // [N·mm] Total tightening torque
T_tightening_Nm = T_tightening_Nmm / 1000   // [N·m] Tightening torque
sigma_tensile = F_preload_max / A_stress   // [MPa] Tensile stress in shank
tau_torsion = (T_thread * (d2_pitch_diam / 2)) / (0.196 * POWER(d3_minor_diam, 3))   // [MPa] Torsional shear stress
sigma_vonMises = SQRT(POWER(sigma_tensile, 2) + 3 * POWER(tau_torsion, 2))   // [MPa] Combined Von Mises stress — VDI 2230 Eq.(R9)
Utilisation_pct = (sigma_vonMises / yield_strength_mpa) * 100   // [%] Yield utilisation
K_nut_factor = T_tightening_Nmm / (F_preload_max * nominal_diameter_mm)   // [—] K-factor (Nut factor) — torque verification

pitch_vs_diameter.error_msg: Thread pitch cannot equal or exceed nominal diameter — geometrically impossible for ISO metric threads.

bearing_diameter_minimum.error_msg: Bearing face diameter (dw) must be greater than nominal diameter. Typical: dw ≈ 1.4–1.7 × d.

preload_pct_range.error_msg: Preload utilisation > 95%: Torsional component will cause yielding during tightening. VDI 2230 maximum for torque wrench: 90%. Use yield-controlled (TTY) method if 100% is required.

yield_strength_grade.error_msg: Yield strength > 1400 MPa: Exceeds standard property class range (12.9 = 1080 MPa). Hydrogen embrittlement risk is significant above 1200 MPa. Verify material specification.

smart_warnings.message: BOLT YIELDS DURING ASSEMBLY: Combined Von Mises stress exceeds yield strength. Bolt will neck and lose preload or fracture during tightening. Actions: (1) Lubricate to reduce μG and μK, (2) Reduce preload utilisation target to 80%, (3) Use higher-grade bolt.
Near-yield tightening: Utilisation 90–100%. Any friction variation above nominal will cause bolt yield. Calibrated torque wrench mandatory. Re-verify after first thermal cycle.

High thread friction (μG > 0.16): Most of applied torque is consumed overcoming friction — actual preload achieved will be much lower than calculated. Use thread lubricant or anti-seize compound. Re-measure μ with torque-angle method.

High scatter factor (αA > 1.6): Actual preload may be only 60% of target. Joint may not achieve required clamp force. Switch to hydraulic tensioning (αA = 1.2) or angle-controlled tightening (αA = 1.05).

smart_warnings.condition: joint_type === 'Dynamic / Fatigue — vibration, alternating load' && preload_utilisation_pct < 80
smart_warnings.message: FATIGUE JOINT — INSUFFICIENT PRELOAD: Dynamic joints require high preload to prevent fretting and self-loosening (Junker effect). Preload below 80% Rp0.2 with vibration loading is a known failure mode. Use thread-locking compound or prevailing torque nut.

High K-factor (>0.25): Friction-dominated joint. Standard torque tables assume K≈0.20. Your actual preload from nameplate torque values may be 25–40% lower than expected. Re-calibrate using measured K-factor for this specific surface combination.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: thread_pitch_mm >= nominal_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Thread pitch cannot equal or exceed nominal diameter — geometrically impossible for ISO metric threads.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bearing_diameter_dw_mm < nominal_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Bearing face diameter (dw) must be greater than nominal diameter. Typical: dw ≈ 1.4–1.7 × d.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: preload_utilisation_pct > 95
fmea.effect: Preload utilisation > 95%: Torsional component will cause yielding during tightening. VDI 2230 maximum for torque wrench: 90%. Use yield-controlled (TTY) method if 100% is required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: yield_strength_mpa > 1400
fmea.effect: Yield strength > 1400 MPa: Exceeds standard property class range (12.9 = 1080 MPa). Hydrogen embrittlement risk is significant above 1200 MPa. Verify material specification.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: BOLT YIELDS DURING ASSEMBLY: Combined Von Mises stress exceeds yield strength. Bolt will neck and lose preload or fracture during tightening. Actions: (1) Lubricate to reduce μG and μK, (2) Reduce preload utilisation target to 80%, (3) Use higher-grade bolt.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Utilisation_pct > 100

fmea.failureMode: Near-yield tightening: Utilisation 90–100%. Any friction variation above nominal will cause bolt yield. Calibrated torque wrench mandatory. Re-verify after first thermal cycle.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Utilisation_pct > 90 && Utilisation_pct <= 100

fmea.failureMode: High thread friction (μG > 0.16): Most of applied torque is consumed overcoming friction — actual preload achieved will be much lower than calculated. Use thread lubricant or anti-seize compound. Re-measure μ with torque-angle method.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: friction_thread_mu_g > 0.16

fmea.failureMode: High scatter factor (αA > 1.6): Actual preload may be only 60% of target. Joint may not achieve required clamp force. Switch to hydraulic tensioning (αA = 1.2) or angle-controlled tightening (αA = 1.05).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: alpha_val > 1.6

fmea.failureMode: FATIGUE JOINT — INSUFFICIENT PRELOAD: Dynamic joints require high preload to prevent fretting and self-loosening (Junker effect). Preload below 80% Rp0.2 with vibration loading is a known failure mode. Use thread-locking compound or prevailing torque nut.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: joint_type === 'Dynamic / Fatigue — vibration, alternating load' && preload_utilisation_pct < 80

fmea.failureMode: High K-factor (>0.25): Friction-dominated joint. Standard torque tables assume K≈0.20. Your actual preload from nameplate torque values may be 25–40% lower than expected. Re-calibrate using measured K-factor for this specific surface combination.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: K_nut_factor > 0.25

tool_name: CNC Cycle Time Breakdown, Spindle Utilisation & OEE-Spindle Analysis

inputs.name: Cutting Speed (Vc)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Feed per Tooth (fz)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Tool Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Number of Cutting Edges (z)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Total Programmed Cutting Length
inputs.hint: Sum of all tool path cutting segments from NC program
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Approach + Overtravel per Pass
inputs.hint: Tool entry ramp + exit overtravel — typically 0.5×D to 1×D per pass
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Total Rapid (G00) Travel Distance
inputs.hint: Non-cutting repositioning moves — from NC program or simulation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Machine Rapid Traverse Rate (G00)
inputs.hint: From machine specification — typically 15,000–40,000 mm/min
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Number of Tool Changes
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tool-to-Tool Change Time (T-T)
inputs.hint: From machine spec — BT40: 3–6s | HSK63: 2–4s | ATC with arm: 4–8s
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Part Load / Unload Time
inputs.hint: Manual: 1–3 min | Pallet changer: 0.1–0.3 min | Robot: 0.2–0.5 min
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: In-Process Probing / Inspection Time
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Parts per Fixture Setup
inputs.hint: Multi-part fixturing reduces effective cycle time per part
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Machine Hourly Rate (fully burdened)
inputs.hint: Include depreciation, maintenance, energy, floor space, operator — typically $40–150/h
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Machine Maximum Spindle Speed
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

formulas: n_rpm = (1000 * cutting_speed_vc) / (3.14159 * tool_diameter_mm)   // [RPM] Required spindle speed
n_actual = MIN(n_rpm, machine_max_rpm)   // [RPM] Actual spindle speed (capped at machine limit)
Vc_actual = (n_actual * 3.14159 * tool_diameter_mm) / 1000   // [m/min] Actual cutting speed
Vf_feed_rate = feed_per_tooth_fz * num_teeth_z * n_actual   // [mm/min] Table feed rate
T_cut_min = (total_cut_length_mm + approach_overtravel_mm) / Vf_feed_rate   // [min] Pure cutting time
T_rapid_min = rapid_distance_mm / rapid_velocity_mm_min   // [min] Rapid positioning time
T_toolchange_min = (num_tool_changes * tool_change_time_sec) / 60   // [min] ATC time
T_aux_min = load_unload_time_min + probing_inspection_min   // [min] Auxiliary non-cutting time
T_cycle_gross_min = T_cut_min + T_rapid_min + T_toolchange_min + T_aux_min   // [min] Gross cycle time
T_cycle_per_part_min = T_cycle_gross_min / parts_per_fixture   // [min] Cycle time per part
Spindle_Utilisation_pct = (T_cut_min / T_cycle_gross_min) * 100   // [%] Cutting time fraction
OEE_Spindle = Spindle_Utilisation_pct * 0.95 * 0.98   // [%] OEE-spindle (×avail×quality approx)
Cost_per_Part_USD = (T_cycle_per_part_min / 60) * machine_hourly_rate_usd   // [USD] Machining cost per part
Parts_per_Hour = 60 / T_cycle_per_part_min   // [parts/h] Production rate
RPM_Utilisation_pct = (n_actual / machine_max_rpm) * 100   // [%] Spindle speed utilisation

tool_diameter_minimum.error_msg: Tool diameter cannot be zero — division by zero in RPM calculation.

rapid_velocity_minimum.error_msg: Rapid traverse rate cannot be zero or unrealistically low. Minimum 100 mm/min.

feed_rate_physical.error_msg: Calculated feed rate exceeds 100,000 mm/min — physically impossible. Check Vc, fz, z, and D inputs.

smart_warnings.message: CRITICAL SPINDLE WASTE: Only cutting for less than 30% of cycle. Machine spends over 70% of time on non-value-adding operations (rapids, ATC, load/unload). Immediate actions: (1) Zero-point clamping system, (2) Pre-set offline tooling, (3) Optimise G00 paths, (4) Pallet changer evaluation.
Low spindle utilisation (30–50%): Significant non-cutting time losses. Target is >65% for general machining. Review ATC time, rapid distances, and fixture strategy.

Tool change time >30% of cutting time: ATC is a major cycle-time driver. Evaluate: (1) Reduce tool count via multi-function tools, (2) Sub-spindle or twin-pallet, (3) Upgrade to faster ATC (arm-type vs. umbrella).

Required RPM exceeds machine maximum. Actual cutting speed reduced. Recalculate with Vc_actual shown. Consider: (1) Smaller tool diameter, (2) Higher-RPM spindle machine, (3) Reduce cutting speed to match machine limit.
Auxiliary time (load/unload + probing) exceeds actual cutting time. Automation of part handling or in-process gauging should be evaluated for ROI.

Machine spindle speed capability underused (<40% of max RPM). Machine may be oversized for this operation, or cutting parameters are conservative. Review tool and material data for speed increase potential.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Vf_feed_rate > 100000
fmea.effect: Calculated feed rate exceeds 100,000 mm/min — physically impossible. Check Vc, fz, z, and D inputs.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL SPINDLE WASTE: Only cutting for less than 30% of cycle. Machine spends over 70% of time on non-value-adding operations (rapids, ATC, load/unload). Immediate actions: (1) Zero-point clamping system, (2) Pre-set offline tooling, (3) Optimise G00 paths, (4) Pallet changer evaluation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Spindle_Utilisation_pct < 30

fmea.failureMode: Low spindle utilisation (30–50%): Significant non-cutting time losses. Target is >65% for general machining. Review ATC time, rapid distances, and fixture strategy.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Spindle_Utilisation_pct >= 30 && Spindle_Utilisation_pct < 50

fmea.failureMode: Tool change time >30% of cutting time: ATC is a major cycle-time driver. Evaluate: (1) Reduce tool count via multi-function tools, (2) Sub-spindle or twin-pallet, (3) Upgrade to faster ATC (arm-type vs. umbrella).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_toolchange_min > T_cut_min * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Required RPM exceeds machine maximum. Actual cutting speed reduced. Recalculate with Vc_actual shown. Consider: (1) Smaller tool diameter, (2) Higher-RPM spindle machine, (3) Reduce cutting speed to match machine limit.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm > machine_max_rpm

fmea.failureMode: Auxiliary time (load/unload + probing) exceeds actual cutting time. Automation of part handling or in-process gauging should be evaluated for ROI.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_aux_min > T_cut_min

fmea.failureMode: Machine spindle speed capability underused (<40% of max RPM). Machine may be oversized for this operation, or cutting parameters are conservative. Review tool and material data for speed increase potential.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: RPM_Utilisation_pct < 40

tool_name: CNC Machining True Unit Cost (TCO) — Material, Tooling, Overhead & Scrap

inputs.name: Raw Material Billet / Blank Volume
inputs.hint: Billet volume before machining — NOT finished part volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Material Density (ρ)
inputs.hint: Steel: 7850 | Aluminium: 2700 | Titanium: 4430 | Brass: 8500 | Cast iron: 7200
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Raw Material Unit Price
inputs.hint: Current market price — Al 6061: $2–4 | Steel 4140: $1–2 | Ti6Al4V: $25–45 | Inconel 718: $60–120
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Finished Part Net Volume
inputs.hint: Volume of finished part — used to calculate buy-to-fly ratio
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Machine Hourly Rate (fully burdened)
inputs.hint: Depreciation + maintenance + energy + operator + floor space
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Total Cycle Time per Part
inputs.hint: From PRO_021 output or time study
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Insert / Cutting Edge Cost
inputs.hint: Purchase price per single insert (not per cutting edge)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Number of Cutting Edges per Insert
inputs.hint: CNMG/SNMG=4 | WNMG=6 | Round insert=1 | Milling insert=2–4
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Tool Life per Cutting Edge (T)
inputs.hint: Measured or from Taylor equation — change at VB=0.3mm (ISO 3685)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Coolant System Cost Rate
inputs.hint: Coolant consumption + treatment + disposal — typically $1–5/h
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Process Scrap / Rework Rate
inputs.hint: First-pass yield defect rate — target <1% for mature processes
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Factory Overhead Absorption Rate
inputs.hint: Indirect costs absorbed to product: 20–60% typical job shop
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Annual Production Volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

formulas: Raw_Material_kg = raw_material_volume_m3 * material_density_kg_m3   // [kg] Raw billet weight
Material_Cost_USD = Raw_Material_kg * material_price_usd_kg   // [USD] Raw material cost per part
Buy_to_Fly_Ratio = raw_material_volume_m3 / finished_part_volume_m3   // [—] Billet-to-part volume ratio — waste indicator
Chip_Weight_kg = (raw_material_volume_m3 - finished_part_volume_m3) * material_density_kg_m3   // [kg] Swarf weight
Machining_Cost_USD = (cycle_time_min / 60) * machine_hourly_rate_usd   // [USD] Machine time cost
Cost_per_Edge_USD = insert_cost_usd / cutting_edges_per_insert   // [USD] Cost per usable cutting edge
Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD = (cycle_time_min / tool_life_per_edge_min) * Cost_per_Edge_USD   // [USD] Tooling consumed per part
Coolant_Cost_per_Part_USD = (cycle_time_min / 60) * coolant_cost_usd_hr   // [USD] Coolant cost per part
Direct_Cost_USD = Material_Cost_USD + Machining_Cost_USD + Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD + Coolant_Cost_per_Part_USD   // [USD] Total direct cost
Overhead_Cost_USD = Direct_Cost_USD * (overhead_pct / 100)   // [USD] Absorbed overhead
Pre_Scrap_Cost_USD = Direct_Cost_USD + Overhead_Cost_USD   // [USD] Cost before scrap penalty
Scrap_Penalty_USD = Pre_Scrap_Cost_USD * (scrap_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD] Scrap amortised cost
Total_Unit_Cost_USD = Pre_Scrap_Cost_USD + Scrap_Penalty_USD   // [USD] True unit manufacturing cost
Material_Cost_Fraction_pct = (Material_Cost_USD / Total_Unit_Cost_USD) * 100   // [%] Material cost share
Tooling_Cost_Fraction_pct = (Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD / Total_Unit_Cost_USD) * 100   // [%] Tooling cost share
Annual_Cost_USD = annual_volume_parts > 0 ? Total_Unit_Cost_USD * annual_volume_parts : 0   // [USD/year] Annual cost

tool_life_minimum.error_msg: Tool life cannot be zero — division by zero in tooling cost calculation.

cutting_edges_minimum.error_msg: Cutting edges per insert must be at least 1.

finished_vs_raw_volume.error_msg: Finished part volume cannot be equal to or larger than raw billet volume. Material is removed during machining.

buy_to_fly_extreme.error_msg: Buy-to-fly ratio > 20: Over 95% of material becomes swarf. Verify volumes. For aerospace titanium forgings, ratios up to 10 are common — above 20 is exceptional.

smart_warnings.message: TOOLING COST EXCEEDS MACHINE TIME COST: Cutting speed is too high — tools are being consumed faster than economically optimal. Reduce Vc to extend tool life. Optimal Vc for minimum cost (Vcopt) is typically 20–30% below Vcmax.

High buy-to-fly ratio (>5): More than 80% of raw material becomes chip. Material waste is a dominant cost driver. Evaluate: (1) Near-net-shape forging or casting, (2) Additive manufacturing + hybrid machining, (3) Billet size optimisation.
Material cost >60% of total unit cost: Material is the dominant cost element. Price negotiation, alternative alloys, or supply chain changes will have greater impact than process optimisation.
Scrap rate > 5%: Significant quality cost. At this rate, scrap penalty is adding meaningful cost per part. Root cause analysis (8D / Ishikawa) required. Target: <1% for mature CNC processes.
Scrap rate 1–5%: Above world-class target. Each percentage point of scrap adds direct cost penalty. SPC implementation and tooling condition monitoring recommended.

Tool life < 15 minutes per edge: Very short tool life indicates aggressive cutting conditions or workpiece hardness issues. Economic tool life target is typically 20–45 min. Reduce Vc or check for built-up edge (BUE) conditions.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: finished_part_volume_m3 >= raw_material_volume_m3
fmea.effect: Finished part volume cannot be equal to or larger than raw billet volume. Material is removed during machining.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Buy_to_Fly_Ratio > 20
fmea.effect: Buy-to-fly ratio > 20: Over 95% of material becomes swarf. Verify volumes. For aerospace titanium forgings, ratios up to 10 are common — above 20 is exceptional.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TOOLING COST EXCEEDS MACHINE TIME COST: Cutting speed is too high — tools are being consumed faster than economically optimal. Reduce Vc to extend tool life. Optimal Vc for minimum cost (Vcopt) is typically 20–30% below Vcmax.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD > Machining_Cost_USD

fmea.failureMode: High buy-to-fly ratio (>5): More than 80% of raw material becomes chip. Material waste is a dominant cost driver. Evaluate: (1) Near-net-shape forging or casting, (2) Additive manufacturing + hybrid machining, (3) Billet size optimisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Buy_to_Fly_Ratio > 5

fmea.failureMode: Material cost >60% of total unit cost: Material is the dominant cost element. Price negotiation, alternative alloys, or supply chain changes will have greater impact than process optimisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Material_Cost_Fraction_pct > 60

fmea.failureMode: Scrap rate > 5%: Significant quality cost. At this rate, scrap penalty is adding meaningful cost per part. Root cause analysis (8D / Ishikawa) required. Target: <1% for mature CNC processes.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scrap_rate_pct > 5

fmea.failureMode: Scrap rate 1–5%: Above world-class target. Each percentage point of scrap adds direct cost penalty. SPC implementation and tooling condition monitoring recommended.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scrap_rate_pct > 1 && scrap_rate_pct <= 5

fmea.failureMode: Tool life < 15 minutes per edge: Very short tool life indicates aggressive cutting conditions or workpiece hardness issues. Economic tool life target is typically 20–45 min. Reduce Vc or check for built-up edge (BUE) conditions.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: tool_life_per_edge_min < 15

tool_name: Process Capability (Cp / Cpk / Pp / Ppk), PPM Defect Rate & Quality Loss Function

inputs.name: Upper Specification Limit (USL)
inputs.hint: From engineering drawing or customer specification
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification limit, zero uncertainty

inputs.name: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)
inputs.hint: From engineering drawing or customer specification
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification limit, zero uncertainty

inputs.name: Process Mean (X̄)
inputs.hint: Grand average of sample measurements — from SPC chart or data set
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Within-Subgroup (Short-Term) Std Dev (σ̂)
inputs.hint: σ̂ = R̄/d2 from X̄-R chart — represents inherent process variation only
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Overall (Long-Term) Std Dev (s)
inputs.hint: Sample std dev of all data — for Pp/Ppk calculation. If blank, Cpk = Ppk assumed.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Subgroup Sample Size (n)
inputs.hint: AIAG SPC: recommended n=5 for X̄-R | n=10 for X̄-S
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Total Number of Samples Measured (N)
inputs.hint: AIAG SPC: minimum 25 subgroups × n samples for valid Cpk
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Daily Production Volume
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Cost per Defective Part (scrap + rework + warranty)
inputs.hint: Include: scrap material + rework labour + warranty + customer return penalty
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Target Cpk Requirement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Target Cpk Numerical Value
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Tolerance_Band = usl - lsl   // [unit] Total tolerance width
Cp = Tolerance_Band / (6 * within_sigma)   // [—] Process capability ratio (potential)
Z_USL = (usl - process_mean) / within_sigma   // [—] Distance to USL in sigma units
Z_LSL = (process_mean - lsl) / within_sigma   // [—] Distance to LSL in sigma units
Cpk = MIN(Z_USL, Z_LSL) / 3   // [—] Process capability index (actual) — AIAG SPC
Pp = overall_sigma > 0 ? Tolerance_Band / (6 * overall_sigma) : Cp   // [—] Process performance ratio (long-term)
Ppk = overall_sigma > 0 ? MIN((usl - process_mean) / (3 * overall_sigma), (process_mean - lsl) / (3 * overall_sigma)) : Cpk   // [—] Process performance index
P_defect_USL = 1 - NORMSDIST(Z_USL)   // [—] Fraction above USL
P_defect_LSL = NORMSDIST(-ABS(Z_LSL))   // [—] Fraction below LSL
Total_PPM = (P_defect_USL + P_defect_LSL) * 1000000   // [PPM] Defects per million opportunities
Sigma_Level_ST = Cpk * 3   // [σ] Short-term sigma level (without 1.5σ shift)
Sigma_Level_LT = Cpk * 3 - 1.5   // [σ] Long-term sigma level (with 1.5σ Motorola shift)
Daily_Defects = (Total_PPM / 1000000) * daily_production_volume   // [parts/day] Expected daily defects
Daily_Quality_Cost_USD = Daily_Defects * cost_per_defective_usd   // [USD/day] Daily quality loss
Annual_Quality_Cost_USD = Daily_Quality_Cost_USD * 250   // [USD/year] Annual quality cost (250 working days)
Cpk_Gap = target_cpk_val - Cpk   // [—] Gap to target (positive = improvement needed)
Centering_Index = 1 - ABS(2 * process_mean - usl - lsl) / Tolerance_Band   // [—] Process centering ratio (1.0 = perfectly centred)

standards: ASQ Six Sigma Body of Knowledge

usl_lsl_check.error_msg: LSL must be strictly less than USL. Check specification limits — bilateral tolerance requires LSL < target < USL.

std_dev_positive.error_msg: Standard deviation cannot be zero or negative. Zero variation is physically impossible in any real process.

mean_outside_spec.error_msg: Process mean is outside specification limits. PPM = 500,000+. Process is fundamentally out of control — all output may be non-conforming. Stop production and investigate immediately.

sample_minimum.error_msg: AIAG SPC requires minimum 25 subgroups for statistically valid Cpk. Current sample insufficient — Cpk estimate unreliable.

smart_warnings.message: PROCESS INCAPABLE — Cpk < 1.0: Process cannot meet specification. Defect rate is measured in percent, not PPM. Immediate containment (100% inspection) mandatory. Root cause analysis and process redesign required before resuming normal production.

CAPABILITY BELOW REQUIREMENT: Cpk is below the required target. Customer PPAP submission will be rejected. For automotive (IATF 16949): Cpk ≥ 1.33 is mandatory for production approval. Variance reduction required.

PROCESS OFF-CENTRE: Cp is significantly higher than Cpk. Process has good potential width but is shifted from nominal. Re-centering alone (tool offset, fixture adjustment) could reduce PPM dramatically without improving process variation.

smart_warnings.source: AIAG SPC — Cp vs Pp Analysis
smart_warnings.message: SIGNIFICANT Cpk–Ppk GAP (>0.3): Long-term variation substantially exceeds short-term. Special causes (tool wear, material lot changes, operator shifts, temperature drift) are present. SPC control chart review and MSA re-evaluation required.

Annual quality cost exceeds $50,000: This Cpk level is generating significant financial loss. Investment in process improvement, SPC implementation, or machine capability upgrade will deliver measurable ROI. Present to management with this annual cost figure.

Poor process centering (index < 0.70): Mean is more than 30% of tolerance from nominal. Asymmetric defect distribution — one side generating significantly more rejects. Investigate systematic bias: fixture wear, tool offset error, or raw material dimensional shift.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lsl >= usl
fmea.effect: LSL must be strictly less than USL. Check specification limits — bilateral tolerance requires LSL < target < USL.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: process_mean > usl || process_mean < lsl
fmea.effect: Process mean is outside specification limits. PPM = 500,000+. Process is fundamentally out of control — all output may be non-conforming. Stop production and investigate immediately.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: total_samples_N < 25
fmea.effect: AIAG SPC requires minimum 25 subgroups for statistically valid Cpk. Current sample insufficient — Cpk estimate unreliable.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: PROCESS INCAPABLE — Cpk < 1.0: Process cannot meet specification. Defect rate is measured in percent, not PPM. Immediate containment (100% inspection) mandatory. Root cause analysis and process redesign required before resuming normal production.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: CAPABILITY BELOW REQUIREMENT: Cpk is below the required target. Customer PPAP submission will be rejected. For automotive (IATF 16949): Cpk ≥ 1.33 is mandatory for production approval. Variance reduction required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk >= 1.0 && Cpk < target_cpk_val

fmea.failureMode: PROCESS OFF-CENTRE: Cp is significantly higher than Cpk. Process has good potential width but is shifted from nominal. Re-centering alone (tool offset, fixture adjustment) could reduce PPM dramatically without improving process variation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cp > Cpk * 1.33

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT Cpk–Ppk GAP (>0.3): Long-term variation substantially exceeds short-term. Special causes (tool wear, material lot changes, operator shifts, temperature drift) are present. SPC control chart review and MSA re-evaluation required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk > 0 && Ppk > 0 && (Cpk - Ppk) > 0.3
fmea.control_measure: AIAG SPC — Cp vs Pp Analysis

fmea.failureMode: Annual quality cost exceeds $50,000: This Cpk level is generating significant financial loss. Investment in process improvement, SPC implementation, or machine capability upgrade will deliver measurable ROI. Present to management with this annual cost figure.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_Quality_Cost_USD > 50000

fmea.failureMode: Poor process centering (index < 0.70): Mean is more than 30% of tolerance from nominal. Asymmetric defect distribution — one side generating significantly more rejects. Investigate systematic bias: fixture wear, tool offset error, or raw material dimensional shift.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Centering_Index < 0.7

tool_name: CPM Schedule Delay, Liquidated Damages & Acceleration (Crashing) Analysis

inputs.name: Contract Planned Duration
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Forecast / Actual Duration
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Excusable Delay — Force Majeure, Client-Caused
inputs.hint: Delays entitling EOT (Extension of Time) under contract — weather, client instruction, force majeure
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Compensable Delay — Client-Caused, Recoverable
inputs.hint: Client-caused delays for which contractor may claim additional cost (prolongation cost)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Liquidated Damages Rate
inputs.hint: From contract clause — pre-agreed LD rate per day of non-excusable delay
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: LD Cap (% of Contract Value)
inputs.hint: Most contracts cap LD at 5–15% of contract value — FIDIC: typically 10%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Total Contract Value
inputs.hint: Required to apply LD cap
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Acceleration Cost per Day Recovered
inputs.hint: Overtime + additional resources + expediting — marginal cost to compress 1 critical path day
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Maximum Compressible Days (Critical Path Limit)
inputs.hint: Physical limit of schedule compression — beyond this, additional resources yield no time saving
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Contractor Prolongation Cost (site overhead/day)
inputs.hint: Site overhead rate per day: site manager, equipment standing, temporary facilities
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Disruption / Productivity Loss Cost
inputs.hint: Loss of productivity from concurrent delays — Measured Mile or Earned Value method
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: Gross_Delay_days = MAX(0, forecast_duration_days - planned_duration_days)   // [days] Total schedule overrun
NonExcusable_Delay_days = MAX(0, Gross_Delay_days - excusable_delay_days - compensable_delay_days)   // [days] Contractor-culpable delay
Base_LD_Exposure_USD = NonExcusable_Delay_days * daily_ld_rate_usd   // [USD] LD before cap
LD_Cap_USD = contract_value_usd > 0 ? contract_value_usd * (ld_cap_pct / 100) : 999999999   // [USD] Maximum LD payable
Capped_LD_USD = MIN(Base_LD_Exposure_USD, LD_Cap_USD)   // [USD] LD after contractual cap
Optimal_Crash_Days = daily_ld_rate_usd > crash_cost_per_day_usd ? MIN(NonExcusable_Delay_days, max_crashable_days) : 0   // [days] Economically optimal days to crash
Total_Crash_Cost_USD = Optimal_Crash_Days * crash_cost_per_day_usd   // [USD] Total acceleration cost
Residual_LD_USD = MIN((NonExcusable_Delay_days - Optimal_Crash_Days) * daily_ld_rate_usd, LD_Cap_USD)   // [USD] LD after crashing
Net_Financial_Impact_USD = Total_Crash_Cost_USD + Residual_LD_USD   // [USD] Minimum total cost scenario
Savings_From_Crashing_USD = Capped_LD_USD - Net_Financial_Impact_USD   // [USD] Financial benefit of acceleration
Prolongation_Claim_USD = compensable_delay_days * prolongation_cost_per_day_usd + disruption_cost_usd   // [USD] Recoverable prolongation claim
Net_Contractor_Exposure_USD = Capped_LD_USD - Prolongation_Claim_USD   // [USD] Net exposure after claims offset
Crash_ROI_ratio = Total_Crash_Cost_USD > 0 ? Savings_From_Crashing_USD / Total_Crash_Cost_USD : 0   // [—] Return on acceleration investment

planned_duration_minimum.error_msg: Planned duration must be at least 1 day.

excusable_exceeds_delay.error_msg: Excusable delay cannot exceed total gross delay. Check inputs — contractor may be entitled to EOT only up to the actual overrun.

crash_exceeds_delay.error_msg: Maximum crashable days exceeds total delay. You cannot recover more time than the delay incurred.

smart_warnings.message: CRASHING NOT ECONOMICAL: Acceleration cost per day exceeds LD rate. Paying liquidated damages is financially preferable to accelerating — unless client relationship, repeat business, or reputational risk justifies the premium.

smart_warnings.source: FIDIC / Contract Law — LD Cap Reached
smart_warnings.message: LD CAP REACHED: Liquidated damages have hit the contractual maximum. Any additional delay beyond this point no longer increases LD exposure — however, client may pursue general damages if LD is found to be a penalty clause. Seek legal advice.

CRITICAL DELAY: Non-excusable overrun exceeds 20% of planned duration. This is a major contract performance failure. Formal delay analysis (TIA — Time Impact Analysis) required immediately. Consider formal acceleration notice and schedule recovery plan submission.

RECOVERABLE CLAIM EXCEEDS LD: Prolongation costs attributable to client-caused delays exceed LD exposure. Net position may be a client payment obligation to contractor. Formal contractual notice and substantiation records are essential.
SCHEDULE CANNOT BE RECOVERED: No crashable days available on critical path. Full LD exposure is unavoidable. Focus on EOT entitlement claims, LD cap, and dispute avoidance strategy.
HIGH CRASH ROI (>3×): Acceleration investment yields more than 3× return in LD savings. Full crash programme to maximum compressible days is strongly recommended on financial grounds alone.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: excusable_delay_days > Gross_Delay_days
fmea.effect: Excusable delay cannot exceed total gross delay. Check inputs — contractor may be entitled to EOT only up to the actual overrun.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: max_crashable_days > Gross_Delay_days && Gross_Delay_days > 0
fmea.effect: Maximum crashable days exceeds total delay. You cannot recover more time than the delay incurred.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRASHING NOT ECONOMICAL: Acceleration cost per day exceeds LD rate. Paying liquidated damages is financially preferable to accelerating — unless client relationship, repeat business, or reputational risk justifies the premium.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: crash_cost_per_day_usd > daily_ld_rate_usd

fmea.failureMode: LD CAP REACHED: Liquidated damages have hit the contractual maximum. Any additional delay beyond this point no longer increases LD exposure — however, client may pursue general damages if LD is found to be a penalty clause. Seek legal advice.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Base_LD_Exposure_USD >= LD_Cap_USD && LD_Cap_USD > 0
fmea.control_measure: FIDIC / Contract Law — LD Cap Reached

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL DELAY: Non-excusable overrun exceeds 20% of planned duration. This is a major contract performance failure. Formal delay analysis (TIA — Time Impact Analysis) required immediately. Consider formal acceleration notice and schedule recovery plan submission.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NonExcusable_Delay_days > planned_duration_days * 0.2

fmea.failureMode: RECOVERABLE CLAIM EXCEEDS LD: Prolongation costs attributable to client-caused delays exceed LD exposure. Net position may be a client payment obligation to contractor. Formal contractual notice and substantiation records are essential.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Prolongation_Claim_USD > Capped_LD_USD && compensable_delay_days > 0

fmea.failureMode: SCHEDULE CANNOT BE RECOVERED: No crashable days available on critical path. Full LD exposure is unavoidable. Focus on EOT entitlement claims, LD cap, and dispute avoidance strategy.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Gross_Delay_days > 0 && max_crashable_days === 0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH CRASH ROI (>3×): Acceleration investment yields more than 3× return in LD savings. Full crash programme to maximum compressible days is strongly recommended on financial grounds alone.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Crash_ROI_ratio > 3

tool_name: Industrial Roof Structural Load, Area Calculation & Material Cost (ASCE 7 / EN 1991)

inputs.name: Building Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Building Width (W)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Roof Pitch Angle (α)
inputs.hint: Flat roof: 0–5° | Low pitch: 5–15° | Medium: 15–30° | Steep: >30°
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Eave Overhang Width
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Roof System Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Roof Dead Load (self-weight + purlins + services)
inputs.hint: Include cladding + purlins + insulation + MEP hangers — structural engineer to confirm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Roof Live Load (maintenance access)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7: min 0.96 kN/m² (20 psf) | EN 1991-1-1: category H = 0.40 kN/m²
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Ground Snow Load (sk)
inputs.hint: From national annex / ASCE 7 Figure 7.2-1 | EN 1991-1-3 map | Turkey TEC 2018
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Snow Exposure Factor (Ce)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7 Table 7.3-1: Fully exposed=0.7 | Partially exposed=1.0 | Sheltered=1.2
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Snow Thermal Factor (Ct)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7 Table 7.3-2: Heated building=1.0 | Cold storage=1.1 | Unheated=1.2
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Importance Factor (Is)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7 Table 1.5-2: Normal occupancy=1.0 | Essential facility=1.1 | Low hazard=0.8
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Basic Wind Speed (V)
inputs.hint: 3-second gust at 10m height — from national wind map | EN 1991-1-4 or ASCE 7 Fig. 26.5-1
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Terrain / Exposure Category
options: C / III — Open terrain, scattered obstructions (Cp=0.85)
D / IV — Flat open coast, unobstructed (Cp=1.0)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Wind Pressure Coefficient (Cp)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Roofing Material Unit Price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Installation Labour Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Material Waste / Cutting Allowance
inputs.hint: Trapezoidal sheet: 5–8% | Tiles: 10–15% | Membrane: 3–5%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: Pitch_rad = (roof_pitch_deg * 3.14159) / 180   // [rad] Pitch angle in radians
Slant_Half_Width_m = (building_width_m / 2) / MAX(COS(Pitch_rad), 0.0001)   // [m] Rafter length (half span)
Gross_Roof_Area_m2 = 2 * Slant_Half_Width_m * building_length_m   // [m²] Total sloped roof area
Overhang_Area_m2 = eave_overhang_m * (2 * building_length_m + 2 * building_width_m)   // [m²] Eave overhang perimeter area
Total_Roof_Area_m2 = Gross_Roof_Area_m2 + Overhang_Area_m2   // [m²] Total area to clad
Order_Area_m2 = Total_Roof_Area_m2 * (1 + waste_allowance_pct / 100)   // [m²] Material order quantity
Material_Cost_USD = Order_Area_m2 * material_cost_usd_m2   // [USD] Roofing material cost
Labour_Cost_USD = Total_Roof_Area_m2 * installation_cost_usd_m2   // [USD] Installation cost
Total_Roof_Cost_USD = Material_Cost_USD + Labour_Cost_USD   // [USD] Total roofing cost
Cost_per_m2_USD = Total_Roof_Cost_USD / Total_Roof_Area_m2   // [USD/m²] All-in unit rate
Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 = ground_snow_load_kN_m2 * exposure_factor_Ce * thermal_factor_Ct * importance_factor_Is   // [kN/m²] Design roof snow load — ASCE 7 Eq.7.3-1 / EN 1991-1-3
Dynamic_Wind_Pressure_kN_m2 = 0.5 * 1.25 * POWER(wind_speed_ms, 2) / 1000 * wind_pressure_coeff_Cp   // [kN/m²] Design wind pressure (density=1.25 kg/m³)
Total_Gravity_Load_kN_m2 = dead_load_kN_m2 + live_load_kN_m2 + Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2   // [kN/m²] Total gravity design load
Total_Snow_Force_kN = Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 * building_length_m * building_width_m   // [kN] Total snow load on structure
Total_Snow_Tonnes = Total_Snow_Force_kN / 9.81   // [tonnes] Snow mass equivalent
Uplift_Wind_kN_m2 = -Dynamic_Wind_Pressure_kN_m2 * 0.7   // [kN/m²] Net uplift wind load (suction — approximate)

dimensions_positive.error_msg: Building dimensions must be positive values.

pitch_range.error_msg: Roof pitch must be 0–60°. Angles above 60° are structurally unconventional for industrial buildings.

snow_importance.error_msg: Importance factor must be 0.8–1.2 per ASCE 7 Table 1.5-2.

smart_warnings.message: TOTAL GRAVITY LOAD > 3.0 kN/m²: Heavy combined loading. Structural engineer must design purlins, rafters, and columns specifically for this load. Standard light steel industrial roof frames (Z/C purlins) are typically designed for 0.5–1.5 kN/m². DO NOT use standard span tables.

HIGH SNOW LOAD > 1.5 kN/m²: Significant structural implication. Roof framing must be designed for snow drift accumulation (ASCE 7 Section 7.7) at parapets and higher-level roof adjacencies. Progressive collapse analysis may be required.

FLAT/LOW-PITCH ROOF WITH SNOW: Pitch < 5° prevents snow from sliding. Full unbalanced snow load accumulates. Ponding risk from blocked drainage must also be assessed. Provide minimum 2% fall to drains.

HIGH WIND PRESSURE > 1.2 kN/m²: Cladding fixings and sheet overlaps must be designed for this uplift. Standard trapezoidal sheet fixings rated 0.8–1.0 kN/m² may be insufficient. Verify manufacturer's approved fixing schedule.

Waste allowance < 5% on steeply pitched roof: Hip ends, valleys, and ridge cuts generate significant offcuts. Minimum 8–10% recommended for pitches above 20°. Under-ordering causes installation delays and material price premium on re-order.

WIND UPLIFT EXCEEDS DEAD LOAD: Net uplift force will lift roof structure. Roof-to-wall connections and hold-down anchors are structurally critical elements. Structural engineer must design specific uplift connections — this is not a standard detail.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: building_length_m <= 0 || building_width_m <= 0
fmea.effect: Building dimensions must be positive values.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: roof_pitch_deg < 0 || roof_pitch_deg > 60
fmea.effect: Roof pitch must be 0–60°. Angles above 60° are structurally unconventional for industrial buildings.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: importance_factor_Is < 0.8 || importance_factor_Is > 1.2
fmea.effect: Importance factor must be 0.8–1.2 per ASCE 7 Table 1.5-2.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TOTAL GRAVITY LOAD > 3.0 kN/m²: Heavy combined loading. Structural engineer must design purlins, rafters, and columns specifically for this load. Standard light steel industrial roof frames (Z/C purlins) are typically designed for 0.5–1.5 kN/m². DO NOT use standard span tables.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Total_Gravity_Load_kN_m2 > 3.0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH SNOW LOAD > 1.5 kN/m²: Significant structural implication. Roof framing must be designed for snow drift accumulation (ASCE 7 Section 7.7) at parapets and higher-level roof adjacencies. Progressive collapse analysis may be required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 > 1.5

fmea.failureMode: FLAT/LOW-PITCH ROOF WITH SNOW: Pitch < 5° prevents snow from sliding. Full unbalanced snow load accumulates. Ponding risk from blocked drainage must also be assessed. Provide minimum 2% fall to drains.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: roof_pitch_deg < 5 && Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 > 0.5

fmea.failureMode: HIGH WIND PRESSURE > 1.2 kN/m²: Cladding fixings and sheet overlaps must be designed for this uplift. Standard trapezoidal sheet fixings rated 0.8–1.0 kN/m² may be insufficient. Verify manufacturer's approved fixing schedule.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Dynamic_Wind_Pressure_kN_m2 > 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Waste allowance < 5% on steeply pitched roof: Hip ends, valleys, and ridge cuts generate significant offcuts. Minimum 8–10% recommended for pitches above 20°. Under-ordering causes installation delays and material price premium on re-order.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: waste_allowance_pct < 5 && roof_pitch_deg > 20

fmea.failureMode: WIND UPLIFT EXCEEDS DEAD LOAD: Net uplift force will lift roof structure. Roof-to-wall connections and hold-down anchors are structurally critical elements. Structural engineer must design specific uplift connections — this is not a standard detail.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Uplift_Wind_kN_m2 * -1 > dead_load_kN_m2

tool_name: TOC Bottleneck Throughput Analysis & Constraint Upgrade ROI (Goldratt / APICS)

inputs.name: Customer Takt Time
inputs.hint: Takt = Net available time / Customer demand. Drives required throughput rate.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Current Bottleneck Cycle Time
inputs.hint: Longest station cycle time on critical path — the system constraint
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Target Bottleneck Cycle Time After Investment
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Annual Net Available Production Time
inputs.hint: Shifts × days × (working time – planned downtime). 2-shift 250 days = 240,000 min
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Current Bottleneck OEE
inputs.hint: OEE of the constraint station — drives real effective capacity
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Target OEE After Improvement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Throughput per Unit (Revenue minus Truly Variable Cost)
inputs.hint: TOC Throughput = Selling price minus direct material only — NOT including labour/overhead
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Constraint Upgrade Capital Investment (CAPEX)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Operating Cost Increase (OPEX delta)
inputs.hint: Additional labour, energy, maintenance from upgrade
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Investment Economic Life
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Current_Output_units_yr = (available_time_min_yr / bottleneck_ct_current_min) * (current_oee_pct / 100)   // [units/year] Effective current throughput
Max_Demand_units_yr = available_time_min_yr / takt_time_min   // [units/year] Maximum customer demand
Target_Output_raw = (available_time_min_yr / bottleneck_ct_target_min) * (target_oee_pct / 100)   // [units/year] Raw target output
Realised_Output_units_yr = MIN(Target_Output_raw, Max_Demand_units_yr)   // [units/year] Demand-constrained realised output
Incremental_Units_yr = Realised_Output_units_yr - Current_Output_units_yr   // [units/year] Additional throughput units
Annual_Throughput_Gain_USD = Incremental_Units_yr * unit_throughput_usd   // [USD/year] Gross throughput gain
Net_Annual_Benefit_USD = Annual_Throughput_Gain_USD - opex_increase_usd_yr   // [USD/year] Net annual benefit
Payback_Months = capex_usd > 0 && Net_Annual_Benefit_USD > 0 ? (capex_usd / Net_Annual_Benefit_USD) * 12 : 999   // [months] Simple payback
Annuity_Factor = ((1 - POWER(1 + wacc_pct / 100, -project_life_years)) / (wacc_pct / 100))   // [—] PV annuity factor
Project_NPV_USD = Net_Annual_Benefit_USD * Annuity_Factor - capex_usd   // [USD] Net Present Value
Project_IRR_approx_pct = capex_usd > 0 && Net_Annual_Benefit_USD > 0 ? (Net_Annual_Benefit_USD / capex_usd) * 100 : 0   // [%] Simplified IRR approximation (undiscounted — use for screening only)
Demand_Headroom_pct = ((Max_Demand_units_yr - Current_Output_units_yr) / Max_Demand_units_yr) * 100   // [%] Unfilled demand as % of total
OEE_Improvement_Units = available_time_min_yr / bottleneck_ct_current_min * ((target_oee_pct - current_oee_pct) / 100)   // [units/year] Gain from OEE improvement alone

target_faster_than_current.error_msg: Target cycle time must be less than current cycle time. If no improvement is expected, investment analysis is not applicable.

wacc_range.error_msg: WACC must be positive. Zero or negative discount rate is not valid for NPV calculation.

available_time_minimum.error_msg: Annual available time < 1000 min is unrealistically low. Check units — input should be total annual minutes.

smart_warnings.message: OVER-INVESTMENT: Target cycle time is faster than customer takt time. Output beyond takt rate creates overproduction waste (Muda #1). System is demand-constrained — additional throughput cannot be sold. Reduce investment scope to target CT = Takt Time exactly.

NEGATIVE NPV: Investment destroys value at the given discount rate. Review: (1) Is throughput per unit correctly calculated? (2) Is project life realistic? (3) Can CAPEX be reduced? (4) Is there a phased investment option?
Payback period exceeds 36 months: Above typical manufacturing investment approval threshold (12–24 months for constraint upgrades). Present risk-adjusted scenario analysis to management.

Low demand headroom (<10%): Market is nearly saturated at current output. Throughput gain from constraint removal is limited by demand, not capacity. Validate sales forecast before committing CAPEX — demand growth must be confirmed.

More than 50% of incremental throughput comes from OEE improvement (not cycle time reduction). Consider TPM/autonomous maintenance programme as low-cost alternative before CAPEX. OEE improvement is typically 5–10× cheaper than hardware upgrade.

BOTTLENECK OEE < 65%: Constraint station is severely underperforming before investment. Adding capacity to a poorly-maintained machine multiplies waste. Mandatory: reach OEE ≥ 75% through TPM before hardware upgrade. Otherwise investment ROI will be materially worse than projected.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bottleneck_ct_target_min >= bottleneck_ct_current_min
fmea.effect: Target cycle time must be less than current cycle time. If no improvement is expected, investment analysis is not applicable.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: wacc_pct <= 0
fmea.effect: WACC must be positive. Zero or negative discount rate is not valid for NPV calculation.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: OVER-INVESTMENT: Target cycle time is faster than customer takt time. Output beyond takt rate creates overproduction waste (Muda #1). System is demand-constrained — additional throughput cannot be sold. Reduce investment scope to target CT = Takt Time exactly.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bottleneck_ct_target_min < takt_time_min

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE NPV: Investment destroys value at the given discount rate. Review: (1) Is throughput per unit correctly calculated? (2) Is project life realistic? (3) Can CAPEX be reduced? (4) Is there a phased investment option?
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Project_NPV_USD < 0
