fmea.failureMode: Payback period exceeds 36 months: Above typical manufacturing investment approval threshold (12–24 months for constraint upgrades). Present risk-adjusted scenario analysis to management.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Payback_Months > 36

fmea.failureMode: Low demand headroom (<10%): Market is nearly saturated at current output. Throughput gain from constraint removal is limited by demand, not capacity. Validate sales forecast before committing CAPEX — demand growth must be confirmed.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Demand_Headroom_pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: More than 50% of incremental throughput comes from OEE improvement (not cycle time reduction). Consider TPM/autonomous maintenance programme as low-cost alternative before CAPEX. OEE improvement is typically 5–10× cheaper than hardware upgrade.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OEEImprovementUnits > Incremental_Units_yr * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: BOTTLENECK OEE < 65%: Constraint station is severely underperforming before investment. Adding capacity to a poorly-maintained machine multiplies waste. Mandatory: reach OEE ≥ 75% through TPM before hardware upgrade. Otherwise investment ROI will be materially worse than projected.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: current_oee_pct < 65

tool_name: SMED Setup Time Reduction, Capacity Recovery & EOQ Optimisation (Shingo / Harris)

inputs.name: Current Internal Setup Time (machine stopped)
inputs.hint: From time study — time machine is non-productive during changeover
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Current External Setup Time (pre/post while running)
inputs.hint: Preparation activities already done while machine is running previous job
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Internal-to-External Conversion Achievement
inputs.hint: % of current internal time successfully converted to external via SMED programme
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Setup Streamlining Reduction (remaining internal)
inputs.hint: Additional reduction from quick-clamps, standardisation, mistake-proofing of remaining internal time
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Annual Number of Changeovers
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Machine Opportunity Cost Rate (fully burdened)
inputs.hint: Cost of machine standing idle — includes depreciation, operator, overhead
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Product Demand
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Unit Material / Variable Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Inventory Holding Cost Rate
inputs.hint: Warehousing + capital tied up + obsolescence + insurance — typically 20–35% of unit value
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: SMED Project Investment (tooling, fixtures, training)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD = unit_material_cost_usd * (holding_cost_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD/unit/year] Annual holding cost
Internal_After_Conversion_min = internal_setup_current_min * (1 - internal_to_external_conversion_pct / 100)   // [min] Internal time after conversion step
Internal_After_Streamlining_min = Internal_After_Conversion_min * (1 - streamlining_reduction_pct / 100)   // [min] Internal time after all SMED improvements
Target_Total_Setup_min = Internal_After_Streamlining_min   // [min] New internal setup time — the SMED target
Setup_Time_Saved_min = internal_setup_current_min - Target_Total_Setup_min   // [min] Internal time recovered per changeover
Current_Setup_Cost_USD = (internal_setup_current_min / 60) * machine_hourly_rate_usd   // [USD/changeover] Current cost
New_Setup_Cost_USD = (Target_Total_Setup_min / 60) * machine_hourly_rate_usd   // [USD/changeover] After SMED cost
Annual_Setup_Cost_Saving_USD = (Current_Setup_Cost_USD - New_Setup_Cost_USD) * changeovers_per_year   // [USD/year]
Annual_Capacity_Recovered_min = Setup_Time_Saved_min * changeovers_per_year   // [min/year] Machine time recovered
Current_EOQ_units = SQRT((2 * annual_demand_units * Current_Setup_Cost_USD) / Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD)   // [units] Economic Order Qty — current
New_EOQ_units = SQRT((2 * annual_demand_units * New_Setup_Cost_USD) / Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD)   // [units] Economic Order Qty — after SMED
Average_Inventory_Reduction_units = (Current_EOQ_units - New_EOQ_units) / 2   // [units] Reduction in average cycle stock
Inventory_Value_Released_USD = Average_Inventory_Reduction_units * unit_material_cost_usd   // [USD] Working capital released
Inventory_Cost_Saving_USD = Average_Inventory_Reduction_units * Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD   // [USD/year] Annual holding cost saving
Total_Annual_Benefit_USD = Annual_Setup_Cost_Saving_USD + Inventory_Cost_Saving_USD   // [USD/year] Total financial benefit
Simple_Payback_Months = smed_project_cost_usd > 0 && Total_Annual_Benefit_USD > 0 ? (smed_project_cost_usd / Total_Annual_Benefit_USD) * 12 : 0   // [months]
Setup_Reduction_Pct = (Setup_Time_Saved_min / internal_setup_current_min) * 100   // [%] Overall setup reduction achieved

target_faster.error_msg: Both conversion and streamlining reductions are zero — no SMED improvement defined. Enter expected improvement percentages.

conversion_range.error_msg: Conversion rate cannot exceed 100% of internal time.

holding_rate_range.error_msg: Holding cost rate < 5% is unrealistically low. Minimum components: capital cost (WACC) + warehousing + insurance. Typical industrial: 20–30%.

smart_warnings.message: BATCH PRODUCTION MODE: Current EOQ exceeds 2 months of demand. High setup cost is forcing large batch sizes, creating excess WIP, long lead times, and poor flexibility. SMED is the correct intervention — reducing setup cost directly enables smaller, leaner batches.

Setup reduction < 50%: Below typical SMED first-pass target. Shingo's methodology targets >75% reduction in internal time. Review: Have all convertible internal activities been identified? Are quick-clamp fixtures and standardised toolholder systems being used?

Significant capacity recovered (>8 hours equivalent): At this machine rate, recovered capacity has high monetary value. If demand exists, this freed capacity can be converted to throughput rather than cost saving — model the revenue upside separately.
SMED enables weekly or sub-weekly batch sizes: Approaching one-piece-flow conditions. Consider implementing Pull / Kanban replenishment system to replace EOQ-based push scheduling.

Low conversion rate with long internal setup: SMED Stage 1 (separation of internal/external) is incomplete. Most setups > 30 min have 40–60% of activities convertible to external. Conduct video-based setup analysis to identify conversion opportunities.

WORKING CAPITAL RELEASE EXCEEDS PROJECT COST: Inventory value released from smaller batch sizes alone covers the SMED investment. Present to finance as a working capital project, not just a productivity initiative.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: internal_to_external_conversion_pct === 0 && streamlining_reduction_pct === 0
fmea.effect: Both conversion and streamlining reductions are zero — no SMED improvement defined. Enter expected improvement percentages.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: internal_to_external_conversion_pct > 100
fmea.effect: Conversion rate cannot exceed 100% of internal time.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: holding_cost_rate_pct < 5
fmea.effect: Holding cost rate < 5% is unrealistically low. Minimum components: capital cost (WACC) + warehousing + insurance. Typical industrial: 20–30%.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: BATCH PRODUCTION MODE: Current EOQ exceeds 2 months of demand. High setup cost is forcing large batch sizes, creating excess WIP, long lead times, and poor flexibility. SMED is the correct intervention — reducing setup cost directly enables smaller, leaner batches.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Current_EOQ_units > annual_demand_units / 6

fmea.failureMode: Setup reduction < 50%: Below typical SMED first-pass target. Shingo's methodology targets >75% reduction in internal time. Review: Have all convertible internal activities been identified? Are quick-clamp fixtures and standardised toolholder systems being used?
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Setup_Reduction_Pct < 50

fmea.failureMode: Significant capacity recovered (>8 hours equivalent): At this machine rate, recovered capacity has high monetary value. If demand exists, this freed capacity can be converted to throughput rather than cost saving — model the revenue upside separately.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_Capacity_Recovered_min > 480 && machine_hourly_rate_usd > 50

fmea.failureMode: SMED enables weekly or sub-weekly batch sizes: Approaching one-piece-flow conditions. Consider implementing Pull / Kanban replenishment system to replace EOQ-based push scheduling.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: New_EOQ_units < annual_demand_units / 52

fmea.failureMode: Low conversion rate with long internal setup: SMED Stage 1 (separation of internal/external) is incomplete. Most setups > 30 min have 40–60% of activities convertible to external. Conduct video-based setup analysis to identify conversion opportunities.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: internal_to_external_conversion_pct < 30 && internal_setup_current_min > 30

fmea.failureMode: WORKING CAPITAL RELEASE EXCEEDS PROJECT COST: Inventory value released from smaller batch sizes alone covers the SMED investment. Present to finance as a working capital project, not just a productivity initiative.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Inventory_Value_Released_USD > smed_project_cost_usd && smed_project_cost_usd > 0

tool_name: Warehouse 3D Cube Utilisation, Pallet Position Count & Storage Capacity Analysis

inputs.name: Gross Warehouse Floor Area
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Net Storage Area Ratio
inputs.hint: Gross area minus: docks, offices, charging bays, columns. Typical: 50–65% for conventional rack
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Clear Stacking Height (under sprinkler/beam)
inputs.hint: Usable height after deducting sprinkler clearance (min 0.5m) and lighting
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Pallet Length
inputs.hint: EUR pallet: 1.20m | ISO pallet: 1.20m | US GMA: 1.22m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Pallet Width
inputs.hint: EUR pallet: 0.80m | ISO pallet: 1.00m | US GMA: 1.02m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Loaded Pallet Height (pallet + load)
inputs.hint: EUR pallet 0.15m + load. Standard consumer goods: 1.0–1.5m | Industrial: 1.5–2.0m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Beam / Safety Clearance per Level
inputs.hint: Vertical clearance between top of load and next beam: min 100mm per SEMA / FEM 10.2.02
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Operating Aisle Width
inputs.hint: Counterbalance FLT: 3.5–4.0m | Reach truck: 2.6–3.0m | VNA (VFLT): 1.6–1.8m | Manual: 1.5m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Racking / Storage System Type
options: Double-deep rack — access factor 0.5 (50% directly accessible)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Storage Selectivity Factor
inputs.hint: From system type above: Selective=1.0 | Double-deep=0.5 | Drive-in=0.25
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Target Storage Occupancy Rate
inputs.hint: Operating above 85% causes pick congestion. WMS best practice: 80–85% max
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: Net_Storage_Area_m2 = gross_floor_area_m2 * (net_storage_ratio_pct / 100)   // [m²] Usable racking footprint
Rack_Bay_Footprint_m2 = (pallet_length_m + aisle_width_m / 2) * pallet_width_m   // [m²] Footprint per pallet position (incl. aisle share)
Floor_Positions = FLOOR(Net_Storage_Area_m2 / Rack_Bay_Footprint_m2)   // [positions] Ground-level pallet locations
Height_per_Level_m = loaded_pallet_height_m + rack_beam_clearance_m   // [m] Vertical pitch per rack level
Max_Rack_Levels = FLOOR(clear_height_m / Height_per_Level_m)   // [levels] Maximum stacking levels within clearance
Total_Pallet_Positions = Floor_Positions * Max_Rack_Levels   // [positions] Total rack positions
Accessible_Positions = FLOOR(Total_Pallet_Positions * selectivity_factor)   // [positions] Directly accessible positions
Required_Height_m = Max_Rack_Levels * Height_per_Level_m   // [m] Height required by rack structure
Theoretical_Volume_m3 = gross_floor_area_m2 * clear_height_m   // [m³] Total building envelope volume
Storage_Volume_m3 = Total_Pallet_Positions * (pallet_length_m * pallet_width_m * loaded_pallet_height_m)   // [m³] Actual inventory volume
Cube_Utilisation_pct = (Storage_Volume_m3 / Theoretical_Volume_m3) * 100   // [%] 3D volume efficiency
Operating_Positions = FLOOR(Total_Pallet_Positions * (occupancy_target_pct / 100))   // [positions] Usable positions at target occupancy
Positions_per_m2 = Total_Pallet_Positions / gross_floor_area_m2   // [positions/m²] Storage density KPI
Aisle_Area_m2 = gross_floor_area_m2 * (net_storage_ratio_pct / 100) * (aisle_width_m / (pallet_length_m + aisle_width_m))   // [m²] Estimated aisle area

standards: SEMA Code of Practice for Static Racking

height_exceeds_clear.error_msg: PHYSICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: Required rack height exceeds available clear height. Reduce rack levels, decrease loaded pallet height, or increase building clear height.

net_storage_ratio_max.error_msg: Net storage ratio > 75% is physically unrealistic. Minimum space is needed for: dock doors, charging bays, staging areas, columns, fire exits. Maximum achievable: 70% for automated warehouses.

aisle_minimum.error_msg: Aisle width < 1.5m is below minimum safe passage for any powered truck. Fire safety regulations also require minimum 1.2m emergency egress width.

smart_warnings.message: WIDE AISLE SYSTEM (≥3.5m): Aisle width suitable for counterbalance forklift only. Approximately 35–40% of floor area is dedicated to aisles. Switching to reach truck (aisle 2.6–2.8m) or VNA (1.6–1.8m) could increase pallet positions by 20–40% in the same building.

POOR CUBE UTILISATION (<25%): Building volume is severely underused. Root causes: insufficient rack height, too many aisles, low stacking, or low occupancy. World-class warehouses achieve 40–60% cube utilisation. Evaluate: (1) Taller racking, (2) Narrower aisles, (3) Double-deep or mobile racking.

OCCUPANCY TARGET > 90%: Operating above 90% causes severe congestion, pick path blockages, and forces multiple product relocations per pick. WMS systems degrade significantly above 85%. Required buffer for operational flexibility: minimum 15% empty positions.

Only 1–2 rack levels achievable: Building clear height is limiting vertical density. If building modification is not feasible, consider mezzanine floor installation or multi-tier picking to improve m³ utilisation.

Storage density < 0.8 positions/m² gross: Below industry benchmark for conventional racking (target: 1.0–1.5 positions/m²). Analyse: wide aisle ratios, low stacking height, and low net storage ratio as primary contributors.

Low accessibility + small absolute accessible count: With drive-in or pushback systems, FIFO rotation becomes operationally difficult. For perishable goods, pharmaceuticals, or date-coded products, minimum selectivity factor 0.8 is required to ensure FEFO compliance.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Required_Height_m > clear_height_m
fmea.effect: PHYSICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: Required rack height exceeds available clear height. Reduce rack levels, decrease loaded pallet height, or increase building clear height.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: net_storage_ratio_pct > 75
fmea.effect: Net storage ratio > 75% is physically unrealistic. Minimum space is needed for: dock doors, charging bays, staging areas, columns, fire exits. Maximum achievable: 70% for automated warehouses.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: aisle_width_m < 1.5
fmea.effect: Aisle width < 1.5m is below minimum safe passage for any powered truck. Fire safety regulations also require minimum 1.2m emergency egress width.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: WIDE AISLE SYSTEM (≥3.5m): Aisle width suitable for counterbalance forklift only. Approximately 35–40% of floor area is dedicated to aisles. Switching to reach truck (aisle 2.6–2.8m) or VNA (1.6–1.8m) could increase pallet positions by 20–40% in the same building.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: aisle_width_m >= 3.5

fmea.failureMode: POOR CUBE UTILISATION (<25%): Building volume is severely underused. Root causes: insufficient rack height, too many aisles, low stacking, or low occupancy. World-class warehouses achieve 40–60% cube utilisation. Evaluate: (1) Taller racking, (2) Narrower aisles, (3) Double-deep or mobile racking.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cube_Utilisation_pct < 25

fmea.failureMode: OCCUPANCY TARGET > 90%: Operating above 90% causes severe congestion, pick path blockages, and forces multiple product relocations per pick. WMS systems degrade significantly above 85%. Required buffer for operational flexibility: minimum 15% empty positions.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: occupancy_target_pct > 90

fmea.failureMode: Only 1–2 rack levels achievable: Building clear height is limiting vertical density. If building modification is not feasible, consider mezzanine floor installation or multi-tier picking to improve m³ utilisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Max_Rack_Levels <= 2

fmea.failureMode: Storage density < 0.8 positions/m² gross: Below industry benchmark for conventional racking (target: 1.0–1.5 positions/m²). Analyse: wide aisle ratios, low stacking height, and low net storage ratio as primary contributors.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Positions_per_m2 < 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Low accessibility + small absolute accessible count: With drive-in or pushback systems, FIFO rotation becomes operationally difficult. For perishable goods, pharmaceuticals, or date-coded products, minimum selectivity factor 0.8 is required to ensure FEFO compliance.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: selectivity_factor < 0.5 && accessible_positions < 200

tool_name: Digital Twin Prototype ROI vs Physical Build — Cost Avoidance, TTM & NPV (ISO 23247)

inputs.name: Physical Prototype Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Full cost: materials + machining + tooling + assembly + testing per build
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Average Physical Iteration Count (baseline)
inputs.hint: Historical average design cycles before release — typical product development: 3–8
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Expected Digital Iterations Before First Physical Build
inputs.hint: Virtual design cycles replacing physical builds — typically 5–15 per 1 physical
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Remaining Physical Builds Required After DT Validation
inputs.hint: Even with DT, at least 1 physical validation build is typically required
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Field / Validation Test Cost (per programme)
inputs.hint: Environmental, durability, regulatory testing costs per full programme
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Field Test Cost Reduction from DT Preflight
inputs.hint: DT-validated designs require fewer field test cycles — typical: 20–50% reduction
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Digital Twin Platform License Cost
inputs.hint: Ansys Twin Builder / Siemens Simcenter / MATLAB SimScape / PTC ThingWorx annual fee
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: HPC / Cloud Simulation Compute Cost
inputs.hint: AWS/Azure HPC instances for FEA, CFD, MBD simulations — per product programme
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: IoT Sensor & Data Infrastructure CAPEX
inputs.hint: Sensors, edge devices, connectivity, historian — one-time or amortised per programme
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: DT Model Engineering & Calibration Effort
inputs.hint: Engineer hours for model build, parameter identification, and validation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Fully Burdened Engineer Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Time-to-Market Acceleration (days gained)
inputs.hint: Calendar days saved by parallel virtual validation vs sequential physical build
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Daily Market Revenue at Launch
inputs.hint: Expected daily revenue from early market entry — opportunity value per day of acceleration
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Expected Warranty / Field Failure Reduction
inputs.hint: DT-validated designs typically show 15–35% reduction in first-year warranty claims
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Current Annual Warranty Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: WACC / Discount Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Product Programme Life (DT reuse period)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: Physical_Proto_Cost_Baseline_USD = physical_proto_unit_cost_usd * physical_iterations_avg + field_test_cost_usd   // [USD] Historical physical programme cost
Physical_Proto_Cost_With_DT_USD = physical_proto_unit_cost_usd * physical_iterations_with_dt + field_test_cost_usd * (1 - field_test_reduction_pct / 100)   // [USD] Physical cost after DT validation
DT_Engineering_Cost_USD = engineering_effort_hrs * engineer_rate_usd_hr   // [USD] Model build labour cost
DT_Total_Investment_USD = dt_software_license_usd_yr + hpc_compute_cost_usd + iot_sensor_capex_usd + DT_Engineering_Cost_USD   // [USD] Total DT programme investment
Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD = Physical_Proto_Cost_Baseline_USD - Physical_Proto_Cost_With_DT_USD   // [USD] Direct savings vs baseline
TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD = ttm_gain_days * daily_market_revenue_usd   // [USD] Revenue from early market entry
Warranty_Saving_USD = annual_warranty_cost_usd * (warranty_reduction_pct / 100)   // [USD/year] Annual warranty saving
Annual_Benefit_USD = Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD + TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD + Warranty_Saving_USD   // [USD] Total year-1 benefit (cost avoidance + revenue + warranty)
Recurring_Annual_Benefit_USD = Warranty_Saving_USD   // [USD/year] Ongoing annual benefit (warranty only)
Annuity_Factor = (1 - POWER(1 + wacc_pct / 100, -programme_life_years)) / (wacc_pct / 100)   // [—] PV annuity factor
NPV_USD = Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD + TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD + Warranty_Saving_USD * Annuity_Factor - DT_Total_Investment_USD   // [USD] Net Present Value
ROI_pct = (Annual_Benefit_USD / DT_Total_Investment_USD) * 100   // [%] First-year ROI
Simple_Payback_Months = DT_Total_Investment_USD > 0 && Annual_Benefit_USD > 0 ? (DT_Total_Investment_USD / Annual_Benefit_USD) * 12 : 999   // [months] Simple payback
Breakeven_Proto_Reduction = DT_Total_Investment_USD / physical_proto_unit_cost_usd   // [units] Physical builds avoided needed to break even

dt_iterations_vs_physical.error_msg: Physical builds after DT must be less than baseline. If DT reduces zero physical iterations, there is no prototype cost avoidance benefit.

engineer_rate_minimum.error_msg: Engineer rate cannot be below $10/h. Use fully burdened rate including overhead, benefits, and indirect costs.

programme_life_range.error_msg: Programme life must be 1–15 years for meaningful NPV analysis.

smart_warnings.message: NEGATIVE NPV: Digital Twin investment does not recover its cost at this discount rate and programme scale. Review: (1) Increase programme reuse across multiple products, (2) Negotiate platform licence sharing, (3) Quantify previously excluded benefits (regulatory approval acceleration, recall avoidance).

Engineering labour >60% of DT investment: Model build effort is dominant cost. Evaluate: (1) Reuse existing simulation models, (2) Use vendor-supplied component models, (3) Phased modelling — start with highest-risk subsystems only.

Zero TTM gain entered: Time-to-market acceleration is typically the highest-value DT benefit. If parallel virtual/physical development is not planned, significant ROI is being excluded. Replan development timeline to capture TTM benefit.

Payback >24 months: Above typical threshold for product development tool investment. Consider: (1) Shared licence across multiple simultaneous programmes, (2) Phased IoT sensor rollout, (3) Open-source simulation platforms to reduce licence cost.

smart_warnings.source: Digital Twin Best Practice — McKinsey / Deloitte
smart_warnings.message: Low virtual iteration count (<5): DT value scales with simulation depth. Less than 5 virtual iterations suggests the model is not being used to its potential. Target 10–20 virtual design cycles per physical build for full benefit realisation.

IoT CAPEX exceeds 50% of prototype cost baseline: Sensor investment may be disproportionate. Evaluate: (1) Virtual sensor placement using FEA instead of physical measurement, (2) Shared sensor infrastructure across production lines, (3) Edge AI for data reduction before cloud transmission.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: physical_iterations_with_dt >= physical_iterations_avg
fmea.effect: Physical builds after DT must be less than baseline. If DT reduces zero physical iterations, there is no prototype cost avoidance benefit.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: programme_life_years < 1 || programme_life_years > 15
fmea.effect: Programme life must be 1–15 years for meaningful NPV analysis.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE NPV: Digital Twin investment does not recover its cost at this discount rate and programme scale. Review: (1) Increase programme reuse across multiple products, (2) Negotiate platform licence sharing, (3) Quantify previously excluded benefits (regulatory approval acceleration, recall avoidance).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPV_USD < 0

fmea.failureMode: Engineering labour >60% of DT investment: Model build effort is dominant cost. Evaluate: (1) Reuse existing simulation models, (2) Use vendor-supplied component models, (3) Phased modelling — start with highest-risk subsystems only.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DT_Engineering_Cost_USD > DT_Total_Investment_USD * 0.6

fmea.failureMode: Zero TTM gain entered: Time-to-market acceleration is typically the highest-value DT benefit. If parallel virtual/physical development is not planned, significant ROI is being excluded. Replan development timeline to capture TTM benefit.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ttm_gain_days === 0

fmea.failureMode: Payback >24 months: Above typical threshold for product development tool investment. Consider: (1) Shared licence across multiple simultaneous programmes, (2) Phased IoT sensor rollout, (3) Open-source simulation platforms to reduce licence cost.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Simple_Payback_Months > 24

fmea.failureMode: Low virtual iteration count (<5): DT value scales with simulation depth. Less than 5 virtual iterations suggests the model is not being used to its potential. Target 10–20 virtual design cycles per physical build for full benefit realisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dt_iterations_expected < 5
fmea.control_measure: Digital Twin Best Practice — McKinsey / Deloitte

fmea.failureMode: IoT CAPEX exceeds 50% of prototype cost baseline: Sensor investment may be disproportionate. Evaluate: (1) Virtual sensor placement using FEA instead of physical measurement, (2) Shared sensor infrastructure across production lines, (3) Edge AI for data reduction before cloud transmission.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: iot_sensor_capex_usd > physical_proto_unit_cost_usd * physical_iterations_avg * 0.5

tool_name: Assembly Line Balancing, SMV Analysis & Takt Compliance (IE / MTM-2 / MOST)

inputs.name: Net Shift Available Time
inputs.hint: Gross shift minus: planned breaks, cleaning, TPM. Typical: 450 min (8h shift – 30 min break)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Daily Production Demand (MPS)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Number of Workstations on Line
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Station 1 — Standard Minute Value (SMV)
inputs.hint: From time study, MTM-2, or MOST — includes allowances (fatigue, personal, delay)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 2 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 3 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 4 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 5 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 6 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 7 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Station 8 — SMV
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Operator Allowance (fatigue + personal + delay)
inputs.hint: ILO: 10–20% for light assembly | Heavy manual: 20–30% | MTM standard: 15%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Actual Number of Direct Operators
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Fully Burdened Labour Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Shifts per Day
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Takt_Time_min = shift_net_time_min / (daily_demand_units / num_shifts)   // [min/unit] Required cycle rate — pace of customer demand
Total_SMV_min = station_1_smv + station_2_smv + station_3_smv + station_4_smv + station_5_smv + station_6_smv + station_7_smv + station_8_smv   // [min/unit] Sum of all station work content
Bottleneck_SMV_min = MAX(station_1_smv, station_2_smv, station_3_smv, station_4_smv, station_5_smv, station_6_smv, station_7_smv, station_8_smv)   // [min/unit] Constraining station — sets line rate
Theoretical_Min_Operators = Total_SMV_min / Takt_Time_min   // [persons] Minimum operators to meet takt
Line_Efficiency_pct = (Total_SMV_min / (actual_operators * Bottleneck_SMV_min)) * 100   // [%] Work content utilisation
Balance_Delay_pct = 100 - Line_Efficiency_pct   // [%] Idle time fraction across all stations
Actual_Output_per_Shift = shift_net_time_min / Bottleneck_SMV_min   // [units/shift] Maximum achievable at bottleneck
Daily_Output_Capacity = Actual_Output_per_Shift * num_shifts   // [units/day] Total daily capacity
Capacity_vs_Demand_pct = (Daily_Output_Capacity / daily_demand_units) * 100   // [%] Capacity coverage

Labour_Cost_per_Unit_USD = (actual_operators * (shift_net_time_min / 60) * labour_rate_usd_hr * num_shifts) / MIN(Daily_Output_Capacity, daily_demand_units)   // [USD/unit] Labour cost per produced unit
Idle_Operator_Equivalent = actual_operators - Theoretical_Min_Operators   // [persons] Excess operators vs theoretical minimum
Annual_Labour_Waste_USD = Idle_Operator_Equivalent * labour_rate_usd_hr * shift_net_time_min / 60 * num_shifts * 250   // [USD/year] Cost of imbalance waste
SMV_per_Operator = Total_SMV_min / actual_operators   // [min] Average work content per operator

standards: IE Line Balancing (Kilbridge & Wester / RPW Method)

daily_demand_positive.error_msg: Daily demand must be at least 1 unit. Zero demand makes line balancing undefined.

station_smv_minimum.error_msg: Station 1 SMV must be greater than zero — at least one workstation must have content.

operators_minimum.error_msg: At least 1 operator is required on the line.

smart_warnings.message: TAKT VIOLATION: Bottleneck station cycle time exceeds takt time. Meeting daily demand target is MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE without: (1) splitting the bottleneck task, (2) adding a parallel station, or (3) increasing shift time. Calculate: required output deficit = (Bottleneck_SMV - Takt) × demand units.

LOW LINE EFFICIENCY (<75%): Over 25% of operator time is idle due to imbalance. Rebalance by: (1) relocating work elements from overloaded to underloaded stations, (2) applying RPW or Kilbridge-Wester algorithm, (3) combining or splitting stations. Target: >85% for high-volume lines.

EXCESS OPERATORS IDENTIFIED: Line has more operators than theoretical minimum. Each excess operator represents avoidable annual labour cost. Conduct kaizen event to redistribute work elements before adding headcount.

CAPACITY SHORTFALL: Line cannot meet daily demand at current bottleneck rate. Immediate actions: (1) Overtime authorisation, (2) Bottleneck relief (parallel resource), (3) Demand smoothing with customer. Quantify units short per day and escalate to production manager.

Low allowance (<10%): ILO recommends minimum 10% for light assembly work. Insufficient allowance leads to: (1) fatigue-induced quality defects, (2) absenteeism increase, (3) operator injury claims. Verify time study method includes all required rest periods.

VERY SHORT TAKT (<0.5 min): Cycle time under 30 seconds. Ergonomic risk of repetitive strain injury is high at this pace. EU Directive 2002/44/EC requires risk assessment. Consider task variety rotation and automation for highly repetitive operations.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: station_1_smv <= 0
fmea.effect: Station 1 SMV must be greater than zero — at least one workstation must have content.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_operators < 1
fmea.effect: At least 1 operator is required on the line.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TAKT VIOLATION: Bottleneck station cycle time exceeds takt time. Meeting daily demand target is MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE without: (1) splitting the bottleneck task, (2) adding a parallel station, or (3) increasing shift time. Calculate: required output deficit = (Bottleneck_SMV - Takt) × demand units.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Bottleneck_SMV_min > Takt_Time_min

fmea.failureMode: LOW LINE EFFICIENCY (<75%): Over 25% of operator time is idle due to imbalance. Rebalance by: (1) relocating work elements from overloaded to underloaded stations, (2) applying RPW or Kilbridge-Wester algorithm, (3) combining or splitting stations. Target: >85% for high-volume lines.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Line_Efficiency_pct < 75

fmea.failureMode: EXCESS OPERATORS IDENTIFIED: Line has more operators than theoretical minimum. Each excess operator represents avoidable annual labour cost. Conduct kaizen event to redistribute work elements before adding headcount.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Idle_Operator_Equivalent >= 1

fmea.failureMode: CAPACITY SHORTFALL: Line cannot meet daily demand at current bottleneck rate. Immediate actions: (1) Overtime authorisation, (2) Bottleneck relief (parallel resource), (3) Demand smoothing with customer. Quantify units short per day and escalate to production manager.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Capacity_vs_Demand_pct < 100

fmea.failureMode: Low allowance (<10%): ILO recommends minimum 10% for light assembly work. Insufficient allowance leads to: (1) fatigue-induced quality defects, (2) absenteeism increase, (3) operator injury claims. Verify time study method includes all required rest periods.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: allowance_pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: VERY SHORT TAKT (<0.5 min): Cycle time under 30 seconds. Ergonomic risk of repetitive strain injury is high at this pace. EU Directive 2002/44/EC requires risk assessment. Consider task variety rotation and automation for highly repetitive operations.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Takt_Time_min < 0.5

tool_name: Power Factor, Reactive Energy Penalty & Industrial Electricity Bill Analysis (ISO 50001)

inputs.name: Total Active Energy Consumption
inputs.hint: From electricity meter or energy management system — monthly billing period
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Inductive Reactive Energy (lagging)
inputs.hint: Motors, transformers, inductors — lagging current. From reactive energy meter.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Capacitive Reactive Energy (leading)
inputs.hint: Over-compensated PFC capacitor banks, VFDs, light loads. Usually small.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Peak Demand (Maximum Measured Demand)
inputs.hint: Maximum 15-min average demand in billing period — from demand meter or SCADA
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Contracted / Subscribed Demand
inputs.hint: Grid connection agreement demand — exceeded demand may incur penalty multiplier
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Active Energy Tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Reactive Energy Penalty Rate
inputs.hint: Charged only on reactive energy above the free allowance threshold
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Peak Demand Charge
inputs.hint: Monthly capacity charge on peak demand — significantly impacts bill at low load factor
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Inductive Reactive Free Allowance Threshold
inputs.hint: Turkey TEDAŞ: 20% | EU typical: 33% (equivalent to PF 0.95) | Varies by tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Capacitive Reactive Free Allowance Threshold
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Power Factor Correction Capacitor Bank Cost
inputs.hint: For ROI calculation — automatic PFC panel for target power factor correction
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Target Power Factor After Correction
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Apparent_Energy_kVAh = SQRT(POWER(active_energy_kwh, 2) + POWER(inductive_reactive_kvarh - capacitive_reactive_kvarh, 2))   // [kVAh] Apparent energy
Power_Factor = active_energy_kwh / Apparent_Energy_kVAh   // [—] Displacement power factor
Inductive_Ratio_pct = (inductive_reactive_kvarh / active_energy_kwh) * 100   // [%] Inductive reactive ratio
Capacitive_Ratio_pct = (capacitive_reactive_kvarh / active_energy_kwh) * 100   // [%] Capacitive reactive ratio
Billable_Inductive_kVArh = MAX(0, inductive_reactive_kvarh - active_energy_kwh * inductive_free_limit_pct / 100)   // [kVArh] Penalised inductive reactive
Billable_Capacitive_kVArh = MAX(0, capacitive_reactive_kvarh - active_energy_kwh * capacitive_free_limit_pct / 100)   // [kVArh] Penalised capacitive reactive
Active_Energy_Cost_USD = active_energy_kwh * active_energy_rate_usd_kwh   // [USD] Active energy charge
Inductive_Penalty_USD = Billable_Inductive_kVArh * reactive_penalty_rate_usd_kvarh   // [USD] Inductive reactive penalty
Capacitive_Penalty_USD = Billable_Capacitive_kVArh * reactive_penalty_rate_usd_kvarh   // [USD] Capacitive reactive penalty
Demand_Charge_USD = peak_demand_kw * demand_charge_usd_kw   // [USD] Peak demand charge
Total_Monthly_Bill_USD = Active_Energy_Cost_USD + Inductive_Penalty_USD + Capacitive_Penalty_USD + Demand_Charge_USD   // [USD/month]
Annual_Bill_USD = Total_Monthly_Bill_USD * 12   // [USD/year]
Required_Correction_kVAr = active_energy_kwh * (SQRT(1 / POWER(Power_Factor, 2) - 1) - SQRT(1 / POWER(target_pf, 2) - 1))   // [kVAr] Capacitor bank sizing
Annual_Penalty_Savings_USD = (Inductive_Penalty_USD + Capacitive_Penalty_USD) * 12   // [USD/year] Savings from PFC
PFC_Payback_Months = pfc_capex_usd > 0 && Annual_Penalty_Savings_USD > 0 ? (pfc_capex_usd / Annual_Penalty_Savings_USD) * 12 : 0   // [months] PFC investment payback
Load_Factor_pct = (active_energy_kwh / (peak_demand_kw * 720)) * 100   // [%] Monthly load factor (720h/month)

active_energy_positive.error_msg: Active energy consumption must be positive — division by zero in power factor calculation.

pf_physical_range.error_msg: Calculated power factor exceeds 1.0 — physically impossible. Check reactive energy meter readings. Possible cause: capacitive reactive (leading) is over-reported.

demand_vs_contracted.error_msg: Peak demand exceeds contracted demand by >10%. Grid operator will apply demand overage penalty. Immediate load management or contract renegotiation required.

smart_warnings.message: POOR POWER FACTOR (<0.90): Significant reactive energy penalty and oversized distribution equipment. Automatic power factor correction (APFC) capacitor bank is almost certainly economically justified. Every 0.01 improvement in PF reduces reactive penalty and may enable demand reduction.
Suboptimal power factor (0.90–0.95): Below most utility target of PF ≥ 0.95. Reactive penalty charges are accumulating. PFC capacitor payback is typically 12–24 months at this level.

REACTIVE PENALTY >10% OF ACTIVE COST: Reactive energy is adding over 10% to your energy bill. This is avoidable with power factor correction hardware. Annual penalty cost shown — compare to PFC CAPEX for immediate payback analysis.

LOW LOAD FACTOR (<40%): High peak demand relative to average consumption. Demand charge is disproportionately large. Strategies: (1) Peak shaving with battery storage or generator, (2) Load shifting to off-peak hours, (3) Demand response programme, (4) Contract demand renegotiation.

Demand charge >30% of energy cost: Peak demand management has high financial leverage. Each kW of peak demand reduction saves demand_charge_usd_kw USD per month permanently. Industrial battery storage or VFD-based soft starters should be evaluated.

OVER-COMPENSATION DETECTED: Capacitive reactive energy penalty is being charged. Existing PFC capacitor bank is over-sized for current load. Partial deactivation of capacitor stages during light-load periods required. Check APFC controller thresholds.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Power_Factor > 1.0
fmea.effect: Calculated power factor exceeds 1.0 — physically impossible. Check reactive energy meter readings. Possible cause: capacitive reactive (leading) is over-reported.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: contracted_demand_kw > 0 && peak_demand_kw > contracted_demand_kw * 1.1
fmea.effect: Peak demand exceeds contracted demand by >10%. Grid operator will apply demand overage penalty. Immediate load management or contract renegotiation required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: POOR POWER FACTOR (<0.90): Significant reactive energy penalty and oversized distribution equipment. Automatic power factor correction (APFC) capacitor bank is almost certainly economically justified. Every 0.01 improvement in PF reduces reactive penalty and may enable demand reduction.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Power_Factor < 0.90

fmea.failureMode: Suboptimal power factor (0.90–0.95): Below most utility target of PF ≥ 0.95. Reactive penalty charges are accumulating. PFC capacitor payback is typically 12–24 months at this level.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Power_Factor >= 0.90 && Power_Factor < 0.95

fmea.failureMode: REACTIVE PENALTY >10% OF ACTIVE COST: Reactive energy is adding over 10% to your energy bill. This is avoidable with power factor correction hardware. Annual penalty cost shown — compare to PFC CAPEX for immediate payback analysis.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Inductive_Penalty_USD > Active_Energy_Cost_USD * 0.1

fmea.failureMode: LOW LOAD FACTOR (<40%): High peak demand relative to average consumption. Demand charge is disproportionately large. Strategies: (1) Peak shaving with battery storage or generator, (2) Load shifting to off-peak hours, (3) Demand response programme, (4) Contract demand renegotiation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Load_Factor_pct < 40

fmea.failureMode: Demand charge >30% of energy cost: Peak demand management has high financial leverage. Each kW of peak demand reduction saves demand_charge_usd_kw USD per month permanently. Industrial battery storage or VFD-based soft starters should be evaluated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Demand_Charge_USD > Active_Energy_Cost_USD * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: OVER-COMPENSATION DETECTED: Capacitive reactive energy penalty is being charged. Existing PFC capacitor bank is over-sized for current load. Partial deactivation of capacitor stages during light-load periods required. Check APFC controller thresholds.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Capacitive_Penalty_USD > 0

tool_name: Project Cost Escalation, Real Discount Rate & Inflation-Adjusted Budget (AACE / Fisher Equation)

inputs.name: Base Estimate — Material & Equipment Cost
inputs.hint: Cost in today's money (base date). Include bulk materials, major equipment, vendor supply.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Base Estimate — Labour Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Base Estimate — Subcontract Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Material / Equipment Escalation Rate
inputs.hint: PPI (Producer Price Index) for relevant commodity — steel: check LME | cement: local | energy equipment: IHS Markit
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Labour / Wage Escalation Rate
inputs.hint: National wage inflation or collective agreement rate for project location
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Subcontract / Indirect Escalation Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Project / Contract Duration
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Spend Profile (spend timing)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Effective Escalation Timing Factor
inputs.hint: Front-loaded: 0.35 | Uniform: 0.50 | Back-loaded: 0.65 | Single mid-point: 0.50
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Nominal Discount Rate (WACC or project hurdle)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: General CPI Inflation Rate (project economy)
inputs.hint: National CPI forecast for project country — Turkey 2024: ~45% | EU: ~3% | USD zone: ~3%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Risk Contingency Reserve
inputs.hint: AACE Class 3 estimate: 10–20% | Class 2: 5–15% | Class 1: 3–10%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Management Reserve (known-unknowns)
inputs.hint: PMBOK: separate from contingency — for scope changes beyond current baseline
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: Base_Total_Cost_USD = base_material_cost_usd + base_labour_cost_usd + base_subcontract_cost_usd   // [USD] Total base estimate
Effective_Duration_yrs = project_duration_years * spend_timing_factor   // [years] Weighted spend timing
Escalated_Material_USD = base_material_cost_usd * POWER(1 + material_escalation_pct_yr / 100, Effective_Duration_yrs)   // [USD] Escalated material cost
Escalated_Labour_USD = base_labour_cost_usd * POWER(1 + labour_escalation_pct_yr / 100, Effective_Duration_yrs)   // [USD] Escalated labour cost
Escalated_Subcontract_USD = base_subcontract_cost_usd * POWER(1 + subcontract_escalation_pct_yr / 100, Effective_Duration_yrs)   // [USD] Escalated subcontract
Total_Escalated_Base_USD = Escalated_Material_USD + Escalated_Labour_USD + Escalated_Subcontract_USD   // [USD] Total escalated estimate
Escalation_Amount_USD = Total_Escalated_Base_USD - Base_Total_Cost_USD   // [USD] Pure escalation delta
Escalation_Pct = (Escalation_Amount_USD / Base_Total_Cost_USD) * 100   // [%] Escalation as % of base
Contingency_USD = Total_Escalated_Base_USD * (risk_contingency_pct / 100)   // [USD] Risk contingency
Budget_with_Contingency_USD = Total_Escalated_Base_USD + Contingency_USD   // [USD] Authorised project budget
Management_Reserve_USD = Budget_with_Contingency_USD * (management_reserve_pct / 100)   // [USD] Management reserve
Total_Project_Authorisation_USD = Budget_with_Contingency_USD + Management_Reserve_USD   // [USD] Total cost at completion (TCA)
Real_Discount_Rate_pct = ((1 + nominal_discount_rate_pct / 100) / (1 + general_inflation_rate_pct / 100) - 1) * 100   // [%] Real rate — Fisher Equation
NPV_Cost_Real_USD = Total_Project_Authorisation_USD / POWER(1 + Real_Discount_Rate_pct / 100, project_duration_years)   // [USD] Present value of total cost in real terms

base_cost_positive.error_msg: Total base cost must be positive. Both material and labour cannot be zero simultaneously.

duration_minimum.error_msg: Project duration minimum 0.1 years (approximately 5 weeks). Below this, escalation is negligible.

inflation_vs_discount.error_msg: Inflation rate equals or exceeds nominal discount rate — Real discount rate is zero or negative (Fisher Equation). This represents hyperinflationary conditions. NPV analysis in real terms becomes unreliable. Use nominal cash flows instead.

smart_warnings.message: NEGATIVE REAL DISCOUNT RATE: Inflation exceeds nominal WACC. Every deferred cost actually costs more in real terms — there is no financial benefit to delaying project expenditure. This typically signals a hyperinflationary environment. Nominal cash flow analysis is required instead of real.

HIGH ESCALATION (>20%): Cost escalation exceeds 20% of base estimate. This level typically represents a material budget risk. Project sanction budget must include full escalation provision — underfunded projects are the primary cause of cost overruns in capital projects. Escalate to project sponsor.

MATERIAL ESCALATION OUTPACING LABOUR >2×: Commodity price volatility is the dominant risk. Evaluate: (1) Fixed-price supply contracts with early procurement, (2) Material price escalation clauses in contracts, (3) Alternative materials or supplier diversification.

LOW CONTINGENCY (<5%): Insufficient contingency for any estimate class. AACE Class 3 (budget estimate) requires 10–20%. Underfunding contingency is the most common cause of cost overruns on capital projects.

NO MANAGEMENT RESERVE on multi-year project: Projects >3 years face significant scope uncertainty. Zero management reserve leaves no headroom for authorised scope additions. PMBOK recommends 5–10% management reserve separate from risk contingency.

ESCALATION EXCEEDS CONTINGENCY: Price escalation alone is larger than the entire contingency reserve. Contingency will be fully consumed by inflation before any technical risk event. Increase contingency or renegotiate fixed-price contracts to include escalation clauses.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: base_material_cost_usd + base_labour_cost_usd <= 0
fmea.effect: Total base cost must be positive. Both material and labour cannot be zero simultaneously.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: general_inflation_rate_pct >= nominal_discount_rate_pct
fmea.effect: Inflation rate equals or exceeds nominal discount rate — Real discount rate is zero or negative (Fisher Equation). This represents hyperinflationary conditions. NPV analysis in real terms becomes unreliable. Use nominal cash flows instead.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE REAL DISCOUNT RATE: Inflation exceeds nominal WACC. Every deferred cost actually costs more in real terms — there is no financial benefit to delaying project expenditure. This typically signals a hyperinflationary environment. Nominal cash flow analysis is required instead of real.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Real_Discount_Rate_pct < 0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH ESCALATION (>20%): Cost escalation exceeds 20% of base estimate. This level typically represents a material budget risk. Project sanction budget must include full escalation provision — underfunded projects are the primary cause of cost overruns in capital projects. Escalate to project sponsor.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Escalation_Pct > 20

fmea.failureMode: MATERIAL ESCALATION OUTPACING LABOUR >2×: Commodity price volatility is the dominant risk. Evaluate: (1) Fixed-price supply contracts with early procurement, (2) Material price escalation clauses in contracts, (3) Alternative materials or supplier diversification.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: material_escalation_pct_yr > labour_escalation_pct_yr * 2

fmea.failureMode: LOW CONTINGENCY (<5%): Insufficient contingency for any estimate class. AACE Class 3 (budget estimate) requires 10–20%. Underfunding contingency is the most common cause of cost overruns on capital projects.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: risk_contingency_pct < 5

fmea.failureMode: NO MANAGEMENT RESERVE on multi-year project: Projects >3 years face significant scope uncertainty. Zero management reserve leaves no headroom for authorised scope additions. PMBOK recommends 5–10% management reserve separate from risk contingency.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: project_duration_years > 3 && management_reserve_pct === 0

fmea.failureMode: ESCALATION EXCEEDS CONTINGENCY: Price escalation alone is larger than the entire contingency reserve. Contingency will be fully consumed by inflation before any technical risk event. Increase contingency or renegotiate fixed-price contracts to include escalation clauses.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Escalation_Amount_USD > Contingency_USD

tool_name: Industrial Waste Stream Cost, Circularity Rate & ESG Compliance Analysis (ISO 14001 / EU Taxonomy)

inputs.name: Non-Hazardous Solid Waste Generated
inputs.hint: General industrial waste, packaging, non-contaminated scrap — Annex III EU WFD
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Hazardous Waste Generated
inputs.hint: Oils, solvents, heavy metal swarf, contaminated rags, chemical waste — EU Directive 2008/98/EC Annex III
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Industrial Wastewater Volume
inputs.hint: Process effluent, coolant wash, surface treatment discharge
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Waste Diverted from Landfill (recycled + recovered)
inputs.hint: Metal recycling, paper/cardboard, plastic, energy recovery — must not exceed total waste
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Non-Hazardous Waste Disposal Rate (landfill/incineration)
inputs.hint: Gate fee to licensed disposal facility. EU range: €50–150/t | Turkey: $30–80/t
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Hazardous Waste Disposal Rate
inputs.hint: Licensed hazardous waste contractor. EU range: €200–800/t depending on category
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Wastewater Treatment / Discharge Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Recycled Material Revenue (metal scrap / recovered material)
inputs.hint: Average net scrap value — steel scrap: $200–400/t | Al: $800–1200/t | Cu: $5000–8000/t
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: CO₂e per Tonne Non-Hazardous Waste (landfill)
inputs.hint: IPCC AR6: mixed industrial landfill ≈ 300–600 kgCO₂e/t | Incineration: 700–1000 kgCO₂e/t
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Potential Regulatory Fine (per violation event)
inputs.hint: From local environmental regulation — EU ELD: up to €1M+ | Turkey: per ÇevKan tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Probability of Regulatory Enforcement
inputs.hint: Based on inspection frequency and current compliance status — non-compliant sites: 20–40%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: ESG Reporting Framework
options: EU CSRD / ESRS E5 Resource Use
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: Total_Waste_Generated_t = non_hazardous_waste_t + hazardous_waste_t   // [t/year] Total solid waste
Waste_to_Landfill_t = MAX(0, non_hazardous_waste_t - recycled_diverted_t)   // [t/year] Non-hazardous waste sent to disposal
Landfill_Cost_USD = Waste_to_Landfill_t * landfill_disposal_rate_usd_t   // [USD/year] Non-hazardous disposal cost
Hazardous_Disposal_Cost_USD = hazardous_waste_t * hazardous_disposal_rate_usd_t   // [USD/year] Hazardous waste cost
Wastewater_Cost_USD = wastewater_m3 * wastewater_treatment_rate_usd_m3   // [USD/year] Wastewater treatment cost
Recycling_Revenue_USD = recycled_diverted_t * scrap_metal_revenue_usd_t   // [USD/year] Revenue from recovered materials
Risk_Weighted_Fine_USD = (violation_probability_pct / 100) * regulatory_fine_usd   // [USD/year] Expected regulatory penalty
Total_Gross_Waste_Cost_USD = Landfill_Cost_USD + Hazardous_Disposal_Cost_USD + Wastewater_Cost_USD + Risk_Weighted_Fine_USD   // [USD/year]
Net_Waste_Cost_USD = Total_Gross_Waste_Cost_USD - Recycling_Revenue_USD   // [USD/year] Net cost after recycling income
Circularity_Rate_pct = Total_Waste_Generated_t > 0 ? (recycled_diverted_t / Total_Waste_Generated_t) * 100 : 0   // [%] EU Taxonomy circularity KPI
Hazardous_Fraction_pct = Total_Waste_Generated_t > 0 ? (hazardous_waste_t / Total_Waste_Generated_t) * 100 : 0   // [%] Hazardous waste intensity
Waste_Intensity_t_per_MtUSD = Net_Waste_Cost_USD > 0 ? Total_Waste_Generated_t / (Net_Waste_Cost_USD / 1000000) : 0   // [t/M USD cost] Waste intensity ratio
Carbon_Footprint_Waste_tCO2e = Waste_to_Landfill_t * co2_per_tonne_waste_kg / 1000   // [tCO₂e/year] GHG from waste disposal
Cost_per_Tonne_Generated_USD = Total_Waste_Generated_t > 0 ? Net_Waste_Cost_USD / Total_Waste_Generated_t : 0   // [USD/t] All-in waste cost per tonne

standards: EU CSRD / ESRS E5 Resource Use & Circular Economy
IPCC AR6 Waste Sector Emission Factors

recycled_vs_generated.error_msg: Diverted waste cannot exceed total waste generated. Check input data — diverted fraction is a subset of total waste.

hazardous_rate_minimum.error_msg: Hazardous disposal rate below $50/tonne is unrealistically low. Licensed hazardous waste disposal is strictly regulated. Check units or confirm contractor rate — legal disposal typically costs $200–800/tonne.

smart_warnings.message: HIGH HAZARDOUS WASTE FRACTION (>20%): Hazardous waste disposal represents a significant cost and liability. Mandatory: (1) Waste audit to identify substitution opportunities (REACH compliance), (2) Solvent recovery system ROI analysis, (3) Licensed contractor insurance verification. Regulatory exposure is elevated.

smart_warnings.source: EU CSRD / ESRS E5 / EU Taxonomy
smart_warnings.message: CIRCULARITY RATE <20%: EU Taxonomy and CSRD require disclosure of circularity performance. Institutional investors and EU supply chain customers will classify this as a sustainability red flag. Circular economy action plan required for continued EU market access and ESG-linked financing eligibility.

EXPECTED REGULATORY PENALTY EXCEEDS RECYCLING REVENUE: Compliance risk is financially material. Investment in waste management systems (proper segregation, licensed storage, treatment capacity) should be evaluated against risk-weighted fine cost. Environmental insurance may be warranted.

SIGNIFICANT WASTE-RELATED EMISSIONS (>100 tCO₂e/year): This waste stream generates reportable Scope 3 emissions under GHG Protocol Category 5. Must be disclosed in CDP, GRI 306, and CSRD reporting. Diversion from landfill (composting, energy recovery, recycling) reduces both cost and carbon footprint.

UNTAPPED RECYCLING REVENUE: Scrap value of recyclable materials exceeds current landfill cost, but less than 50% of non-hazardous waste is being diverted. Increasing recovery rate will reduce costs AND generate additional revenue. Conduct waste composition audit to identify high-value streams.

HIGH ALL-IN WASTE COST (>$200/tonne generated): Above typical manufacturing benchmark. Dominant driver may be hazardous waste, wastewater treatment, or regulatory risk premium. Waste minimisation at source (lean process, material substitution) delivers permanent cost reduction.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: recycled_diverted_t > non_hazardous_waste_t + hazardous_waste_t
fmea.effect: Diverted waste cannot exceed total waste generated. Check input data — diverted fraction is a subset of total waste.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: violation_probability_pct < 0 || violation_probability_pct > 100
fmea.effect: Violation probability must be 0–100%.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: hazardous_waste_t > 0 && hazardous_disposal_rate_usd_t < 50
fmea.effect: Hazardous disposal rate below $50/tonne is unrealistically low. Licensed hazardous waste disposal is strictly regulated. Check units or confirm contractor rate — legal disposal typically costs $200–800/tonne.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: HIGH HAZARDOUS WASTE FRACTION (>20%): Hazardous waste disposal represents a significant cost and liability. Mandatory: (1) Waste audit to identify substitution opportunities (REACH compliance), (2) Solvent recovery system ROI analysis, (3) Licensed contractor insurance verification. Regulatory exposure is elevated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Hazardous_Fraction_pct > 20

fmea.failureMode: CIRCULARITY RATE <20%: EU Taxonomy and CSRD require disclosure of circularity performance. Institutional investors and EU supply chain customers will classify this as a sustainability red flag. Circular economy action plan required for continued EU market access and ESG-linked financing eligibility.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Circularity_Rate_pct < 20
fmea.control_measure: EU CSRD / ESRS E5 / EU Taxonomy

fmea.failureMode: EXPECTED REGULATORY PENALTY EXCEEDS RECYCLING REVENUE: Compliance risk is financially material. Investment in waste management systems (proper segregation, licensed storage, treatment capacity) should be evaluated against risk-weighted fine cost. Environmental insurance may be warranted.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Risk_Weighted_Fine_USD > Recycling_Revenue_USD

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT WASTE-RELATED EMISSIONS (>100 tCO₂e/year): This waste stream generates reportable Scope 3 emissions under GHG Protocol Category 5. Must be disclosed in CDP, GRI 306, and CSRD reporting. Diversion from landfill (composting, energy recovery, recycling) reduces both cost and carbon footprint.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Carbon_Footprint_Waste_tCO2e > 100

fmea.failureMode: UNTAPPED RECYCLING REVENUE: Scrap value of recyclable materials exceeds current landfill cost, but less than 50% of non-hazardous waste is being diverted. Increasing recovery rate will reduce costs AND generate additional revenue. Conduct waste composition audit to identify high-value streams.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Recycling_Revenue_USD > Landfill_Cost_USD && recycled_diverted_t < non_hazardous_waste_t * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: HIGH ALL-IN WASTE COST (>$200/tonne generated): Above typical manufacturing benchmark. Dominant driver may be hazardous waste, wastewater treatment, or regulatory risk premium. Waste minimisation at source (lean process, material substitution) delivers permanent cost reduction.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cost_per_Tonne_Generated_USD > 200

tool_name: Pump System Curve & BEP Deviation — Hydraulic Power Loss & Cavitation Risk

inputs.name: Rated (BEP) Flow Rate (Q_BEP)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Actual Operating Flow Rate (Q_act)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Rated (BEP) Total Head (H_BEP)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: BEP Hydraulic Efficiency (η_BEP)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Fluid Density (ρ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: NPSHr (Required Net Positive Suction Head)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: NPSHa (Available Net Positive Suction Head)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Installed Motor Shaft Power (P_motor)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

formulas: Q_ratio = actual_flow_m3h / rated_flow_m3h   // [dimensionless] Flow Deviation Ratio Reference: HI 9.6.3-2017 §4.1
eta_act = rated_efficiency_pct * (1 - 0.3 * (1 - Q_ratio)^2)   // [%] Affinity-Law Corrected Efficiency Reference: ISO 9906:2012 Annex B — parabolic efficiency correction
P_hyd = (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81 * actual_flow_m3h/3600 * rated_head_m) / 1000   // [kW] Actual Hydraulic Power Reference: API 610-12th Ed. §6.1.8
P_overload_pct = ((P_hyd / (eta_act/100)) - motor_power_kw) / motor_power_kw * 100   // [%] Power Overshoot (Overload Risk) Reference: HI 9.6.3-2017 §4.3 — motor overload threshold
npsh_margin = npsha_m / npshr_m   // [dimensionless] NPSH Margin Ratio Reference: HI 9.6.1-2012 — minimum margin = 1.1 for clear water
E_loss_kwh = (motor_power_kw - P_hyd/(eta_act/100)) * 8760   // [kWh/yr] Annual Hydraulic Energy Loss vs BEP Reference: ISO 50001:2018 energy baseline method

V1.error_msg: Operation below 50% BEP flow: recirculation risk — increase flow or install bypass

V2.error_msg: Operation above 120% BEP: cavitation and overload risk — throttle or trim impeller

V3.error_msg: NPSHa margin < 1.1× NPSHr: cavitation onset likely — lower suction lift or increase inlet pressure

smart_warnings.message: Severe left-of-BEP operation: axial thrust reversal and bearing fatigue risk per HI 9.6.3
Right-of-BEP runout: NPSH curve steep rise — verify NPSHa at this flow
NPSHa < NPSHr: cavitation active — impeller erosion will occur within 500 operating hours
Motor overload >5%: thermal trip risk — verify service factor or upsize motor
Operating efficiency <75% of BEP: energy waste exceeds ISO 50001 baseline threshold
Large pump at low BEP efficiency: consider hydraulic redesign or variable-speed drive

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_flow_m3h < rated_flow_m3h * 0.5
fmea.effect: Operation below 50% BEP flow: recirculation risk — increase flow or install bypass
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_flow_m3h > rated_flow_m3h * 1.2
fmea.effect: Operation above 120% BEP: cavitation and overload risk — throttle or trim impeller
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: npsha_m < npshr_m * 1.1
fmea.effect: NPSHa margin < 1.1× NPSHr: cavitation onset likely — lower suction lift or increase inlet pressure
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Severe left-of-BEP operation: axial thrust reversal and bearing fatigue risk per HI 9.6.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_ratio < 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Right-of-BEP runout: NPSH curve steep rise — verify NPSHa at this flow
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_ratio > 1.15

fmea.failureMode: NPSHa < NPSHr: cavitation active — impeller erosion will occur within 500 operating hours
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: npsh_margin < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Motor overload >5%: thermal trip risk — verify service factor or upsize motor
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_overload_pct > 5

fmea.failureMode: Operating efficiency <75% of BEP: energy waste exceeds ISO 50001 baseline threshold
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_act_pct < rated_efficiency_pct * 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Large pump at low BEP efficiency: consider hydraulic redesign or variable-speed drive
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rated_efficiency_pct < 60 && motor_power_kw > 75

tool_name: Bolted Flange Joint Integrity — Gasket Seating Stress, Bolt Load & Leak Tightness Class

inputs.name: Internal Design Pressure (P_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Flange Outside Diameter (OD)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Gasket Inner Diameter (G_ID)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Gasket Outer Diameter (G_OD)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Gasket Material Factor (m)
inputs.note: ASME VIII App 2 Table 2-5.1: spiral-wound SS/graphite m=3.0, full-face rubber m=0.5
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Minimum Gasket Seating Stress (y)
inputs.note: ASME VIII App 2 Table 2-5.1
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Number of Bolts (n_b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bolt Root Tensile Stress Area (A_b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Bolt Material Yield Strength (S_y)
inputs.note: ASTM A193 B7: S_y=724 MPa at ambient
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Yield/ultimate strength (steel): CoV=0.07

formulas: N = (gasket_od_mm - gasket_id_mm) / 2; b_eff_mm = (N <= 12.7) ? N/2 : 2.53*Math.sqrt(N/2)   // [mm] Effective Gasket Seating Width (b_eff_mm) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 App.2 §2-5

G_mean_m = gasket_id_mm + 2*b_eff_mm; H_kN = Math.PI/4 * (G_mean_m/1000)^2 * design_pressure_bar * 1e5 / 1000   // [kN] Hydrostatic End Force (H_kN) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII App.2 — hydrostatic load on mean gasket diameter

H_p_kN = 2*Math.PI*G_mean_m*b_eff_mm*1e-3 * gasket_m_factor * design_pressure_bar*1e5/1000; W_op_total_kN = H_kN + H_p_kN   // [kN] Minimum Required Bolt Load — Operating Condition (W_op) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII App.2 Eq.(2)
W_seat_kN = Math.PI * G_mean_m * b_eff_mm*1e-3 * gasket_y_mpa * 1000   // [kN] Minimum Required Bolt Load — Seating Condition (W_seat) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII App.2 Eq.(3)
W_avail_kN = bolt_count * bolt_root_area_mm2 * 1e-6 * bolt_material_yield_mpa * 1000 * 0.7   // [kN] Total Available Bolt Load
bolt_ratio = W_avail_kN / Math.max(W_op_total_kN, W_seat_kN)   // [dimensionless] Bolt Load Adequacy Ratio Reference: EN 1591-1:2013 §7 — ratio ≥ 1.0 required

V1.error_msg: G_OD must exceed G_ID — check gasket dimension inputs

V2.error_msg: Gasket OD cannot exceed flange OD — invalid geometry

V3.error_msg: Available bolt load insufficient — joint will leak or gasket will not seat

smart_warnings.message: Bolt load deficit: add bolts, upgrade bolt grade, or use lower-y gasket per ASME App.2 §2-5
Excessive bolt load: gasket crush risk — verify gasket crush limit per manufacturer data
High-pressure service with low m-factor: soft gasket inadequate — use spiral-wound or ring-type joint
Wide effective seating width: bolt load demand increases sharply — verify torque values per PCC-1-2019
Few bolts at elevated pressure: flange rotation risk — minimum 8 bolts recommended per ASME B16.5
High-strength bolting: stress corrosion cracking susceptibility in H₂S service per NACE MR0175

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: gasket_od_mm <= gasket_id_mm
fmea.effect: G_OD must exceed G_ID — check gasket dimension inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: gasket_od_mm > flange_od_mm
fmea.effect: Gasket OD cannot exceed flange OD — invalid geometry
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bolt_ratio < 1.0
fmea.effect: Available bolt load insufficient — joint will leak or gasket will not seat
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Bolt load deficit: add bolts, upgrade bolt grade, or use lower-y gasket per ASME App.2 §2-5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_ratio < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Excessive bolt load: gasket crush risk — verify gasket crush limit per manufacturer data
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_ratio > 2.5

fmea.failureMode: High-pressure service with low m-factor: soft gasket inadequate — use spiral-wound or ring-type joint
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_pressure_bar > 100 && gasket_m_factor < 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Wide effective seating width: bolt load demand increases sharply — verify torque values per PCC-1-2019
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: b_eff_mm > 19

fmea.failureMode: Few bolts at elevated pressure: flange rotation risk — minimum 8 bolts recommended per ASME B16.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_count < 8 && design_pressure_bar > 40

fmea.failureMode: High-strength bolting: stress corrosion cracking susceptibility in H₂S service per NACE MR0175
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_material_yield_mpa > 1200

tool_name: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger Thermal Sizing — LMTD, NTU-ε & Fouling Margin

inputs.name: Thermal Duty (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Overall Clean Heat Transfer Coefficient (U_clean)
inputs.note: Typical: water/water 800–1500, gas/gas 50–300, condensers 500–2000
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Hot-Side Fouling Resistance (R_f,hot)
inputs.note: TEMA Table RGP-T-2.4: cooling water R_f = 0.176 m²K/kW
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Cold-Side Fouling Resistance (R_f,cold)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: LMTD Correction Factor (F) — Multi-Pass
inputs.note: F=1 for true counterflow; F≥0.75 required per TEMA §7
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

formulas: dT1 = T_hot_in_c - T_cold_out_c; dT2 = T_hot_out_c - T_cold_in_c; LMTD_cf_K = (Math.abs(dT1 - dT2) < 0.001) ? dT1 : (dT1 - dT2) / Math.log(dT1/dT2)   // [K] Log Mean Temperature Difference — Counterflow (LMTD_cf_K) Reference: ISO 15547-1:2005 §5.2 — L'Hôpital limit applied when dT1 ≈ dT2
LMTD_eff_K = F_correction * LMTD_cf_K   // [K] Corrected LMTD Reference: TEMA §7 — multi-pass correction
U_dirty_w_m2k = 1 / (1/U_clean_w_m2k + (Rf_hot_m2k_kw + Rf_cold_m2k_kw) * 1000)   // [W/m²·K] Dirty (Fouled) Overall HTC Reference: TEMA C-7.1 — combined fouling resistance
A_req = (Q_duty_kw * 1000) / (U_dirty_w_m2k * LMTD_eff_K)   // [m²] Required Heat Transfer Area — Fouled (A_req) Reference: ASME Section VIII / TEMA sizing basis
