CF = U_dirty_w_m2k / U_clean_w_m2k * 100   // [%] Cleanliness Factor Reference: TEMA §T-2.4 — CF < 85% triggers cleaning inspection

Q_max = Math.min(Q_duty_kw / Math.abs(T_hot_in_c - T_cold_in_c)) * Math.abs(T_hot_in_c - T_cold_in_c); epsilon = Q_duty_kw / (Q_duty_kw / (1 - (T_hot_out_c - T_hot_in_c)/(T_cold_in_c - T_hot_in_c)))   // [%] Thermal Effectiveness (ε) Reference: NTU-ε method — Incropera 7th Ed. Ch.11

V1.error_msg: Hot-side outlet must be cooler than inlet — check terminal temperatures

V2.error_msg: Cold-side outlet must be hotter than inlet — check terminal temperatures

V3.error_msg: Hot outlet approaches cold inlet temperature: cross-temperature pinch — reduce duty or add surface area

smart_warnings.message: LMTD F-factor < 0.75: thermal inefficiency and flow maldistribution risk — split into two units or use pure counterflow
Cleanliness factor < 85%: fouling penalty severe — schedule CIP or mechanical cleaning within 6 months
Effective LMTD < 5 K: very large surface area required — evaluate stream mixing or upstream temperature shift
Low U-value for high-duty service: excessive area — verify fluid velocities and baffling
High combined fouling resistance: fouling margin > 35% — verify TEMA class selection for this service

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_hot_out_c >= T_hot_in_c
fmea.effect: Hot-side outlet must be cooler than inlet — check terminal temperatures
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_cold_out_c <= T_cold_in_c
fmea.effect: Cold-side outlet must be hotter than inlet — check terminal temperatures
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_hot_out_c < T_cold_in_c + 5
fmea.effect: Hot outlet approaches cold inlet temperature: cross-temperature pinch — reduce duty or add surface area
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: LMTD F-factor < 0.75: thermal inefficiency and flow maldistribution risk — split into two units or use pure counterflow
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_correction < 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Cleanliness factor < 85%: fouling penalty severe — schedule CIP or mechanical cleaning within 6 months
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cleanliness_factor_pct < 85

fmea.failureMode: Effective LMTD < 5 K: very large surface area required — evaluate stream mixing or upstream temperature shift
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: LMTD_eff_K < 5

fmea.failureMode: Low U-value for high-duty service: excessive area — verify fluid velocities and baffling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: U_clean_w_m2k < 200 && Q_duty_kw > 1000

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rf_hot_m2k_kw + Rf_cold_m2k_kw > 0.5

tool_name: Electrical Motor Energy Audit — Load Factor, Efficiency Penalty & VFD Retrofit ROI

inputs.name: Motor Nameplate Power (P_rated)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Measured Input Power at Operating Point
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Nameplate Full-Load Efficiency (η_FL)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Electricity Tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Average Operating Load Factor (% of rated output)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: VFD Capital Cost (installed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Expected Energy Saving with VFD
inputs.note: Fan/pump affinity laws: saving ≈ 1-(load_ratio)³; typical 20–40% for centrifugal loads
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: LF = avg_load_pct / 100   // [dimensionless] Load Factor Reference: IEC 60034-30-1 §4 — load factor definition

eta_partload = nameplate_efficiency_pct * LF / (LF^2 * (1 - nameplate_efficiency_pct/100) * 2 + LF * nameplate_efficiency_pct/100 + (1-LF)^2 * 0.05 * (1-nameplate_efficiency_pct/100))   // [%] Part-Load Efficiency (Eff@LF) Reference: NEMA MG1-2016 Part 30 — semi-empirical part-load efficiency model
E_current_kwh_yr = measured_input_kw * operating_hours_yr   // [kWh/yr] Annual Energy Consumption — Current
E_vfd_kwh_yr = E_current_kwh_yr * (1 - vfd_energy_saving_pct/100)   // [kWh/yr] Annual Energy Consumption — With VFD
saving_usd_yr = (E_current_kwh_yr - E_vfd_kwh_yr) * electricity_rate_usd_kwh   // [USD/yr] Annual Energy Cost Saving Reference: ISO 50001:2018 §6.3 — energy baseline and saving verification
payback_yr = vfd_cost_usd / saving_usd_yr   // [years] VFD Simple Payback Period

V1.error_msg: Measured input exceeds 115% of rated power: motor overloaded or power measurement error

V2.error_msg: Load factor <40%: motor significantly oversized — downsizing or VFD mandatory for IE3 compliance

V3.error_msg: Negative saving: check VFD saving input or operating hours

smart_warnings.message: Load factor < 50%: motor running in low-efficiency zone — IE3 motors below 50% load see 5–15% efficiency drop per IEC 60034-30-1
VFD payback > 5 years: marginal ROI — re-evaluate saving estimate or negotiate capital cost
VFD payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritize for next capital cycle
Motor below IE2 threshold for this power class: EU Regulation 640/2009 compliance risk — IE3 minimum required >7.5 kW
High-utilisation + high saving: evaluate premium IE4/IE5 motor replacement in parallel with VFD

fmea.effect: Measured input exceeds 115% of rated power: motor overloaded or power measurement error
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Negative saving: check VFD saving input or operating hours
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: LF < 0.5

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_yr > 5

tool_name: Compressed Air System Leak Loss Quantification & Repair ROI

inputs.name: System Operating Pressure (P_sys)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Total Installed Compressor Power
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Observed Load/Unload Duty Cycle (loaded fraction)
inputs.note: Measure with cycle timer: loaded time / total cycle time
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Estimated Legitimate Demand (% of compressor capacity)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Compressor Specific Power
inputs.note: Typical oil-flooded rotary screw at 7 bar(g): 5.5–7 kW/(m³/min)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Electricity Tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Estimated Total Leak Repair Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: leak_fraction = load_unload_ratio - baseline_demand_pct/100   // [fraction] Leak Fraction of Compressor Output Reference: DOE Compressed Air Challenge — Fundamentals §3.2
P_leak_kw = compressor_power_kw * Math.max(leak_fraction, 0)   // [kW] Wasted Compressor Power Due to Leaks Reference: ISO 11011:2013 §7.3 — leak power calculation
Q_leak_m3min = P_leak_kw / specific_power_kw_m3min   // [m³/min] Leaked Air Flow Rate Equivalent Reference: CAGI Data Sheet method — volume flow from specific power
E_leak_kwh_yr = P_leak_kw * operating_hours_yr   // [kWh/yr] Annual Wasted Energy — Leaks
leak_cost_usd_yr = E_leak_kwh_yr * electricity_rate_usd_kwh   // [USD/yr] Annual Cost of Leakage Reference: ISO 11011:2013 §8 — monetised loss
payback_months = (repair_cost_usd / leak_cost_usd_yr) * 12   // [months] Repair Payback Period

V1.error_msg: Duty cycle below demand estimate: leak fraction is zero — re-measure duty cycle during production

V2.error_msg: Leak fraction >40%: measurement or demand estimate may be unreliable — validate with ultrasonic leak detection

Repair payback < 6 months: high-priority action — schedule within current maintenance cycle
Repair payback > 24 months: re-evaluate repair scope — prioritise high-flow leaks first (orifice Q ∝ d²)
Leaked flow >30% of compressor capacity: system pressure stability compromised — repair before load expansion
High utilisation: consider automated leak monitoring (ultrasonic IoT sensors) for continuous detection per DOE guidelines

fmea.effect: Duty cycle below demand estimate: leak fraction is zero — re-measure duty cycle during production

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: High pressure with low specific power: verify compressor data — possible instrument error
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: leak_fraction > 0.2

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_months < 6

tool_name: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness & MAWP — Cylindrical & Spherical Shell Design

inputs.name: Design Internal Pressure (P)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Shell Inside Diameter (D_i)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Material Allowable Stress at Design Temp (S)
inputs.note: ASME BPVC II-D: SA-516-70 at 38°C → S=138 MPa; SA-240-316L → S=115 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Weld Joint Efficiency Factor (E)
inputs.note: E=1.0: full RT; E=0.85: spot RT; E=0.70: no RT — ASME VIII UW-12
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Corrosion Allowance (CA)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Shell Geometry
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Design Temperature
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Mill Undertolerance (MT)
inputs.note: ASME B36.10M: 12.5% standard mill undertolerance for plate
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: P_mpa = design_pressure_bar * 0.1; t_circ_mm = (shell_geometry === 'cylindrical') ? (P_mpa * inside_diameter_mm / 2) / (allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.6 * P_mpa) : (P_mpa * inside_diameter_mm / 2) / (2 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.2 * P_mpa)   // [mm] Required Wall Thickness — Circumferential Stress (t_circ_mm) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 UG-27(c)(1) — cylindrical; UG-27(d) — spherical

t_nominal_mm = (t_circ_mm + corrosion_allowance_mm) / (1 - mill_undertolerance_pct / 100)   // [mm] Nominal Required Thickness (incl. CA + mill tolerance) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII UG-16(b) — minimum ordered thickness

t_eff_mm = t_nominal_mm * (1 - mill_undertolerance_pct/100) - corrosion_allowance_mm; MAWP_bar = (shell_geometry === 'cylindrical') ? (allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency * t_eff_mm) / (inside_diameter_mm/2 + 0.6*t_eff_mm) : (2 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency * t_eff_mm) / (inside_diameter_mm/2 + 0.2*t_eff_mm)   // [bar(g)] Maximum Allowable Working Pressure — Cylindrical (MAWP) Reference: ASME BPVC VIII UG-27 rearranged for MAWP
Dt_ratio = inside_diameter_mm / t_nominal_mm   // [dimensionless] Pressure-Thickness Product (D/t Ratio) Reference: Thin-wall criterion: D/t > 20 validates membrane stress approach
safety_factor = MAWP_bar / design_pressure_bar   // [dimensionless] MAWP Safety Factor vs Design Pressure Reference: ASME BPVC VIII UG-99: hydrostatic test at 1.3× MAWP
P_test_bar = MAWP_bar * 1.3   // [bar(g)] Hydrostatic Test Pressure Reference: ASME BPVC VIII UG-99(b) — 1.3× MAWP at ambient temperature

standards: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

V1.error_msg: D/t < 20: thick-wall condition — use Lamé equations per ASME VIII Div.1 Appendix 1-2; membrane formula is non-conservative

V2.error_msg: Temperature > 425°C: verify material allowable includes creep derating per ASME BPVC II-D Table 1A

V3.error_msg: MAWP < design pressure: vessel underdesigned — increase wall thickness or reduce operating pressure

smart_warnings.message: MAWP within 5% of design pressure: no inspection margin — increase nominal thickness by minimum 10%
smart_warnings.source: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

smart_warnings.message: High-pressure service with reduced joint efficiency: full radiographic testing (RT) strongly recommended — E=1.0 reduces wall thickness requirement
smart_warnings.source: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

smart_warnings.message: Low corrosion allowance at elevated temperature: enhanced inspection frequency required per API 510
smart_warnings.source: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

smart_warnings.message: Sub-zero service: impact testing mandatory per ASME BPVC VIII UCS-66 — verify MDMT (Minimum Design Metal Temperature)
smart_warnings.source: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

smart_warnings.message: D/t < 10: ultra-thick wall — membrane stress theory invalid; perform finite element or Div.2 Part 5 analysis
smart_warnings.source: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

smart_warnings.message: Large-diameter high-pressure vessel: wind/seismic loading and nozzle reinforcement analysis required per ASME UG-37
smart_warnings.source: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Dt_ratio < 20
fmea.effect: D/t < 20: thick-wall condition — use Lamé equations per ASME VIII Div.1 Appendix 1-2; membrane formula is non-conservative
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: design_temp_c > 425 && allowable_stress_mpa > 138
fmea.effect: Temperature > 425°C: verify material allowable includes creep derating per ASME BPVC II-D Table 1A
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: mawp_safety_factor < 1.0
fmea.effect: MAWP < design pressure: vessel underdesigned — increase wall thickness or reduce operating pressure
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: MAWP within 5% of design pressure: no inspection margin — increase nominal thickness by minimum 10%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mawp_safety_factor < 1.05
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: High-pressure service with reduced joint efficiency: full radiographic testing (RT) strongly recommended — E=1.0 reduces wall thickness requirement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: weld_joint_efficiency < 1.0 && design_pressure_bar > 100
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: Low corrosion allowance at elevated temperature: enhanced inspection frequency required per API 510
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: corrosion_allowance_mm < 1.5 && design_temp_c > 200
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_temp_c < -29

tool_name: Structural Steel Beam — Combined Bending, Shear & Lateral-Torsional Buckling Check

inputs.name: Applied Factored Bending Moment (M_u / M_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Applied Factored Shear Force (V_u / V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Elastic Section Modulus About Strong Axis (S_x / W_el,y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Plastic Section Modulus About Strong Axis (Z_x / W_pl,y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Web Shear Area (A_w)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (F_y / f_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Unbraced Length of Compression Flange (L_b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Limiting Laterally Unbraced Length for Full Plasticity (L_p)
inputs.note: From section tables or AISC 360 F2-5: L_p = 1.76·r_y·√(E/F_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Limiting Laterally Unbraced Length for Inelastic LTB (L_r)
inputs.note: From section tables or AISC 360 F2-6
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: M_p_knm = Z_x_cm3 * 1e-6 * Fy_mpa * 1000   // [kN·m] Nominal Plastic Moment Capacity (M_p) Reference: AISC 360-22 F2-1 / EC3 §6.2.5

M_n_knm = (Lb_m <= Lp_m) ? M_p_knm : (Lb_m >= Lr_m) ? (0.877 * Math.PI^2 * 200000 / ((Lb_m*1000)^2) * S_x_cm3 * 1e-6 * 1000) : (M_p_knm - (M_p_knm - 0.7*Fy_mpa*S_x_cm3*1e-6*1000) * (Lb_m - Lp_m)/(Lr_m - Lp_m))   // [kN·m] LTB-Reduced Nominal Moment Capacity (M_n) Reference: AISC 360-22 F2-2 (plastic), F2-3 (inelastic LTB), F2-4 (elastic LTB)
phi_Mn_knm = 0.9 * M_n_knm   // [kN·m] Design Moment Capacity (φM_n — LRFD) Reference: AISC 360-22 F1 — φ_b = 0.90
V_n_kn = 0.6 * Fy_mpa * Aw_cm2 * 1e-4 * 1000   // [kN] Nominal Shear Capacity (V_n) Reference: AISC 360-22 G2-1 — web shear yielding (C_v1 = 1.0 for compact web)
phi_Vn_kn = 1.0 * V_n_kn   // [kN] Design Shear Capacity (φV_n — LRFD) Reference: AISC 360-22 G1 — φ_v = 1.0 for rolled I-sections

DCR_moment = M_demand_knm / phi_Mn_knm; DCR_shear = V_demand_kn / phi_Vn_kn; DCR_combined = Math.max(DCR_moment, DCR_shear)   // [dimensionless] Combined Utilization Ratios Reference: AISC 360-22 — demand-capacity ratio; ≤ 1.0 required

V1.error_msg: L_r must exceed L_p — verify section table data inputs

V2.error_msg: Bending demand exceeds capacity: increase section depth, add lateral bracing, or use higher-grade steel

V3.error_msg: Shear demand exceeds capacity: increase web area (deeper section) or add web stiffeners

smart_warnings.message: Section overstressed: structural failure mode risk — do not proceed to fabrication without redesign
Unbraced length in elastic LTB zone: significant capacity reduction — add lateral restraints at midspan
High bending utilization with LTB active: consider intermediate bracing to restore full plastic capacity
High-strength steel with long unbraced length: LTB controls over yielding — bracing at L_p intervals mandatory
High shape factor (> 1.20): significant plastic reserve — verify section compactness per AISC Table B4.1b
High combined loading: check shear-moment interaction per AISC 360-22 G2 for non-compact webs

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Lr_m <= Lp_m
fmea.effect: L_r must exceed L_p — verify section table data inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR_moment > 1.0
fmea.effect: Bending demand exceeds capacity: increase section depth, add lateral bracing, or use higher-grade steel
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR_shear > 1.0
fmea.effect: Shear demand exceeds capacity: increase web area (deeper section) or add web stiffeners
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_combined > 1.0

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lb_m > Lr_m

fmea.failureMode: High bending utilization with LTB active: consider intermediate bracing to restore full plastic capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_moment > 0.85 && Lb_m > Lp_m

tool_name: Arc Welding Heat Input, HAZ Width & Preheat Temperature Calculation

inputs.name: Arc Voltage (U)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Welding Current (I)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Weld Travel Speed (v)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Process Thermal Efficiency Factor (η)
inputs.note: AWS D1.1-2020 Commentary §C-4.11 / EN 1011-1 Table B.1
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Base Metal Carbon Equivalent (CE_IIW)
inputs.note: CE = C + Mn/6 + (Cr+Mo+V)/5 + (Ni+Cu)/15 — IIW formula
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Base Metal Plate Thickness (t)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Diffusible Hydrogen Content of Weld Metal (H_d)
inputs.note: Per AWS A4.3 / ISO 3690 classification
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: HI_kj_mm = (arc_voltage_v * welding_current_a * thermal_efficiency * 60) / (travel_speed_mm_min * 1000)   // [kJ/mm] Arc Energy (Heat Input per Unit Length) Reference: AWS D1.1-2020 §4.11 / EN 1011-1:2009 §7
HAZ_mm = 0.9 * HI_kj_mm / (4.13e-3 * plate_thickness_mm)   // [mm] Estimated HAZ Width (Rosenthal 2D Model) Reference: Rosenthal (1946) thin-plate 2D heat flow; applicable when t < HAZ_mm/2

CET = base_metal_CE * 0.75 + 0.25; T_preheat_c = 350 * Math.sqrt(CET - 0.1 * Math.log10(HI_kj_mm * 1000) + 0.015 * Math.log10(hydrogen_content) + 0.005 * Math.log10(plate_thickness_mm)) - 0.08 * plate_thickness_mm   // [°C] Preheat Temperature — CET Method (EN 1011-2 Annex C) Reference: EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C — CET method (simplified; contact WPS engineer for full calculation)

T_preheat_aws_c = (base_metal_CE < 0.40) ? 10 : (base_metal_CE < 0.45) ? 66 : (base_metal_CE < 0.50) ? 107 : 150   // [°C] AWS D1.1 Preheat (CE-based, Table 3.2) Reference: AWS D1.1-2020 Table 3.2 — minimum preheat based on CE and thickness category
T_preheat_gov_c = Math.max(T_preheat_c, T_preheat_aws_c, 10)   // [°C] Governing Preheat Temperature Reference: Conservative: maximum of EN 1011-2 and AWS D1.1 requirements; minimum 10°C

t85_s = (6700 - 5 * T_preheat_gov_c) * HI_kj_mm * ((1/(300 - T_preheat_gov_c))^2 - (1/(700 - T_preheat_gov_c))^2) * 1e-3   // [s] Cooling Rate Indicator (t8/5 Proxy) Reference: Seyffarth & Kassatkin cooling time t8/5 — thin-plate approximation; governs HAZ hardness

V1.error_msg: Heat input > 3.5 kJ/mm: excessive for most structural steels — grain coarsening and toughness loss in HAZ

V2.error_msg: Heat input < 0.3 kJ/mm: very high cooling rate — hard HAZ and hydrogen cracking risk even with preheat

V3.error_msg: High CE + high hydrogen: severe cold cracking risk — use H5 or H2.5 consumables and increase preheat by 30°C

smart_warnings.message: Preheat > 150°C: verify interpass temperature control — max interpass typically 250°C for structural steels
CE > 0.50: high cold cracking susceptibility — post-weld hydrogen release bake (200°C × 2h) strongly recommended per EN 1011-2
HAZ width > 8 mm: large heat-affected zone — CTOD toughness testing at HAZ required per BS 7448-2
t8/5 < 5 s: rapid cooling — risk of martensite formation in HAZ; verify hardness < 350 HV per ISO 15614-1
GTAW at high current: consider GMAW for productivity — TIG heat input advantage diminishes above 200 A

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HI_kj_mm > 3.5
fmea.effect: Heat input > 3.5 kJ/mm: excessive for most structural steels — grain coarsening and toughness loss in HAZ
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HI_kj_mm < 0.3
fmea.effect: Heat input < 0.3 kJ/mm: very high cooling rate — hard HAZ and hydrogen cracking risk even with preheat
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: base_metal_CE > 0.65 && hydrogen_content > 5
fmea.effect: High CE + high hydrogen: severe cold cracking risk — use H5 or H2.5 consumables and increase preheat by 30°C
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Preheat > 150°C: verify interpass temperature control — max interpass typically 250°C for structural steels
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_preheat_gov_c > 150

fmea.failureMode: CE > 0.50: high cold cracking susceptibility — post-weld hydrogen release bake (200°C × 2h) strongly recommended per EN 1011-2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: base_metal_CE > 0.50

fmea.failureMode: HAZ width > 8 mm: large heat-affected zone — CTOD toughness testing at HAZ required per BS 7448-2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: HAZ_width_mm > 8

fmea.failureMode: t8/5 < 5 s: rapid cooling — risk of martensite formation in HAZ; verify hardness < 350 HV per ISO 15614-1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: t85_s < 5

fmea.failureMode: GTAW at high current: consider GMAW for productivity — TIG heat input advantage diminishes above 200 A
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: thermal_efficiency === 0.60 && welding_current_a > 250

tool_name: Refrigeration System COP, Refrigerant Mass Flow & Condenser Heat Rejection

inputs.name: Refrigeration Capacity / Evaporator Duty (Q_evap)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Compressor Isentropic Efficiency (η_is)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Refrigerant Enthalpy at Compressor Suction (h_1)
inputs.note: From refrigerant P-h chart or REFPROP at T_evap + superheat
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Refrigerant Enthalpy at Expansion Valve Inlet (h_3)
inputs.note: From refrigerant P-h chart at T_cond - subcooling
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Ideal Compressor Discharge Enthalpy (h_2s)
inputs.note: From refrigerant P-h chart: isentropic compression endpoint at P_cond
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

formulas: q_evap_kj_kg = h_evap_out_kj_kg - h_cond_out_kj_kg   // [kJ/kg] Specific Refrigerating Effect (q_evap) Reference: ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021 Ch.2 — vapour-compression cycle

h_2act_kj_kg = h_evap_out_kj_kg + (h_cond_in_kj_kg - h_evap_out_kj_kg) / (compressor_isentropic_eff_pct / 100)   // [kJ/kg] Actual Compressor Discharge Enthalpy (h_2act) Reference: η_is = (h_2s - h_1) / (h_2act - h_1)
w_comp_kj_kg = h_2act_kj_kg - h_evap_out_kj_kg   // [kJ/kg] Specific Compressor Work (w_comp)
m_dot_kg_s = Q_evap_kw / q_evap_kj_kg   // [kg/s] Refrigerant Mass Flow Rate
W_comp_kw = m_dot_kg_s * w_comp_kj_kg   // [kW] Compressor Power Input Reference: ASHRAE 15-2022 §8
COP = Q_evap_kw / W_comp_kw; Q_cond_kw = Q_evap_kw + W_comp_kw   // [dimensionless, kW] System COP and Condenser Heat Rejection Reference: First Law of Thermodynamics energy balance; EN 378-1:2016 §5

V1.error_msg: Condensing temperature must exceed evaporating temperature by at least 10 K — check cycle temperature inputs

V2.error_msg: Specific refrigerating effect ≤ 0: h_1 must exceed h_3 — verify P-h chart enthalpy values

V3.error_msg: COP < 1.0: very low system efficiency — verify enthalpy inputs and compressor efficiency

smart_warnings.message: Lift (T_cond - T_evap) > 55 K: consider two-stage compression or economiser circuit per ASHRAE 15 §8.4
Isentropic efficiency < 55%: compressor in degraded condition — check valve condition, clearance volume, and refrigerant charge
Suction superheat < 5 K: liquid slugging risk — compressor mechanical damage imminent; adjust expansion valve
Low COP for moderate evaporating temperature: check for non-condensables in system or refrigerant contamination
Condenser duty >80% above evaporator: high heat rejection load — verify cooling tower or condenser coil capacity

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_cond_c <= T_evap_c + 10
fmea.effect: Condensing temperature must exceed evaporating temperature by at least 10 K — check cycle temperature inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: q_evap_kj_kg <= 0
fmea.effect: Specific refrigerating effect ≤ 0: h_1 must exceed h_3 — verify P-h chart enthalpy values
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: COP < 1.0
fmea.effect: COP < 1.0: very low system efficiency — verify enthalpy inputs and compressor efficiency
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Lift (T_cond - T_evap) > 55 K: consider two-stage compression or economiser circuit per ASHRAE 15 §8.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_cond_c - T_evap_c > 55

fmea.failureMode: Isentropic efficiency < 55%: compressor in degraded condition — check valve condition, clearance volume, and refrigerant charge
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: compressor_isentropic_eff_pct < 55

fmea.failureMode: Suction superheat < 5 K: liquid slugging risk — compressor mechanical damage imminent; adjust expansion valve
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: superheat_k < 5

fmea.failureMode: Low COP for moderate evaporating temperature: check for non-condensables in system or refrigerant contamination
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP < 2.0 && T_evap_c > -10

fmea.failureMode: Condenser duty >80% above evaporator: high heat rejection load — verify cooling tower or condenser coil capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_cond_kw > Q_evap_kw * 1.8

tool_name: Industrial Noise Attenuation — Source SPL to Receiver Level & OSHA Dose Calculation

inputs.name: Sound Power Level of Source (L_W)
inputs.note: From equipment data sheet or measured per ISO 3744/3746
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Source-to-Receiver Distance (r)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Source Directivity Factor (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Excess Attenuation (barriers, air absorption, ground) (A_exc)
inputs.note: Per ISO 9613-2: sum of A_atm + A_gr + A_bar; use 3 dB as conservative indoor default
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Worker Exposure Duration per Shift
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: OSHA Permissible Criterion Level

options.label: 85 dB(A) — NIOSH REL / EU Action Level
80 dB(A) — EU Lower Exposure Action Value

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Identical Sources (N)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_div_db = 20 * Math.log10(distance_m) + 10 * Math.log10(4 * Math.PI / directivity_factor_Q)   // [dB] Geometric Spreading Attenuation (A_div) Reference: ISO 9613-2:1996 §7.1 — geometrical divergence
DeltaL_sources_db = 10 * Math.log10(num_identical_sources)   // [dB] Multi-Source Combination Correction Reference: Energy summation: 10 identical sources → +10 dB

Lp_receiver_db = Lw_source_db + DeltaL_sources_db - A_div_db - excess_attenuation_db   // [dB(A)] Receiver Sound Pressure Level (L_p) Reference: ISO 9613-2 §4 — basic outdoor sound propagation equation

T_perm_h = (osha_criterion_db === 90) ? 8 / Math.pow(2, (Lp_receiver_db - 90)/5) : 8 / Math.pow(2, (Lp_receiver_db - 85)/3)   // [h] OSHA Permissible Exposure Duration at L_p (T_perm) Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.95 Table G-16 (5 dB exchange rate) / NIOSH 3 dB exchange rate
dose_pct = (exposure_hours_per_shift / T_perm_h) * 100   // [%] Noise Dose (%) Reference: OSHA dose D = Σ(C_i/T_i) × 100; dose ≤ 100% mandatory
dB_reduction_needed = Math.max(0, Lp_receiver_db - osha_criterion_db)   // [dB] Required Attenuation for Compliance Reference: Hierarchy of noise control: engineering controls first per ISO 1996-1 §8

V1.error_msg: Very close range to high-power source: near-field effects dominate — ISO 9613 far-field model not valid below 1 m

V2.error_msg: Noise dose > 100%: OSHA PEL exceeded — mandatory engineering controls, hearing protection, or exposure reduction

V3.error_msg: SPL > 115 dB(A): OSHA ceiling limit — no unprotected exposure permissible regardless of duration

smart_warnings.message: Noise dose > 50%: OSHA Hearing Conservation Program mandatory — audiometric testing, training, hearing protection per 29 CFR 1910.95(c)
SPL >10 dB over criterion: hearing protection alone insufficient — engineering noise control required per ISO 1996-1 hierarchy
Required attenuation > 15 dB: barrier or enclosure study needed — verify with ISO 9613-2 barrier insertion loss calculation
Multiple sources (>10): total SPL increase plateaus — adding sources beyond 10× gives <1 dB additional increment
Permissible exposure < 1 hour: extreme noise environment — restrict access, mandatory enclosures, and continuous monitoring
Low excess attenuation at long range: re-evaluate air absorption per ISO 9613-1 — significant at >100 m above 1 kHz

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: distance_m < 1 && Lw_source_db > 120
fmea.effect: Very close range to high-power source: near-field effects dominate — ISO 9613 far-field model not valid below 1 m
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dose_pct > 100
fmea.effect: Noise dose > 100%: OSHA PEL exceeded — mandatory engineering controls, hearing protection, or exposure reduction
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Lp_receiver_db > 115
fmea.effect: SPL > 115 dB(A): OSHA ceiling limit — no unprotected exposure permissible regardless of duration
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Noise dose > 50%: OSHA Hearing Conservation Program mandatory — audiometric testing, training, hearing protection per 29 CFR 1910.95(c)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dose_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: SPL >10 dB over criterion: hearing protection alone insufficient — engineering noise control required per ISO 1996-1 hierarchy
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lp_receiver_db > osha_criterion_db + 10

fmea.failureMode: Required attenuation > 15 dB: barrier or enclosure study needed — verify with ISO 9613-2 barrier insertion loss calculation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dB_reduction_needed > 15

fmea.failureMode: Multiple sources (>10): total SPL increase plateaus — adding sources beyond 10× gives <1 dB additional increment
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: num_identical_sources > 10

fmea.failureMode: Permissible exposure < 1 hour: extreme noise environment — restrict access, mandatory enclosures, and continuous monitoring
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_perm_h < 1

fmea.failureMode: Low excess attenuation at long range: re-evaluate air absorption per ISO 9613-1 — significant at >100 m above 1 kHz
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: excess_attenuation_db < 2 && distance_m > 100

tool_name: Pipeline Wall Thickness, MAOP & Burst Pressure — Onshore Transmission Design

inputs.name: Maximum Allowable Operating Pressure (MAOP)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Pipe Outside Diameter (D_o)
inputs.note: API 5L / ISO 3183 standard sizes: DN50–DN1400
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Specified Minimum Yield Strength (SMYS)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Location Class (Population Density Zone)
inputs.note: ASME B31.8 §841.114 — design factor F per location class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Longitudinal Joint Factor (E)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Temperature Derating Factor (T)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Corrosion Allowance (CA)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Mill Wall Thickness Undertolerance
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: F_factor = {1: 0.72, 2: 0.60, 3: 0.50, 4: 0.40}[location_class]   // [dimensionless] Design Factor (F) from Location Class Reference: ASME B31.8-2022 §841.114 Table 841.114A

P_mpa = MAOP_bar * 0.1; t_min_mm = (P_mpa * pipe_OD_mm) / (2 * SMYS_mpa * F_factor * joint_factor_E * temperature_derating_T)   // [mm] Minimum Required Wall Thickness (t_min) — Barlow Formula Reference: ASME B31.8 §841.11 Eq.(1) / API 5L §A.3 — Barlow hoop stress formula

t_nominal_mm = (t_min_mm + corrosion_allowance_mm) / (1 - mill_tolerance_pct / 100)   // [mm] Nominal Ordered Thickness (incl. CA + mill tolerance) Reference: API 5L PSL2 §9.14 — dimensional tolerance on wall thickness
Dt_ratio = pipe_OD_mm / t_nominal_mm   // [dimensionless] D/t Ratio (Diameter-to-Thickness) Reference: API 5L — thin-wall criterion D/t < 100 for Barlow applicability

t_eff_mm = t_nominal_mm * (1 - mill_tolerance_pct/100) - corrosion_allowance_mm; P_burst_bar = (2 * SMYS_mpa * 1.4 * t_eff_mm / pipe_OD_mm) * 10   // [bar(g)] Theoretical Burst Pressure (Barlow Ultimate)
safety_margin = P_burst_bar / MAOP_bar   // [dimensionless] Safety Margin — Burst vs MAOP Reference: ASME B31.8 — minimum burst/MAOP margin depends on location class; Class 1 → ~2.78

V1.error_msg: D/t > 100: Barlow membrane formula loses accuracy — consult API RP 1111 for thin-wall pipes

V2.error_msg: Burst-to-MAOP margin < 1.5: insufficient structural reserve — increase wall thickness

V3.error_msg: Very thin wall on large-diameter pipe: handling and installation damage risk — minimum 4 mm recommended per pipeline construction practice

smart_warnings.message: Location class mismatch: Class 3/4 requires F≤0.50 per ASME B31.8 §841.114 — re-enter correct location class
Burst margin < 2.0×MAOP: marginal for Class 1 service — consider corrosion management plan per NACE SP0169
Low CA on high-pressure pipeline: establish inline inspection (ILI) baseline within first 5 years per API RP 1163
ERW pipe above X60: verify seam weld toughness qualification per ISO 3183 PSL2 CVN requirements
D/t < 15: heavy-wall pipe — evaluate buckle propagation arrest capacity per API RP 1111 §4.3 for offshore or steep terrain
High MAOP in populated area: quantitative risk assessment (QRA) may be required by local authority

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Dt_ratio > 100
fmea.effect: D/t > 100: Barlow membrane formula loses accuracy — consult API RP 1111 for thin-wall pipes
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: safety_margin < 1.5
fmea.effect: Burst-to-MAOP margin < 1.5: insufficient structural reserve — increase wall thickness
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: t_min_mm < 3.0 && pipe_OD_mm > 300
fmea.effect: Very thin wall on large-diameter pipe: handling and installation damage risk — minimum 4 mm recommended per pipeline construction practice
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Location class mismatch: Class 3/4 requires F≤0.50 per ASME B31.8 §841.114 — re-enter correct location class
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: location_class >= 3 && F_factor === 0.72

fmea.failureMode: Burst margin < 2.0×MAOP: marginal for Class 1 service — consider corrosion management plan per NACE SP0169
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: safety_margin < 2.0

fmea.failureMode: Low CA on high-pressure pipeline: establish inline inspection (ILI) baseline within first 5 years per API RP 1163
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: corrosion_allowance_mm < 1.5 && MAOP_bar > 70

fmea.failureMode: ERW pipe above X60: verify seam weld toughness qualification per ISO 3183 PSL2 CVN requirements
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: joint_factor_E < 1.0 && SMYS_mpa > 414

fmea.failureMode: D/t < 15: heavy-wall pipe — evaluate buckle propagation arrest capacity per API RP 1111 §4.3 for offshore or steep terrain
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Dt_ratio < 15

fmea.failureMode: High MAOP in populated area: quantitative risk assessment (QRA) may be required by local authority
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: MAOP_bar > 150 && location_class >= 2

tool_name: Fatigue Life Estimation — S-N Curve, Miner's Rule Cumulative Damage & Safety Factor

inputs.name: Applied Stress Range (Δσ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Stress Concentration Factor (K_t)
inputs.note: K_t=1 smooth bar; ~2 shoulder fillet; ~3–4 hole/notch — from Peterson's or FEA
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §7 — Analytical chemistry: reproducibility ±5%

inputs.name: Weld Detail Fatigue Class (FAT / Δσ_C at 2×10⁶ cycles)

options.label: FAT 125 — butt weld, full penetration, RT
FAT 71 — cruciform joint, fillet weld toe

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: S-N Curve Slope Exponent (m)
inputs.note: m=3 for welded details (IIW); m=5 base metal; second slope m=5 above knee per ASME
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total Expected Load Cycles Over Design Life (N_applied)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Mean Stress (σ_mean)
inputs.note: Positive = tensile; Goodman correction applied if σ_mean > 0
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Material Ultimate Tensile Strength (UTS / S_u)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Target Safety Factor on Life (SF_target)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: delta_sigma_eff_mpa = stress_range_mpa * stress_concentration_Kt   // [MPa] Effective Stress Range with Stress Concentration Reference: IIW Recommendations Doc. XIII-2460-13 §5.2

delta_sigma_corr_mpa = (mean_stress_mpa > 0) ? delta_sigma_eff_mpa / (1 - mean_stress_mpa / material_UTS_mpa) : delta_sigma_eff_mpa   // [MPa] Goodman Mean Stress Correction (Modified) Reference: Modified Goodman criterion — Shigley's 10th Ed. §6-12; IIW §5.5

N_ref = 2e6; N_allow = N_ref * Math.pow(fatigue_class_mpa / delta_sigma_corr_mpa, sn_slope_m)   // [cycles] Allowable Fatigue Life (N_allow) from S-N Curve Reference: IIW / EN 13445-3 Annex B — S-N curve: N = N_ref × (FAT/Δσ)^m
D_miner = applied_cycles_N / N_allow_cycles   // [dimensionless] Miner's Cumulative Damage Ratio (D) Reference: Palmgren-Miner rule — D < 1.0 required; D < 0.5 recommended for welded joints per IIW
SF_actual = N_allow_cycles / applied_cycles_N   // [dimensionless] Calculated Safety Factor on Life Reference: Life-based safety factor; compare to SF_target

delta_sigma_perm = fatigue_class_mpa * Math.pow(2e6 / (applied_cycles_N * safety_factor_target), 1/sn_slope_m)   // [MPa] Permissible Stress Range for Target Life (Δσ_perm) Reference: Design stress range ensuring N_allow = N_applied × SF_target

V1.error_msg: Applied stress range > 3× FAT class: extremely short fatigue life predicted — verify stress inputs or select higher FAT class

V2.error_msg: Mean stress ≥ UTS: static fracture condition — Goodman correction invalid; reduce mean stress

V3.error_msg: Fatigue life insufficient: Miner damage D > 1.0 — redesign geometry, reduce loads, or improve weld class

smart_warnings.message: D > 0.5 on welded joint: IIW §9.5.3 recommends D_limit = 0.5 for welded structures without fracture mechanics backup
Safety factor below target: reduce applied cycles, increase section, improve weld quality (higher FAT), or add postweld treatment
K_t > 3.0: severe notch effect — consider redesign to smooth geometry; shot peening or HFMI treatment can increase FAT class by 1–2 steps
Very high cycle regime (>10⁸): below ASTM E466 endurance limit boundary — verify S-N curve includes infinite-life region
Mean stress >50% UTS: Goodman correction significant — consider residual stress relief (PWHT) to reduce effective mean stress

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: delta_sigma_corr_mpa > fatigue_class_mpa * 3
fmea.effect: Applied stress range > 3× FAT class: extremely short fatigue life predicted — verify stress inputs or select higher FAT class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: mean_stress_mpa >= material_UTS_mpa
fmea.effect: Mean stress ≥ UTS: static fracture condition — Goodman correction invalid; reduce mean stress
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SF_actual < 1.0
fmea.effect: Fatigue life insufficient: Miner damage D > 1.0 — redesign geometry, reduce loads, or improve weld class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: D > 0.5 on welded joint: IIW §9.5.3 recommends D_limit = 0.5 for welded structures without fracture mechanics backup
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: D_miner > 0.5 && fatigue_class_mpa <= 90

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_actual < safety_factor_target

fmea.failureMode: K_t > 3.0: severe notch effect — consider redesign to smooth geometry; shot peening or HFMI treatment can increase FAT class by 1–2 steps
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stress_concentration_Kt > 3.0

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: applied_cycles_N > 1e8
