fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mean_stress_mpa > material_UTS_mpa * 0.5

tool_name: Conveyor Belt Power, Drive Tension & Sag Ratio — Bulk Material Handling Design

inputs.name: Design Throughput Capacity (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Belt Speed (v)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Conveyor Centre-to-Centre Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Lift Height (H) — positive = uphill
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Artificial Friction Factor (f) — ISO 5048
inputs.note: ISO 5048: f=0.012 well-maintained, f=0.020 standard, f=0.030 dusty/misaligned
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Belt Mass per Unit Length (m_belt)
inputs.note: From belt manufacturer data: EP400/3-6+3 ≈ 12 kg/m per metre width
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Total Rotating Idler Mass per Unit Length (m_idler)
inputs.note: Carrying + return idler sets combined; depends on idler pitch and roll diameter
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Drive System Efficiency (η_drive)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: q_m_kg_m = (capacity_t_h * 1000) / (belt_speed_m_s * 3600)   // [kg/m] Material Linear Mass (q_m) Reference: ISO 5048:1989 §5.1
F_H_kN = friction_factor_f * conveyor_length_m * 9.81 * (q_m_kg_m + belt_mass_kg_m + idler_mass_kg_m) / 1000   // [kN] Main Resistance — Level Running (F_H) Reference: ISO 5048 §6.2 — main resistance
F_st_kN = q_m_kg_m * 9.81 * lift_height_m / 1000   // [kN] Gradient Resistance (F_st) Reference: ISO 5048 §6.3 — slope resistance (positive = uphill load)

F_u_kN = F_H_kN * 2 + F_st_kN; P_motor_kw = (F_u_kN * 1000 * belt_speed_m_s) / (drive_efficiency_pct / 100) / 1000   // [kN, kW] Total Peripheral Drive Force (F_u) & Required Motor Power Reference: ISO 5048 §6.1 — total effective tension; CEMA 7th Ed. §6

mu_wrap = 0.35; wrap_rad = Math.PI; T2_kN = F_u_kN / (Math.exp(mu_wrap * wrap_rad) - 1); T1_kN = T2_kN + F_u_kN   // [kN] Tight-Side and Slack-Side Tension (T1, T2) — Euler Drive Reference: Euler–Eytelwein equation: T1/T2 = e^(μα); μ=0.35 rubber-on-steel, α=π (180° wrap)

idler_pitch_m = 1.5; sag_ratio = (q_m_kg_m + belt_mass_kg_m) * 9.81 * idler_pitch_m^2 / (8 * T2_kN * 1000)   // [%] Maximum Belt Sag Ratio Reference: ISO 5048 / CEMA — sag limit ≤ 2% (0.02) at carry side; excessive sag = spillage and mistracking

standards: CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials 7th Ed.

V1.error_msg: Belt sag > 3%: increase take-up tension T2 or reduce idler pitch — material spillage and belt mistracking risk

V2.error_msg: High speed on high-capacity conveyor: verify belt rating (kN/m width) and idler bearing life per ISO 281

V3.error_msg: Long downhill conveyor: regenerative power possible — evaluate regenerative drives; braking tension controls design

smart_warnings.message: Sag ratio > 2%: CEMA recommends maximum 2% for lump material — reduce idler pitch from 1.5 m to 1.2 m or increase T2
High power with moderate drive efficiency: each 1% η improvement saves significant annual energy — evaluate direct-drive or gearless technology
Dominant regenerative gradient: conveyor may run away under load — dynamic braking or regenerative drive mandatory
T1/T2 ratio > 5: drive friction barely adequate — consider dual drive, higher wrap angle, or lagged pulley to prevent belt slip
Long conveyor (>5 km): intermediate drive or distributed drive topology recommended — starting tension wave analysis required per DIN 22101

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sag_ratio_pct > 0.03
fmea.effect: Belt sag > 3%: increase take-up tension T2 or reduce idler pitch — material spillage and belt mistracking risk
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: belt_speed_m_s > 6 && capacity_t_h > 5000
fmea.effect: High speed on high-capacity conveyor: verify belt rating (kN/m width) and idler bearing life per ISO 281
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lift_height_m < -100
fmea.effect: Long downhill conveyor: regenerative power possible — evaluate regenerative drives; braking tension controls design
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Sag ratio > 2%: CEMA recommends maximum 2% for lump material — reduce idler pitch from 1.5 m to 1.2 m or increase T2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sag_ratio_pct > 0.02

fmea.failureMode: High power with moderate drive efficiency: each 1% η improvement saves significant annual energy — evaluate direct-drive or gearless technology
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_motor_kw > 500 && drive_efficiency_pct < 92

fmea.failureMode: Dominant regenerative gradient: conveyor may run away under load — dynamic braking or regenerative drive mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_st_kN < -F_H_kN * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: T1/T2 ratio > 5: drive friction barely adequate — consider dual drive, higher wrap angle, or lagged pulley to prevent belt slip
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T1_kN / T2_kN > 5

fmea.failureMode: Long conveyor (>5 km): intermediate drive or distributed drive topology recommended — starting tension wave analysis required per DIN 22101
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: conveyor_length_m > 5000

tool_name: Power Transformer Loading, No-Load/Load Losses & Economic Loading Factor

inputs.name: Transformer Rated Power (S_rated)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: No-Load (Core / Iron) Loss (P0)
inputs.note: From factory test report — IEC 60076-1 §10
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Full-Load (Copper / Short-Circuit) Loss (Pk)
inputs.note: From factory test report at rated current
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Actual Operating Load (S_load)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Load Power Factor (cos φ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Electricity Cost for Loss Valuation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: beta = actual_load_kva / rated_kva   // [p.u.] Per-Unit Load Factor (β) Reference: IEC 60076-1:2011 §7 — loading factor definition
P_total_w = no_load_loss_w + beta^2 * load_loss_w   // [W] Total Losses at Operating Load Reference: IEC 60076-1 §10 — total transformer loss: P = P0 + β²·Pk

P_out_w = actual_load_kva * load_power_factor * 1000; eta_transformer_pct = P_out_w / (P_out_w + P_total_w) * 100   // [%] Transformer Efficiency at Operating Point Reference: IEC 60076-1 §10.3 — transformer efficiency

beta_eco = Math.sqrt(no_load_loss_w / load_loss_w)   // [p.u.] Economic Loading Factor (β_eco) — Minimum Loss Point Reference: IEC 60354:1991 §5 — economic loading for minimum total losses: β_eco = √(P0/Pk)
E_loss_kwh = (P_total_w / 1000) * operating_hours_yr   // [kWh/yr] Annual Energy Lost in Transformer Reference: IEC 60354 — annual loss calculation

loss_cost_usd_yr = E_loss_kwh_yr * electricity_rate_usd_kwh; beta_max_ambient = (ambient_temp_c <= 40) ? 1.0 : 1.0 - (ambient_temp_c - 40) * 0.01   // [USD/yr, p.u.] Annual Loss Cost & Temperature-Corrected Load Limit Reference: IEC 60354 §4 — continuous rating derating above 40°C ambient

V1.error_msg: Loading > 120%: overload condition — IEEE C57.12 §12 limits short-time overload; check thermal withstand duration

V2.error_msg: Load exceeds temperature-derated capacity: winding insulation ageing accelerated per IEC 60076-7 thermal model

V3.error_msg: Transformer heavily under-loaded (<20%): consider downsizing — no-load losses dominate total annual loss cost

smart_warnings.message: Loading > 90%: limited headroom for fault current — coordinate protection relay settings and evaluate parallel transformer option
Operating load deviates >30% from economic load point: significant excess losses — reconfigure load balance across transformers if multiple units in service
Efficiency < 97.5% on medium-power transformer: below IEC 60076-1 typical threshold — check for aged core or damaged windings
Ambient > 45°C: forced-air cooling (ONAN→ONAF) may be required per IEC 60076-7 §7; verify cooling class
Annual loss cost exceeds USD 50,000: evaluate amorphous core transformer (P0 reduction ~70%) — typically pays back in 3–5 years

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: beta > 1.2
fmea.effect: Loading > 120%: overload condition — IEEE C57.12 §12 limits short-time overload; check thermal withstand duration
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: beta > beta_max_ambient
fmea.effect: Load exceeds temperature-derated capacity: winding insulation ageing accelerated per IEC 60076-7 thermal model
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: beta < 0.2 && rated_kva > 500
fmea.effect: Transformer heavily under-loaded (<20%): consider downsizing — no-load losses dominate total annual loss cost
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Loading > 90%: limited headroom for fault current — coordinate protection relay settings and evaluate parallel transformer option
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: beta > 0.90

fmea.failureMode: Operating load deviates >30% from economic load point: significant excess losses — reconfigure load balance across transformers if multiple units in service
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Math.abs(beta - beta_eco) > 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Efficiency < 97.5% on medium-power transformer: below IEC 60076-1 typical threshold — check for aged core or damaged windings
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_pct < 97.5 && rated_kva > 1000

fmea.failureMode: Ambient > 45°C: forced-air cooling (ONAN→ONAF) may be required per IEC 60076-7 §7; verify cooling class
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c > 45

fmea.failureMode: Annual loss cost exceeds USD 50,000: evaluate amorphous core transformer (P0 reduction ~70%) — typically pays back in 3–5 years
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: loss_cost_usd_yr > 50000

tool_name: Chemical Reactor Heat of Reaction, Adiabatic Temperature Rise & Thermal Runaway Risk

inputs.name: Heat of Reaction (ΔH_r) — per mole of key reactant
inputs.note: Negative = exothermic (correct sign convention); from calorimetry (DSC/RC1) or Hess's law
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Key Reactant Concentration in Charge (c_r)
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Specific Heat Capacity of Reaction Mass (c_p)
inputs.note: Aqueous systems ≈ 3.5–4.2; organic ≈ 1.5–2.5 kJ/(kg·K)
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Onset Temperature of Decomposition (T_onset)
inputs.note: From DSC or ARC testing per ASTM E537 / IEC 62675

inputs.name: Activation Energy of Desired Reaction (E_a)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total Batch Mass
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Reactor Cooling System Capacity (Q_cool)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

formulas: delta_T_ad_k = Math.abs(delta_Hr_kj_mol) * reactant_concentration_mol_kg / cp_reaction_mass_kj_kg_k   // [K] Adiabatic Temperature Rise (ΔT_ad) Reference: Stoessel 'Thermal Safety of Chemical Processes' §3.2 — ΔT_ad = |ΔH_r|·c_r / c_p
MTSR_c = T_process_c + delta_T_ad_K   // [°C] Maximum Temperature of Synthesis Reaction (MTSR) Reference: Stoessel §3.3 — MTSR = T_p + ΔT_ad; critical threshold for thermal runaway initiation

R = 8.314e-3; Q_rx_kw = Math.abs(delta_Hr_kj_mol) * reactant_concentration_mol_kg * batch_mass_kg * 1e6 * Math.exp(-Ea_kj_mol / (R * (T_process_c + 273.15))) / 3600   // [kW] Peak Reaction Rate Power (Q_rx at T_process)

cooling_ratio = cooling_capacity_kw / Math.max(Q_rx_kw, 0.001)   // [dimensionless] Cooling Adequacy — Heat Removal vs Heat Generation Reference: IEC 62675 §5 — cooling ratio > 2.0 required for safe operation

delta_T_safety = T_onset_decomp_c - MTSR_c; criticality = (MTSR_c < T_process_c + 25) ? 1 : (MTSR_c < T_onset_decomp_c) ? (delta_T_safety > 50 ? 2 : 3) : (delta_T_safety > 0 ? 4 : 5)   // [class 1–5] Stoessel Criticality Index Reference: Stoessel 5-class criticality diagram §4.4: Class 1 = safe, Class 5 = explosive decomposition risk

T_safety_margin_k = T_onset_decomp_c - MTSR_c   // [K] Temperature Safety Margin — MTSR to Decomposition Reference: Minimum recommended margin: ΔT_safety > 50 K per Stoessel; > 100 K preferred per BASF thermal safety guidelines

V1.error_msg: CRITICAL: MTSR ≥ T_onset — thermal runaway triggers decomposition: Stoessel Class 5. Do not operate without full process safety review

V2.error_msg: Cooling capacity insufficient to remove reaction heat at process temperature: runaway risk — increase cooling or reduce batch size

V3.error_msg: Adiabatic temperature rise > 200 K: high-energy reaction — consider semi-batch dosing to limit accumulation

smart_warnings.message: Stoessel Class 4/5: MTSR approaches or exceeds T_onset — emergency relief sizing per AIChE DIERS mandatory before scale-up
Temperature safety margin < 50 K: insufficient buffer — consider lower process temperature, controlled dosing, or additional cooling per Stoessel §4.4
Cooling ratio 1–2: marginal safety — any cooling failure leads to thermal runaway; install high-temperature interlock per IEC 62675
High ΔT_ad at high concentration: implement semi-batch (fed-batch) operation to limit reactant accumulation per Stoessel §7
Large-scale Class 3+ reaction: HAZOP and Layer of Protection Analysis (LOPA) required per IEC 61511 before production

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: MTSR_c >= T_onset_decomp_c
fmea.effect: CRITICAL: MTSR ≥ T_onset — thermal runaway triggers decomposition: Stoessel Class 5. Do not operate without full process safety review
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cooling_ratio < 1.0
fmea.effect: Cooling capacity insufficient to remove reaction heat at process temperature: runaway risk — increase cooling or reduce batch size
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: delta_T_ad_K > 200
fmea.effect: Adiabatic temperature rise > 200 K: high-energy reaction — consider semi-batch dosing to limit accumulation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Stoessel Class 4/5: MTSR approaches or exceeds T_onset — emergency relief sizing per AIChE DIERS mandatory before scale-up
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: criticality_class >= 4

fmea.failureMode: Temperature safety margin < 50 K: insufficient buffer — consider lower process temperature, controlled dosing, or additional cooling per Stoessel §4.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_safety_margin_K < 50

fmea.failureMode: Cooling ratio 1–2: marginal safety — any cooling failure leads to thermal runaway; install high-temperature interlock per IEC 62675
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cooling_ratio < 2.0 && cooling_ratio >= 1.0

fmea.failureMode: High ΔT_ad at high concentration: implement semi-batch (fed-batch) operation to limit reactant accumulation per Stoessel §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_T_ad_K > 100 && reactant_concentration_mol_kg > 5

fmea.failureMode: Large-scale Class 3+ reaction: HAZOP and Layer of Protection Analysis (LOPA) required per IEC 61511 before production
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: batch_mass_kg > 10000 && criticality_class >= 3

tool_name: Cylindrical Gear Pair — Bending & Pitting (Contact) Stress Rating per ISO 6336

inputs.name: Transmitted Power (P)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Pinion Speed (n_1)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Gear Ratio (u = z_2 / z_1)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Normal Module (m_n)
inputs.note: ISO 54 preferred module series
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Pinion Teeth (z_1)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Gear Facewidth (b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Allowable Contact Stress Number (σ_Hlim) — Pinion Material
inputs.note: ISO 6336-5: case-hardened 20MnCr5 → σ_Hlim=1500 MPa; through-hardened 42CrMo4 → σ_Hlim=700 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Allowable Bending Stress Number (σ_Flim) — Pinion Material
inputs.note: ISO 6336-5: case-hardened → σ_Flim=430 MPa; through-hardened → σ_Flim=275 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Application Factor (K_A)
inputs.note: ISO 6336-1 Table A.1
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: d1_mm = module_mm * z1_teeth; T1_Nm = P_kw * 9549.3 / n1_rpm; Ft_N = 2000 * T1_Nm / d1_mm   // [mm, N] Pitch Circle Diameter & Tangential Force Reference: ISO 6336-1:2019 §5.3

v_m_s = Math.PI * d1_mm * n1_rpm / 60000; K_v = 1 + (0.0125 * v_m_s * Math.pow(z1_teeth * gear_ratio_u, 0.5)) / (1 + 0.0125 * v_m_s)   // [dimensionless] Dynamic Factor (K_v) — ISO Method B Reference: ISO 6336-1 §6.3.3 — simplified dynamic factor for quality grade 6

Z_E = 189.8; Z_H = 2.5; u = gear_ratio_u; sigma_H_mpa = Z_E * Z_H * Math.sqrt(Ft_N * KA_app_factor * K_v * (u+1) / (facewidth_mm * d1_mm * u))   // [MPa] Contact Stress (σ_H) — Hertz Pressure Reference: ISO 6336-2:2019 §4 — contact stress; Z_E=189.8 MPa^0.5 for steel/steel; Z_H=2.5 standard helix

Y_F = 2.7; Y_S = 1.6; sigma_F_mpa = Ft_N * KA_app_factor * K_v * Y_F * Y_S / (facewidth_mm * module_mm)   // [MPa] Bending Stress at Root (σ_F) Reference: ISO 6336-3:2019 §4 — root bending stress; Y_F=2.7 form factor z=20; Y_S=1.6 stress correction
S_H = sigma_Hlim_mpa / sigma_H_mpa   // [dimensionless] Contact Safety Factor (S_H) Reference: ISO 6336-2 §5 — S_H ≥ 1.0 minimum; ≥ 1.1–1.2 recommended
S_F = sigma_Flim_mpa / sigma_F_mpa   // [dimensionless] Bending Safety Factor (S_F) Reference: ISO 6336-3 §5 — S_F ≥ 1.4 minimum for industrial gearing

V1.error_msg: Contact safety factor S_H < 1.0: pitting failure at operating load — increase facewidth, module, or use higher σ_Hlim material

V2.error_msg: Bending safety factor S_F < 1.4: ISO 6336 minimum for industrial drives — increase module or facewidth

V3.error_msg: Facewidth exceeds 12×module×z_1: excessive width relative to pinion diameter — shaft deflection and load distribution factor K_Hβ will be critical

smart_warnings.message: S_H < 1.1: marginal contact safety — case-harden to σ_Hlim ≥ 1400 MPa or apply profile modification (tip relief) per ISO 6336-1 §7
Pitch-line velocity > 25 m/s: high dynamic loads — verify gear quality grade ≤ 5 per ISO 1328-1 and check lubrication viscosity
High ratio with few pinion teeth: undercut risk — verify minimum tooth count for standard addendum without profile shift
Dynamic factor K_v > 1.5: significant dynamic load amplification — reduce speed, improve gear quality, or add mass damping
Low bending safety under heavy shock loading: risk of tooth fracture at peak transients — consider shot-peened root fillet per ISO 6336-5 §5.4

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: S_H < 1.0
fmea.effect: Contact safety factor S_H < 1.0: pitting failure at operating load — increase facewidth, module, or use higher σ_Hlim material
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: S_F < 1.4
fmea.effect: Bending safety factor S_F < 1.4: ISO 6336 minimum for industrial drives — increase module or facewidth
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: facewidth_mm > 12 * module_mm * z1_teeth
fmea.effect: Facewidth exceeds 12×module×z_1: excessive width relative to pinion diameter — shaft deflection and load distribution factor K_Hβ will be critical
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: S_H < 1.1: marginal contact safety — case-harden to σ_Hlim ≥ 1400 MPa or apply profile modification (tip relief) per ISO 6336-1 §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: S_H < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: Pitch-line velocity > 25 m/s: high dynamic loads — verify gear quality grade ≤ 5 per ISO 1328-1 and check lubrication viscosity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_m_s > 25

fmea.failureMode: High ratio with few pinion teeth: undercut risk — verify minimum tooth count for standard addendum without profile shift
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: gear_ratio_u > 6 && z1_teeth < 17

fmea.failureMode: Dynamic factor K_v > 1.5: significant dynamic load amplification — reduce speed, improve gear quality, or add mass damping
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: K_v > 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Low bending safety under heavy shock loading: risk of tooth fracture at peak transients — consider shot-peened root fillet per ISO 6336-5 §5.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: S_F < 2.0 && KA_app_factor >= 1.5

tool_name: Chilled Water Plant Energy Performance — System COP, IPLV & Carbon Footprint

inputs.name: Chiller Rated Cooling Capacity (Q_chiller)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Chiller COP at Full Load (rated condition)
inputs.note: AHRI 551/591: water-cooled centrifugal at 7/12°C CHWS/R, 30/35°C CWS/R
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Chiller COP at 50% Part Load (IPLV test point)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total CHW & CW Pump Power (installed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Cooling Tower Fan Power (installed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Annual Cooling Energy Delivered (measured or estimated)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Grid Carbon Emission Factor (EF)
inputs.note: Country-specific: US avg ≈ 0.39; EU avg ≈ 0.28; CN ≈ 0.58 kg CO₂e/kWh
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

formulas: W_chiller_kw = chiller_capacity_kw / chiller_COP_full; W_plant_kw = W_chiller_kw + pump_power_kw + cooling_tower_power_kw   // [kW] Total Plant Electrical Input Power Reference: ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.1 — plant-level power accounting
COP_system = chiller_capacity_kw / W_plant_kw   // [dimensionless] System COP (plant-level, full load) Reference: AHRI 551/591-2023 — system boundary includes chiller + auxiliary equipment

IPLV = 1 / (0.01/chiller_COP_full + 0.42/chiller_COP_part + 0.45/(chiller_COP_part*1.1) + 0.12/(chiller_COP_part*0.85))   // [dimensionless] IPLV (Integrated Part-Load Value) — AHRI Weighted Reference: AHRI 551/591-2023 §6.3 — IPLV weighting: A=1%, B=42%, C=45%, D=12%
E_plant_kwh_yr = annual_cooling_kwh / COP_system   // [kWh/yr] Annual Plant Electrical Consumption Reference: ISO 50001:2018 energy baseline
CO2_tonne_yr = E_plant_kwh_yr * grid_emission_factor / 1000   // [tCO₂e/yr] Annual Carbon Footprint — Plant Operation Reference: GHG Protocol Scope 2 (location-based) — IPCC AR6 emission factors
m_dot_chw_kg_s = chiller_capacity_kw / (4.18 * chilled_water_dT_k)   // [kg/s] Chilled Water Flow Rate Reference: Q = ṁ·c_p·ΔT; c_p=4.18 kJ/(kg·K) for water — ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch.22

V1.error_msg: Part-load COP lower than full-load COP: atypical — most variable-speed chillers improve efficiency at part load; verify equipment data

V2.error_msg: System COP < 2.5: poor plant efficiency — auxiliary loads (pumps, CT) may be oversized or operating at poor duty point

V3.error_msg: CHW ΔT < 4 K: low delta-T syndrome — excessive pump energy; investigate coil fouling, valve hunting, or oversized coils

smart_warnings.message: IPLV < 4.0: below ASHRAE 90.1-2022 minimum efficiency requirement for water-cooled chillers > 528 kW — non-compliant for new installations
Pump power >10% of cooling capacity: excessive hydraulic loop losses — evaluate VFD on pumps, pipe sizing, and valve pressure drops
System COP significantly below IPLV: large auxiliary penalty — optimise cooling tower and pump controls for off-peak savings
Annual CO₂ > 1,000 tonnes: material Scope 2 emission — consider PPA for renewable electricity or free-cooling economiser
High CHW ΔT (>10 K): verify coil capacity and pump sizing — beneficial for energy but may indicate undersized distribution

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: chiller_COP_part < chiller_COP_full
fmea.effect: Part-load COP lower than full-load COP: atypical — most variable-speed chillers improve efficiency at part load; verify equipment data
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: COP_system < 2.5
fmea.effect: System COP < 2.5: poor plant efficiency — auxiliary loads (pumps, CT) may be oversized or operating at poor duty point
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: chilled_water_dT_k < 4
fmea.effect: CHW ΔT < 4 K: low delta-T syndrome — excessive pump energy; investigate coil fouling, valve hunting, or oversized coils
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: IPLV < 4.0: below ASHRAE 90.1-2022 minimum efficiency requirement for water-cooled chillers > 528 kW — non-compliant for new installations
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: IPLV < 4.0

fmea.failureMode: Pump power >10% of cooling capacity: excessive hydraulic loop losses — evaluate VFD on pumps, pipe sizing, and valve pressure drops
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pump_power_kw / chiller_capacity_kw > 0.10

fmea.failureMode: System COP significantly below IPLV: large auxiliary penalty — optimise cooling tower and pump controls for off-peak savings
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP_system < IPLV * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Annual CO₂ > 1,000 tonnes: material Scope 2 emission — consider PPA for renewable electricity or free-cooling economiser
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CO2_tonne_yr > 1000

fmea.failureMode: High CHW ΔT (>10 K): verify coil capacity and pump sizing — beneficial for energy but may indicate undersized distribution
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: chilled_water_dT_k > 10

tool_name: Anchor Bolt Group — Tension, Shear & Combined Interaction for Column Baseplates

inputs.name: Number of Anchor Bolts in Group (n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Anchor Bolt Nominal Diameter (d_a)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Effective Embedment Depth (h_ef)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Concrete Compressive Strength (f'_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Anchor Steel Ultimate Tensile Strength (f_uta)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Factored Tension Demand on Bolt Group (N_u / N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Factored Shear Demand on Bolt Group (V_u / V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Minimum Edge Distance to Any Bolt (c_a1)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: As_mm2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(bolt_dia_mm * 0.9, 2)   // [mm²] Net Tensile Stress Area per Bolt
phi_Nsa_kn = 0.75 * N_bolts * As_mm2 * Futa_mpa / 1000   // [kN] Steel Tensile Capacity — Bolt Group (φN_sa) Reference: ACI 318-19 §17.6.1 — φ=0.75 for ductile anchors in tension

Nb_kn = 16 * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * Math.pow(embed_depth_mm, 1.5) / 1000; AN = Math.pow(3 * embed_depth_mm, 2); AN0 = 9 * embed_depth_mm^2; phi_Ncb_kn = 0.70 * (AN/AN0) * Nb_kn   // [kN] Concrete Breakout Capacity — Tension (φN_cb) Reference: ACI 318-19 §17.6.2 — concrete breakout for single bolt, φ=0.70 (uncracked concrete assumed)
phi_Vsa_kn = 0.65 * N_bolts * 0.6 * As_mm2 * Futa_mpa / 1000   // [kN] Steel Shear Capacity — Bolt Group (φV_sa) Reference: ACI 318-19 §17.7.1.2 — shear: φ=0.65; V_sa = 0.6·A_se·f_uta
kcp = (embed_depth_mm >= 65) ? 2.0 : 1.0; phi_Vcp_kn = 0.70 * kcp * phi_Ncb_kn / 0.70   // [kN] Concrete Pryout Capacity (φV_cp) Reference: ACI 318-19 §17.7.3 — pryout: k_cp=2 for h_ef≥65 mm

DCR_tension = Nu_kn / Math.min(phi_Nsa_kn, phi_Ncb_kn); DCR_shear = Vu_kn / Math.min(phi_Vsa_kn, phi_Vcp_kn); interaction = Math.pow(DCR_tension, 5/3) + Math.pow(DCR_shear, 5/3)   // [dimensionless] Combined Tension-Shear Interaction Check Reference: ACI 318-19 §17.8.3 — trilinear interaction: sum ≤ 1.2 required

V1.error_msg: Combined tension-shear interaction > 1.2: ACI 318 §17.8.3 exceeded — increase bolt diameter, embedment, or bolt count

V2.error_msg: Edge distance < 6×d_a: concrete edge breakout controls — check ACI §17.7.2; may require edge reinforcement

V3.error_msg: Embedment < 8×d_a: shallow anchor — concrete breakout likely governs; increase h_ef to minimum 8×d_a per AISC DG1

smart_warnings.message: Tension DCR > 0.8: low reserve — verify load combinations include seismic overstrength per ASCE 7-22 §12.4.3 if applicable
Concrete breakout capacity <70% of steel capacity: brittle failure mode governs — increase embedment or add supplemental reinforcement per ACI §17.3.3
Low-strength concrete with high tension: consider post-installed adhesive anchors (ETAG 001) for higher concrete capacity
Less than 4 bolts under combined loading: all bolts in tension and shear simultaneously — verify baseplate rotational stiffness per AISC DG1
High-strength anchor with moderate embedment: steel fracture may govern before concrete breakout — desirable for ductile design per ACI §17.3.3(a)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: interaction > 1.2
fmea.effect: Combined tension-shear interaction > 1.2: ACI 318 §17.8.3 exceeded — increase bolt diameter, embedment, or bolt count
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: edge_dist_min_mm < 6 * bolt_dia_mm
fmea.effect: Edge distance < 6×d_a: concrete edge breakout controls — check ACI §17.7.2; may require edge reinforcement
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: embed_depth_mm < 8 * bolt_dia_mm
fmea.effect: Embedment < 8×d_a: shallow anchor — concrete breakout likely governs; increase h_ef to minimum 8×d_a per AISC DG1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Tension DCR > 0.8: low reserve — verify load combinations include seismic overstrength per ASCE 7-22 §12.4.3 if applicable
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_tension > 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Concrete breakout capacity <70% of steel capacity: brittle failure mode governs — increase embedment or add supplemental reinforcement per ACI §17.3.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: phi_Ncb_kn < phi_Nsa_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength concrete with high tension: consider post-installed adhesive anchors (ETAG 001) for higher concrete capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fc_prime_mpa < 25 && Nu_kn > 100

fmea.failureMode: Less than 4 bolts under combined loading: all bolts in tension and shear simultaneously — verify baseplate rotational stiffness per AISC DG1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_bolts < 4 && Vu_kn > 0 && Nu_kn > 0

fmea.failureMode: High-strength anchor with moderate embedment: steel fracture may govern before concrete breakout — desirable for ductile design per ACI §17.3.3(a)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Futa_mpa > 800 && embed_depth_mm < 200

tool_name: Wet Scrubber / Packed Column Sizing — Mass Transfer NTU, HTU & Pressure Drop

inputs.name: Actual Gas Flow Rate (Q_gas)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5167-1:2022 §6 — Orifice plate: ±2% (Re>10⁴)

inputs.name: Inlet Contaminant Concentration (y_in)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §7 — Analytical chemistry: reproducibility ±5%

inputs.name: Required Outlet Concentration (y_out)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §7 — Analytical chemistry: reproducibility ±5%

inputs.name: Henry's Law Constant (H) — dimensionless (c_gas / c_liquid)
inputs.note: H = p / (H_cc × RT); SO₂/water at 25°C ≈ 0.008; NH₃/water ≈ 0.0006; HCl/water ≈ 0.0001
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Liquid-to-Gas Ratio (L/G) — volumetric
inputs.note: Typical: acid gas scrubbing 1–3 L/m³; particulate 0.5–1.5 L/m³
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Overall Gas-Phase Mass Transfer Coefficient × Packing Area (K_ya)
inputs.note: Depends on packing type and gas velocity; 25 mm Raschig rings ≈ 0.05–0.15; structured packing ≈ 0.15–0.5
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Superficial Gas Velocity in Column (u_s)
inputs.note: Typically 60–80% of flood velocity; GPSA recommends ≤75%
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Packing Pressure Drop per Unit Height (ΔP/Z)
inputs.note: GPSA Fig. 9-2: random packing at operating velocity; structured packing 50–150 Pa/m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: eta_pct = (inlet_conc_ppm - outlet_conc_ppm) / inlet_conc_ppm * 100; R = inlet_conc_ppm / outlet_conc_ppm   // [%, dimensionless] Collection Efficiency & Removal Ratio Reference: VDI 2440 §4 — removal ratio R = y_in / y_out
A_factor = L_G_ratio / Henrys_H   // [dimensionless] Absorption Factor (A) Reference: AIChE mass transfer — absorption factor; A > 1.2 recommended for effective absorption

NTU = (A_factor / (A_factor - 1)) * Math.log(R * (1 - 1/A_factor) + 1/A_factor)   // [dimensionless] Number of Transfer Units (NTU) Reference: Kremser equation for dilute systems — Perry's Chemical Engineers' Handbook 9th Ed. §14
gas_vel_m_s = column_velocity_m_s; HTU_m = gas_vel_m_s / Kya_1_m; Z_m = NTU * HTU_m   // [m] Height of Transfer Unit (HTU) & Required Packing Height (Z) Reference: Z = NTU × HTU — GPSA 14th Ed. §9

Q_gas_m3_s = gas_flow_am3_h / 3600; A_col_m2 = Q_gas_m3_s / column_velocity_m_s; D_col_m = 2 * Math.sqrt(A_col_m2 / Math.PI)   // [m², m] Column Cross-Section & Diameter Reference: Continuity equation — column sizing based on design gas velocity
dP_total_pa = packing_dP_m_per_m * Z_m   // [Pa] Total Column Pressure Drop Reference: GPSA §9 — total packed bed ΔP; allowable typically < 2500 Pa for atmospheric scrubbers

V1.error_msg: Outlet concentration must be less than inlet — check concentration inputs

V2.error_msg: Absorption factor < 1.0: L/G ratio insufficient for equilibrium — infinite packing height required; increase L/G or reduce H by cooling liquid

V3.error_msg: Column pressure drop > 2500 Pa: high fan/blower energy penalty — consider lower-ΔP structured packing or reduce packing height

smart_warnings.message: Absorption factor < 1.2: marginal driving force — increase L/G by 50% to improve reliability and handle inlet concentration spikes
NTU > 10: very high packing height required — verify Kya with pilot test; consider two-stage scrubbing
Large column (D>3 m, Z>6 m): consider multiple smaller columns in parallel — liquid distribution and wall effects critical above 3 m diameter
Removal efficiency > 99.9%: verify NTU calculation at very high R values — small errors in inlet/outlet measurement dominate uncertainty
High Henry's constant (H > 1): sparingly soluble contaminant — scrubbing may be ineffective; consider activated carbon or catalytic destruction instead

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: outlet_conc_ppm >= inlet_conc_ppm
fmea.effect: Outlet concentration must be less than inlet — check concentration inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: A_factor < 1.0
fmea.effect: Absorption factor < 1.0: L/G ratio insufficient for equilibrium — infinite packing height required; increase L/G or reduce H by cooling liquid
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_total_pa > 2500
fmea.effect: Column pressure drop > 2500 Pa: high fan/blower energy penalty — consider lower-ΔP structured packing or reduce packing height
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Absorption factor < 1.2: marginal driving force — increase L/G by 50% to improve reliability and handle inlet concentration spikes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_factor < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: NTU > 10: very high packing height required — verify Kya with pilot test; consider two-stage scrubbing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NTU > 10

fmea.failureMode: Large column (D>3 m, Z>6 m): consider multiple smaller columns in parallel — liquid distribution and wall effects critical above 3 m diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: D_col_m > 3 && Z_m > 6

fmea.failureMode: Removal efficiency > 99.9%: verify NTU calculation at very high R values — small errors in inlet/outlet measurement dominate uncertainty
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_pct > 99.9

fmea.failureMode: High Henry's constant (H > 1): sparingly soluble contaminant — scrubbing may be ineffective; consider activated carbon or catalytic destruction instead
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Henrys_H > 1.0

tool_name: Lightning Protection — Rolling Sphere Radius, LPZ Classification & SPD Coordination

inputs.name: Lightning Protection Level (LPL)

options.label: LPL I — 99% interception, R=20 m (highest risk)

inputs.note: IEC 62305-1 §5.3 — LPL determined by risk assessment per IEC 62305-2
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Structure Height (H)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Structure Footprint Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Structure Footprint Width (W)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Ground Flash Density (N_g)
inputs.note: From local thunderstorm isokeraunic map or CIGRE TB 549; Europe avg ≈ 0.5–4; tropics ≈ 5–30
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Structure Use / Occupancy Risk Factor (C_d)
inputs.note: IEC 62305-2 Table A.2 — structure type factor
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Down Conductor Spacing (s_dc)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: Rs_m = {1: 20, 2: 30, 3: 45, 4: 60}[LPL_class]   // [m] Rolling Sphere Radius (R_s) per LPL Reference: IEC 62305-3:2010 Table 2 — rolling sphere radius by LPL

A_e_km2 = ((structure_length_m + 2*structure_height_m) * (structure_width_m + 2*structure_height_m) + Math.PI * structure_height_m^2) / 1e6   // [km²] Equivalent Collection Area of Structure (A_e) Reference: IEC 62305-2 §A.2 Eq.(A.1) — collection area of isolated structure
N_d_yr = ground_flash_density * A_e_km2 * structure_use   // [strikes/yr] Annual Lightning Strike Frequency (N_d) Reference: IEC 62305-2 §A.2 — N_d = N_g × A_e × C_d

perimeter_m = 2 * (structure_length_m + structure_width_m); n_dc_min = Math.ceil(perimeter_m / down_conductor_spacing_m)   // [count] Minimum Number of Down Conductors Required Reference: IEC 62305-3 §5.3.3 — minimum spacing per LPL: LPL I/II → 10 m; LPL III → 15 m; LPL IV → 20 m
s_dc_req_m = {1: 10, 2: 10, 3: 15, 4: 20}[LPL_class]   // [m] Recommended Down Conductor Spacing by LPL Reference: IEC 62305-3 Table 4 — down conductor mesh spacing by LPL

spd_class = (LPL_class <= 2) ? 'Type 1 (Class I) — 10/350 μs, I_imp ≥ 25 kA/pole' : 'Type 2 (Class II) — 8/20 μs, I_n ≥ 20 kA/pole'   // [text] SPD Impulse Current Class Required Reference: IEC 62305-4 §6.3.3 / IEC 61643-11 — SPD class by lightning protection zone

V1.error_msg: Down conductor spacing exceeds IEC 62305-3 requirement for selected LPL — increase number of conductors

V2.error_msg: Structure height exceeds rolling sphere radius: LPL II or I may be required — re-run risk assessment per IEC 62305-2

V3.error_msg: Expected > 1 direct strike per year: comprehensive LPS mandatory — evaluate LPMS (Lightning Protection Management System)

smart_warnings.message: LPL I on hazardous structure: full mesh air termination network required per IEC 62305-3 §5.2.2; equipotential bonding of all metallic services mandatory
High strike frequency with LPL III/IV: reassess LPL — IEC 62305-2 risk assessment may require upgrade to LPL II
Structure > 60 m: lateral flash risk on upper 20% of structure — side flash air termination required per IEC 62305-3 §5.2.3
High Ng (>10 fl/km²/yr): tropical/subtropical zone — verify local isokeraunic data with national meteorological service; keraunic level may vary ±50%
LPL I/II: Type 1 SPD at service entrance required — coordinate with Type 2 at distribution board and Type 3 at sensitive equipment per IEC 62305-4 §6.3

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: down_conductor_spacing_m > s_dc_req_m
fmea.effect: Down conductor spacing exceeds IEC 62305-3 requirement for selected LPL — increase number of conductors
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: structure_height_m > Rs_m && LPL_class > 2
fmea.effect: Structure height exceeds rolling sphere radius: LPL II or I may be required — re-run risk assessment per IEC 62305-2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: N_d_yr > 1.0
fmea.effect: Expected > 1 direct strike per year: comprehensive LPS mandatory — evaluate LPMS (Lightning Protection Management System)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: LPL I on hazardous structure: full mesh air termination network required per IEC 62305-3 §5.2.2; equipotential bonding of all metallic services mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: LPL_class === 1 && structure_use >= 3.0

fmea.failureMode: High strike frequency with LPL III/IV: reassess LPL — IEC 62305-2 risk assessment may require upgrade to LPL II
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_d_yr > 0.1 && LPL_class >= 3

fmea.failureMode: Structure > 60 m: lateral flash risk on upper 20% of structure — side flash air termination required per IEC 62305-3 §5.2.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: structure_height_m > 60

fmea.failureMode: High Ng (>10 fl/km²/yr): tropical/subtropical zone — verify local isokeraunic data with national meteorological service; keraunic level may vary ±50%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ground_flash_density > 10

fmea.failureMode: LPL I/II: Type 1 SPD at service entrance required — coordinate with Type 2 at distribution board and Type 3 at sensitive equipment per IEC 62305-4 §6.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: LPL_class <= 2

tool_name: Rolling Element Bearing Rated Life L10h, ISO 281 Adjusted Life & Lubrication Factor

inputs.name: Bearing Basic Dynamic Load Rating (C)
inputs.note: From manufacturer catalogue — ISO 281:2007 definition
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Equivalent Dynamic Bearing Load (P)
inputs.note: P = X·Fr + Y·Fa per ISO 76; for pure radial: P = Fr
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Rotational Speed (n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Bearing Type — Life Exponent (p)
inputs.note: ISO 281:2007 §5 — fatigue life exponent
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Lubrication Viscosity Ratio (κ = ν_actual / ν_required)
inputs.note: κ = actual kinematic viscosity at operating temp / reference viscosity ν_1 per ISO 281 Fig.1
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Contamination Factor (e_C)
inputs.note: ISO 281:2007 Annex A — contamination factor per cleanliness class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bearing Basic Static Load Rating (C_0)
inputs.note: From manufacturer catalogue — ISO 76:2006
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Required Reliability Level
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: L10_Mr = Math.pow(C_kn / P_kn, bearing_type)   // [million rev] Basic Rating Life (L10) in Millions of Revolutions Reference: ISO 281:2007 §5.1 Eq.(1) — L10 = (C/P)^p
L10h = L10_Mr * 1e6 / (60 * n_rpm)   // [hours] Basic Rating Life in Operating Hours (L10h) Reference: ISO 281:2007 §5.1 — hours from million revolutions

a1_map = {90: 1.00, 95: 0.64, 96: 0.55, 97: 0.47, 98: 0.37, 99: 0.25}; a1 = a1_map[target_reliability_pct]   // [dimensionless] Reliability Factor (a_1) Reference: ISO 281:2007 Table 1 — reliability factors a_1

kappa_eff = Math.min(Math.max(viscosity_ratio_kappa, 0.1), 4.0); a_ISO = contamination_factor_eC * Math.pow(kappa_eff, 0.54) * Math.pow(C0_kn / P_kn, 0.3)   // [dimensionless] ISO Life Modification Factor (a_ISO) — Lubrication & Contamination Reference: ISO 281:2007 Annex A §A.4 — simplified a_ISO from κ and e_C (full calculation requires fatigue load limit C_u)
L_nm_h = a1 * a_ISO * L10h   // [hours] Modified Rating Life (L_nm) — ISO 281 System Approach Reference: ISO 281:2007 §5.2 — L_nm = a_1 · a_ISO · L_10
S0 = C0_kn / P_kn   // [dimensionless] Static Safety Factor (S_0) Reference: ISO 76:2006 §4 — S_0 ≥ 1.5 for normal; ≥ 2.0 for shock loads; ≥ 4.0 for vibration

V1.error_msg: Equivalent load P exceeds dynamic rating C: L10 < 1 million revolutions — bearing critically undersized; select larger bore or higher capacity series

V2.error_msg: Static safety factor S_0 < 1.0: permanent deformation of raceway at peak load — increase C_0 (larger bearing) or reduce peak load

V3.error_msg: κ < 0.4: severe mixed lubrication regime — catastrophic wear within weeks; increase oil viscosity or reduce operating temperature

smart_warnings.message: Modified life L_nm < 20,000 h: below typical industrial minimum — improve lubrication (κ), reduce contamination (e_C), or upsize bearing
κ < 1.0: insufficient lubricant film — metal-to-metal contact risk; switch to higher viscosity grade or add EP additive
High L10h but severe contamination: actual life will be far shorter than calculated — implement effective sealing and re-lubrication programme
Ball bearing S_0 < 1.5: insufficient static reserve for machinery with vibration or shock loading per ISO 76 §4.2
High speed × load factor: verify bearing speed limit (n·dm) from catalogue — thermal and cage stability may limit life independently of fatigue

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: P_kn > C_kn
fmea.effect: Equivalent load P exceeds dynamic rating C: L10 < 1 million revolutions — bearing critically undersized; select larger bore or higher capacity series
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: S0 < 1.0
fmea.effect: Static safety factor S_0 < 1.0: permanent deformation of raceway at peak load — increase C_0 (larger bearing) or reduce peak load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: viscosity_ratio_kappa < 0.4
fmea.effect: κ < 0.4: severe mixed lubrication regime — catastrophic wear within weeks; increase oil viscosity or reduce operating temperature
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Modified life L_nm < 20,000 h: below typical industrial minimum — improve lubrication (κ), reduce contamination (e_C), or upsize bearing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_nm_h < 20000

fmea.failureMode: κ < 1.0: insufficient lubricant film — metal-to-metal contact risk; switch to higher viscosity grade or add EP additive
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: viscosity_ratio_kappa < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: High L10h but severe contamination: actual life will be far shorter than calculated — implement effective sealing and re-lubrication programme
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: contamination_factor_eC < 0.4 && L10h > 50000

fmea.failureMode: Ball bearing S_0 < 1.5: insufficient static reserve for machinery with vibration or shock loading per ISO 76 §4.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: S0 < 1.5 && bearing_type === 3

fmea.failureMode: High speed × load factor: verify bearing speed limit (n·dm) from catalogue — thermal and cage stability may limit life independently of fatigue
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm * bearing_type > 500000

tool_name: Substation Earthing Grid — Ground Resistance, Touch & Step Voltage Safety Check

inputs.name: Soil Resistivity (ρ)
inputs.note: Field measurement per IEEE Std 81 (Wenner method); sandy/dry ≈ 1000 Ω·m; clay wet ≈ 20 Ω·m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Grounding Grid Area (A)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Total Buried Conductor Length (L_C)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Grid Burial Depth (h)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Maximum Ground Fault Current (I_f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Fault Clearing Time (t_f)
inputs.note: Protection relay clearing time; IEEE 80 uses t_f for shock duration
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Surface Crushed Rock / Gravel Layer Resistivity (ρ_s)
inputs.note: Dry crushed granite ≈ 3000–10000 Ω·m; wet gravel ≈ 500–2000 Ω·m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Surface Layer Thickness (h_s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

formulas: Rg_ohm = soil_resistivity_ohm_m * (1/(4*Math.sqrt(Math.PI*grid_area_m2)) + 1/grid_conductor_length_m * (1 + 1/(1 + burial_depth_m*Math.sqrt(20/grid_area_m2))))   // [Ω] Grid Ground Resistance (R_g) — Sverak Formula Reference: IEEE Std 80-2013 Eq.(57) — Sverak simplified formula for grid resistance
GPR_v = fault_current_kA * 1000 * Rg_ohm   // [V] Ground Potential Rise (GPR) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §16.1 — GPR = I_f × R_g
IB_mA = 1000 * 0.116 / Math.sqrt(fault_duration_s)   // [mA] Body Current Limit — Fibrillation Threshold (I_B) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §7.4 Eq.(10) — Dalziel formula: I_B = 0.116/√t_s (50 kg body)

Cs = 1 - 0.09*(1 - soil_resistivity_ohm_m/surface_layer_resistivity) / (2*surface_layer_depth_m + 0.09); Rfoot_ohm = 1.5 * Cs * surface_layer_resistivity; E_touch_v = IB_mA/1000 * (1000 + 2*Rfoot_ohm)   // [V] Tolerable Touch Voltage (E_touch) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §8.3 Eq.(32) — E_touch50 with surface layer reduction factor C_s
E_step_v = IB_mA/1000 * (1000 + 6*Rfoot_ohm)   // [V] Tolerable Step Voltage (E_step) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §8.3 Eq.(33) — E_step50 with surface layer

D_m = Math.sqrt(grid_area_m2 / Math.round(grid_conductor_length_m / Math.sqrt(grid_area_m2))); Km = 1/(2*Math.PI) * (Math.log(D_m^2/(16*0.005*burial_depth_m)) + Math.log(D_m/(3.84*burial_depth_m))); Ki = 0.644 + 0.148*4; E_mesh_v = soil_resistivity_ohm_m * fault_current_kA * 1000 * Km * Ki / grid_conductor_length_m   // [V] Mesh Voltage Estimate (E_m) — Simplified Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §16.4 Eq.(80) — mesh voltage; conductor diameter assumed 10 mm

V1.error_msg: Mesh voltage exceeds tolerable touch voltage: increase conductor density (reduce mesh spacing), add conductors, or increase burial depth

V2.error_msg: GPR > 5000 V with R_g > 1 Ω: high-voltage hazard zone — telecommunications and fence isolation required per IEEE 80 §17

V3.error_msg: Conductor density < 0.5 m/m²: sparse grid — mesh voltage will be high; add parallel conductors to achieve minimum 1 m/m² density

smart_warnings.message: Mesh voltage within 20% of tolerable limit: marginal safety margin — add equalising conductors or equipotential mats at operator locations
High soil resistivity (> 1000 Ω·m): achieving low grid resistance requires extensive grid or deep-driven rods — consider chemical ground enhancement
R_g > 1 Ω at high fault current: GPR will be dangerously high — verify grid extends beyond substation fence; add remote earth electrodes
Surface layer resistivity < 500 Ω·m: low benefit from surface treatment — increase layer thickness or use higher-resistivity dry crushed stone
Fault clearing time > 1.0 s: tolerable voltages reduce significantly — upgrade protection relay speed or re-evaluate system protection coordination

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: E_mesh_v > E_touch_v
fmea.effect: Mesh voltage exceeds tolerable touch voltage: increase conductor density (reduce mesh spacing), add conductors, or increase burial depth
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: GPR_v > 5000 && Rg_ohm > 1.0
fmea.effect: GPR > 5000 V with R_g > 1 Ω: high-voltage hazard zone — telecommunications and fence isolation required per IEEE 80 §17
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: grid_conductor_length_m / grid_area_m2 < 0.5
fmea.effect: Conductor density < 0.5 m/m²: sparse grid — mesh voltage will be high; add parallel conductors to achieve minimum 1 m/m² density
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Mesh voltage within 20% of tolerable limit: marginal safety margin — add equalising conductors or equipotential mats at operator locations
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_mesh_v > E_touch_v * 0.8

fmea.failureMode: High soil resistivity (> 1000 Ω·m): achieving low grid resistance requires extensive grid or deep-driven rods — consider chemical ground enhancement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: soil_resistivity_ohm_m > 1000

fmea.failureMode: R_g > 1 Ω at high fault current: GPR will be dangerously high — verify grid extends beyond substation fence; add remote earth electrodes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rg_ohm > 1.0 && fault_current_kA > 10

fmea.failureMode: Surface layer resistivity < 500 Ω·m: low benefit from surface treatment — increase layer thickness or use higher-resistivity dry crushed stone
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: surface_layer_resistivity < 500

fmea.failureMode: Fault clearing time > 1.0 s: tolerable voltages reduce significantly — upgrade protection relay speed or re-evaluate system protection coordination
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fault_duration_s > 1.0

tool_name: Fired Heater Thermal Efficiency — Stack Loss, Excess Air Optimisation & Process Duty

inputs.name: Fuel Lower Heating Value (LHV)
inputs.note: Natural gas ≈ 47500; refinery fuel gas ≈ 42000–46000; fuel oil ≈ 40000–42000 kJ/kg
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Fuel Firing Rate (ṁ_fuel)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5167-1:2022 §6 — Orifice plate: ±2% (Re>10⁴)

inputs.name: Flue Gas O₂ Content at Stack (dry basis)
inputs.note: Measured by portable O₂ analyser at stack; typical target 2–3% for gas fuel
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Fuel Carbon Content (mass fraction)
inputs.note: Natural gas ≈ 75%; fuel oil ≈ 86%; coal ≈ 70–80%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Radiation & Convection Losses (% of fired duty)
inputs.note: API 560: typically 1–2% for well-insulated heaters; up to 3–5% for small units
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Measured Process Fluid Heat Absorption (Q_proc)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

formulas: XS_air_pct = O2_stack_pct * 100 / (21 - O2_stack_pct)   // [%] Excess Air Percentage from O₂ Stack Reading Reference: API 560 §A.3 — excess air from dry O₂: EA% = O₂% × 100 / (21 − O₂%)

cp_fg_kj_kg_k = 1.004 + 0.000254 * stack_temp_c   // [kJ/(kg·K)] Stoichiometric Flue Gas Specific Heat Approximation (c_p,fg) Reference: ASME PTC 4-2013 — linearised flue gas c_p; accurate to ±2% over 100–600°C

m_air_stoich = 11.5 * fuel_carbon_content_pct / 100; m_fg_kg_kg = m_air_stoich * (1 + XS_air_pct/100) + 1; Q_stack_pct = m_fg_kg_kg * cp_fg_kj_kg_k * (stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / fuel_LHV_kj_kg * 100   // [%] Stack (Dry Flue Gas) Heat Loss Reference: API 560 / ISO 13705:2006 §8.2 — dry flue gas stack loss; m_air stoich from carbon content

eta_th_pct = 100 - Q_stack_pct - radiation_loss_pct   // [%] Net Thermal Efficiency (η_th) Reference: ISO 13705:2006 §8 — efficiency by losses method; unburned hydrocarbon loss neglected for clean fuel

Q_fired_kw = fuel_flow_kg_h / 3600 * fuel_LHV_kj_kg; fuel_utilisation_pct = process_fluid_duty_kw / Q_fired_kw * 100   // [kW, %] Fired Duty & Fuel Utilisation Reference: API 560 §4.3 — fired duty = fuel rate × LHV
fuel_saving_kgh = fuel_flow_kg_h * 0.01; annual_saving_kj = fuel_saving_kgh * 8760 * fuel_LHV_kj_kg / 3600   // [kg/h, GJ/yr] Annual Fuel Saving Potential — 1% Efficiency Improvement

standards: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

V1.error_msg: Stack O₂ < 1%: risk of sub-stoichiometric combustion — CO formation, afterburning, tube coking; increase air supply immediately

V2.error_msg: Stack temperature > 600°C: severe stack heat loss — verify convection section fouling; consider air preheater installation

V3.error_msg: Process duty significantly below thermal efficiency × fired duty: check process-side fouling, flow maldistribution, or tube scaling

smart_warnings.message: Excess air > 25%: significant stack loss penalty — each 10% excess air at 300°C stack increases stack loss ≈ 0.5%; install O₂ trim controller
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: Thermal efficiency < 85%: below API 560 design target for natural gas fuel — assess convection section tube cleaning and air preheater condition
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: Stack ΔT > 350 K: air preheater economiser has strong ROI — each 20°C stack temp reduction raises efficiency ≈ 1%
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: High O₂ (>6%) on large heater: major energy waste — reduce excess air to 2–3% target; NOₓ and stack loss both decrease
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

smart_warnings.message: High radiation loss (>3%): check casing and refractory integrity — hot spots and casing leaks waste energy and create safety hazard
smart_warnings.source: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: O2_stack_pct < 1.0
fmea.effect: Stack O₂ < 1%: risk of sub-stoichiometric combustion — CO formation, afterburning, tube coking; increase air supply immediately
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: stack_temp_c > 600
fmea.effect: Stack temperature > 600°C: severe stack heat loss — verify convection section fouling; consider air preheater installation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fuel_utilisation_pct < eta_th_pct - 5
fmea.effect: Process duty significantly below thermal efficiency × fired duty: check process-side fouling, flow maldistribution, or tube scaling
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 25%: significant stack loss penalty — each 10% excess air at 300°C stack increases stack loss ≈ 0.5%; install O₂ trim controller
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: XS_air_pct > 25
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: Thermal efficiency < 85%: below API 560 design target for natural gas fuel — assess convection section tube cleaning and air preheater condition
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_th_pct < 85
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: Stack ΔT > 350 K: air preheater economiser has strong ROI — each 20°C stack temp reduction raises efficiency ≈ 1%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c > 350
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: High O₂ (>6%) on large heater: major energy waste — reduce excess air to 2–3% target; NOₓ and stack loss both decrease
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: O2_stack_pct > 6 && fuel_flow_kg_h > 1000
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: High radiation loss (>3%): check casing and refractory integrity — hot spots and casing leaks waste energy and create safety hazard
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: radiation_loss_pct > 3
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

tool_name: Storm Water Peak Runoff — Rational Method, Time of Concentration & Culvert Capacity

inputs.name: Catchment Area (A)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Weighted Runoff Coefficient (C)
inputs.note: ASCE Manual 36: impervious pavement C=0.9; lawns C=0.25–0.35; mixed urban C=0.55–0.75
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Design Rainfall Intensity (i) for Return Period
inputs.note: From IDF (Intensity-Duration-Frequency) curve at T_c for selected return period; NOAA Atlas 14 (US)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Design Storm Return Period
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Hydraulic Length of Catchment (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Average Catchment Slope (S)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Proposed Culvert/Pipe Internal Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Culvert / Pipe Invert Slope (S_pipe)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Manning's Roughness Coefficient (n)
inputs.note: Concrete pipe n=0.013; corrugated metal n=0.024; smooth plastic n=0.010
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Tc_min = 0.0195 * Math.pow(catchment_length_m, 0.77) * Math.pow(slope_pct / 100, -0.385)   // [min] Time of Concentration — Kirpich Formula (T_c) Reference: Kirpich (1940) — widely adopted in FHWA HEC-22 and ASCE practice for small catchments

Q_peak_m3s = runoff_coeff_C * rainfall_intensity_mm_h / 1000 / 3600 * catchment_area_ha * 10000   // [m³/s] Peak Runoff Rate — Rational Method (Q_peak) Reference: Rational Method: Q = C·i·A — ASCE 7-22 / EN 752:2017 §6.3; valid for A < 80 ha
AEP_pct = 100 / return_period_yr   // [%] Annual Exceedance Probability Reference: AEP = 1/T_r — FHWA HEC-22 §3.1

A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(culvert_diameter_mm/1000, 2); R_h_m = culvert_diameter_mm/1000/4; Q_pipe_m3s = (1/manning_n) * A_pipe_m2 * Math.pow(R_h_m, 2/3) * Math.pow(pipe_slope_pct/100, 0.5)   // [m³/s] Full-Bore Pipe Capacity — Manning's Equation (Q_pipe) Reference: Manning's equation — full-bore circular pipe; hydraulic radius R = D/4
cap_ratio = Q_pipe_m3s / Q_peak_m3s   // [dimensionless] Hydraulic Capacity Adequacy Ratio Reference: cap_ratio ≥ 1.0 required; ≥ 1.2 recommended to accommodate blockage allowance per EN 752 §7.3
v_pipe_m_s = Q_pipe_m3s / A_pipe_m2   // [m/s] Flow Velocity in Full-Bore Pipe Reference: FHWA HEC-22 §4.2 — minimum self-cleansing velocity 0.9 m/s; maximum erosion limit 3–4 m/s for concrete

V1.error_msg: Catchment > 80 ha: Rational Method loses accuracy for large areas — use TR-55 or SCS unit hydrograph method per USDA NEH-4

V2.error_msg: Pipe capacity insufficient for design storm: increase diameter or slope — surface flooding will occur at design return period

V3.error_msg: Flow velocity < 0.7 m/s: below self-cleansing threshold — sediment deposition and blockage; increase slope or reduce diameter

smart_warnings.message: Capacity ratio < 1.2: insufficient blockage margin per EN 752 §7.3 — upsize by one standard diameter
Velocity > 4 m/s: concrete pipe erosion risk — consider energy dissipator at outlet or use abrasion-resistant liner
T_c < 5 min: very short time of concentration — peak rainfall intensity assumption very sensitive; verify IDF curve resolution
Long return period with low C: verify C accounts for soil saturation at extended storms — effective C increases with event duration
Pipe slope < 0.3%: hydraulic gradient very flat — air entrainment, surcharge, and odour release risk; maintain minimum 0.5% per EN 752 §7

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: catchment_area_ha > 80 && runoff_coeff_C > 0.5
fmea.effect: Catchment > 80 ha: Rational Method loses accuracy for large areas — use TR-55 or SCS unit hydrograph method per USDA NEH-4
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cap_ratio < 1.0
fmea.effect: Pipe capacity insufficient for design storm: increase diameter or slope — surface flooding will occur at design return period
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_pipe_m_s < 0.7
fmea.effect: Flow velocity < 0.7 m/s: below self-cleansing threshold — sediment deposition and blockage; increase slope or reduce diameter
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Capacity ratio < 1.2: insufficient blockage margin per EN 752 §7.3 — upsize by one standard diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cap_ratio < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Velocity > 4 m/s: concrete pipe erosion risk — consider energy dissipator at outlet or use abrasion-resistant liner
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_pipe_m_s > 4.0

fmea.failureMode: T_c < 5 min: very short time of concentration — peak rainfall intensity assumption very sensitive; verify IDF curve resolution
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tc_min < 5

fmea.failureMode: Long return period with low C: verify C accounts for soil saturation at extended storms — effective C increases with event duration
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: return_period_yr >= 50 && runoff_coeff_C < 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Pipe slope < 0.3%: hydraulic gradient very flat — air entrainment, surcharge, and odour release risk; maintain minimum 0.5% per EN 752 §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pipe_slope_pct < 0.3

tool_name: Counterbalance Forklift Stability — Load Centre Shift, Residual Capacity & Tipping Moment

inputs.name: Forklift Rated Capacity at Standard Load Centre (Q_rated)
inputs.note: Per nameplate at standard load centre (typically 500 mm for IC; 600 mm for large trucks)
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Standard (Nameplate) Load Centre Distance (D_std)

options.label: 500 mm — standard IC up to 5 t

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Actual Load Centre of Specific Payload (D_act)
inputs.note: Distance from fork face to load centre of gravity; non-standard pallet or long load will shift this
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Maximum Lift Height (H_lift)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Mast Maximum Forward Tilt Angle
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Actual Payload Mass (W_load)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Attachment / Sideshifter Mass (W_att)
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Attachment Centre of Gravity from Fork Face (D_att)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: M_load_kg_mm = payload_kg * actual_load_centre_mm + attachment_weight_kg * attachment_centre_mm   // [kg·mm] Combined Load Moment at Fork Face Reference: ISO 22915-1:2016 §4.3 — load moment principle; attachment adds moment at its own CG
D_equiv_mm = M_load_kg_mm / (payload_kg + attachment_weight_kg)   // [mm] Equivalent Load Centre at Actual Payload Reference: Equivalent load centre for combined load + attachment — ISO 22915-1 §4.3

Q_residual_kg = rated_capacity_kg * std_load_centre_mm / D_equiv_mm   // [kg] Residual Capacity at Actual Load Centre (Q_residual) Reference: ISO 22915-1 capacity derating: Q_residual = Q_rated × D_std / D_equiv
util_ratio = (payload_kg + attachment_weight_kg) / Q_residual_kg   // [dimensionless] Capacity Utilisation Ratio Reference: Utilisation ≤ 1.0 mandatory — OSHA 1910.178(o)(2)

tipping_factor = Q_residual_kg / (payload_kg + attachment_weight_kg); tip_margin_pct = (tipping_factor - 1) * 100   // [%] Longitudinal Tipping Moment Margin Reference: ISO 22915-1 §5 — stability test margin; > 0% = stable

height_factor = 1 + (lift_height_mm - 3000) * 0.00005; Q_highlift_kg = (lift_height_mm > 3000) ? Q_residual_kg / height_factor : Q_residual_kg   // [kg] High-Lift Stability Derating Factor Reference: Approximate high-lift derating: mast deflection raises effective load centre at height > 3 m per EN 16307-1:2019

V1.error_msg: OVERLOAD: payload exceeds residual capacity at actual load centre — operation prohibited per OSHA 1910.178(o)(2) and ISO 22915-1

V2.error_msg: Load centre >150% of standard with significant payload: verify mast and carriage deflection with OEM — structural rating may limit further

V3.error_msg: High lift (>6 m) at >85% capacity: dynamic longitudinal stability severely reduced — reduce lift height or reduce load

smart_warnings.message: Tipping margin < 10%: dangerously close to longitudinal tip-over — reduce payload or move load CG closer to fork face; operator training mandatory per OSHA 1910.178(l)
Utilisation > 90% of residual capacity: marginal stability — prohibit operation on ramps, uneven surfaces, or with mast fully tilted forward
Attachment mass >15% of rated capacity: significant derating effect — obtain attachment data plate per ISO 22915-1 §4.3 mandatory
Forward tilt >5° at elevated height: pendulum effect amplifies tip-over moment — restrict forward tilt to minimum when elevated
Load centre > 800 mm: long or oversized load — reposition forks, use extended carriage, or engage slinging — standard stability assumptions invalid

tool_name: Cooling Tower Performance — Thermal Range, Approach, NTU & Makeup Water Rate

inputs.name: Total Heat Rejection Duty (Q_rej)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Ambient Wet-Bulb Temperature (T_wb)
inputs.note: Design wet-bulb from ASHRAE 0.4% cooling design data for location

inputs.name: Cycles of Concentration (COC)
inputs.note: COC = TDS_blowdown / TDS_makeup; typical 3–6; higher COC = less water use but more scaling risk
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §7 — Analytical chemistry: reproducibility ±5%

inputs.name: Total Tower Fan Motor Power (P_fan)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Drift Loss Rate (% of circulating water flow)
inputs.note: Modern fills with drift eliminators: 0.001–0.003%; CTI STD-201 design default
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: range_k = T_hot_in_c - T_cold_out_c; approach_k = T_cold_out_c - T_wb_c   // [K] Cooling Range & Approach Temperature Reference: CTI STD-201 RS §3.1 — Range = T_in − T_out; Approach = T_out − T_wb
m_dot_cw_kg_s = Q_rejected_kw / (4.18 * range_k)   // [kg/s] Circulating Water Flow Rate Reference: Energy balance Q = ṁ·c_p·ΔT; c_p = 4.18 kJ/(kg·K)

h_sat_out = 4186 * T_cold_out_c * 0.62198 / (0.622 + 0.62198) + 2501000 * 0.008; h_sat_in = 4186 * T_hot_in_c * 0.62198 / (0.622 + 0.62198) + 2501000 * 0.014; h_air_in = 1006 * T_wb_c + (0.622 + 0.001) * (2501000 + 1860 * T_wb_c); NTU = range_k * 4186 / ((h_sat_in + h_sat_out)/2 - h_air_in)   // [dimensionless] Number of Transfer Units (NTU) — Merkel Equation Approximation Reference: Merkel method (simplified) — CTI STD-201; NTU = c_pw·ΔT / ΔLMHD; typical tower NTU 0.8–2.5
E_loss_kg_s = 0.00085 * range_k * m_dot_cw_kg_s   // [kg/s] Evaporation Loss Rate Reference: CTI §4.2 — evaporation ≈ 0.085% per °C range; rule of thumb for preliminary sizing

drift_kg_s = drift_loss_pct / 100 * m_dot_cw_kg_s; blowdown_kg_s = E_loss_kg_s / (cycles_of_concentration - 1); MU_kg_s = E_loss_kg_s + blowdown_kg_s + drift_kg_s   // [kg/s] Total Makeup Water Requirement Reference: CTI / ASHRAE — makeup = evaporation + blowdown + drift; MU = E/(1 − 1/COC) + D

RT_capacity = Q_rejected_kw / 3.517; kW_per_RT = fan_power_kw / RT_capacity; annual_water_m3 = MU_kg_s * 3600 * 8760 / 1000   // [kW/RT, m³/yr] Tower Energy Performance Ratio (kW/RT) & Annual Water Consumption Reference: ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.2 — fan power limit; 1 RT = 3.517 kW cooling

V1.error_msg: Approach < 3 K: near theoretical minimum — tower will be very large and sensitive to wet-bulb fluctuation; verify CTI certification data

V2.error_msg: Cold water outlet ≤ wet-bulb temperature: thermodynamically impossible for evaporative cooling — increase T_CW,out above T_wb

V3.error_msg: COC > 7: elevated scaling and corrosion risk — verify water treatment programme and Langelier Saturation Index per ASHRAE

smart_warnings.message: Approach < 5 K: performance highly sensitive to wet-bulb exceedances — model with 99.6% wet-bulb value, not just design point
Fan power > 0.075 kW/RT: above ASHRAE 90.1-2022 fan power limit for centrifugal towers — evaluate VFD on fans for part-load savings
Annual makeup water > 100,000 m³: significant water cost — evaluate side-stream filtration, higher COC, or hybrid dry/wet tower
COC < 3: excessive blowdown — water and chemical waste; increase COC to 4–5 with proper chemical treatment to reduce makeup by 20–30%
Thermal range < 4 K: low-range system requires high water flow — verify pump sizing; consider larger ΔT to reduce circulating flow and pump energy

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: approach_k < 3
fmea.effect: Approach < 3 K: near theoretical minimum — tower will be very large and sensitive to wet-bulb fluctuation; verify CTI certification data
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_cold_out_c <= T_wb_c
fmea.effect: Cold water outlet ≤ wet-bulb temperature: thermodynamically impossible for evaporative cooling — increase T_CW,out above T_wb
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cycles_of_concentration > 7 && Q_rejected_kw > 1000
fmea.effect: COC > 7: elevated scaling and corrosion risk — verify water treatment programme and Langelier Saturation Index per ASHRAE
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Approach < 5 K: performance highly sensitive to wet-bulb exceedances — model with 99.6% wet-bulb value, not just design point
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: approach_k < 5

fmea.failureMode: Fan power > 0.075 kW/RT: above ASHRAE 90.1-2022 fan power limit for centrifugal towers — evaluate VFD on fans for part-load savings
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: kW_per_RT > 0.075

fmea.failureMode: Annual makeup water > 100,000 m³: significant water cost — evaluate side-stream filtration, higher COC, or hybrid dry/wet tower
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: annual_water_m3 > 100000

fmea.failureMode: COC < 3: excessive blowdown — water and chemical waste; increase COC to 4–5 with proper chemical treatment to reduce makeup by 20–30%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cycles_of_concentration < 3

fmea.failureMode: Thermal range < 4 K: low-range system requires high water flow — verify pump sizing; consider larger ΔT to reduce circulating flow and pump energy
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: range_k < 4

tool_name: Reciprocating Compressor Cylinder Sizing — Displacement, Volumetric Efficiency & Rod Load

inputs.name: Suction Pressure (P_s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span
