inputs.name: Discharge Pressure (P_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Required Actual Volume Flow at Suction (Q_act)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Gas Isentropic Exponent (k = c_p / c_v)
inputs.note: Natural gas ≈ 1.27–1.31; air = 1.40; H₂ = 1.41; CO₂ = 1.28; propane = 1.13
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Cylinder Bore Diameter (D_cyl)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Piston Stroke (S)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Crankshaft Speed (n)
inputs.note: Slow-speed integral: 200–360 rpm; high-speed separable: 900–1800 rpm — API 618 Table 1
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Cylinder Clearance Volume (c)
inputs.note: Fixed clearance typical range 8–20%; adjustable clearance pockets can reach 30–35%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: r_p = P_discharge_bar / P_suction_bar   // [dimensionless] Compression Ratio (r_p) Reference: API 618-2007 §3.1; single-stage r_p typically ≤ 4.5 for reliability
eta_v = 1 - clearance_pct/100 * (Math.pow(r_p, 1/k_isentropic) - 1)   // [dimensionless] Volumetric Efficiency (η_v) Reference: ISO 1217:2009 §3.4 / API 618 — isentropic re-expansion model

V_d_m3_rev = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(bore_mm/1000, 2) * (stroke_mm/1000)   // [m³/rev] Piston Displacement Volume per Revolution (V_d) Reference: Displacement = π/4 × D² × S (double-acting: multiply by 2 minus rod area)
Q_cyl_m3h = V_d_m3_rev * speed_rpm * 60 * eta_v   // [Am³/h] Actual Flow Capacity of Single Cylinder Reference: API 618 §5.3 — cylinder capacity with volumetric efficiency

P_is_kw = (k_isentropic/(k_isentropic-1)) * P_suction_bar*1e5 * (Q_cyl_m3h/3600) * (Math.pow(r_p, (k_isentropic-1)/k_isentropic) - 1) / 1000   // [kW] Isentropic Power per Cylinder Reference: Isentropic compression work — ASME PTC 9 §5.3

A_bore_m2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(bore_mm/1000, 2); rod_load_comp_kn = (P_discharge_bar - P_suction_bar) * 1e5 * A_bore_m2 / 1000; rod_load_tension_kn = rod_load_comp_kn * 0.85   // [kN] Maximum Gas Rod Load (Tension & Compression) Reference: API 618 §3.11 — combined rod load; tension ≈ 85% of compression for standard rod ratio

V1.error_msg: Compression ratio > 4.5: API 618 §3.1 recommends single-stage r_p ≤ 4.5 — consider two-stage compression with inter-cooling

V2.error_msg: Volumetric efficiency < 55%: cylinder re-expansion dominates — clearance volume excessive or compression ratio too high; add unloader pockets

V3.error_msg: Single cylinder capacity insufficient for required flow — add cylinders in parallel or increase bore/speed

smart_warnings.message: r_p > 6: very high ratio — discharge temperature will exceed 150°C for most gases; inter-stage cooling mandatory per API 618 §3.1.2
Rod load > 450 kN: check against frame rated rod load in API 618 datasheet — exceeded frame load causes crankshaft and crosshead failure
High speed on large bore: piston velocity > 4 m/s — verify ring and packing wear rates; API 618 Table 1 speed limit
High clearance (>25%): volumetric efficiency severely reduced — verify unloader pocket sizing and gas passage flow velocity
Discharge pressure > 100 bar: high-pressure classification — ASME B31.3 Class 600 piping, material qualification, and pressure testing per API 618 §7 required

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: r_p > 4.5
fmea.effect: Compression ratio > 4.5: API 618 §3.1 recommends single-stage r_p ≤ 4.5 — consider two-stage compression with inter-cooling
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: eta_v < 0.55
fmea.effect: Volumetric efficiency < 55%: cylinder re-expansion dominates — clearance volume excessive or compression ratio too high; add unloader pockets
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Q_cyl_m3h < Q_actual_m3h * 0.9
fmea.effect: Single cylinder capacity insufficient for required flow — add cylinders in parallel or increase bore/speed
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: r_p > 6: very high ratio — discharge temperature will exceed 150°C for most gases; inter-stage cooling mandatory per API 618 §3.1.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: r_p > 6

fmea.failureMode: Rod load > 450 kN: check against frame rated rod load in API 618 datasheet — exceeded frame load causes crankshaft and crosshead failure
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rod_load_comp_kn > 450

fmea.failureMode: High speed on large bore: piston velocity > 4 m/s — verify ring and packing wear rates; API 618 Table 1 speed limit
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: speed_rpm > 600 && bore_mm > 400

fmea.failureMode: High clearance (>25%): volumetric efficiency severely reduced — verify unloader pocket sizing and gas passage flow velocity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: clearance_pct > 25

fmea.failureMode: Discharge pressure > 100 bar: high-pressure classification — ASME B31.3 Class 600 piping, material qualification, and pressure testing per API 618 §7 required
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_discharge_bar > 100

tool_name: RC Column Axial-Bending Interaction — Capacity Check, Eccentricity & Confinement

inputs.name: Factored Axial Load (P_u / N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Factored Bending Moment (M_u / M_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Column Width (b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Column Depth in Bending Direction (h)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Concrete Compressive Strength (f'_c / f_ck)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Longitudinal Steel Yield Strength (f_y / f_yk)

options.label: 420 MPa — ASTM A615 Gr60 / ASTM A706

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Longitudinal Reinforcement Ratio (ρ_g)
inputs.note: ACI 318-19 §10.6.1.1: 1% ≤ ρ_g ≤ 8%; practical max 4–5% for constructability
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Clear Cover to Ties (d_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Unsupported Column Height (L_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

formulas: Ag_mm2 = b_mm * h_mm; Ast_mm2 = rho_g_pct / 100 * Ag_mm2   // [mm²] Gross Section Area & Steel Area Reference: ACI 318-19 §10.6.1.1

phi_Pn_max_kn = 0.65 * 0.80 * (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa * (Ag_mm2 - Ast_mm2) + fy_mpa * Ast_mm2) / 1000   // [kN] Maximum Pure Axial Capacity (φP_n,max) Reference: ACI 318-19 §22.4.2.1 — tied column: φ=0.65; factor 0.80 for accidental eccentricity

d_mm = h_mm - cover_mm - 8 - 16; cb_mm = 0.003/(0.003 + fy_mpa/200000) * d_mm; ab_mm = 0.85 * cb_mm; Pb_kn = 0.65 * (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa * b_mm * ab_mm - Ast_mm2/2 * fy_mpa + Ast_mm2/2 * fy_mpa) / 1000   // [kN] Balanced Failure Point — Balanced Axial Load (P_b) Reference: ACI 318-19 §22.4 — balanced strain condition; assumes symmetric reinforcement
e_mm = (Mu_knm * 1e6) / (Pu_kn * 1000); e_ratio = e_mm / h_mm   // [mm, dimensionless] Eccentricity & Eccentricity Ratio Reference: Eccentricity e = M_u / P_u; slender column check if e > h/10

r_gyration = 0.3 * h_mm; kL_r = 1.0 * column_height_m * 1000 / r_gyration; slender = (kL_r > 22) ? 'SLENDER — moment magnification required per ACI §6.6.4' : 'SHORT — slenderness effects negligible'   // [dimensionless, text] Slenderness Ratio & Slender Column Flag Reference: ACI 318-19 §6.2.5 — braced frame slender if kL/r > 34 − 12(M1/M2); conservative k=1.0 assumed

DCR_axial = Pu_kn / phi_Pn_max_kn; phi_Mn_knm = 0.65 * Ast_mm2/2 * fy_mpa * (h_mm - 2*cover_mm - 16) / 1e6; DCR_moment = Mu_knm / phi_Mn_knm; interaction_ACI = (Pu_kn >= phi_Pn_max_kn * 0.2) ? DCR_axial + 8/9 * DCR_moment : DCR_axial/2 + DCR_moment   // [dimensionless] Interaction Demand-Capacity Ratio (Simplified) Reference: AISC-style interaction check approximation; full P-M curve requires iterative strain compatibility per ACI §22.4

V1.error_msg: Column overstressed: increase cross-section dimensions, concrete strength, or reinforcement ratio — full P-M interaction diagram required

V2.error_msg: ρ_g > 6%: congestion risk — lap splice and concrete consolidation problems; split into two columns or increase section size

V3.error_msg: kL/r > 100: highly slender column — second-order effects dominant; P-delta analysis and moment magnification required per ACI §6.6.4.5

smart_warnings.message: Interaction ratio > 0.9: near capacity — seismic or wind load combination check with overstrength factor per ACI §18.7
Slender column under low eccentricity: buckling controls over material failure — verify EI_eff and compute moment magnifier δ_ns per ACI §6.6.4.4
Low-strength concrete at high axial load: creep and shrinkage deformations significant — long-term P-delta effects must be included per ACI §6.6.4.4
Lightly reinforced column at high axial demand: minimum 1.5% steel recommended for ductility and construction tolerance per ACI §10.6.1
Low cover in moderate-strength concrete: consider 50 mm cover for exposed or marine environments per ACI §20.6.1.3

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: interaction_ACI > 1.0
fmea.effect: Column overstressed: increase cross-section dimensions, concrete strength, or reinforcement ratio — full P-M interaction diagram required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rho_g_pct > 6
fmea.effect: ρ_g > 6%: congestion risk — lap splice and concrete consolidation problems; split into two columns or increase section size
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: kL_r > 100
fmea.effect: kL/r > 100: highly slender column — second-order effects dominant; P-delta analysis and moment magnification required per ACI §6.6.4.5
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Interaction ratio > 0.9: near capacity — seismic or wind load combination check with overstrength factor per ACI §18.7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: interaction_ACI > 0.9

fmea.failureMode: Slender column under low eccentricity: buckling controls over material failure — verify EI_eff and compute moment magnifier δ_ns per ACI §6.6.4.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: kL_r > 22 && e_ratio < 0.1

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength concrete at high axial load: creep and shrinkage deformations significant — long-term P-delta effects must be included per ACI §6.6.4.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fc_prime_mpa < 25 && Pu_kn > phi_Pn_max_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Lightly reinforced column at high axial demand: minimum 1.5% steel recommended for ductility and construction tolerance per ACI §10.6.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rho_g_pct < 1.5 && DCR_axial > 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Low cover in moderate-strength concrete: consider 50 mm cover for exposed or marine environments per ACI §20.6.1.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cover_mm < 30 && fc_prime_mpa < 30

tool_name: Industrial Ventilation Duct Sizing — Air Volume, Velocity, Friction Loss & Fan Selection

inputs.name: Design Air Volume Flow Rate (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Duct Cross-Section Shape
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Duct Diameter (circular) or Width a (rectangular)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Duct Height b (rectangular only; enter 0 for circular)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Duct Run Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Duct Wall Roughness (ε)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sum of Local Loss Coefficients (ΣC_local) — Fittings, Bends, Diffusers
inputs.note: ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch.21 fitting coefficients: 90° elbow R/D=1.5 → C≈0.22; branch tee → C≈0.5–1.0
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Air Density at Operating Conditions (ρ_air)
inputs.note: Standard air at 20°C, sea level: 1.204 kg/m³; hot process air reduces density
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

formulas: D_h_mm = (duct_shape === 'circular') ? duct_dim_1_mm : (2 * duct_dim_1_mm * duct_dim_2_mm) / (duct_dim_1_mm + duct_dim_2_mm)   // [mm] Hydraulic Diameter (D_h) Reference: D_h = 4A/P; for rectangle: D_h = 2ab/(a+b) — ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch.21

A_duct_m2 = (duct_shape === 'circular') ? Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(duct_dim_1_mm/1000, 2) : (duct_dim_1_mm * duct_dim_2_mm) / 1e6; v_m_s = Q_air_m3h / 3600 / A_duct_m2   // [m², m/s] Duct Cross-Section Area & Air Velocity Reference: Continuity equation — velocity = Q/A

Re = air_density_kg_m3 * v_m_s * D_h_mm/1000 / 1.81e-5; f = 0.02; for(let i=0;i<50;i++){f=1/Math.pow(-2*Math.log10(roughness_mm/(3.7*D_h_mm)+2.51/(Re*Math.sqrt(f))),2)}   // [dimensionless] Friction Factor — Colebrook-White (iterative, MaxIter=50) Reference: Colebrook-White equation — Moody chart; iterative convergence per ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch.21

dP_f_pa = f_darcy * (duct_length_m / (D_h_mm/1000)) * air_density_kg_m3 * Math.pow(v_m_s, 2) / 2   // [Pa] Friction Pressure Loss (ΔP_f) Reference: Darcy-Weisbach equation — ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021 Ch.21 Eq.(19)

dP_local_pa = local_loss_coeff_sum * air_density_kg_m3 * Math.pow(v_m_s, 2) / 2   // [Pa] Local (Dynamic) Pressure Loss (ΔP_local) Reference: ΔP = C × ρv²/2 — SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards 3rd Ed.

dP_total_pa = dP_f_pa + dP_local_pa; dP_per_m_pa = dP_f_pa / duct_length_m   // [Pa, Pa/m] Total System Pressure Drop & Friction Rate Reference: SMACNA design target: 0.8–1.2 Pa/m for low-pressure systems; EN 16798-1 comfort ventilation

V1.error_msg: Rectangular duct velocity > 12 m/s: high noise generation (NC > 45) and structural stress — reduce velocity or use circular duct

V2.error_msg: Friction rate > 3.0 Pa/m: excessive pressure drop — increase duct size; energy cost will be significant

V3.error_msg: Supply air velocity < 3 m/s: oversized duct — risk of dust settlement in horizontal runs; minimum 5 m/s for dust-laden air streams

smart_warnings.message: Circular duct velocity > 10 m/s: exceeds SMACNA medium-pressure classification — verify duct pressure class and seam/joint rating
Friction rate > 2 Pa/m: increase duct diameter by one standard size — velocity reduces by ~20%, friction drops ~36%
Fitting losses dominate: local ΔP > 50% of friction ΔP — optimise fitting selection (long-radius elbows, conical transitions) to reduce system resistance
High roughness at elevated velocity: flexible duct in high-velocity application significantly increases fan energy — replace with rigid duct
Low air density (high temp or altitude): fan performance curves must be corrected for density — rated pressure and flow will be reduced

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_m_s > 12 && duct_shape === 'rectangular'
fmea.effect: Rectangular duct velocity > 12 m/s: high noise generation (NC > 45) and structural stress — reduce velocity or use circular duct
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_per_m_pa > 3.0
fmea.effect: Friction rate > 3.0 Pa/m: excessive pressure drop — increase duct size; energy cost will be significant
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_m_s < 3.0 && Q_air_m3h > 500
fmea.effect: Supply air velocity < 3 m/s: oversized duct — risk of dust settlement in horizontal runs; minimum 5 m/s for dust-laden air streams
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Circular duct velocity > 10 m/s: exceeds SMACNA medium-pressure classification — verify duct pressure class and seam/joint rating
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_m_s > 10 && duct_shape === 'circular'

fmea.failureMode: Friction rate > 2 Pa/m: increase duct diameter by one standard size — velocity reduces by ~20%, friction drops ~36%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dP_per_m_pa > 2.0

fmea.failureMode: Fitting losses dominate: local ΔP > 50% of friction ΔP — optimise fitting selection (long-radius elbows, conical transitions) to reduce system resistance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: local_loss_coeff_sum > duct_length_m * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: High roughness at elevated velocity: flexible duct in high-velocity application significantly increases fan energy — replace with rigid duct
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: roughness_mm > 0.5 && v_m_s > 7

fmea.failureMode: Low air density (high temp or altitude): fan performance curves must be corrected for density — rated pressure and flow will be reduced
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: air_density_kg_m3 < 1.0

tool_name: Process Capability — Cpk, Ppk, DPMO & Sigma Level from Measurement Data

inputs.name: Upper Specification Limit (USL)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification limit, zero uncertainty

inputs.name: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification limit, zero uncertainty

inputs.name: Process Sample Mean (x̄)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Process Short-Term Standard Deviation (σ_ST / s̄/d₂)
inputs.note: From control chart: R̄/d₂ or s̄/c₄; for Ppk use overall std deviation s
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Process Long-Term Standard Deviation (σ_LT / s_overall)
inputs.note: Overall std dev from all data points (includes between-subgroup variation); used for Ppk
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total Number of Measurements (n)
inputs.note: Minimum 30 per AIAG MSA; minimum 50 recommended for reliable Ppk
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Gauge R&R as % of Tolerance (GRR%)
inputs.note: AIAG MSA 4th Ed.: GRR% < 10% = acceptable; 10–30% = marginal; > 30% = unacceptable
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Number of Defect Opportunities per Unit (O)
inputs.note: For DPMO calculation: O=1 for single CTQ; multi-characteristic assembly O=10–100
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: Cp = (USL - LSL) / (6 * process_std_dev)   // [dimensionless] Process Potential Capability (Cp) Reference: ISO 22514-2:2017 §4.3 — Cp = (USL−LSL) / 6σ_ST; measures spread vs tolerance

Cpu = (USL - process_mean) / (3 * process_std_dev); Cpl = (process_mean - LSL) / (3 * process_std_dev); Cpk = Math.min(Cpu, Cpl)   // [dimensionless] Process Centering Indices (Cpk) Reference: AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Ed. / ISO 22514-2 §4.5 — Cpk = min(Cpu, Cpl)

Ppu = (USL - process_mean) / (3 * process_std_dev_lt); Ppl = (process_mean - LSL) / (3 * process_std_dev_lt); Ppk = Math.min(Ppu, Ppl)   // [dimensionless] Long-Term Performance Indices (Ppk) Reference: AIAG / ISO 22514-2 §4.7 — Ppk uses overall σ_LT including special causes

Z_upper = (USL - process_mean) / process_std_dev_lt; Z_lower = (process_mean - LSL) / process_std_dev_lt; p_defect = (1 - 0.5*(1+Math.sign(Z_upper)*Math.min(1, Math.abs(Z_upper)*0.3989))) + (0.5*(1+Math.sign(Z_lower)*Math.min(1, Math.abs(Z_lower)*0.3989)) - 0); DPMO = p_defect * 1e6 / defect_opportunity_per_unit   // [defects/million] Defects Per Million Opportunities (DPMO) Reference: Six Sigma DPMO = P(defect) × 10⁶; Z-score from long-term σ — Motorola Six Sigma methodology
Z_bench = (Ppk > 0) ? Ppk * 3 + 1.5 : 0   // [sigma] Sigma Level (Z_bench) Reference: Six Sigma convention: Z_bench = Ppk × 3 + 1.5σ shift allowance; 6σ → Cpk=1.5, Ppk=1.33

sigma_true = Math.sqrt(Math.max(0, process_std_dev^2 - Math.pow(gage_RR_pct/100*(USL-LSL)/6, 2))); Cpk_adj = (USL - LSL) / (6 * sigma_true) * (Cpk/Cp)   // [dimensionless] Gauge R&R Corrected Capability (Cpk_adj) Reference: AIAG MSA 4th Ed. §III — GRR-corrected true process capability; removes measurement system contribution

V1.error_msg: USL must exceed LSL — check specification limit inputs

V2.error_msg: GRR% > 30%: measurement system is inadequate per AIAG MSA — Cpk values are unreliable; improve gauge before process study

V3.error_msg: Long-term σ < short-term σ: statistically impossible — verify correct assignment of within-subgroup vs overall standard deviation

smart_warnings.message: Cpk < 1.33: below IATF 16949 / automotive PPAP minimum — process not capable for launch; corrective action plan mandatory
Cpk > 1.0 but Ppk < 1.0: process capable short-term but unstable long-term — identify and eliminate special causes per SPC reaction plan
Centering imbalance |Cpu − Cpl| > 0.3: process shifted off-target — adjust process mean; off-centre processes waste specification width
DPMO > 3400: below 4.5σ level — customer escape risk significant; initiate DMAIC improvement project
Sample size < 30: insufficient for reliable Cpk/Ppk estimation — confidence interval on Cpk is very wide; collect minimum 30–50 points before reporting

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: USL <= LSL
fmea.effect: USL must exceed LSL — check specification limit inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: gage_RR_pct > 30
fmea.effect: GRR% > 30%: measurement system is inadequate per AIAG MSA — Cpk values are unreliable; improve gauge before process study
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: process_std_dev_lt < process_std_dev
fmea.effect: Long-term σ < short-term σ: statistically impossible — verify correct assignment of within-subgroup vs overall standard deviation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Cpk < 1.33: below IATF 16949 / automotive PPAP minimum — process not capable for launch; corrective action plan mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk < 1.33

fmea.failureMode: Cpk > 1.0 but Ppk < 1.0: process capable short-term but unstable long-term — identify and eliminate special causes per SPC reaction plan
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk > 1.0 && Ppk < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Centering imbalance |Cpu − Cpl| > 0.3: process shifted off-target — adjust process mean; off-centre processes waste specification width
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Math.abs(Cpu - Cpl) > 0.3

fmea.failureMode: DPMO > 3400: below 4.5σ level — customer escape risk significant; initiate DMAIC improvement project
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DPMO > 3400

fmea.failureMode: Sample size < 30: insufficient for reliable Cpk/Ppk estimation — confidence interval on Cpk is very wide; collect minimum 30–50 points before reporting
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sample_size_n < 30

tool_name: Wire Rope Sling — Safe Working Load, D/d Ratio Derating & Fatigue Life Assessment

inputs.name: Wire Rope Nominal Diameter (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Wire Rope Grade / Minimum Breaking Force Category
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Rope Construction — Fill Factor (f_m)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sling Configuration & Included Angle
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Design Factor (Safety Factor on MBF)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sheave / Hook Radius of Contact (D_sheave)
inputs.note: For hook contact in basket/choker: D = hook groove diameter; ISO 2408 §8
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Annual Number of Lift Cycles
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: MBF_kn = rope_grade * rope_construction * Math.pow(rope_diameter_mm, 2) / 1000   // [kN] Minimum Breaking Force (MBF) — ISO 2408 Reference: ISO 2408:2023 §7 — MBF = R_r × f_m × d²; R_r in N/mm², d in mm → kN
WLL_leg_kn = MBF_kn / design_factor   // [kN] Sling Rated Capacity — Single Leg (WLL_leg) Reference: ASME B30.9-2022 §9-1.3 — WLL = MBF / DF
SWL_eff_kn = WLL_leg_kn * sling_configuration   // [kN] Effective Safe Working Load with Configuration (SWL_eff) Reference: EN 13414-1:2003 §4.3 — configuration mode factor applied to single-leg WLL

Dd_ratio = sheave_diameter_mm / rope_diameter_mm; bend_eff = (Dd_ratio >= 25) ? 1.0 : (Dd_ratio >= 18) ? 0.90 : (Dd_ratio >= 12) ? 0.75 : (Dd_ratio >= 8) ? 0.65 : 0.50   // [dimensionless] D/d Ratio & Bending Efficiency Factor Reference: ISO 2408 §8 / LEEA COPSULE — bending efficiency factor; D/d < 12 causes rapid fatigue degradation

SWL_derated_kn = SWL_eff_kn * bend_eff   // [kN] Derated SWL Accounting for Bending Reference: ASME B30.9 Commentary — SWL must be reduced for D/d < 25; contact between strands causes accelerated fatigue

N_fatigue = Math.pow(bend_eff * MBF_kn / (SWL_eff_kn * design_factor), 3) * 100000; years_life = N_fatigue / lift_cycles_per_year   // [cycles, years] Indicative Fatigue Life (Cycle-Based) at Operating Load Reference: Power-law fatigue estimate — indicative only; governed by actual inspection per ASME B30.9 §9-1.7 discard criteria

V1.error_msg: D/d < 8: extreme bending — rope fatigue life is negligible; minimum D/d = 12 for sling service per ISO 2408 §8

V2.error_msg: Choker hitch with DF < 6: pinching reduces effective breaking load unpredictably — apply DF ≥ 6 for choker per ASME B30.9

V3.error_msg: Estimated fatigue life < 1 year: high-cycle duty — implement formal inspection programme at minimum 3-month intervals per EN 13414

smart_warnings.message: D/d < 18: significant bending penalty (efficiency < 90%) — replace contact hardware with larger radius hook or thimble; or upsize rope
Bending efficiency < 75%: severe rope fatigue zone — mandatory inspection after every 50 lift cycles per LEEA COPSULE §7
Single-leg vertical: simplest configuration — confirm load centre of gravity alignment; off-vertical > 5° effectively applies choker mode factor
High-grade rope with small D/d: high-strength wires are more notch-sensitive — bending fatigue is disproportionately severe; use D/d ≥ 25 for Rr ≥ 1960
High-cycle application (>5000 cycles/yr): wire rope slings unsuitable — consider chain slings or permanent rigging with engineered fatigue life per ISO 1704

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Dd_ratio < 8
fmea.effect: D/d < 8: extreme bending — rope fatigue life is negligible; minimum D/d = 12 for sling service per ISO 2408 §8
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling_configuration === 0.75 && design_factor < 6
fmea.effect: Choker hitch with DF < 6: pinching reduces effective breaking load unpredictably — apply DF ≥ 6 for choker per ASME B30.9
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: years_life < 1 && lift_cycles_per_year > 100
fmea.effect: Estimated fatigue life < 1 year: high-cycle duty — implement formal inspection programme at minimum 3-month intervals per EN 13414
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: D/d < 18: significant bending penalty (efficiency < 90%) — replace contact hardware with larger radius hook or thimble; or upsize rope
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Dd_ratio < 18

fmea.failureMode: Bending efficiency < 75%: severe rope fatigue zone — mandatory inspection after every 50 lift cycles per LEEA COPSULE §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bend_eff < 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Single-leg vertical: simplest configuration — confirm load centre of gravity alignment; off-vertical > 5° effectively applies choker mode factor
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sling_configuration === 1.0 && design_factor === 5

fmea.failureMode: High-grade rope with small D/d: high-strength wires are more notch-sensitive — bending fatigue is disproportionately severe; use D/d ≥ 25 for Rr ≥ 1960
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rope_grade >= 1960 && Dd_ratio < 20

fmea.failureMode: High-cycle application (>5000 cycles/yr): wire rope slings unsuitable — consider chain slings or permanent rigging with engineered fatigue life per ISO 1704
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lift_cycles_per_year > 5000

tool_name: Grid-Tied PV System Sizing — Array Capacity, Annual Yield, Performance Ratio & Financial ROI

inputs.name: Annual Electrical Energy Demand (E_demand)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Peak Sun Hours at Site (PSH)
inputs.note: From NASA POWER / PVGIS irradiance database for location; annual average daily insolation at optimal tilt
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: PV Module Rated Power (STC) per Panel
inputs.note: STC: 1000 W/m², 25°C, AM1.5 — IEC 61215 rating
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Total System Losses (soiling, wiring, MPPT, mismatch, shading)
inputs.note: IEC 61724-1 loss budget: inverter 3%; soiling 3%; wiring 2%; MPPT 2%; temp derating 5–10%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Module Power Temperature Coefficient (γ_Pmax)
inputs.note: Typical mono-PERC: −0.37 to −0.40 %/°C; TOPCon: −0.30 %/°C — from datasheet

inputs.name: Average Operating Cell Temperature (T_cell,avg)
inputs.note: T_cell = T_amb + NOCT − 20°C; NOCT ≈ 42–47°C; cell runs 20–30°C above ambient at peak

inputs.name: Total Installed System Cost (CAPEX)
inputs.note: Utility-scale 2024 ≈ 0.60–0.85 USD/Wp; commercial rooftop ≈ 0.90–1.20 USD/Wp
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Grid Electricity Rate Avoided (USD/kWh)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: temp_derating = 1 + module_temp_coeff_pct_c / 100 * (avg_cell_temp_c - 25)   // [dimensionless] Temperature Derating Factor at Operating Cell Temp Reference: IEC 61215 §11.7 — temperature-corrected power: P_act = P_STC × [1 + γ·(T−25)]
PR = (1 - system_losses_pct / 100) * temp_derating   // [dimensionless] Performance Ratio (PR) Reference: IEC 61724-1:2021 §3.8 — PR = E_yield / (G_irr × P_STC); target PR ≥ 0.78 for good systems
P_array_kwp = annual_energy_demand_kwh / (PSH_h_day * 365 * PR)   // [kWp] Required DC Array Capacity (P_array) Reference: Array sizing: E_demand = P_array × PSH × 365 × PR — ASTM E2848 energy model

N_modules = Math.ceil(P_array_kwp * 1000 / module_wp); E_yield_kwh = P_array_kwp * PSH_h_day * 365 * PR   // [count, kWh/yr] Number of Modules Required & Annual Yield Reference: N = P_array / P_module; E_yield verified against demand — IEC 61724-1 §5

CAPEX_usd = P_array_kwp * 1000 * installed_cost_usd_wp; annual_saving_usd = E_yield_kwh * electricity_rate_usd_kwh   // [USD, USD/yr] Total CAPEX & Annual Revenue / Savings Reference: Simple LCOE basis — NREL System Advisor Model (SAM) methodology

payback_yr = CAPEX_usd / annual_saving_usd; specific_yield_kwh_kwp = E_yield_kwh / P_array_kwp   // [years, kWh/kWp/yr] Simple Payback Period & Specific Yield Reference: Specific yield benchmark: Mediterranean 1400–1700 kWh/kWp; UK 850–1000; Middle East 1700–2000

V1.error_msg: Performance ratio < 0.65: exceptionally low — verify loss inputs; shading, soiling, and temperature derating may be overstated

V2.error_msg: Specific yield < 700 kWh/kWp despite adequate PSH: large system losses — re-examine soiling, shading, or inverter sizing

V3.error_msg: Payback > 20 years: marginal project economics — re-evaluate CAPEX, tariff structure, and incentives (ITC, MACRS, FiT)

smart_warnings.message: Cell temperature > 60°C: severe thermal derating — evaluate bifacial modules with elevated racking, ventilated mounting, or high-NOCT-resistant technology
System losses > 25%: excessive — each 1% loss reduces yield by ~600 kWh/MWp/yr; audit soiling frequency, cable sizing, and inverter clipping
PR < 0.78: below IEC 61724 benchmark for well-designed systems — opportunity for O&M optimisation; cleaning and loss audit recommended
Large array at high CAPEX/Wp: utility-scale pricing should be below 0.85 USD/Wp — renegotiate EPC contract or review scope
Payback < 5 years: strong project economics — confirm energy model with P90 yield (10th percentile) for bankability assessment per IFC PS3

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: PR < 0.65
fmea.effect: Performance ratio < 0.65: exceptionally low — verify loss inputs; shading, soiling, and temperature derating may be overstated
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: specific_yield_kwh_kwp < 700 && PSH_h_day > 3
fmea.effect: Specific yield < 700 kWh/kWp despite adequate PSH: large system losses — re-examine soiling, shading, or inverter sizing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: payback_yr > 20
fmea.effect: Payback > 20 years: marginal project economics — re-evaluate CAPEX, tariff structure, and incentives (ITC, MACRS, FiT)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Cell temperature > 60°C: severe thermal derating — evaluate bifacial modules with elevated racking, ventilated mounting, or high-NOCT-resistant technology
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: avg_cell_temp_c > 60

fmea.failureMode: System losses > 25%: excessive — each 1% loss reduces yield by ~600 kWh/MWp/yr; audit soiling frequency, cable sizing, and inverter clipping
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: system_losses_pct > 25

fmea.failureMode: PR < 0.78: below IEC 61724 benchmark for well-designed systems — opportunity for O&M optimisation; cleaning and loss audit recommended
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: PR < 0.78

fmea.failureMode: Large array at high CAPEX/Wp: utility-scale pricing should be below 0.85 USD/Wp — renegotiate EPC contract or review scope
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_array_kwp > 1000 && installed_cost_usd_wp > 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Payback < 5 years: strong project economics — confirm energy model with P90 yield (10th percentile) for bankability assessment per IFC PS3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_yr < 5

tool_name: Shallow Foundation Bearing Capacity — Ultimate & Allowable Load per Terzaghi-Meyerhof

inputs.name: Soil Cohesion Intercept (c')
inputs.note: Undrained: c_u from vane shear or UU triaxial; drained: c' from CD/CU triaxial with pore pressure
inputs.uncertainty_source: Eurocode 7 (EN 1997) §2.4 — Geotechnical cohesion: CoV=0.4

inputs.name: Soil Friction Angle (φ')
inputs.note: Loose sand ≈ 28–32°; dense sand ≈ 35–42°; soft clay φ_u=0 (undrained)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Unit Weight of Soil (γ)
inputs.note: Saturated sand ≈ 19–21; clay ≈ 16–20; loose fill ≈ 14–17 kN/m³
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Foundation Embedment Depth (D_f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Foundation Width (B)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Foundation Length (L)
inputs.note: For strip footings: L >> B; enter L = 10×B; for square: L = B
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Depth to Groundwater Table (D_w)
inputs.note: GWT at or above D_f requires buoyant unit weight γ' = γ_sat − γ_w in bearing capacity
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Target Factor of Safety (FOS)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: phi_rad = phi_deg * Math.PI / 180; Nq = Math.exp(Math.PI * Math.tan(phi_rad)) * Math.pow(Math.tan(Math.PI/4 + phi_rad/2), 2); Nc = (phi_deg > 0) ? (Nq - 1) / Math.tan(phi_rad) : 5.14; Ng = 2 * (Nq + 1) * Math.tan(phi_rad)   // [dimensionless] Meyerhof Bearing Capacity Factors (N_c, N_q, N_γ) Reference: Meyerhof (1963) — N_q = e^(π·tanφ)·tan²(45+φ/2); N_c = (N_q−1)/tanφ; N_γ = 2(N_q+1)·tanφ

B_L = foundation_width_m / foundation_length_m; sc = 1 + B_L * Nq / Nc; sq = 1 + B_L * Math.tan(phi_rad); sg = Math.max(0.6, 1 - 0.4 * B_L)   // [dimensionless] Shape Factors (s_c, s_q, s_γ) Reference: Meyerhof shape factors — Bowles Foundation Analysis 5th Ed. §4.4

gamma_eff = (groundwater_depth_m <= foundation_depth_m) ? gamma_soil_kn_m3 - 9.81 : gamma_soil_kn_m3; q_overburden_kpa = gamma_soil_kn_m3 * foundation_depth_m   // [kPa, kN/m³] Effective Overburden Pressure & GWT Correction Reference: EC7 EN 1997-1 §6.5.3 — buoyant weight when GWT within foundation influence zone

q_ult_kpa = cohesion_kpa * Nc * sc + q_overburden_kpa * Nq * sq + 0.5 * gamma_eff * foundation_width_m * Ng * sg   // [kPa] Ultimate Bearing Capacity (q_ult) — General Shear Reference: Meyerhof general bearing capacity equation — Terzaghi (1943) base + Meyerhof (1963) shape factors

q_net_ult_kpa = q_ult_kpa - q_overburden_kpa; q_allowable_kpa = q_net_ult_kpa / FOS_target + q_overburden_kpa   // [kPa] Net Ultimate & Allowable Bearing Capacity Reference: q_allow = q_net_ult / FOS + γ·D_f — Bowles §4.5; net allowable excludes overburden contribution
P_allow_kn = q_allowable_kpa * foundation_width_m * foundation_length_m   // [kN] Maximum Allowable Column Load on Foundation Reference: Structural load capacity: P = q_allow × A_foundation

V1.error_msg: Both c and φ are zero: soil has no shear strength — check soil parameters; at minimum one parameter must be non-zero

V2.error_msg: GWT above foundation level but gamma_eff appears too high — verify buoyant unit weight γ' = γ_sat − 9.81 is applied correctly

V3.error_msg: Very high allowable pressure for moderate friction angle: verify soil parameters with in-situ testing (SPT/CPT) before finalising

smart_warnings.message: Foundation depth < 0.5 m: shallow embedment vulnerable to frost heave, erosion, and surface disturbance — minimum 0.6 m below frost line per ASCE 7
Very high friction angle (>40°): verify with laboratory tests — field estimates of φ > 40° are often optimistic; use conservative lower bound
GWT within influence zone (D_w < D_f + B): full buoyant correction required for γ in N_γ term — EC7 §6.5.3(4) applies
FOS < 2.5 for cohesionless soil: increased uncertainty in φ and soil variability — EC7 recommends partial factor approach (γ_φ = 1.25) as verification
Square/circular footing: shape factors significantly increase capacity vs strip — verify punching shear capacity of footing in structural design

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: phi_deg === 0 && cohesion_kpa === 0
fmea.effect: Both c and φ are zero: soil has no shear strength — check soil parameters; at minimum one parameter must be non-zero
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: groundwater_depth_m < foundation_depth_m && gamma_eff > 12
fmea.effect: GWT above foundation level but gamma_eff appears too high — verify buoyant unit weight γ' = γ_sat − 9.81 is applied correctly
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: q_allowable_kpa > 600 && phi_deg < 35
fmea.effect: Very high allowable pressure for moderate friction angle: verify soil parameters with in-situ testing (SPT/CPT) before finalising
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Foundation depth < 0.5 m: shallow embedment vulnerable to frost heave, erosion, and surface disturbance — minimum 0.6 m below frost line per ASCE 7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: foundation_depth_m < 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Very high friction angle (>40°): verify with laboratory tests — field estimates of φ > 40° are often optimistic; use conservative lower bound
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: phi_deg > 40 && cohesion_kpa === 0

fmea.failureMode: GWT within influence zone (D_w < D_f + B): full buoyant correction required for γ in N_γ term — EC7 §6.5.3(4) applies
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: groundwater_depth_m < foundation_depth_m + foundation_width_m

fmea.failureMode: FOS < 2.5 for cohesionless soil: increased uncertainty in φ and soil variability — EC7 recommends partial factor approach (γ_φ = 1.25) as verification
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: FOS_target < 2.5 && cohesion_kpa === 0

fmea.failureMode: Square/circular footing: shape factors significantly increase capacity vs strip — verify punching shear capacity of footing in structural design
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: foundation_width_m / foundation_length_m > 0.9

tool_name: Centrifugal Fan Selection — Specific Speed, Tip Speed, Blade Loading & Noise Rating

inputs.name: Design Volume Flow Rate (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Required Fan Total Pressure Rise (ΔP_fan)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Fan Rotational Speed (n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Fan Total Efficiency (η_fan) at Design Point
inputs.note: Centrifugal backward-curved: 70–85%; forward-curved: 55–70%; axial: 75–88%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Air / Gas Density at Fan Inlet (ρ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Impeller Outside Diameter (D_2)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Blade Profile Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: omega_rad_s = n_fan_rpm * 2 * Math.PI / 60; Ns_dim = omega_rad_s * Math.sqrt(Q_m3s) / Math.pow(dP_fan_pa / rho_air_kg_m3, 0.75)   // [dimensionless] Specific Speed (N_s) — Dimensionless Reference: ISO 13349:2010 §3.3 — N_s = ω·√Q / (ΔP/ρ)^0.75; backward-curved optimal Ns: 0.3–1.0
u2_m_s = Math.PI * impeller_dia_mm / 1000 * n_fan_rpm / 60   // [m/s] Impeller Tip Speed (u_2) Reference: u_2 = π·D_2·n/60 — ISO 13349 §3.7; backward-curved limit ≈ 150 m/s; forward-curved ≈ 60 m/s
psi = dP_fan_pa / (rho_air_kg_m3 * Math.pow(u2_m_s, 2))   // [dimensionless] Euler Head Coefficient (ψ) Reference: AMCA 210-16 — pressure coefficient ψ = ΔP/(ρ·u₂²); BC fans: ψ ≈ 0.4–0.7
P_shaft_kw = Q_m3s * dP_fan_pa / (fan_efficiency_pct / 100) / 1000   // [kW] Shaft Power Input Reference: P_shaft = Q·ΔP / η_fan — EN ISO 5801:2017 §8.2

N_blades_est = (blade_type === 'backward') ? 9 : (blade_type === 'forward') ? 36 : 6; BPF_hz = N_blades_est * n_fan_rpm / 60   // [Hz] Blade Passage Frequency (BPF) — Noise Tone Reference: BPF = Z_b × n/60 — AMCA 300 noise standard; BPF tone audibility peak

Kw_dB = 10 * Math.log10(Q_m3s * Math.pow(dP_fan_pa, 2)); Lw_estimated_dB = Kw_dB + ((blade_type === 'backward') ? 55 : (blade_type === 'forward') ? 63 : 60)   // [dB(A)] Specific Sound Power Level (K_w) — AMCA Estimate Reference: AMCA 301-14 — estimated fan sound power; BC fans quietest, FC fans noisiest; ±5 dB accuracy

V1.error_msg: Tip speed > 150 m/s for BC fan: stress limit exceeded for standard steel impeller — reduce speed or use high-tensile alloy; verify per ISO 13349 §6.4

V2.error_msg: FC fan tip speed > 60 m/s: blade bending stress critical — forward-curved blades have low structural efficiency; FC fans unsuitable above 60 m/s

V3.error_msg: Pressure coefficient ψ > 0.9: overloaded impeller — fan operating far from design point; surge and aerodynamic instability risk

smart_warnings.message: Specific speed outside centrifugal fan optimal range (0.2–2.5): consider axial fan (Ns > 2.5) or different impeller type — efficiency will be poor
Forward-curved fan at high pressure: FC fans have overloading characteristic — motor may trip; backward-curved or radial tip preferred above 1500 Pa
Estimated sound power > 95 dB(A): high-noise installation — acoustic treatment, silencer, or vibration isolation required; OSHA noise assessment mandatory
Fan efficiency < 60%: poor energy performance — consider replacing with backward-curved or airfoil blade design; each 10% η improvement reduces motor kW proportionally
Blade passage frequency > 500 Hz: mid-frequency tonal noise — structural resonance check of ductwork at BPF mandatory; add flexible connection at fan outlet

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: u2_m_s > 150 && blade_type === 'backward'
fmea.effect: Tip speed > 150 m/s for BC fan: stress limit exceeded for standard steel impeller — reduce speed or use high-tensile alloy; verify per ISO 13349 §6.4
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: u2_m_s > 60 && blade_type === 'forward'
fmea.effect: FC fan tip speed > 60 m/s: blade bending stress critical — forward-curved blades have low structural efficiency; FC fans unsuitable above 60 m/s
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: psi > 0.9
fmea.effect: Pressure coefficient ψ > 0.9: overloaded impeller — fan operating far from design point; surge and aerodynamic instability risk
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Specific speed outside centrifugal fan optimal range (0.2–2.5): consider axial fan (Ns > 2.5) or different impeller type — efficiency will be poor
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ns_dim < 0.2 || Ns_dim > 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Forward-curved fan at high pressure: FC fans have overloading characteristic — motor may trip; backward-curved or radial tip preferred above 1500 Pa
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: blade_type === 'forward' && dP_fan_pa > 1500

fmea.failureMode: Estimated sound power > 95 dB(A): high-noise installation — acoustic treatment, silencer, or vibration isolation required; OSHA noise assessment mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lw_estimated_dB > 95

fmea.failureMode: Fan efficiency < 60%: poor energy performance — consider replacing with backward-curved or airfoil blade design; each 10% η improvement reduces motor kW proportionally
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fan_efficiency_pct < 60

fmea.failureMode: Blade passage frequency > 500 Hz: mid-frequency tonal noise — structural resonance check of ductwork at BPF mandatory; add flexible connection at fan outlet
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BPF_hz > 500

tool_name: Elevated Storage Tank — Shell Hoop Stress, Hydrostatic Pressure & Wind Overturning Stability

inputs.name: Tank Internal Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Maximum Water Depth (H_w)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Shell Plate Thickness (t)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Shell Steel Yield Strength (F_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tank Bottom Elevation Above Ground (H_support)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Design Wind Speed (V_design)
inputs.note: 3-second gust speed at 10 m height for site — ASCE 7-22 Fig. 26.5-1A or national wind map
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tank Steel Shell Empty Weight (W_shell)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Weld Joint Efficiency (E)
inputs.note: API 650 §5.2 / AWWA D100 — joint efficiency by radiographic inspection class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: p_h_kpa = 9.81 * water_height_m   // [kPa] Maximum Hydrostatic Pressure at Tank Base (p_h) Reference: p = ρ_w·g·H_w; ρ_w = 1000 kg/m³ — hydrostatic pressure at maximum fill

sigma_hoop_mpa = p_h_kpa * (tank_diameter_m * 1000 / 2) / (shell_thickness_mm * 1000)   // [MPa] Hoop (Circumferential) Tensile Stress in Shell (σ_hoop) Reference: Barlow / thin-shell hoop stress: σ_H = p·D/(2t) — AWWA D100 §14.2 / API 650 §5.6

sigma_allow_mpa = joint_efficiency_E * Fy_mpa / 1.5; hoop_DCR = sigma_hoop_mpa / sigma_allow_mpa   // [MPa, dimensionless] Allowable Hoop Stress & Utilisation Ratio Reference: AWWA D100 §14.2 — allowable = E × F_y / 1.5 (FOS=1.5 on yield)

Cd_tank = 0.6; A_proj_m2 = tank_diameter_m * water_height_m; q_wind_pa = 0.5 * 1.25 * Math.pow(design_wind_speed_m_s, 2); F_wind_kn = Cd_tank * q_wind_pa * A_proj_m2 / 1000; H_centroid_m = support_height_m + water_height_m / 2; M_OT_knm = F_wind_kn * H_centroid_m   // [kN, kN·m] Wind Base Shear & Overturning Moment Reference: ASCE 7-22 §29.4 — wind force on cylindrical vessel; C_d=0.6 for circular cross-section; ρ_air=1.25 kg/m³

W_water_kn = 9.81 * (Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(tank_diameter_m, 2) * water_height_m * 1000) / 1000; W_total_kn = tank_empty_weight_kn + W_water_kn; M_restore_knm = W_total_kn * tank_diameter_m / 2   // [kN·m, kN] Restoring Moment (Gravity) — Empty Tank Reference: Restoring moment = W_total × D/2 (about tank base edge) — ASCE 7-22 §C15.4
OTF = M_restore_knm / M_OT_knm   // [dimensionless] Overturning Safety Factor (OTF) Reference: AWWA D100 §15.3 / ASCE 7-22 — OTF ≥ 1.5 for tanks; ≥ 2.0 for seismic-critical zones

V1.error_msg: Hoop stress exceeds allowable: shell wall thickness insufficient — increase shell thickness or reduce water depth per AWWA D100 §14.2

V2.error_msg: Overturning safety factor < 1.5: wind stability not met — anchor tank to foundation or increase base diameter; evaluate seismic per ASCE 7-22 §15.4

V3.error_msg: D/t ratio > 300: thin shell — check buckling under wind external pressure per API 650 Annex V and EN 1993-4-2 §5.3

smart_warnings.message: Hoop utilisation > 85%: marginal shell capacity — verify corrosion allowance is maintained over design life; 1 mm corrosion reduces capacity by ~5% on 20 mm plate
Elevated tank with marginal OTF: tall structures amplify wind overturning — detailed dynamic analysis and anchor bolt design required per ASCE 7-22 §15.7
Design wind speed > 60 m/s: hurricane / typhoon zone — enhanced wind analysis per ASCE 7-22 §26.11 and wind tunnel study recommended for D > 20 m
Non-radiographed welds on deep tank: upgrade to Class 2 (spot RT) minimum — AWWA D100 requires RT for shells above 5 m water depth in Class B service
Support height > 20 m: structural analysis of support columns/pedestal under combined gravity, wind, and seismic mandatory per ASCE 7-22 §15.4.2

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: hoop_DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Hoop stress exceeds allowable: shell wall thickness insufficient — increase shell thickness or reduce water depth per AWWA D100 §14.2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: OTF < 1.5
fmea.effect: Overturning safety factor < 1.5: wind stability not met — anchor tank to foundation or increase base diameter; evaluate seismic per ASCE 7-22 §15.4
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: tank_diameter_m / shell_thickness_mm * 1000 > 300
fmea.effect: D/t ratio > 300: thin shell — check buckling under wind external pressure per API 650 Annex V and EN 1993-4-2 §5.3
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Hoop utilisation > 85%: marginal shell capacity — verify corrosion allowance is maintained over design life; 1 mm corrosion reduces capacity by ~5% on 20 mm plate
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: hoop_DCR > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Elevated tank with marginal OTF: tall structures amplify wind overturning — detailed dynamic analysis and anchor bolt design required per ASCE 7-22 §15.7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OTF < 2.0 && support_height_m > 15

fmea.failureMode: Design wind speed > 60 m/s: hurricane / typhoon zone — enhanced wind analysis per ASCE 7-22 §26.11 and wind tunnel study recommended for D > 20 m
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_wind_speed_m_s > 60

fmea.failureMode: Non-radiographed welds on deep tank: upgrade to Class 2 (spot RT) minimum — AWWA D100 requires RT for shells above 5 m water depth in Class B service
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: joint_efficiency_E < 0.85 && water_height_m > 10

fmea.failureMode: Support height > 20 m: structural analysis of support columns/pedestal under combined gravity, wind, and seismic mandatory per ASCE 7-22 §15.4.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: support_height_m > 20

tool_name: Hydraulic Cylinder Actuator Sizing — Bore, Rod, Force, Speed & Buckling Check

inputs.name: Required Extension Force (F_ext)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Required Retraction Force (F_ret)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: System Operating Pressure (P_sys)
inputs.note: Industrial hydraulic: 150–350 bar; mobile: 250–450 bar; high-pressure: up to 700 bar
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Back Pressure on Return Side (P_back)
inputs.note: Tank line back pressure; typical 3–10 bar; higher with counterbalance valves
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Full Stroke Length (L_stroke)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Required Extension Speed (v_ext)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Cylinder Mounting / Buckling Condition (Euler case)
inputs.note: Most hydraulic cylinders: pin-pin (k=1.0) or fixed-pin (k=0.7) per ISO 6020 mounting
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Piston Rod Material Yield Strength (S_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: P_net_bar = system_pressure_bar - back_pressure_bar; A_bore_cm2 = extend_force_kn * 10 / P_net_bar; D_bore_mm = 2 * Math.sqrt(A_bore_cm2 / Math.PI) * 10   // [mm] Required Bore Diameter — Extension (D_bore) Reference: F = P·A; A = F/P; D = 2√(A/π) — ISO 6020-2 bore sizing from force and pressure

A_bore_m2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(D_bore_mm/1000, 2); A_ann_req_m2 = retract_force_kn * 1000 / ((system_pressure_bar - back_pressure_bar) * 1e5); A_rod_m2 = A_bore_m2 - A_ann_req_m2; D_rod_mm = 2 * Math.sqrt(Math.max(A_rod_m2, 0.001) / Math.PI) * 1000   // [mm] Required Rod Diameter — Retraction Check Reference: Annular area = bore area − rod area; A_ann = F_ret / (P_sys − P_back)

A_bore_m2_act = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(D_bore_mm/1000, 2); Q_ext_lpm = A_bore_m2_act * extend_speed_mm_s / 1000 * 1000 * 60   // [L/min] Flow Rate Required for Extension Speed (Q_ext) Reference: Q = A × v — ISO 6022 flow rate for cylinder actuation

E_steel = 210000; I_rod = Math.PI/64 * Math.pow(D_rod_mm, 4); L_eff_mm = mounting_condition * stroke_mm; F_cr_kn = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E_steel * I_rod / Math.pow(L_eff_mm, 2) / 1e6   // [kN] Buckling Load — Euler Critical Force (F_cr) Reference: Euler buckling: F_cr = π²EI/(kL)²; E=210 GPa; I=πd⁴/64 — ISO 6020-2 Annex B
SF_buck = F_cr_kn / extend_force_kn   // [dimensionless] Buckling Safety Factor (SF_buck) Reference: ISO 6020-2 Annex B — minimum SF_buck ≥ 3.0 for static; ≥ 5.0 for dynamic/shock loading

A_rod_act_mm2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(D_rod_mm, 2); sigma_rod_mpa = extend_force_kn * 1000 / A_rod_act_mm2; rod_util = sigma_rod_mpa / (rod_material_yield_mpa / 2)   // [MPa, dimensionless] Rod Compressive Stress Check Reference: Rod stress σ = F/A; allowable = S_y/2 (FOS=2 on yield per NFPA T3.6.7)

V1.error_msg: Buckling safety factor < 3.0: rod buckling risk — increase rod diameter, reduce effective length, or change mounting to fixed-pinned (k=0.7)

V2.error_msg: Rod compressive stress exceeds allowable (S_y/2): rod failure risk — increase rod diameter or upgrade material grade

V3.error_msg: Rod diameter > 70% of bore: very small annular area — retraction force may be inadequate; consider differential circuit or tandem cylinder

smart_warnings.message: Low buckling SF at high speed: dynamic loads from deceleration shock amplify column force — target SF ≥ 5 for speeds > 200 mm/s per Parker handbook §4
Flow demand > 200 L/min: verify pump capacity and line sizing — pressure drop in supply line will reduce effective force; use ΔP = 0.003 L/D·v²·ρ for line check
System pressure > 350 bar: high-pressure category — NFPA T3.6.7 Class 5000 psi seals and fittings required; hydraulic shock transients may exceed 500 bar
Long stroke with pin-pin mounting: effective buckling length equals full stroke — consider intermediate rod support or reduce to fixed-pin mounting
Back pressure > 20 bar: high counterbalance valve setting — reduces net extension force significantly; verify thermal relief valve setting is ≥ P_back + 20% per ISO 6022

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SF_buck < 3.0
fmea.effect: Buckling safety factor < 3.0: rod buckling risk — increase rod diameter, reduce effective length, or change mounting to fixed-pinned (k=0.7)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rod_util > 1.0
fmea.effect: Rod compressive stress exceeds allowable (S_y/2): rod failure risk — increase rod diameter or upgrade material grade
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: D_rod_mm > D_bore_mm * 0.7
fmea.effect: Rod diameter > 70% of bore: very small annular area — retraction force may be inadequate; consider differential circuit or tandem cylinder
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Low buckling SF at high speed: dynamic loads from deceleration shock amplify column force — target SF ≥ 5 for speeds > 200 mm/s per Parker handbook §4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_buck < 5 && extend_speed_mm_s > 200

fmea.failureMode: Flow demand > 200 L/min: verify pump capacity and line sizing — pressure drop in supply line will reduce effective force; use ΔP = 0.003 L/D·v²·ρ for line check
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_ext_lpm > 200

fmea.failureMode: System pressure > 350 bar: high-pressure category — NFPA T3.6.7 Class 5000 psi seals and fittings required; hydraulic shock transients may exceed 500 bar
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: system_pressure_bar > 350

fmea.failureMode: Long stroke with pin-pin mounting: effective buckling length equals full stroke — consider intermediate rod support or reduce to fixed-pin mounting
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stroke_mm > 3000 && mounting_condition >= 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Back pressure > 20 bar: high counterbalance valve setting — reduces net extension force significantly; verify thermal relief valve setting is ≥ P_back + 20% per ISO 6022
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: back_pressure_bar > 20

tool_name: Power Cable Ampacity, Derating & Voltage Drop Sizing — LV / MV Distribution

inputs.name: Design Load Current (I_L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Proposed Cable Conductor Cross-Section (A_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Cable Base Ampacity at Reference Conditions (I_z0)
inputs.note: From IEC 60364-5-52 Tables B.52.1–B.52.12 for installation method; e.g. 95mm² XLPE 3-core direct buried: I_z0 = 225 A
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Ambient / Ground Temperature at Installation (T_amb)
inputs.note: IEC 60364-5-52 reference: 30°C air, 20°C ground

inputs.name: Maximum Conductor Operating Temperature (T_max)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Cable Grouping Derating Factor (C_g)
inputs.note: IEC 60364-5-52 Table B.52.17 — grouping factors for multicore cables
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: One-Way Cable Run Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: System Voltage (U_n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: T_ref = (max_conductor_temp_c === 70) ? 30 : 30; Ct = Math.sqrt((max_conductor_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / (max_conductor_temp_c - T_ref))   // [dimensionless] Temperature Derating Factor (C_t) Reference: IEC 60364-5-52 §523.4 — C_t = √[(T_max − T_amb)/(T_max − T_ref)]; T_ref = 30°C (air) or 20°C (ground)
Iz_a = base_ampacity_a * Ct * grouping_factor   // [A] Derated Ampacity (I_z) Reference: IEC 60364-5-52 §523 — I_z = I_z0 × C_t × C_g × C_soil; C_soil = 1.0 assumed
amp_util = load_current_a / Iz_a   // [dimensionless] Ampacity Utilisation Ratio Reference: I_L / I_z ≤ 1.0 required per IEC 60364-5-52 §523.1

rho_20 = (cable_mm2 <= 630) ? 0.0172 : 0.0172; R_dc_ohm_km = rho_20 * 1000 / cable_mm2 * (1 + 0.00393 * (max_conductor_temp_c - 20))   // [Ω/km] DC Resistance at Operating Temperature (R_dc) Reference: IEC 60228:2004 — ρ_Cu = 0.0172 Ω·mm²/m at 20°C; α_Cu = 0.00393/°C

phases = (supply_voltage_v < 400) ? 1 : 3; pf = 0.85; sin_phi = Math.sqrt(1 - pf*pf); X_ohm_km = 0.08; R_total = R_dc_ohm_km * cable_length_m / 1000; X_total = X_ohm_km * cable_length_m / 1000; dU_v = (phases === 3) ? Math.sqrt(3) * load_current_a * (R_total * pf + X_total * sin_phi) : 2 * load_current_a * (R_total * pf + X_total * sin_phi)   // [V] Voltage Drop — Three-Phase (ΔU) Reference: IEC 60364-5-52 Annex G / BS 7671 App.12 — ΔU = √3·I·(R·cosφ + X·sinφ)·L for 3-phase; pf=0.85, X=0.08 Ω/km typical
dU_pct = dU_v / supply_voltage_v * 100   // [%] Voltage Drop Percentage Reference: IEC 60364-5-52 / NEC §210.19 — max 3% for branch circuits; 5% total (source to load) per IEC

V1.error_msg: Load current exceeds derated ampacity: cable will overheat — upsize conductor cross-section or reduce grouping density

V2.error_msg: Voltage drop > 5%: exceeds IEC 60364 recommendation — upsize cable or reduce run length; motor starting and equipment performance affected

V3.error_msg: Heavy grouping derating at high utilisation: thermal accumulation risk in cable bundle — verify bundle temperature with IEEE 835 or IEC 60287 detailed calculation

smart_warnings.message: Voltage drop > 3% on LV system: motor terminal voltage will drop below 95% of rated — IEC 60034-1 requires ±5% supply voltage tolerance; upsize cable
Ampacity utilisation > 85%: limited headroom for future load growth or harmonic derating — consider upsize to next standard cross-section
PVC cable in high-ambient environment: C_t severely derated above 40°C — upgrade to XLPE (90°C) insulation for 40–60°C installations
Long 400 V run (>500 m): voltage drop likely dominates over ampacity — size cable for ΔU ≤ 3%, not ampacity; significant upsize typically required
Large cables in groups: skin and proximity effect add to AC resistance — verify with IEC 60287-1-1 AC resistance correction for cables > 150 mm²

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: amp_util > 1.0
fmea.effect: Load current exceeds derated ampacity: cable will overheat — upsize conductor cross-section or reduce grouping density
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dU_pct > 5
fmea.effect: Voltage drop > 5%: exceeds IEC 60364 recommendation — upsize cable or reduce run length; motor starting and equipment performance affected
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: grouping_factor < 0.6 && amp_util > 0.8
fmea.effect: Heavy grouping derating at high utilisation: thermal accumulation risk in cable bundle — verify bundle temperature with IEEE 835 or IEC 60287 detailed calculation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Voltage drop > 3% on LV system: motor terminal voltage will drop below 95% of rated — IEC 60034-1 requires ±5% supply voltage tolerance; upsize cable
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dU_pct > 3 && supply_voltage_v === 400

fmea.failureMode: Ampacity utilisation > 85%: limited headroom for future load growth or harmonic derating — consider upsize to next standard cross-section
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: amp_util > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: PVC cable in high-ambient environment: C_t severely derated above 40°C — upgrade to XLPE (90°C) insulation for 40–60°C installations
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c > 40 && max_conductor_temp_c === 70

fmea.failureMode: Long 400 V run (>500 m): voltage drop likely dominates over ampacity — size cable for ΔU ≤ 3%, not ampacity; significant upsize typically required
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cable_length_m > 500 && supply_voltage_v === 400

fmea.failureMode: Large cables in groups: skin and proximity effect add to AC resistance — verify with IEC 60287-1-1 AC resistance correction for cables > 150 mm²
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cable_mm2 >= 300 && grouping_factor < 0.70

tool_name: EOQ, Reorder Point & Safety Stock Optimisation with Demand & Lead Time Variability

inputs.name: Annual Demand (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Cost per Purchase Order (S)
inputs.note: Include: admin time, receiving, inspection, system processing per order event
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Unit Purchase Cost (C)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Inventory Holding Cost Rate (h)
inputs.note: Typical 20–30%: capital cost of inventory + warehouse + obsolescence + insurance
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Supplier Lead Time — Average (L̄)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Lead Time Standard Deviation (σ_L)
inputs.note: Measure from historical PO data; σ_L = 0 for fixed lead time
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Daily Demand Standard Deviation (σ_d)
inputs.note: σ_d from historical demand data; σ_d = 0 for deterministic demand
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Target Service Level (cycle service level)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: H_usd = unit_cost_usd * holding_rate_pct / 100; EOQ = Math.sqrt((2 * annual_demand_units * ordering_cost_usd) / H_usd)   // [units] Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) Reference: Wilson (1913) EOQ = √(2DS/H); H = h·C annual holding cost per unit

T_cycle_days = EOQ / (annual_demand_units / 365); orders_per_yr = annual_demand_units / EOQ   // [days, orders/yr] Average Cycle Time & Order Frequency Reference: T = EOQ/d̄; number of orders = D/EOQ — APICS CPIM Dictionary

sl_map = {90: 1.282, 95: 1.645, 97.5: 1.960, 99: 2.326, 99.9: 3.090}; z = sl_map[service_level_pct]   // [dimensionless] Safety Factor (z) from Service Level Reference: Standard normal z-value for cycle service level — Silver, Pyke & Thomas §6.3

d_avg_day = annual_demand_units / 365; sigma_dLT = Math.sqrt(lead_time_days * Math.pow(demand_std_day, 2) + Math.pow(d_avg_day, 2) * Math.pow(lead_time_std_days, 2)); SS = z * sigma_dLT   // [units] Safety Stock (SS) — Combined Demand & Lead Time Variability Reference: Hadley-Within formula: σ_DLT = √(L̄·σ_d² + d̄²·σ_L²) — combines demand and lead time uncertainty
d_avg_day = annual_demand_units / 365; ROP = d_avg_day * lead_time_days + SS   // [units] Reorder Point (ROP) Reference: ROP = d̄·L̄ + z·σ_DLT — trigger order when on-hand inventory reaches ROP

H_usd = unit_cost_usd * holding_rate_pct / 100; TAC_usd = (EOQ/2 + SS) * H_usd + (annual_demand_units / EOQ) * ordering_cost_usd   // [USD/yr] Total Annual Inventory Cost (TAC) Reference: TAC = (Q/2 + SS)·H + (D/Q)·S — total holding + ordering cost excluding purchase cost

V1.error_msg: Daily demand CV > 50%: extremely high variability — safety stock will be very large; consider demand smoothing or VMI arrangement with supplier

V2.error_msg: Lead time CV > 30%: unreliable supplier — safety stock dominated by supply uncertainty; negotiate lead time reliability or qualify second source

V3.error_msg: Safety stock > 2× EOQ: carrying cost heavily influenced by uncertainty — re-evaluate service level target or work to reduce lead time variability

smart_warnings.message: More than 52 orders per year: weekly ordering frequency — evaluate blanket PO or kanban system to reduce transaction costs
Cycle time > 90 days: large batch order — obsolescence and damage risk increases with long holding periods; verify shelf life vs cycle time
High service level (≥99%) with variable demand: safety stock cost increases exponentially above 97.5% — verify cost of stockout justifies the holding cost premium
High-value item with large EOQ: capital tied up in inventory is significant — consider just-in-time or consignment stock arrangement to reduce order quantity
Holding rate < 15%: may understate true cost — verify capital cost of inventory (WACC), storage cost, and obsolescence risk are fully included

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: demand_std_day > annual_demand_units / 365 * 0.5
fmea.effect: Daily demand CV > 50%: extremely high variability — safety stock will be very large; consider demand smoothing or VMI arrangement with supplier
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lead_time_std_days > lead_time_days * 0.3
fmea.effect: Lead time CV > 30%: unreliable supplier — safety stock dominated by supply uncertainty; negotiate lead time reliability or qualify second source
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SS > EOQ * 2
fmea.effect: Safety stock > 2× EOQ: carrying cost heavily influenced by uncertainty — re-evaluate service level target or work to reduce lead time variability
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: More than 52 orders per year: weekly ordering frequency — evaluate blanket PO or kanban system to reduce transaction costs
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: orders_per_yr > 52

fmea.failureMode: Cycle time > 90 days: large batch order — obsolescence and damage risk increases with long holding periods; verify shelf life vs cycle time
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_cycle_days > 90

fmea.failureMode: High service level (≥99%) with variable demand: safety stock cost increases exponentially above 97.5% — verify cost of stockout justifies the holding cost premium
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: service_level_pct >= 99 && demand_std_day > 0

fmea.failureMode: High-value item with large EOQ: capital tied up in inventory is significant — consider just-in-time or consignment stock arrangement to reduce order quantity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: unit_cost_usd > 1000 && EOQ > 100

fmea.failureMode: Holding rate < 15%: may understate true cost — verify capital cost of inventory (WACC), storage cost, and obsolescence risk are fully included
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: holding_rate_pct < 15

tool_name: Pipe & Vessel Thermal Insulation Thickness — Economic & Process Optimisation

inputs.name: Pipe / Vessel Outside Diameter (D_o)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Proposed Insulation Thickness (t_ins)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Insulation Thermal Conductivity (λ_ins)
inputs.note: Mineral wool at 100°C ≈ 0.045; PIR foam ≈ 0.025; calcium silicate ≈ 0.065 W/(m·K)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASTM D5334-22 §9 — Thermal conductivity: ±10% (guarded hot plate)

inputs.name: Outer Surface Heat Transfer Coefficient (h_o)
inputs.note: Natural convection + radiation still air ≈ 8–12 W/(m²·K); wind-swept outdoor ≈ 20–30
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Energy Cost (Heat / Fuel)
inputs.note: Natural gas ≈ 5–10 USD/GJ; steam ≈ 15–25 USD/GJ; electricity ≈ 25–40 USD/GJ
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: r1_m = pipe_OD_mm / 2000; r2_m = r1_m + insulation_thickness_mm / 1000; delta_T = Math.abs(T_process_c - T_ambient_c)   // [m, m, K] Insulation Outer Radius & Geometry Reference: Radial geometry — ISO 12241:1998 §5.2

R_ins = Math.log(r2_m / r1_m) / (2 * Math.PI * lambda_ins_w_mk); R_conv = 1 / (2 * Math.PI * r2_m * h_outer_w_m2k); R_total = R_ins + R_conv   // [m·K/W] Thermal Resistance per Unit Length (R_total) Reference: ISO 12241 §5.2 — cylindrical: R_ins = ln(r2/r1)/(2πλ); R_conv = 1/(2πr2·h_o)
q_L_w_m = delta_T / R_total   // [W/m] Heat Loss per Unit Length (q_L) Reference: Fourier's law for cylindrical geometry — ASTM C680 §5 / EN ISO 12241

r_cr_m = lambda_ins_w_mk / h_outer_w_m2k   // [m] Critical Insulation Radius (r_cr) — Minimum Heat Loss Reference: r_cr = λ/h_o — below this radius, adding insulation INCREASES heat loss; applies to small pipes

E_loss_gj_yr = q_L_w_m * operating_hours_yr * 3600 / 1e9; cost_loss_usd_yr = E_loss_gj_yr * energy_cost_usd_gj   // [GJ/m·yr, USD/m·yr] Annual Heat Loss Energy & Cost Reference: Annual energy loss per metre of pipe — CINI Manual §3.4 cost basis

T_surface_c = T_ambient_c + q_L_w_m / (2 * Math.PI * r2_m * h_outer_w_m2k)   // [°C] Outer Surface Temperature (Anti-Condensation / Touch Safety Check) Reference: Surface temp for personnel protection: < 60°C per EN 13202; condensation prevention: T_surf > T_dew

V1.error_msg: Insulated radius < critical radius: adding insulation increases heat loss on this small pipe — minimum thickness is r_cr − r1; verify application intent

V2.error_msg: Cold service: surface temperature near ambient — condensation and ice formation risk; increase thickness to maintain T_surface > T_dew point

V3.error_msg: Surface temperature > 60°C: personnel burn risk — EN 13202 requires T_surface ≤ 60°C for accessible surfaces; increase insulation thickness

smart_warnings.message: Surface temperature 50–60°C: borderline personnel protection threshold — increase thickness by 25 mm or add protective cladding/barrier per EN 13202 §5
High thermal conductivity insulation on high-ΔT service: consider aerogel blanket (λ ≈ 0.018) or calcium silicate for service above 200°C differential
High annual heat loss cost: evaluate economic insulation thickness — each additional 25 mm typically reduces heat loss by 30–50% on medium pipes
Very thin insulation on high-ΔT service: thermal efficiency poor — minimum 50 mm recommended for ΔT > 150 K and pipe OD > 50 mm
Cold insulation (cryogenic / chilled) service: vapour barrier on warm side mandatory — moisture ingress destroys cold insulation performance within months

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: r2_m < r_cr_m && pipe_OD_mm < 50
fmea.effect: Insulated radius < critical radius: adding insulation increases heat loss on this small pipe — minimum thickness is r_cr − r1; verify application intent
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_process_c < T_ambient_c && T_surface_c > T_ambient_c - 5
fmea.effect: Cold service: surface temperature near ambient — condensation and ice formation risk; increase thickness to maintain T_surface > T_dew point
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_surface_c > 60 && T_process_c > 100
fmea.effect: Surface temperature > 60°C: personnel burn risk — EN 13202 requires T_surface ≤ 60°C for accessible surfaces; increase insulation thickness
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Surface temperature 50–60°C: borderline personnel protection threshold — increase thickness by 25 mm or add protective cladding/barrier per EN 13202 §5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_surface_c > 50 && T_process_c > 80

fmea.failureMode: High thermal conductivity insulation on high-ΔT service: consider aerogel blanket (λ ≈ 0.018) or calcium silicate for service above 200°C differential
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lambda_ins_w_mk > 0.08 && delta_T > 200

fmea.failureMode: High annual heat loss cost: evaluate economic insulation thickness — each additional 25 mm typically reduces heat loss by 30–50% on medium pipes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_loss_gj_yr > 5 && energy_cost_usd_gj > 10

fmea.failureMode: Very thin insulation on high-ΔT service: thermal efficiency poor — minimum 50 mm recommended for ΔT > 150 K and pipe OD > 50 mm
