fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: insulation_thickness_mm < 25 && delta_T > 150

fmea.failureMode: Cold insulation (cryogenic / chilled) service: vapour barrier on warm side mandatory — moisture ingress destroys cold insulation performance within months
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_process_c < 0

tool_name: Flare Stack Thermal Radiation — Exclusion Zone, Flame Length & Stack Height

inputs.name: Peak Relief / Flare Flow Rate (ṁ_f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5167-1:2022 §6 — Orifice plate: ±2% (Re>10⁴)

inputs.name: Flare Gas Lower Heating Value (LHV)
inputs.note: Natural gas ≈ 47500; H₂ ≈ 120000; propane ≈ 46000; refinery gas ≈ 40000 kJ/kg
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Flare Combustion Efficiency (η_c)
inputs.note: USEPA AP-42 §13.5: well-designed elevated flare ≥ 98%; ground flare ≥ 99%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Fraction of Heat Radiated (F)
inputs.note: API 521 §5.15.3: F=0.2 typical for natural gas; F=0.3 for flares with smoking
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Allowable Incident Radiation at Exclusion Zone Boundary
inputs.note: API 521-2020 Table 5 — radiation limits by exposure scenario
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Proposed Flare Stack Height (H_stack)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Design Wind Speed at Flare Tip (u_w)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

formulas: Q_f_kw = relief_flow_kg_h / 3600 * fuel_LHV_kj_kg * combustion_efficiency_pct / 100   // [kW] Total Heat Release Rate (Q_f) Reference: API 521-2020 §5.15.2 — Q_f = ṁ_f × LHV × η_c / 3600
Q_rad_kw = fraction_heat_radiated_F * Q_f_kw   // [kW] Radiated Heat Power (Q_rad) Reference: API 521 §5.15.3 — Q_rad = F × Q_f; F from flare gas composition and smokiness
L_f_m = 0.00325 * Math.pow(Q_f_kw * 0.9478, 0.478)   // [m] Flame Length (L_f) — Brzustowski & Sommer Reference: API 521 §5.15.4.1 — Brzustowski & Sommer correlation; flame length from heat release

U_j_m_s = 20; Mach_j = wind_speed_m_s / U_j_m_s; x_h_m = L_f_m * Math.sin(Math.atan(Mach_j * 5)); x_v_m = L_f_m * Math.cos(Math.atan(Mach_j * 5)); flame_tip_H_m = stack_height_m + x_v_m; flame_tip_X_m = x_h_m   // [m] Flame Tip Location (Horizontal & Vertical Deflection by Wind) Reference: API 521 §5.15.4.2 — wind-deflected flame centre approximation; jet velocity U_j assumed 20 m/s

D_excl_m = Math.sqrt(Q_rad_kw / (4 * Math.PI * allowable_radiation_kw_m2))   // [m] Radiation Exclusion Zone Radius (D_excl) Reference: API 521 §5.15.3 — point source model: E = Q_rad / (4πd²); d = √(Q_rad / 4πE_allow)

D_from_base = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(D_excl_m, 2) - Math.pow(flame_tip_X_m, 2)); H_min_m = Math.max(D_from_base - flame_tip_H_m + stack_height_m, stack_height_m)   // [m] Minimum Required Stack Height for Exclusion Zone Reference: Stack height must ensure 3D distance from flame centre to ground-level personnel ≥ D_excl — API 521 §5.15.3

standards: SFPE Handbook of Fire Protection Engineering 5th Ed.

V1.error_msg: Proposed stack height below minimum required: exclusion zone at ground level is violated — increase stack height to H_min or relocate facilities beyond D_excl

V2.error_msg: Low combustion efficiency at high flow: significant unburned hydrocarbon and black smoke — verify flare tip design and steam/air assist per API 537 §5.4

V3.error_msg: F > 0.30: heavy luminous flame (smoking) — smoking flare radiates more heat; address air/steam assist or switch to enclosed flare to reduce F below 0.20

smart_warnings.message: Exclusion zone > 500 m: major hazard area — review relief system design, consider staged blowdown or enclosed ground flare to reduce peak flow
Flame power > 100 MW: large flare event — noise assessment mandatory; stack structural loading under wind and thermal cycling must be verified per API 560 §7
High wind deflection: flame centre shifts significantly downwind — re-calculate exclusion zone at wind direction perpendicular to access road
Personnel exclusion zone > 200 m: verify no permanent occupied facilities within zone; update HAZOP and site emergency response plan
Combustion efficiency < 98%: regulatory compliance risk — US 40 CFR Part 60 Subpart Ja requires ≥ 96.5% DRE; EU IED requires BAT-AEL assessment

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: stack_height_m < H_min_m
fmea.effect: Proposed stack height below minimum required: exclusion zone at ground level is violated — increase stack height to H_min or relocate facilities beyond D_excl
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: combustion_efficiency_pct < 96 && relief_flow_kg_h > 10000
fmea.effect: Low combustion efficiency at high flow: significant unburned hydrocarbon and black smoke — verify flare tip design and steam/air assist per API 537 §5.4
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fraction_heat_radiated_F > 0.30
fmea.effect: F > 0.30: heavy luminous flame (smoking) — smoking flare radiates more heat; address air/steam assist or switch to enclosed flare to reduce F below 0.20
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Exclusion zone > 500 m: major hazard area — review relief system design, consider staged blowdown or enclosed ground flare to reduce peak flow
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: D_excl_m > 500

fmea.failureMode: Flame power > 100 MW: large flare event — noise assessment mandatory; stack structural loading under wind and thermal cycling must be verified per API 560 §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_f_kw > 100000

fmea.failureMode: High wind deflection: flame centre shifts significantly downwind — re-calculate exclusion zone at wind direction perpendicular to access road
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wind_speed_m_s > 15 && flame_tip_X_m > stack_height_m * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Personnel exclusion zone > 200 m: verify no permanent occupied facilities within zone; update HAZOP and site emergency response plan
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: allowable_radiation_kw_m2 < 3.16 && D_excl_m > 200

fmea.failureMode: Combustion efficiency < 98%: regulatory compliance risk — US 40 CFR Part 60 Subpart Ja requires ≥ 96.5% DRE; EU IED requires BAT-AEL assessment
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: combustion_efficiency_pct < 98

tool_name: Screw Conveyor Capacity, Drive Torque & Motor Power Selection

inputs.name: Required Throughput Capacity (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Bulk Density of Material (ρ_bulk)
inputs.note: Wheat ≈ 770; cement ≈ 1200; sand ≈ 1500; fly ash ≈ 700 kg/m³
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Screw Outside Diameter (D_s)
inputs.note: CEMA 350 standard sizes; pitch = D_s for standard full-pitch screw
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Screw Rotational Speed (n)
inputs.note: CEMA max speed: 300 mm screw → 60 rpm; 500 mm → 40 rpm for abrasive materials
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Conveyor Centre-to-Centre Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Conveyor Inclination Angle (α)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Material Flowability / Friction Factor (F_m)
inputs.note: CEMA 350 Table 3-3 — material factor based on flowability class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Trough Loading (% of screw cross-section)
inputs.note: CEMA 350 Table 2-1 — maximum trough loading by material class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_screw_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(screw_diameter_mm / 1000, 2) * trough_loading_pct / 100; Q_theory_m3h = A_screw_m2 * (screw_diameter_mm / 1000) * screw_speed_rpm * 60   // [m², m³/h] Screw Flight Area & Theoretical Capacity Reference: CEMA 350 §3.3 — Q = A_s × pitch × n × 60; standard pitch = D_s

Q_actual_m3h = capacity_t_h / (bulk_density_kg_m3 / 1000); capacity_ratio = Q_actual_m3h / Q_theory_m3h   // [m³/h, dimensionless] Actual Capacity & Capacity Check Reference: Q_vol = Q_mass / ρ_bulk; capacity_ratio ≤ 1.0 required

alpha_rad = inclination_deg * Math.PI / 180; Ci = (inclination_deg === 0) ? 1.0 : (inclination_deg <= 15) ? 1 - inclination_deg * 0.02 : (inclination_deg <= 25) ? 0.7 - (inclination_deg - 15) * 0.025 : 0.45   // [dimensionless] Inclination Capacity Reduction Factor (C_i) Reference: CEMA 350 Table 2-3 — incline factor: 0°=1.0; 15°≈0.70; 25°≈0.45; 45°≈0.25

W_material_kn_m = capacity_t_h * 9.81 * conveyor_length_m / 3600 / screw_speed_rpm * 60 / (2 * Math.PI); T_friction_knm = 0.055 * Math.pow(screw_diameter_mm / 1000, 3) * bulk_density_kg_m3 * conveyor_length_m * material_factor_Fm / 1000; T_drive_knm = W_material_kn_m + T_friction_knm   // [kN·m] Drive Torque — Material & Friction Components (T_drive) Reference: CEMA 350 §4 — drive torque = material torque + friction torque; HP method

P_material_kw = capacity_t_h * 9.81 * conveyor_length_m * Math.sin(inclination_deg * Math.PI / 180) / 3600; P_friction_kw = material_factor_Fm * bulk_density_kg_m3 * Q_actual_m3h / 3600 * conveyor_length_m * 0.055; P_motor_kw = (P_material_kw + P_friction_kw) / 0.88   // [kW] Required Motor Power (P_motor) Reference: CEMA 350 §4.4 — total HP = (HPF + HPM + HPC) / drive efficiency (η=0.88 typical)

v_tip_m_s = Math.PI * screw_diameter_mm / 1000 * screw_speed_rpm / 60   // [m/s] Peripheral Speed & Abrasion Check Reference: CEMA 350 §3.6 — tip speed limit: ≤ 1.0 m/s for abrasive materials; ≤ 1.5 m/s for non-abrasive

V1.error_msg: Required capacity exceeds screw throughput even with inclination correction — increase screw diameter, speed, or add second unit

V2.error_msg: Tip speed > 1.5 m/s: flight and trough wear rate increases exponentially — reduce speed or select harder material (AR400 flights)

V3.error_msg: Steep incline with sluggish material: significant backflow risk — evaluate vertical screw or bucket elevator alternative

smart_warnings.message: High tip speed on sluggish material: rapid liner and flight wear — reduce to < 1.0 m/s per CEMA recommendation for abrasive/sluggish class
45% trough loading on non-free-flowing material: plugging risk — reduce to 30% and increase screw diameter to maintain capacity
Long screw conveyor (> 40 m): intermediate hanger bearing required — shaft deflection and torque accumulation must be checked per CEMA §5
Inclination > 30°: CEMA capacity factors fall to < 40% — consider vertical screw conveyor (IBC) or pneumatic conveying system
High power on small screw: shaft and coupling stress high — verify shaft diameter torque rating per CEMA §6; consider upsize to 350 mm screw

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: capacity_ratio > 1.0 / Ci
fmea.effect: Required capacity exceeds screw throughput even with inclination correction — increase screw diameter, speed, or add second unit
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_tip_m_s > 1.5
fmea.effect: Tip speed > 1.5 m/s: flight and trough wear rate increases exponentially — reduce speed or select harder material (AR400 flights)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: inclination_deg > 20 && material_factor_Fm > 2.0
fmea.effect: Steep incline with sluggish material: significant backflow risk — evaluate vertical screw or bucket elevator alternative
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: High tip speed on sluggish material: rapid liner and flight wear — reduce to < 1.0 m/s per CEMA recommendation for abrasive/sluggish class
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_tip_m_s > 1.0 && material_factor_Fm >= 2.0

fmea.failureMode: 45% trough loading on non-free-flowing material: plugging risk — reduce to 30% and increase screw diameter to maintain capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: trough_loading_pct === 45 && material_factor_Fm > 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Long screw conveyor (> 40 m): intermediate hanger bearing required — shaft deflection and torque accumulation must be checked per CEMA §5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: conveyor_length_m > 40

fmea.failureMode: Inclination > 30°: CEMA capacity factors fall to < 40% — consider vertical screw conveyor (IBC) or pneumatic conveying system
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: inclination_deg > 30

fmea.failureMode: High power on small screw: shaft and coupling stress high — verify shaft diameter torque rating per CEMA §6; consider upsize to 350 mm screw
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_motor_kw > 50 && screw_diameter_mm < 300

tool_name: Toxic Gas Dispersion — Gaussian Plume Ground-Level Concentration & IDLH Exclusion Zone

inputs.name: Continuous Release Rate (Q_r)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §8 — Rate measurement: reproducibility ≈±2%

inputs.name: Wind Speed at 10 m Height (u_10)
inputs.note: Worst case (IDLH zone largest) typically at low wind speed 1–2 m/s; EPA RMP uses 3 m/s
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Pasquill-Gifford Atmospheric Stability Class
inputs.note: Worst-case for dense gas: F class; worst-case for buoyant release: A class; EPA RMP default: F/D
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Effective Release Height (H_s)
inputs.note: Ground-level release: H_s = 0; elevated flange: H_s = stack height + thermal rise (Briggs)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: IDLH Concentration of Gas (Immediately Dangerous to Life/Health)
inputs.note: NIOSH IDLH: H₂S = 100 ppm; NH₃ = 300 ppm; Cl₂ = 10 ppm; HCN = 50 ppm; CO = 1200 ppm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §7 — Analytical chemistry: reproducibility ±5%

inputs.name: Gas Molecular Weight (M)
inputs.note: H₂S=34; NH₃=17; Cl₂=71; HCN=27; CO=28; SO₂=64; CH₄=16
inputs.uncertainty_source: OIML R76-1:2006 §3.7.1 — Class III weighing: ±1.5% (expanded ±2%)

inputs.name: Terrain Roughness
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: const PG = { A:{a:0.22,b:0.0001,c:0.20,d:0}, B:{a:0.16,b:0.0001,c:0.12,d:0}, C:{a:0.11,b:0.0002,c:0.08,d:0.0002}, D:{a:0.08,b:0.0015,c:0.06,d:0.0015}, E:{a:0.06,b:0.0015,c:0.03,d:0.0003}, F:{a:0.04,b:0.0015,c:0.016,d:0.0003} }; const p = PG[stability_class]; const x = 500; sigma_y = p.a * x * Math.pow(1 + p.b * x, -0.5); sigma_z = p.c * x * Math.pow(1 + p.d * x, -0.5)   // [m] Pasquill-Gifford Dispersion Coefficients at Downwind Distance x Reference: Pasquill-Gifford sigma curves — Slade (1968); Turner Workbook on Atmospheric Dispersion §3

C_kg_m3 = release_rate_kg_s / (Math.PI * sigma_y * sigma_z * wind_speed_m_s) * Math.exp(-0.5 * Math.pow(release_height_m / sigma_z, 2)); C_ppm = C_kg_m3 * 1e6 / (molecular_weight_g_mol / 1000) * 22.4 / molecular_weight_g_mol * 1000   // [ppm] Ground-Level Centreline Concentration at Distance x (C_x) Reference: Gaussian plume model — EPA AERMOD guidance; C = Q/(π·σ_y·σ_z·u)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²)

let D = 50; for(let i=0;i<200;i++){ const p = {A:[0.22,0.0001,0.20,0],B:[0.16,0.0001,0.12,0],C:[0.11,0.0002,0.08,0.0002],D:[0.08,0.0015,0.06,0.0015],E:[0.06,0.0015,0.03,0.0003],F:[0.04,0.0015,0.016,0.0003]}[stability_class]; const sy=p[0]*D*Math.pow(1+p[1]*D,-0.5); const sz=p[2]*D*Math.pow(1+p[3]*D,-0.5); const C=release_rate_kg_s/(Math.PI*sy*sz*wind_speed_m_s)*Math.exp(-0.5*Math.pow(release_height_m/sz,2))*1e6/(molecular_weight_g_mol/22400)*1000; if(C < IDLH_ppm) break; D+=25; } D_IDLH_m = D   // [m] IDLH Exclusion Zone Radius (D_IDLH) — Iterative Search Reference: Iterative downwind search to find distance where C_centreline = IDLH — EPA RMP methodology

const p2 = {A:[0.22,0.0001],B:[0.16,0.0001],C:[0.11,0.0002],D:[0.08,0.0015],E:[0.06,0.0015],F:[0.04,0.0015]}[stability_class]; sigma_y_IDLH = p2[0]*D_IDLH_m*Math.pow(1+p2[1]*D_IDLH_m,-0.5); plume_width_m = 2.15 * sigma_y_IDLH   // [m] Plume Width at IDLH Boundary Reference: Plume half-width at IDLH = 2.15σ_y (contains ~97% of crosswind concentration)

rho_gas_kg_m3 = molecular_weight_g_mol / 22.4 * 1000 / 1000; dense_gas = rho_gas_kg_m3 > 1.5   // [boolean] Dense Gas Warning — Richardson Number Proxy Reference: Dense gas criterion: ρ_gas > 1.5 kg/m³ at STP → SLAB / PHAST model recommended over Gaussian

ERPG2_approx_ppm = IDLH_ppm * 0.3; time_in_zone_s = D_IDLH_m / wind_speed_m_s; TL = C_ppm * Math.pow(time_in_zone_s / 600, 1.0)   // [ppm·(10 min), s] Toxic Load (Probit Input) — 1-Hour ERPG-2 Proxy Reference: Toxic load concept — TNO Yellow Book §4; ERPG-2 ≈ 30% of IDLH (AIHA guidance)

V1.error_msg: Dense gas at ground level: Gaussian model underestimates exclusion zone — use SLAB, PHAST, or ALOHA dense gas model for regulatory compliance

V2.error_msg: Wind speed < 1 m/s: Gaussian model breakdown — calm conditions; IDLH zone will be very large; use EPA RMP worst-case 1.5 m/s minimum

V3.error_msg: IDLH exclusion zone > 1 km: major accident scenario — off-site consequence analysis and public notification required per EPA RMP §68.10

smart_warnings.message: Worst-case dispersion conditions (Class F, low wind): exclusion zone maximised — this scenario governs emergency planning zone; shelter-in-place radius = D_IDLH
Very toxic gas (IDLH < 50 ppm): small release rates generate large exclusion zones — verify passive mitigation (water curtain, scrubber) reduces source term
Large zone in urban terrain: building effects invalidate Gaussian model; use AERMOD or CFD simulation for planning purposes per EPA guidance
Large ground-level release: immediate shelter-in-place or evacuation of D_IDLH radius; notify emergency services per OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119
Transit time through IDLH zone > 10 min: self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) mandatory for emergency responders entering zone per OSHA §1910.134

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dense_gas && release_height_m === 0
fmea.effect: Dense gas at ground level: Gaussian model underestimates exclusion zone — use SLAB, PHAST, or ALOHA dense gas model for regulatory compliance
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: wind_speed_m_s < 1.0
fmea.effect: Wind speed < 1 m/s: Gaussian model breakdown — calm conditions; IDLH zone will be very large; use EPA RMP worst-case 1.5 m/s minimum
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: D_IDLH_m > 1000
fmea.effect: IDLH exclusion zone > 1 km: major accident scenario — off-site consequence analysis and public notification required per EPA RMP §68.10
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Worst-case dispersion conditions (Class F, low wind): exclusion zone maximised — this scenario governs emergency planning zone; shelter-in-place radius = D_IDLH
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stability_class === 'F' && wind_speed_m_s < 2

fmea.failureMode: Very toxic gas (IDLH < 50 ppm): small release rates generate large exclusion zones — verify passive mitigation (water curtain, scrubber) reduces source term
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: IDLH_ppm < 50

fmea.failureMode: Large zone in urban terrain: building effects invalidate Gaussian model; use AERMOD or CFD simulation for planning purposes per EPA guidance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: D_IDLH_m > 500 && terrain_type === 'urban'

fmea.failureMode: Large ground-level release: immediate shelter-in-place or evacuation of D_IDLH radius; notify emergency services per OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: release_height_m === 0 && release_rate_kg_s > 1

fmea.failureMode: Transit time through IDLH zone > 10 min: self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) mandatory for emergency responders entering zone per OSHA §1910.134
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: time_in_zone_s > 600

tool_name: Compressed Air Receiver Volume Sizing — Peak Demand Buffer, Pressure Drop & ASME Certification

inputs.name: Compressor Free Air Delivery (C_fad)
inputs.note: FAD at reference conditions: ISO 1217 — 1 bar(a), 20°C, 0% RH
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Peak System Air Demand (D_peak)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Maximum System Pressure — Compressor Cut-Out (P_max)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Minimum Acceptable System Pressure — Cut-In / Process Minimum (P_min)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Duration of Peak Demand Event (t_peak)
inputs.note: Pneumatic tool burst: 5–15 s; production line demand spike: 30–120 s
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §6 — Time measurement: ±1% of reading

inputs.name: Safety Factor for ASME / PSSR Receiver Rating
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Receiver Orientation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: Q_deficit_m3 = Math.max(0, peak_demand_m3min - compressor_capacity_m3min) * peak_duration_s / 60   // [m³ FAD] Net Demand Deficit During Peak (Q_deficit) Reference: Volume of air that must be supplied from storage: Q_def = (D_peak − C_fad) × t_peak

V_r_m3 = Q_deficit_m3 / ((P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / 1.013)   // [m³] Required Receiver Volume — Pressure Drop Method (V_r) Reference: ISO 1217 / CAGI — V_r = Q_FAD / [(P_max − P_min) / P_atm]; P_atm = 1.013 bar

V_rule_m3 = compressor_capacity_m3min / 7   // [m³] Recommended Receiver Volume — Rule-of-Thumb Check Reference: Industry rule of thumb: V_receiver ≈ C_fad (m³/min) / 7 for stable pressure band; CAGI handbook §3

V_design_m3 = Math.max(V_r_m3, V_rule_m3) * receiver_pressure_vessel_factor   // [m³] Design Receiver Volume (with safety factor) Reference: Final design volume: larger of peak-demand and rule-of-thumb, × safety factor

D_vessel_mm = Math.pow(V_design_m3 * 6 / Math.PI, 1/3) * 1000; P_design_bar = P_max_bar * 1.1; S_allow_mpa = 138; E_joint = 0.85; t_wall_mm = P_design_bar * 0.1 * D_vessel_mm / (2 * S_allow_mpa * E_joint - 1.2 * P_design_bar * 0.1)   // [mm] ASME / PSSR Minimum Wall Thickness (t_wall) for Cylindrical Shell Reference: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 UG-27 — Barlow thin-wall; 10% design overpressure; SA-516-70 allowable S=138 MPa

T_on_min = V_design_m3 * (P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / (1.013 * compressor_capacity_m3min); T_cycle_min = V_design_m3 * (P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / (1.013 * (compressor_capacity_m3min - peak_demand_m3min / 2))   // [min] Compressor On/Off Cycle Time (T_cycle) Reference: T_on = V·ΔP/(P_atm·C_fad) — compressor cycle time; minimum 3 min on-time recommended to prevent motor overheating

V1.error_msg: P_min must be less than P_max — check pressure band inputs

V2.error_msg: Compressor on-time < 3 minutes: motor short-cycling risk — increase receiver volume or reduce pressure band

V3.error_msg: Receiver > 1 m³ above 10 bar: PSSR 2000 / PED 2014/68/EU category III vessel — notified body inspection and written scheme of examination mandatory

smart_warnings.message: Peak demand > 150% of compressor FAD: receiver will empty rapidly — evaluate additional compressor or demand-side management; verify peak event frequency
Pressure band < 1 bar: very small storage window — tight band requires frequent compressor cycling; widen band to minimum 1.5 bar or add receiver volume
Large high-pressure receiver: stored energy exceeds 250 bar·L — perform consequence analysis per API 521; locate receiver in blast-resistant area
Calculated wall thickness < 4 mm: minimum practical plate thickness for receiver fabrication is 4 mm per ASME BPVC; use 4 mm minimum
Compressor capacity meets peak demand with no deficit: receiver sized by rule-of-thumb only — primarily for pressure stability and wet air separation, not storage

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: P_min_bar >= P_max_bar
fmea.effect: P_min must be less than P_max — check pressure band inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_on_min < 3 && compressor_capacity_m3min < 10
fmea.effect: Compressor on-time < 3 minutes: motor short-cycling risk — increase receiver volume or reduce pressure band
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: V_design_m3 > 1 && P_max_bar > 10
fmea.effect: Receiver > 1 m³ above 10 bar: PSSR 2000 / PED 2014/68/EU category III vessel — notified body inspection and written scheme of examination mandatory
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Peak demand > 150% of compressor FAD: receiver will empty rapidly — evaluate additional compressor or demand-side management; verify peak event frequency
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: peak_demand_m3min > compressor_capacity_m3min * 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Pressure band < 1 bar: very small storage window — tight band requires frequent compressor cycling; widen band to minimum 1.5 bar or add receiver volume
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_max_bar - P_min_bar < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Large high-pressure receiver: stored energy exceeds 250 bar·L — perform consequence analysis per API 521; locate receiver in blast-resistant area
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: V_design_m3 > 5 && P_max_bar > 50

fmea.failureMode: Calculated wall thickness < 4 mm: minimum practical plate thickness for receiver fabrication is 4 mm per ASME BPVC; use 4 mm minimum
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: t_wall_mm < 4

fmea.failureMode: Compressor capacity meets peak demand with no deficit: receiver sized by rule-of-thumb only — primarily for pressure stability and wet air separation, not storage
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_deficit_m3 === 0 && peak_demand_m3min <= compressor_capacity_m3min

tool_name: Crane Runway Beam — Maximum Wheel Load, Vertical & Lateral Bending, Fatigue Check

inputs.name: Crane Rated Lifting Capacity (W_rated)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Crane Bridge (Girder) Self-Weight (W_bridge)
inputs.note: From crane manufacturer data sheet; rough estimate: W_bridge ≈ 0.3–0.5 × W_rated for standard EOT cranes
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Crane Trolley & Hoist Self-Weight (W_trolley)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Crane Bridge Span (L_bridge)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Runway Beam Span Between Columns (L_runway)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Crane Service Class (CMAA / FEM 1.001)

options.label: CMAA Class C — Light service (2–5 lifts/hr)
CMAA Class F — Severe continuous duty

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Runway Beam Section Modulus — Vertical (I_x)
inputs.note: From standard beam tables: W24×55 → I_x = 1350 cm⁴; W36×135 → I_x = 7800 cm⁴
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Runway Beam Overall Depth (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: N_wheels_per_rail = 2; P_static_kn = (crane_capacity_kn + trolley_weight_kn + bridge_weight_kn / 2) / N_wheels_per_rail   // [kN] Maximum Static Wheel Load (P_wheel) Reference: CMAA 70 §3.3 — max wheel load when trolley at end of bridge; symmetric 2-wheel per rail assumed

phi_v = {A:1.10, C:1.15, D:1.20, E:1.25, F:1.30}[crane_class]; P_factored_kn = P_static_kn * phi_v   // [dimensionless, kN] Dynamic Impact Factor (Φ_v) & Factored Wheel Load Reference: AISC Design Guide 7 Table 2.1 / CMAA 70 — vertical dynamic impact factor by crane class

beam_self_wt_kn_m = 0.8; M_max_knm = P_factored_kn * runway_beam_span_m / 4 + beam_self_wt_kn_m * Math.pow(runway_beam_span_m, 2) / 8   // [kN·m] Maximum Vertical Bending Moment in Runway Beam (M_max) Reference: Midspan moment: moving concentrated load at ¼-span produces near-maximum moment; + UDL self-weight

S_x_cm3 = 2 * Ix_beam_cm4 / beam_depth_mm * 10; sigma_bend_mpa = M_max_knm * 1e6 / (S_x_cm3 * 1e3)   // [MPa] Extreme Fibre Bending Stress (σ_bend) Reference: σ = M/S_x; S_x = 2I_x/d — elastic section modulus

H_lat_kn = 0.20 * (crane_capacity_kn + trolley_weight_kn)   // [kN] Lateral (Horizontal) Crane Thrust Force (H_lat) Reference: AISC DG7 §3.2 / CMAA 70 §3.5 — lateral thrust H = 20% of (lifted load + trolley) for electric overhead cranes

fatigue_cat = {A:'E (120 MPa)', C:'E (110 MPa)', D:'D (110 MPa)', E:'C (165 MPa, check)', F:'B (165 MPa, check)'}[crane_class]; stress_range_mpa = sigma_bend_mpa * 0.6   // [text, MPa] Fatigue Life Category & Allowable Stress Range Reference: AISC DG7 §5 / ASCE 7-22 Table 15.4-2 — runway beam fatigue category per crane class; stress range = σ_max × 0.6 (trolley at end)

V1.error_msg: Bending stress > 165 MPa: exceeds allowable for A36/S275 steel (0.66Fy) — upsize beam section or reduce span

V2.error_msg: L/d ratio > 25: shallow beam relative to span — deflection likely excessive; AISC DG7 recommends L/1000 vertical limit; check I_x

V3.error_msg: Fatigue stress range exceeds AISC Category D allowable (110 MPa) — weld details and connection design critical; consider Category C details

smart_warnings.message: Class E/F crane: heavy fatigue regime — runway beam fatigue life governs over static strength; use AISC Category B/C weld details and specify weld quality per AWS D1.1
High lateral thrust: verify top flange bracing and rail clip design — AISC DG7 §4 requires continuous top flange bracing for heavy crane classes
Long bridge span + moderate duty: trolley skewing forces become significant — verify bumper and end stop design per CMAA 70 §3.6
High bending stress: verify lateral-torsional buckling of top compression flange per AISC 360-22 F2 — crane beams often unbraced between columns
Wheel load > 500 kN: heavy crane — verify column base plate, anchor bolts, and foundation design for combined gravity and lateral crane thrust per AISC DG1

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sigma_bend_mpa > 165
fmea.effect: Bending stress > 165 MPa: exceeds allowable for A36/S275 steel (0.66Fy) — upsize beam section or reduce span
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: runway_beam_span_m / beam_depth_mm * 1000 > 25
fmea.effect: L/d ratio > 25: shallow beam relative to span — deflection likely excessive; AISC DG7 recommends L/1000 vertical limit; check I_x
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: stress_range_mpa > 110 && crane_class === 'D'
fmea.effect: Fatigue stress range exceeds AISC Category D allowable (110 MPa) — weld details and connection design critical; consider Category C details
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Class E/F crane: heavy fatigue regime — runway beam fatigue life governs over static strength; use AISC Category B/C weld details and specify weld quality per AWS D1.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: crane_class === 'E' || crane_class === 'F'

fmea.failureMode: High lateral thrust: verify top flange bracing and rail clip design — AISC DG7 §4 requires continuous top flange bracing for heavy crane classes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: H_lat_kn > crane_capacity_kn * 0.25

fmea.failureMode: Long bridge span + moderate duty: trolley skewing forces become significant — verify bumper and end stop design per CMAA 70 §3.6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bridge_span_m > 20 && crane_class === 'D'

fmea.failureMode: High bending stress: verify lateral-torsional buckling of top compression flange per AISC 360-22 F2 — crane beams often unbraced between columns
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma_bend_mpa > 140

fmea.failureMode: Wheel load > 500 kN: heavy crane — verify column base plate, anchor bolts, and foundation design for combined gravity and lateral crane thrust per AISC DG1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_factored_kn > 500

tool_name: Industrial Evaporator Design — Steam Economy, Boiling Point Rise & Heat Transfer Area

inputs.name: Feed Liquid Flow Rate (F)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Feed Solids Concentration (x_F)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Product Solids Concentration (x_P)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Heating Steam Pressure (P_steam)
inputs.note: Steam saturation temp at 3 bar(a) = 133°C; 1.5 bar(a) = 112°C
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Evaporator Operating Pressure (P_evap)
inputs.note: Vacuum evaporation at 0.3 bar(a) → T_sat = 69°C; reduces thermal degradation
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Boiling Point Rise of Solution (BPR)
inputs.note: At product concentration: NaCl at 20% → BPR ≈ 2.6 K; sugar at 60% → BPR ≈ 5 K; caustic at 40% → BPR ≈ 20 K

inputs.name: Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient (U)
inputs.note: Falling film (water) ≈ 3000–5000; forced circulation (viscous) ≈ 1000–2500; natural circ. ≈ 1500–3000
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

formulas: V_kg_h = feed_flow_kg_h * (1 - feed_conc_pct / product_conc_pct); P_kg_h = feed_flow_kg_h - V_kg_h   // [kg/h] Water Evaporation Rate (V) from Mass Balance Reference: Overall mass balance: F = V + P; solids balance: F·x_F = P·x_P → V = F(1 − x_F/x_P)

T_steam_c = 100 * Math.pow(steam_pressure_bar, 0.25) + 33 * Math.pow(steam_pressure_bar, 0.05) - 33; T_evap_sat_c = 100 * Math.pow(evap_pressure_bar, 0.25) + 33 * Math.pow(evap_pressure_bar, 0.05) - 33; T_boil_c = T_evap_sat_c + BPR_k   // [°C] Steam Saturation Temperatures Reference: Antoine approximation for water saturation temperature — Perry's 9th Ed. §2; BPR elevates boiling point above T_sat
dT_eff = T_steam_c - T_boil_c   // [K] Net Temperature Driving Force (ΔTLM) Reference: Effective ΔT = T_steam − T_boil; T_boil = T_evap,sat + BPR — TEMA / Perry's §11.12

lambda_steam_kj_kg = 2676 - 2.09 * T_steam_c; cp_feed_kj_kgk = 4.18 * (1 - feed_conc_pct/100) + 1.5 * feed_conc_pct/100; lambda_evap_kj_kg = 2676 - 2.09 * T_boil_c; Q_preheat_kw = feed_flow_kg_h / 3600 * cp_feed_kj_kgk * (T_boil_c - feed_temp_c); Q_evap_kw = V_kg_h / 3600 * lambda_evap_kj_kg; Q_steam_kw = Q_preheat_kw + Q_evap_kw   // [kW] Steam Heat Duty — Including Feed Pre-Heating (Q_steam) Reference: Total duty = preheat load + evaporation load; λ_steam from Antoine saturation enthalpy approximation

S_kg_h = Q_steam_kw * 3600 / (2676 - 2.09 * T_steam_c); steam_economy = V_kg_h / S_kg_h   // [kg/h, kg water/kg steam] Steam Consumption & Economy Reference: Steam economy = kg evaporation / kg steam consumed; single-effect ≈ 0.8–0.9; triple-effect ≈ 2.5–2.8

A_evap_m2 = Q_steam_kw * 1000 / (U_overall_w_m2k * dT_eff)   // [m²] Required Heat Transfer Area (A_evap) Reference: A = Q / (U·ΔT) — fundamental heat transfer equation; TEMA / GEA falling film evaporator design

standards: Alfa Laval / GEA Evaporator Engineering Practice

V1.error_msg: Product concentration must exceed feed concentration — check x_F and x_P inputs

V2.error_msg: Effective ΔT < 5 K: very small driving force — heat transfer area will be enormous; increase steam pressure or reduce evaporator pressure (deeper vacuum)

V3.error_msg: Steam economy < 0.7: below single-effect minimum — heat losses may be underestimated; verify feed preheat duty and BPR value

smart_warnings.message: ΔT_eff < 10 K: economically marginal single-effect operation — evaluate multiple-effect evaporation (economy improves linearly with number of effects)
BPR > 15 K: large boiling point rise severely reduces ΔT and increases steam consumption — evaluate mechanical vapour recompression (MVR) for high-BPR solutions
Large heat transfer area (> 1000 m²): multiple evaporator bodies likely required — evaluate falling film with large-diameter tubes for reduced fouling
High boiling temperature at elevated concentration: thermal degradation risk for heat-sensitive products (food, pharma) — deepen vacuum to reduce T_boil
Low economy at large evaporation rate: high operating cost — each additional effect saves ~35–40% steam; payback on triple-effect typically < 2 years

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: product_conc_pct <= feed_conc_pct
fmea.effect: Product concentration must exceed feed concentration — check x_F and x_P inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dT_eff < 5
fmea.effect: Effective ΔT < 5 K: very small driving force — heat transfer area will be enormous; increase steam pressure or reduce evaporator pressure (deeper vacuum)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: steam_economy < 0.7
fmea.effect: Steam economy < 0.7: below single-effect minimum — heat losses may be underestimated; verify feed preheat duty and BPR value
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: ΔT_eff < 10 K: economically marginal single-effect operation — evaluate multiple-effect evaporation (economy improves linearly with number of effects)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dT_eff < 10

fmea.failureMode: BPR > 15 K: large boiling point rise severely reduces ΔT and increases steam consumption — evaluate mechanical vapour recompression (MVR) for high-BPR solutions
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BPR_k > 15

fmea.failureMode: Large heat transfer area (> 1000 m²): multiple evaporator bodies likely required — evaluate falling film with large-diameter tubes for reduced fouling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_evap_m2 > 1000

fmea.failureMode: High boiling temperature at elevated concentration: thermal degradation risk for heat-sensitive products (food, pharma) — deepen vacuum to reduce T_boil
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_boil_c > 100 && product_conc_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: Low economy at large evaporation rate: high operating cost — each additional effect saves ~35–40% steam; payback on triple-effect typically < 2 years
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: steam_economy < 1.0 && V_kg_h > 10000

tool_name: Metallic Expansion Joint Sizing — Axial Displacement, Spring Rate & Fatigue Cycle Life

inputs.name: Pipe Nominal Outside Diameter (D_o)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Pipe Run Length Between Fixed Anchors (L_pipe)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Pipe Material — Thermal Expansion Coefficient (α)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Design Internal Pressure (P_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Convolutions (Bellows Corrugations)
inputs.note: More convolutions → lower spring rate, higher cycle life, lower stress per cycle
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bellows Material Yield Strength at Temperature (S_y)
inputs.note: SS316L at 20°C → 205 MPa; at 400°C → 140 MPa; Inconel 625 → 415 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Yield/ultimate strength (steel): CoV=0.07

formulas: delta_T = T_operating_c - T_install_c; delta_axial_mm = pipe_material * 1e-6 * delta_T * pipe_length_m * 1000   // [mm] Thermal Axial Displacement (δ_axial) Reference: δ = α·ΔT·L — ASME B31.3 §319.4; EJMA 10th Ed. §2.1

delta_req_mm = delta_axial_mm * 1.25   // [mm] Required Axial Movement Capacity per Joint Reference: EJMA §2.2 — 25% safety margin on calculated displacement for uncertainty in anchor loads and fabrication tolerance

D_m_mm = pipe_OD_mm + 30; t_bellow_mm = 0.8; f_ax_n_mm = 1.7 * Math.pow(D_m_mm, 0.5) * bellows_material_yield_mpa * Math.pow(t_bellow_mm, 3) / (Math.pow(30, 3) * num_convolutions)   // [N/mm] Approximate Bellows Axial Spring Rate (f_ax) Reference: EJMA 10th Ed. §5.3 — simplified spring rate; f = 1.7·D_m^0.5·E·t³/(h³·n); h=convolution height assumed 30 mm; t=0.8 mm

F_pressure_kn = design_pressure_bar * 1e5 * Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(pipe_OD_mm/1000, 2) / 1000; F_spring_kn = f_ax_n_mm * delta_axial_mm / 1000; F_anchor_kn = F_pressure_kn + F_spring_kn   // [kN] Axial Force Transmitted to Anchors (F_anchor) Reference: EJMA §4.1 — anchor load = pressure thrust + spring force; pressure thrust = P·A_pipe

sigma_m_mpa = 3 * f_ax_n_mm * delta_req_mm / (num_convolutions * Math.PI * pipe_OD_mm * 0.8)   // [MPa] Meridional Bending Stress in Convolution (σ_m) — Fatigue Driver Reference: EJMA §5.5 — simplified meridional stress from axial deflection

stress_ratio = sigma_m_mpa / bellows_material_yield_mpa; N_life = (stress_ratio < 0.3) ? 100000 : (stress_ratio < 0.5) ? 50000 / stress_ratio : (stress_ratio < 0.8) ? 10000 / Math.pow(stress_ratio, 3) : 1000 / Math.pow(stress_ratio, 5)   // [cycles] Estimated Fatigue Cycle Life (N_life) Reference: EJMA Appendix C fatigue curve — SS316L; N proportional to (S_y/σ_m)^k; indicative only

V1.error_msg: Required displacement exceeds convolution capacity (15 mm/conv limit): add convolutions, use two expansion joints, or reduce anchor spacing

V2.error_msg: Meridional stress > 80% yield: very short fatigue life — increase convolution count or specify lower spring rate bellows with larger crown height

V3.error_msg: Fatigue life < 1000 cycles: bellows will fail rapidly — review displacement calculation; consider tied or hinged joint if lateral movement is present

smart_warnings.message: Anchor load > 500 kN: large main anchors required — pressure thrust on unrestrained bellows is major structural load; evaluate tied universal expansion joint to eliminate thrust
Carbon steel above 450°C: creep domain — ASME B31.3 requires time-dependent allowable stress; verify bellows material rating at temperature
High pressure with few convolutions: instability risk — EJMA §7 squirm (in-plane and column) must be checked; add internal sleeve for pressure > 40 bar
Large displacement with few convolutions: excessive stress per convolution — minimum 6 convolutions for δ > 50 mm per EJMA design practice
Fatigue life < 10,000 cycles: limited life joint — document replacement interval in maintenance plan; N_life cycles at operating temperature ΔT per EJMA C

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: delta_req_mm > num_convolutions * 15
fmea.effect: Required displacement exceeds convolution capacity (15 mm/conv limit): add convolutions, use two expansion joints, or reduce anchor spacing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sigma_m_mpa > bellows_material_yield_mpa * 0.8
fmea.effect: Meridional stress > 80% yield: very short fatigue life — increase convolution count or specify lower spring rate bellows with larger crown height
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: N_life < 1000 && design_pressure_bar > 0
fmea.effect: Fatigue life < 1000 cycles: bellows will fail rapidly — review displacement calculation; consider tied or hinged joint if lateral movement is present
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Anchor load > 500 kN: large main anchors required — pressure thrust on unrestrained bellows is major structural load; evaluate tied universal expansion joint to eliminate thrust
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_anchor_kn > 500

fmea.failureMode: Carbon steel above 450°C: creep domain — ASME B31.3 requires time-dependent allowable stress; verify bellows material rating at temperature
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_operating_c > 450 && pipe_material === 11.7

fmea.failureMode: High pressure with few convolutions: instability risk — EJMA §7 squirm (in-plane and column) must be checked; add internal sleeve for pressure > 40 bar
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_pressure_bar > 40 && num_convolutions < 3

fmea.failureMode: Large displacement with few convolutions: excessive stress per convolution — minimum 6 convolutions for δ > 50 mm per EJMA design practice
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_req_mm > 50 && num_convolutions < 6

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue life < 10,000 cycles: limited life joint — document replacement interval in maintenance plan; N_life cycles at operating temperature ΔT per EJMA C
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_life < 10000

tool_name: Inline Static Mixer — Pressure Drop, Mixing Length & Coefficient of Variation

inputs.name: Pipe Inside Diameter (D_pipe)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Volumetric Flow Rate (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Process Fluid Density (ρ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Process Fluid Dynamic Viscosity (μ)
inputs.note: Water at 20°C = 1.0 mPa·s; light oil ≈ 10; heavy oil ≈ 500–5000 mPa·s
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Static Mixer Element Type
inputs.note: K_p = pressure drop factor: ΔP_mixer = K_p × ΔP_empty_pipe; from manufacturer data
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Mixer Elements (N_e)
inputs.note: Kenics: 6–12 for turbulent; 20–30 for laminar. Sulzer SMX: 5–8 elements typically
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Required Coefficient of Variation at Mixer Exit (CoV_req)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_m_s = flow_rate_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)   // [m/s, dimensionless] Pipe Velocity & Reynolds Number Reference: Re = ρ·v·D/μ — flow regime governs mixing mechanism and ΔP correlation

f_fanning = (Re < 2300) ? 16/Re : 0.0791 * Math.pow(Re, -0.25); L_mixer_m = num_elements * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 * 1.5; dP_pipe_pa = 4 * f_fanning * (L_mixer_m / (pipe_ID_mm/1000)) * fluid_density_kg_m3 * Math.pow(v_m_s, 2) / 2   // [Pa] Empty Pipe Pressure Drop (ΔP_pipe) — Darcy-Weisbach Reference: Fanning friction: laminar f=16/Re; turbulent Blasius f=0.0791Re^−0.25; element length ≈ 1.5D
dP_mixer_pa = mixer_type * dP_pipe_pa   // [Pa] Mixer Total Pressure Drop (ΔP_mixer) Reference: ΔP_mixer = K_p × ΔP_empty_pipe over mixer length — Sulzer / Chemineer product data

CoV_0 = 1.0; CoV_actual = (Re > 10000) ? CoV_0 * Math.pow(0.316, num_elements) : CoV_0 * Math.pow(0.5, num_elements * 0.7)   // [dimensionless] Coefficient of Variation After N Elements (CoV_actual) Reference: Turbulent: CoV reduces by factor ~0.316 per element (Kenics HEV); laminar: slower reduction; Perry's §18.5

decay_factor = (Re > 10000) ? 0.316 : 0.5 ** 0.7; N_req = Math.ceil(Math.log(CoV_required) / Math.log(decay_factor))   // [count] Minimum Elements Required for Target CoV Reference: N_req = log(CoV_req / CoV_0) / log(decay_factor) — Kenics design method

L_total_m = num_elements * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 * 1.5; epsilon_w_kg = dP_mixer_pa * v_m_s / fluid_density_kg_m3   // [m, W/kg] Total Mixer Length & Specific Energy Dissipation (ε) Reference: ε = ΔP·v/ρ — specific energy input; turbulent mixing requires ε > 1 W/kg for fast reactions per AIChE Mixing Fundamentals

standards: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

V1.error_msg: Actual CoV exceeds required mixing quality: add more elements (N_req) or switch to higher-performance mixer type (e.g. SMX from Kenics)

V2.error_msg: Very low Reynolds number (Re < 100): laminar segregation — Kenics HEV/SMXL insufficient for viscous mixing; use Sulzer SMX designed for Re < 10

V3.error_msg: Mixer pressure drop > 5 bar: very high ΔP — consider low-pressure SMXL elements, larger pipe diameter, or reduce flow velocity

smart_warnings.message: Laminar flow with Kenics HEV: designed for turbulent use — switch to Sulzer SMX (K_p=37) optimised for viscous laminar mixing
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: Element count below minimum for target CoV — mixing quality not achieved; add N_req − N_e elements or accept higher CoV
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: High velocity in dense fluid: erosion risk on mixer elements — verify element material hardness; consider ceramic-coated elements for slurries
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: Low specific energy dissipation (<0.1 W/kg): mixing is gentle — suitable for shear-sensitive products (emulsions, cells); otherwise increase velocity
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: High viscosity fluid (>10 Pa·s): static mixer will have very high ΔP — evaluate dynamic (agitated) mixer or reduce viscosity by heating before mixing
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CoV_actual > CoV_required
fmea.effect: Actual CoV exceeds required mixing quality: add more elements (N_req) or switch to higher-performance mixer type (e.g. SMX from Kenics)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Re < 100 && mixer_type <= 8
fmea.effect: Very low Reynolds number (Re < 100): laminar segregation — Kenics HEV/SMXL insufficient for viscous mixing; use Sulzer SMX designed for Re < 10
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_mixer_pa > 500000
fmea.effect: Mixer pressure drop > 5 bar: very high ΔP — consider low-pressure SMXL elements, larger pipe diameter, or reduce flow velocity
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Laminar flow with Kenics HEV: designed for turbulent use — switch to Sulzer SMX (K_p=37) optimised for viscous laminar mixing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Re < 2300 && mixer_type === 6.3
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: Element count below minimum for target CoV — mixing quality not achieved; add N_req − N_e elements or accept higher CoV
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: num_elements < N_req
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: High velocity in dense fluid: erosion risk on mixer elements — verify element material hardness; consider ceramic-coated elements for slurries
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_m_s > 5 && fluid_density_kg_m3 > 800
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: Low specific energy dissipation (<0.1 W/kg): mixing is gentle — suitable for shear-sensitive products (emulsions, cells); otherwise increase velocity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: epsilon_w_kg < 0.1 && Re > 10000
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: High viscosity fluid (>10 Pa·s): static mixer will have very high ΔP — evaluate dynamic (agitated) mixer or reduce viscosity by heating before mixing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_viscosity_mPas > 10000
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

tool_name: Industrial Heat Pump COP, Annual Energy Saving & Carbon Payback vs Steam Boiler

inputs.name: Required Process Heat Delivery (Q_H)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Heat Source Temperature (T_source) — Waste Heat / Ground
inputs.note: Waste cooling water ≈ 25–40°C; ground loop ≈ 8–12°C; exhaust air ≈ 20–35°C

inputs.name: Process Heat Delivery Temperature (T_delivery)
inputs.note: Low-temp drying ≈ 60–80°C; pasteurisation ≈ 85–95°C; HTHP steam ≈ 120–160°C

inputs.name: Heat Pump Carnot Efficiency Factor (η_Carnot)
inputs.note: Industrial heat pumps: η_Carnot ≈ 0.40–0.55; well-optimised: up to 0.60; VDI 4650 §4
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Displaced Natural Gas Boiler Efficiency (η_boiler)
inputs.note: Condensing boiler ≈ 93–95%; conventional ≈ 82–88%; old steam boiler ≈ 75–82%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Natural Gas Carbon Emission Factor
inputs.note: UK BEIS 2024: 0.202 kgCO₂/kWh gas; EU average ≈ 0.198; US EIA ≈ 0.181 kgCO₂/kWh
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Grid Electricity Carbon Emission Factor
inputs.note: UK grid 2024: 0.233 kgCO₂e/kWh; EU avg ≈ 0.275; US avg ≈ 0.386; decarbonised grid → < 0.10
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: T_H_K = T_delivery_c + 273.15; T_L_K = T_source_c + 273.15; COP_Carnot = T_H_K / (T_H_K - T_L_K)   // [dimensionless] Theoretical (Carnot) COP_H Reference: Carnot COP_H = T_H / (T_H − T_L) — thermodynamic upper bound; EN 14511 §3.2
COP_HP = Carnot_efficiency_fraction * COP_Carnot   // [dimensionless] Actual Heat Pump COP (COP_HP) Reference: COP_act = η_Carnot × COP_Carnot; VDI 4650:2009 §4 — practical Carnot fraction

W_elec_kw = Q_delivery_kw / COP_HP; Q_source_kw = Q_delivery_kw - W_elec_kw   // [kW] Electrical Power Input & Heat Source Extraction Reference: First Law: Q_H = W_elec + Q_source; W = Q_H / COP — ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.4

E_elec_kwh_yr = W_elec_kw * operating_hours_yr; Q_heat_kwh_yr = Q_delivery_kw * operating_hours_yr; E_gas_saved_kwh_yr = Q_heat_kwh_yr / (boiler_efficiency_pct / 100)   // [kWh/yr] Annual Electrical Energy & Gas Equivalent Saved Reference: Annual gas displaced = Q_H / η_boiler; electricity consumed = W × h_op

CO2_gas_saved_t = E_gas_saved_kwh_yr * gas_emission_factor_kg_kwh / 1000; CO2_elec_consumed_t = E_elec_kwh_yr * grid_emission_factor_kg_kwh / 1000; CO2_net_saving_t = CO2_gas_saved_t - CO2_elec_consumed_t   // [tCO₂e/yr] Annual CO₂ Savings Reference: Scope 1 gas avoided minus Scope 2 electricity added — GHG Protocol §5

CI_breakeven_kg_kwh = CO2_gas_saved_t * 1000 / E_elec_kwh_yr   // [kgCO₂/kWh] Break-Even Grid Carbon Intensity (Grid Decarbonisation Threshold) Reference: If grid EF > CI_breakeven, heat pump increases net carbon; below CI_breakeven → net carbon saving

V1.error_msg: Delivery temperature ≤ source temperature + 5 K: heat pump provides no thermodynamic lift — direct heat exchange is appropriate instead

V2.error_msg: COP < 2.0: marginal heat pump benefit — evaluate if Carnot efficiency factor is correct; large temperature lift with low η may not justify capital investment

V3.error_msg: Net carbon saving is negative: current grid carbon intensity is too high — heat pump increases CO₂ vs gas boiler; revisit when grid decarbonises below CI_breakeven

smart_warnings.message: Delivery > 120°C: high-temperature heat pump (HTHP) territory — limited commercial units available; verify refrigerant rating (R245fa, R1234ze(Z)) per ASHRAE 34
Low COP on high-carbon grid: net carbon reduction minimal — prioritise grid decarbonisation timeline in investment decision
Very high Carnot COP: small temperature lift — confirm source availability is reliable year-round; COP may fall significantly in winter if source temperature drops
Large heat source extraction (>70% of delivery): verify source stream capacity and minimum outlet temperature — excessive extraction degrades source quality
Break-even grid intensity > 0.5 kgCO₂/kWh: project is carbon-positive now and in most future grid scenarios — strong decarbonisation case; proceed

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_delivery_c <= T_source_c + 5
fmea.effect: Delivery temperature ≤ source temperature + 5 K: heat pump provides no thermodynamic lift — direct heat exchange is appropriate instead
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: COP_HP < 2.0
fmea.effect: COP < 2.0: marginal heat pump benefit — evaluate if Carnot efficiency factor is correct; large temperature lift with low η may not justify capital investment
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CO2_net_saving_t < 0
fmea.effect: Net carbon saving is negative: current grid carbon intensity is too high — heat pump increases CO₂ vs gas boiler; revisit when grid decarbonises below CI_breakeven
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Delivery > 120°C: high-temperature heat pump (HTHP) territory — limited commercial units available; verify refrigerant rating (R245fa, R1234ze(Z)) per ASHRAE 34
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_delivery_c > 120

fmea.failureMode: Low COP on high-carbon grid: net carbon reduction minimal — prioritise grid decarbonisation timeline in investment decision
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP_HP < 3 && grid_emission_factor_kg_kwh > 0.35

fmea.failureMode: Very high Carnot COP: small temperature lift — confirm source availability is reliable year-round; COP may fall significantly in winter if source temperature drops
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP_Carnot > 15

fmea.failureMode: Large heat source extraction (>70% of delivery): verify source stream capacity and minimum outlet temperature — excessive extraction degrades source quality
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_source_kw > Q_delivery_kw * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Break-even grid intensity > 0.5 kgCO₂/kWh: project is carbon-positive now and in most future grid scenarios — strong decarbonisation case; proceed
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CI_breakeven_kg_kwh > 0.5

tool_name: Atmospheric Storage Tank Venting — Normal Breathing, Liquid Movement & Emergency Fire Relief

inputs.name: Tank Net Working Capacity (V_tank)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Maximum Fill / Pump-In Rate (Q_in)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Maximum Draw / Pump-Out Rate (Q_out)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Product Flash Point (T_flash)
inputs.note: Gasoline < 0°C; diesel ≈ 55°C; heavy fuel oil ≈ 70–120°C; crude oil varies

inputs.name: Product Reid Vapour Pressure (RVP)
inputs.note: Gasoline ≈ 55–90 kPa; crude ≈ 40–100 kPa; diesel < 10 kPa; water ≈ 2.3 kPa at 20°C
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Tank Outside Diameter (D_tank)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Maximum Product & Ambient Temperature (T_design)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: V_thermal_inbreath_Nm3h = 0.98 * Math.pow(tank_capacity_m3, 0.9) * 0.001 * 1000 / 24   // [Nm³/h] Thermal In-Breathing (Vacuum) — Daily Temperature Drop Reference: API 2000-7th Ed. Table 2 — thermal in-breathing: Y_IB = 0.98V^0.9 (m³/day); hourly rate

RVP_factor = (product_RVP_kpa > 13.8) ? 1.0 + (product_RVP_kpa - 13.8) * 0.01 : 1.0; V_thermal_outbreath_Nm3h = 0.94 * Math.pow(tank_capacity_m3, 0.9) * RVP_factor * 1000 / 24   // [Nm³/h] Thermal Out-Breathing (Pressure) — Daily Temperature Rise Reference: API 2000 Table 3 — thermal out-breathing with RVP correction; Y_OB = 0.94V^0.9 × F_RVP

V_fill_vent_Nm3h = fill_rate_m3h * 1.01; V_draw_vent_Nm3h = draw_rate_m3h * 1.01   // [Nm³/h] Liquid Movement Venting (In & Out) Reference: API 2000 §5.3.2 — liquid movement venting: 1 m³ liquid in/out displaces 1.01 Nm³ vapour (density correction)

V_total_out_Nm3h = V_thermal_outbreath_Nm3h + V_fill_vent_Nm3h; V_total_in_Nm3h = V_thermal_inbreath_Nm3h + V_draw_vent_Nm3h   // [Nm³/h] Total Normal Vent Capacity Required (Pressure & Vacuum) Reference: API 2000 §5.3.3 — normal vent sizing: combine thermal and liquid movement conservatively

A_wetted_m2 = Math.PI * tank_diameter_m * Math.min(9.14, Math.sqrt(tank_capacity_m3 / (Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(tank_diameter_m, 2)))); Q_fire_kw = 43200 * Math.pow(A_wetted_m2, 0.82) / 3600; lambda_evap_kj_kg = 270; rho_vapour = 3.0; V_fire_Nm3h = Q_fire_kw * 3600 / (lambda_evap_kj_kg * rho_vapour)   // [Nm³/h] Emergency Fire Relief Venting (API 2000 §5.4) Reference: API 2000 §5.4.2 Eq.(1): Q_fire = 43200·F·A^0.82 (BTU/hr) / 3600; λ and ρ_vapour for petroleum product

V_governing_out = Math.max(V_total_out_Nm3h, V_fire_Nm3h); V_governing_in = V_total_in_Nm3h; is_emergency_governing = V_fire_Nm3h > V_total_out_Nm3h   // [Nm³/h] Governing Vent Size — Required Flow vs Emergency Reference: Governing capacity: larger of normal vent and emergency fire vent determines PV valve / conservation vent size

V1.error_msg: Flammable vapour at ambient: tank vapour space above flash point — Class I flammable liquid requires NFPA 30 §22.6 emergency vent; flame arrester mandatory

V2.error_msg: Fill rate > 50% of capacity per hour: very high fill velocity — verify anti-static precautions and bottom fill pipe velocity ≤ 1 m/s per API 2003

V3.error_msg: High RVP product at elevated temperature: significant vapour generation — evaluate floating roof tank or nitrogen blanketing to reduce VOC emissions and fire risk

smart_warnings.message: Emergency fire venting governs: install dedicated emergency pressure-relief device per API 2000 §5.4 — PV valve alone may not provide sufficient fire relief capacity
Large vent flow (>5000 Nm³/h): multiple PV valves or large conservation vent required — coordinate with atmospheric dispersion model for vapour cloud assessment
Large flammable product tank: Category I tank per NFPA 30 — secondary containment bunding, foam deluge system, and thermal radiation analysis required
High temperature + high RVP: thermal out-breathing significantly elevated — verify vent device set pressure and flow rating at T_design per EN 14015 §15.4
No liquid movement: vent sized for thermal breathing only — verify fill/draw rates and confirm tank is not a standby tank with infrequent but fast filling

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: product_flash_c < 23 && design_temp_c > product_flash_c
fmea.effect: Flammable vapour at ambient: tank vapour space above flash point — Class I flammable liquid requires NFPA 30 §22.6 emergency vent; flame arrester mandatory
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fill_rate_m3h > tank_capacity_m3 * 0.5
fmea.effect: Fill rate > 50% of capacity per hour: very high fill velocity — verify anti-static precautions and bottom fill pipe velocity ≤ 1 m/s per API 2003
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: product_RVP_kpa > 110 && design_temp_c > 35
fmea.effect: High RVP product at elevated temperature: significant vapour generation — evaluate floating roof tank or nitrogen blanketing to reduce VOC emissions and fire risk
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Emergency fire venting governs: install dedicated emergency pressure-relief device per API 2000 §5.4 — PV valve alone may not provide sufficient fire relief capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: is_emergency_governing

fmea.failureMode: Large vent flow (>5000 Nm³/h): multiple PV valves or large conservation vent required — coordinate with atmospheric dispersion model for vapour cloud assessment
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: V_governing_out > 5000

fmea.failureMode: Large flammable product tank: Category I tank per NFPA 30 — secondary containment bunding, foam deluge system, and thermal radiation analysis required
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: product_flash_c < 60 && tank_capacity_m3 > 10000

fmea.failureMode: High temperature + high RVP: thermal out-breathing significantly elevated — verify vent device set pressure and flow rating at T_design per EN 14015 §15.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_temp_c > 50 && product_RVP_kpa > 60

fmea.failureMode: No liquid movement: vent sized for thermal breathing only — verify fill/draw rates and confirm tank is not a standby tank with infrequent but fast filling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fill_rate_m3h === 0 && draw_rate_m3h === 0

tool_name: Activated Sludge Aeration Design — Oxygen Demand, Tank Volume, MLSS & Blower Power

inputs.name: Average Daily Wastewater Flow (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Influent BOD₅ Concentration (S_0)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Required Effluent BOD₅ (S_e)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Influent Total Kjeldahl Nitrogen (TKN)
inputs.note: Domestic WW: TKN ≈ 30–60 mg/L; industrial can be higher
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Design Sludge Retention Time (SRT / θ_c)
inputs.note: Nitrification SRT > 8–12 days at 15°C; ATV-DVWK A-131 §4.2
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Design Mixed Liquor Suspended Solids (MLSS)
inputs.note: Conventional AS: 2000–4000 mg/L; MBR: 8000–12000 mg/L
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Aeration System Transfer Efficiency (AE)
inputs.note: Fine bubble diffuser (submerged 4m) ≈ 1.8–2.5 kgO₂/kWh; surface aerator ≈ 1.2–1.8; coarse bubble ≈ 0.8–1.2
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

formulas: BOD_removed_mg_l = BOD5_influent_mg_l - BOD5_effluent_mg_l; Y_obs = 0.5 / (1 + 0.06 * SRT_days); P_x_kg_d = Q_flow_m3d * BOD_removed_mg_l * Y_obs / 1000; OD_carb_kg_d = Q_flow_m3d * BOD_removed_mg_l / 1000 - 1.42 * P_x_kg_d   // [kgO₂/day] BOD Removal & Carbonaceous Oxygen Demand (COD_carb) Reference: ATV-DVWK A-131 §5.2 — carbonaceous oxygen demand: OD_C = Q·ΔS − 1.42·P_x; Y_obs = Y/(1+k_d·SRT)

N_oxidised_mg_l = TKN_influent_mg_l * 0.85; OD_N_kg_d = Q_flow_m3d * N_oxidised_mg_l * 4.57 / 1000   // [kgO₂/day] Nitrification Oxygen Demand (OD_N) Reference: ATV-DVWK A-131 §5.3 — nitrification: 4.57 kgO₂/kg N oxidised; 85% of TKN available

OD_total_kg_d = OD_carb_kg_d + OD_N_kg_d; OD_peak_kg_h = OD_total_kg_d / 24 * 2.0   // [kgO₂/day, kgO₂/h] Total Oxygen Demand & Peak Factor Reference: Peak factor 2.0× average for diurnal load variation — WEF MOP 8 §10; blower sized for peak

VSS_fraction = 0.75; X_VSS = MLSS_mg_l * VSS_fraction; V_tank_m3 = SRT_days * P_x_kg_d * 1000 / X_VSS   // [m³] Aeration Tank Volume (V_tank) Reference: V = SRT × P_x / X_VSS — ATV-DVWK A-131 §6.1; VSS/TSS = 0.75 typical
HRT_h = V_tank_m3 / (Q_flow_m3d / 24)   // [hours] Hydraulic Retention Time (HRT) Reference: HRT = V/Q; minimum 4–6 h for BOD removal; 8–16 h for nitrification per EN 12255-7
P_blower_kw = OD_peak_kg_h / aeration_efficiency_kg_kwh   // [kW] Blower Power Requirement (P_blower) Reference: P_blower = OD_peak / AE; AE from submergence depth and diffuser SOTE per ISO 18358

V1.error_msg: SRT < 8 days with significant TKN: nitrification will be incomplete at 15°C — increase SRT to minimum 10–12 days for reliable nitrification per ATV-DVWK A-131

V2.error_msg: HRT < 4 hours: insufficient contact time for BOD removal — increase tank volume or reduce influent flow; minimum 4 h for carbonaceous removal

V3.error_msg: High MLSS with high AE assumption: alpha factor for fine bubble diffusers drops sharply above 5000 mg/L MLSS — use α ≤ 0.45 correction in actual SOTE calculation

smart_warnings.message: Peak-to-average ratio > 2.5: highly variable load — consider flow equalisation basin to reduce blower oversizing and energy cost
Blower power > 500 kW: aeration is major energy consumer — evaluate turbo blowers over lobe type (15–20% energy saving) and DO control with AMP §4.3
HRT > 20 hours: over-designed for carbonaceous removal — evaluate extended aeration (no primary clarifier) or reduce SRT to match required effluent quality
Low aeration efficiency (<1.2 kgO₂/kWh): coarse bubble or surface aerator — fine bubble diffuser upgrade typically saves 30–40% energy; evaluate capital vs operating cost
Stringent effluent BOD target with short SRT: risk of effluent quality exceedance during peak loads — increase SRT to 12–15 days to ensure robust nitrification and BOD polishing

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SRT_days < 8 && TKN_influent_mg_l > 20
fmea.effect: SRT < 8 days with significant TKN: nitrification will be incomplete at 15°C — increase SRT to minimum 10–12 days for reliable nitrification per ATV-DVWK A-131
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HRT_h < 4
fmea.effect: HRT < 4 hours: insufficient contact time for BOD removal — increase tank volume or reduce influent flow; minimum 4 h for carbonaceous removal
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: MLSS_mg_l > 6000 && aeration_efficiency_kg_kwh > 2.5
fmea.effect: High MLSS with high AE assumption: alpha factor for fine bubble diffusers drops sharply above 5000 mg/L MLSS — use α ≤ 0.45 correction in actual SOTE calculation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Peak-to-average ratio > 2.5: highly variable load — consider flow equalisation basin to reduce blower oversizing and energy cost
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OD_peak_kg_h / OD_total_kg_d * 24 > 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Blower power > 500 kW: aeration is major energy consumer — evaluate turbo blowers over lobe type (15–20% energy saving) and DO control with AMP §4.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_blower_kw > 500

fmea.failureMode: HRT > 20 hours: over-designed for carbonaceous removal — evaluate extended aeration (no primary clarifier) or reduce SRT to match required effluent quality
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: HRT_h > 20

fmea.failureMode: Low aeration efficiency (<1.2 kgO₂/kWh): coarse bubble or surface aerator — fine bubble diffuser upgrade typically saves 30–40% energy; evaluate capital vs operating cost
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: aeration_efficiency_kg_kwh < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Stringent effluent BOD target with short SRT: risk of effluent quality exceedance during peak loads — increase SRT to 12–15 days to ensure robust nitrification and BOD polishing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BOD5_effluent_mg_l < 10 && SRT_days < 12

tool_name: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life

inputs.name: Applied Torque (T)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 6789:2017 §6 — Class B torque tool: ±2% of set value

inputs.name: Wire Diameter (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Mean Coil Diameter (D_m)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Number of Active Coils (N_a)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Wire Material

options.label: Music wire ASTM A228 — E=206 GPa, Su=2060/d^0.163 MPa
Hard-drawn MB ASTM A227 — E=196 GPa, Su=1510/d^0.201 MPa
Chrome-silicon SAE 9254 — E=203 GPa, Su=1960/d^0.108 MPa
Stainless 302/304 ASTM A313 — E=193 GPa, Su=1867/d^0.146 MPa

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Required Angular Deflection (θ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Required Fatigue Life (N_f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: C = mean_coil_diameter_mm / wire_diameter_mm; K_i = (4*C*C - C - 1) / (4*C*(C-1))   // [dimensionless] Spring Index (C) & Wahl Correction Factor (K_i) Reference: SAE HS-795 §7.3 — inner surface correction: K_i = (4C²−C−1)/(4C(C−1)); valid for C = 3–12

sigma_b_mpa = K_i * 32 * torque_Nm * 1000 / (Math.PI * Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, 3))   // [MPa] Maximum Bending Stress in Wire (σ_b) Reference: DIN 2088 §5 / ISO 26909 — σ_b = K_i · 32T / πd³; bending dominates in torsion springs

const SU_params = {A228:[2060,0.163], A227:[1510,0.201], '9254':[1960,0.108], A313:[1867,0.146]}; const p = SU_params[wire_material]; Su_mpa = p[0] / Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, p[1])   // [MPa] Material Ultimate Tensile Strength (S_u) Reference: ASTM material strength curves — SAE HS-795 Table 1; Su = A/d^m (d in mm)

sigma_allow_mpa = 0.78 * Su_mpa; SF_s = sigma_allow_mpa / sigma_b_mpa   // [dimensionless, MPa] Static Safety Factor (SF_s) & Allowable Bending Stress Reference: SAE HS-795 §7.6 — static allowable: σ_allow = 0.78·S_u for torsion springs (bending mode)

const E_map = {A228:206000, A227:196000, '9254':203000, A313:193000}; E_mpa = E_map[wire_material]; R_T_Nm_deg = E_mpa * Math.PI * Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, 4) / (3667 * mean_coil_diameter_mm * num_active_coils); T_calc_Nm = R_T_Nm_deg * angular_deflection_deg   // [N·m/°, N·m] Angular Spring Rate (R_T) & Stiffness Check Reference: DIN 2088 — R_T = Ed⁴/(3667·D_m·N_a) in N·m/°; E in MPa, d,D_m in mm

Se_mpa = (fatigue_cycles >= 1e7) ? 0.45 * Su_mpa : 0.45 * Su_mpa * Math.pow(fatigue_cycles / 1e7, -0.085); SF_f = Se_mpa / sigma_b_mpa   // [dimensionless, MPa] Fatigue Endurance Safety Factor (SF_f) Reference: SAE HS-795 §8 — fatigue allowable: S_e = 0.45·S_u at 10⁷ cycles; slope −0.085 for finite life

V1.error_msg: Spring index C outside 3–16: C < 3 causes severe coiling stress; C > 16 causes tangling — redesign with D_m/d ratio in 4–12 range

V2.error_msg: Static safety factor < 1.0: wire will yield at applied torque — increase wire diameter, reduce torque, or use higher-strength material

V3.error_msg: Torque mismatch > 20%: spring geometry inconsistent with required torque at given deflection — verify N_a, θ, and T inputs

smart_warnings.message: Fatigue safety factor < 1.2: spring fatigue failure likely before target life — increase wire diameter, reduce stress ratio, or apply shot peening (+20% endurance)
Static SF < 1.5: marginal — spring may take permanent set under peak torque; consider SF ≥ 2.0 for critical applications per SAE HS-795 §7.6
Bending stress > 90% of UTS: spring operating near material limit — significant risk of brittle fracture; redesign with larger wire or lower C
SS wire at high stress: austenitic stainless has lower fatigue limit than music wire — consider Elgiloy or 17-7PH for high-cycle SS applications
Deflection approaching coil binding: spring may bind before reaching full deflection — verify body length and free-length tolerance

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: C < 3 || C > 16
fmea.effect: Spring index C outside 3–16: C < 3 causes severe coiling stress; C > 16 causes tangling — redesign with D_m/d ratio in 4–12 range
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SF_s < 1.0
fmea.effect: Static safety factor < 1.0: wire will yield at applied torque — increase wire diameter, reduce torque, or use higher-strength material
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Math.abs(T_calc_Nm - torque_Nm) / torque_Nm > 0.20
fmea.effect: Torque mismatch > 20%: spring geometry inconsistent with required torque at given deflection — verify N_a, θ, and T inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue safety factor < 1.2: spring fatigue failure likely before target life — increase wire diameter, reduce stress ratio, or apply shot peening (+20% endurance)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_f < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Static SF < 1.5: marginal — spring may take permanent set under peak torque; consider SF ≥ 2.0 for critical applications per SAE HS-795 §7.6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_s < 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Bending stress > 90% of UTS: spring operating near material limit — significant risk of brittle fracture; redesign with larger wire or lower C
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma_b_mpa > 0.9 * Su_mpa

fmea.failureMode: SS wire at high stress: austenitic stainless has lower fatigue limit than music wire — consider Elgiloy or 17-7PH for high-cycle SS applications
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wire_material === 'A313' && sigma_b_mpa > 700

fmea.failureMode: Deflection approaching coil binding: spring may bind before reaching full deflection — verify body length and free-length tolerance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: angular_deflection_deg > 360 * num_active_coils * 0.8

tool_name: Dust Explosion Severity — Kst Classification, Pmax & Vent Area per EN 14491 / NFPA 68

inputs.name: Dust Explosion Constant (K_st)
inputs.note: From ISO 6184-1 20-L sphere test; St1: 1–200; St2: 201–300; St3: >300 bar·m/s
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Maximum Explosion Pressure (P_max)
inputs.note: From 20-L sphere test; most organic dusts: P_max = 7–10 bar(g)
