inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Protected Enclosure Volume (V)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Maximum Reduced Explosion Pressure (P_red)
inputs.note: Structural strength of enclosure; thin sheet metal ≈ 0.05–0.1 bar(g); reinforced ≈ 0.5–2.0 bar(g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Vent Closure Opening Pressure (P_stat)
inputs.note: Bursting disc or vent panel opening pressure; must be < P_red; typical 0.02–0.1 bar(g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Enclosure Length-to-Diameter Ratio (L/D)
inputs.note: Silos/bins with L/D > 5: turbulence correction required; EN 14491 §5.2.3
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Dust Explosion Class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_v_m2 = Math.pow(10, (0.569 * Math.log10(Kst_bar_m_s) - 0.5 * Math.log10(Pred_bar + 1) + 0.4 * Math.log10(vessel_volume_m3) - 0.595))   // [m²] Vent Area — EN 14491 Nomograph Equation (A_v) Reference: EN 14491:2012 Eq.(1) — vent area from K_st, P_red, V nomograph; valid for P_stat ≤ 0.1 bar(g)
C_stat = (Pstat_bar > 0.1) ? 1 + 0.6 * Pstat_bar : 1.0   // [dimensionless] P_stat Correction Factor (C_stat) Reference: EN 14491 §5.2.2 — P_stat correction applied when P_stat > 0.1 bar(g)

C_LD = (L_D_ratio <= 2) ? 1.0 : (L_D_ratio <= 5) ? 1 + 0.05 * (L_D_ratio - 2) : 1.15 + 0.10 * (L_D_ratio - 5)   // [dimensionless] L/D Elongated Vessel Correction Factor (C_LD) Reference: EN 14491 §5.2.3 — elongation factor for L/D > 2; St3 dusts in elongated vessels require additional analysis
A_v_design_m2 = A_v_m2 * C_stat * C_LD   // [m²] Final Required Vent Area (A_v_design) Reference: EN 14491 §5.2 — final design area including all correction factors

A_nfpa_m2 = Math.exp(-1.958 + 0.8759 * Math.log(vessel_volume_m3) + 0.5765 * Math.log(Kst_bar_m_s) - 1.3985 * Math.log(Pred_bar + 1))   // [m²] NFPA 68 Vent Area Cross-Check (Low-Strength Enclosures) Reference: NFPA 68-2018 Eq.(6.9) — alternate vent area for cross-verification; EN and NFPA should agree within 20%

Av_V_ratio = A_v_design_m2 / vessel_volume_m3; governing = (A_v_design_m2 >= A_nfpa_m2) ? 'EN 14491 governs' : 'NFPA 68 governs'   // [m²/m³, text] Vent-to-Volume Ratio & Governing Standard Reference: Conservative: use larger of EN and NFPA result; typical ratio 0.005–0.1 m²/m³

V1.error_msg: P_stat ≥ P_red: vent will not open before structural failure — P_stat must be ≤ 0.5 × P_red per EN 14491 §4.2

V2.error_msg: P_red ≥ P_max: structurally the enclosure withstands full explosion — venting not required for protection; verify intent

V3.error_msg: St3 dust in elongated vessel: EN 14491 nomograph validity limit exceeded — perform CFD or consult VDI 2263 Part 7 for St3 + L/D > 5 combinations

smart_warnings.message: St3 dust: highly energetic explosion — venting alone may be insufficient; evaluate suppression system, inherently safe design, or inerting as primary protection per NFPA 69
Vent area > 50% of vessel volume (proxy): very large vent relative to enclosure — verify structural integrity of remaining panels; consider suppression as alternative
EN and NFPA results differ by > 30%: at boundary conditions or outside nomograph validity — seek qualified dust explosion specialist review
Very low P_red on large volume: extremely large vent area required — light-duty enclosures (P_red < 0.07 bar) unsuitable for large dust collectors; reinforce or use suppression
High-end St1 approaching St2 boundary: ensure test conditions (concentration, moisture, particle size) represent worst-case — conservative K_st selection critical for vent sizing

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pstat_bar >= Pred_bar
fmea.effect: P_stat ≥ P_red: vent will not open before structural failure — P_stat must be ≤ 0.5 × P_red per EN 14491 §4.2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pred_bar >= Pmax_bar
fmea.effect: P_red ≥ P_max: structurally the enclosure withstands full explosion — venting not required for protection; verify intent
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dust_class === 'St3' && L_D_ratio > 5
fmea.effect: St3 dust in elongated vessel: EN 14491 nomograph validity limit exceeded — perform CFD or consult VDI 2263 Part 7 for St3 + L/D > 5 combinations
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: St3 dust: highly energetic explosion — venting alone may be insufficient; evaluate suppression system, inherently safe design, or inerting as primary protection per NFPA 69
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Kst_bar_m_s > 300

fmea.failureMode: Vent area > 50% of vessel volume (proxy): very large vent relative to enclosure — verify structural integrity of remaining panels; consider suppression as alternative
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_v_design_m2 > vessel_volume_m3 * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: EN and NFPA results differ by > 30%: at boundary conditions or outside nomograph validity — seek qualified dust explosion specialist review
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Math.abs(A_v_design_m2 - A_nfpa_m2) / A_v_design_m2 > 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Very low P_red on large volume: extremely large vent area required — light-duty enclosures (P_red < 0.07 bar) unsuitable for large dust collectors; reinforce or use suppression
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pred_bar < 0.07 && vessel_volume_m3 > 100

fmea.failureMode: High-end St1 approaching St2 boundary: ensure test conditions (concentration, moisture, particle size) represent worst-case — conservative K_st selection critical for vent sizing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dust_class === 'St1' && Kst_bar_m_s > 150

tool_name: Roof Truss Member Force & Joint Reaction — Simple Warren / Pratt Configuration

inputs.name: Truss Clear Span (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Truss Depth at Mid-Span (h)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Number of Top Chord Panels (N)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Dead Load — Roof Cladding + Self-Weight (g_k)
inputs.note: Metal deck + insulation ≈ 0.4–0.7 kN/m²; concrete slab ≈ 3–5 kN/m²
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Imposed / Snow Load (q_k)
inputs.note: EN 1991-1-3 snow or ASCE 7-22 §7 — regional ground snow × shape factor
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Design Wind Uplift Pressure (w_u)
inputs.note: Negative (uplift) wind pressure; ASCE 7-22 §27 or EN 1991-1-4 Cp,e
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Tributary Width (Truss Spacing) (b_trib)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Steel Grade — Yield Strength (F_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: w_dead_kn_m = dead_load_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m; w_live_kn_m = live_load_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m; w_factored_kn_m = 1.2 * w_dead_kn_m + 1.6 * w_live_kn_m; panel_length_m = truss_span_m / num_panels; P_u_kn = w_factored_kn_m * panel_length_m   // [kN] Factored Panel Point Load (P_u) — LRFD / ULS Reference: ASCE 7-22 §2.3.1 LRFD: 1.2D + 1.6L; EN 1990 ULS: 1.35G + 1.5Q
n_joints = num_panels - 1; R_kn = (n_joints + 2) * P_u_kn / 2   // [kN] End Reactions (R_A = R_B) — Symmetric Loading Reference: Simple truss with symmetric loading: R = n_panel_points × P_u / 2

M_max_knm = R_kn * truss_span_m / 2 - P_u_kn * panel_length_m * num_panels / 4; F_BChord_kn = M_max_knm / truss_height_m   // [kN] Maximum Bottom Chord Tension (F_BChord) — Mid-Span Reference: Method of sections: F_BC = M_max / h; maximum mid-span moment for symmetric loading

panel_diagonal_m = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(panel_length_m, 2) + Math.pow(truss_height_m, 2)); theta_rad = Math.atan(truss_height_m / panel_length_m); F_TChord_kn = F_BChord_kn / Math.cos(theta_rad) * (-1)   // [kN] Maximum Top Chord Compression (F_TChord) Reference: Top chord compression = −F_BC / cosθ at mid-span panel; θ = roof pitch angle

V_max_kn = R_kn - P_u_kn; F_diag_kn = V_max_kn / Math.sin(theta_rad)   // [kN] Maximum Web Diagonal Force (F_diag) — Near Support Reference: Maximum shear occurs at first interior panel; F_diag = V_max / sinθ

phi_t = 0.90; A_req_mm2 = F_BChord_kn * 1000 / (phi_t * steel_grade_fy_mpa); wind_uplift_factor = wind_uplift_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m * panel_length_m   // [mm², kN/panel] Required Section Area — Bottom Chord Tension (A_req) Reference: AISC 360-22 D2-1: φP_n = φ_t·F_y·A_g; φ_t=0.90 for yielding; wind uplift checked separately

V1.error_msg: Truss depth-to-span ratio < 1:20: very shallow truss — deflection and chord force will be excessive; minimum h/L = 1/10 recommended per AISC DG11

V2.error_msg: Wind uplift > 90% of dead load: net uplift possible under 0.9D + 1.0W — bottom chord becomes compression; verify buckling capacity and purlin connections

V3.error_msg: Less than 4 panels: very steep diagonal angle — excessive web member forces; use minimum 6 panels for efficient truss geometry

smart_warnings.message: Long shallow truss: chord forces very large — chord section and connection design critical; camber ≥ L/500 required per AISC Code of Standard Practice
Top chord compression > 2000 kN: heavy member required — verify lateral buckling between purlin points per AISC 360-22 Chapter E; ensure continuous lateral restraint
High-strength steel on long span: deflection (not strength) may govern — S460 reduces weight but deflection limit L/360 still applies; verify serviceability
Light dead load with heavy imposed load: dynamic amplification possible for crowds or machinery — check vibration criteria per SCI P354 or AISC DG11
Required bottom chord area > 15,000 mm²: very large section — consider HSS, built-up section, or reduce span with intermediate column; verify connection capacity at joints

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: truss_height_m / truss_span_m < 0.05
fmea.effect: Truss depth-to-span ratio < 1:20: very shallow truss — deflection and chord force will be excessive; minimum h/L = 1/10 recommended per AISC DG11
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: wind_uplift_kn_m2 > dead_load_kn_m2 * 0.9
fmea.effect: Wind uplift > 90% of dead load: net uplift possible under 0.9D + 1.0W — bottom chord becomes compression; verify buckling capacity and purlin connections
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: num_panels < 4
fmea.effect: Less than 4 panels: very steep diagonal angle — excessive web member forces; use minimum 6 panels for efficient truss geometry
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: truss_span_m > 40 && truss_height_m / truss_span_m < 0.08

fmea.failureMode: Top chord compression > 2000 kN: heavy member required — verify lateral buckling between purlin points per AISC 360-22 Chapter E; ensure continuous lateral restraint
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_TChord_kn > 2000

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_req_mm2 > 15000

tool_name: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure

inputs.name: Pipe Inside Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Design (Maximum) Volume Flow Rate at Operating Conditions (Q_op)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Fluid Density at Operating Conditions (ρ)
inputs.note: Gas: use operating density ρ = PM/(ZRT); liquid: use process temperature density
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Fluid Dynamic Viscosity at Operating Conditions (μ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Maximum Allowable Differential Pressure (ΔP_max)
inputs.note: Transmitter range; higher ΔP → better accuracy; lower ΔP → lower permanent pressure loss
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Pressure Tap Type

options.label: Corner taps — ISO 5167 standard, most common
Flange taps (1 inch from faces) — ASME / AGA 3

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Gas Isentropic Exponent (κ) — Set 1.0 for liquid
inputs.note: Liquid κ = 1.0 (no expansibility correction); air κ = 1.4; steam ≈ 1.3; natural gas ≈ 1.27
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_pipe_m_s = design_flow_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re_D = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)   // [m/s, dimensionless] Pipe Velocity & Reynolds Number at Design Flow Reference: ISO 5167-1:2022 §6.1 — pipe Reynolds number determines discharge coefficient validity

Cd_est = 0.6; epsilon = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - 0.41 * Math.pow(0.5, 1/isentropic_exponent) : 1.0; beta = Math.pow(1 / (1 + Math.pow(Cd_est * epsilon * A_pipe_m2 * Math.sqrt(2 * max_dP_kpa * 1000 / fluid_density_kg_m3) / (design_flow_m3h / 3600), 2)), 0.25)   // [dimensionless] Required Beta Ratio (β) from ΔP and Flow Reference: ISO 5167-2 §5 — iterate β from Q = Cd·ε·(π/4)·d²·√(2ΔP/ρ·(1−β⁴)^−0.5); initial Cd=0.60

b4 = Math.pow(beta, 4); A_coef = (19000 * beta / Re_D) < 1 ? 0 : 0.0188 + 0.0063 * Math.pow(19000 * beta / Re_D, 0.8); Cd = 0.5961 + 0.0261 * beta * beta - 0.216 * Math.pow(beta, 8) + 0.000521 * Math.pow(1e6 * beta / Re_D, 0.7) + (0.0188 + 0.0063 * A_coef) * Math.pow(beta, 3.5) * Math.pow(1e6 / Re_D, 0.3)   // [dimensionless] Refined Discharge Coefficient — Reader-Harris/Gallagher (RHG) Reference: ISO 5167-2:2022 Eq.(1) — Reader-Harris/Gallagher correlation for corner taps; uncertainty ±0.5% for 0.1 ≤ β ≤ 0.75
d_mm = beta * pipe_ID_mm   // [mm] Orifice Bore Diameter (d) Reference: d = β·D — orifice throat diameter

epsilon_act = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - (0.351 + 0.256 * Math.pow(beta, 4) + 0.93 * Math.pow(beta, 8)) * (1 - Math.pow(1 - max_dP_kpa * 1000 / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * v_pipe_m_s / 2 * 100), 1/isentropic_exponent)) : 1.0; dP_actual_kpa = Math.pow(design_flow_m3h / 3600 / (Cd * epsilon_act * A_pipe_m2 * Math.pow(beta, 2) / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4))), 2) * fluid_density_kg_m3 / 2 / 1000   // [kPa] Actual Differential Pressure at Design Flow (ΔP_actual) Reference: ISO 5167-2 Eq.(4) — rearranged for ΔP given Q, Cd, β, ε

E_factor = 1 / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4)); dP_perm_kpa = dP_actual_kpa * (1 - Math.pow(beta, 2)) * (1 - Cd * Math.pow(beta, 2) * E_factor)   // [kPa, dimensionless] Permanent Pressure Loss (ΔP_perm) & Velocity of Approach Factor Reference: ISO 5167-2 §5.1.7 — permanent pressure loss: ΔP_perm = ΔP·(1−β²)·(1−Cd·β²·E)

V1.error_msg: Beta ratio outside 0.20–0.75: ISO 5167-2 validity range exceeded — accuracy degrades sharply; select ΔP range to keep β in 0.30–0.65 for optimal accuracy

V2.error_msg: Pipe Reynolds number < 5000: ISO 5167 RHG equation loses accuracy below Re = 5000; consider Coriolis or magnetic flowmeter for low-Re applications

V3.error_msg: Permanent pressure loss > 80% of ΔP_max: high energy penalty — consider low-loss flow element (Venturi, V-cone, or Annubar) to reduce permanent loss

smart_warnings.message: β > 0.65: high beta ratio — discharge coefficient uncertainty increases; upstream straight pipe requirement extends to 44D (vs 10D at β=0.3); verify straight run length
High gas velocity (>30 m/s): compressibility and expansibility correction significant — verify ε factor is applied; choked flow check required if ΔP/P > 0.03
Actual ΔP exceeds transmitter range: flow measurement will saturate — increase beta ratio or specify higher-range transmitter
High viscosity fluid (>50 mPa·s): orifice plate accuracy degrades for viscous fluids — consider Coriolis or gear meter for μ > 100 mPa·s
Low Re with high β: Cd correction for low-Re is significant — apply RHG equation iteratively; 3–5 iterations typically required for convergence to ±0.1%

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: beta < 0.20 || beta > 0.75
fmea.effect: Beta ratio outside 0.20–0.75: ISO 5167-2 validity range exceeded — accuracy degrades sharply; select ΔP range to keep β in 0.30–0.65 for optimal accuracy
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Re_D < 5000
fmea.effect: Pipe Reynolds number < 5000: ISO 5167 RHG equation loses accuracy below Re = 5000; consider Coriolis or magnetic flowmeter for low-Re applications
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_perm_kpa > max_dP_kpa * 0.8
fmea.effect: Permanent pressure loss > 80% of ΔP_max: high energy penalty — consider low-loss flow element (Venturi, V-cone, or Annubar) to reduce permanent loss
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: β > 0.65: high beta ratio — discharge coefficient uncertainty increases; upstream straight pipe requirement extends to 44D (vs 10D at β=0.3); verify straight run length
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: beta > 0.65

fmea.failureMode: High gas velocity (>30 m/s): compressibility and expansibility correction significant — verify ε factor is applied; choked flow check required if ΔP/P > 0.03
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_pipe_m_s > 30 && fluid_density_kg_m3 < 5

fmea.failureMode: Actual ΔP exceeds transmitter range: flow measurement will saturate — increase beta ratio or specify higher-range transmitter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dP_actual_kpa > max_dP_kpa * 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High viscosity fluid (>50 mPa·s): orifice plate accuracy degrades for viscous fluids — consider Coriolis or gear meter for μ > 100 mPa·s
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_viscosity_mPas > 50

fmea.failureMode: Low Re with high β: Cd correction for low-Re is significant — apply RHG equation iteratively; 3–5 iterations typically required for convergence to ±0.1%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Re_D < 20000 && beta > 0.5

tool_name: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power

inputs.name: Total Part Surface Area to be Plated (A_part)
inputs.note: 1 dm² = 100 cm² = 0.01 m²; measure exposed area only
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Required Plating Thickness (δ)
inputs.note: ISO 2081 Zn: Fe/Zn 8c = 8 µm; Fe/Zn 25c = 25 µm; EN ISO 4527 Ni: min 25 µm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Metal to be Deposited
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Applied Current Density (i)
inputs.note: Zinc acid bath: 2–6 A/dm²; decorative Cr: 15–25 A/dm²; hard Cr: 25–60 A/dm²; Au (connector): 0.1–0.5 A/dm²
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Cathode Current Efficiency (η_c)
inputs.note: Zn acid: 95–99%; Ni sulfamate: 95–98%; Cr (hexavalent): 10–25%; Cu sulfate: 95–100%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Cell (Bath) Voltage (U_cell)
inputs.note: Zn ≈ 4–8 V; Ni ≈ 5–8 V; decorative Cr ≈ 6–12 V; hard Cr ≈ 12–24 V
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: const METALS = {Zn:{rho:7.13,M:65.38,n:2}, Ni:{rho:8.91,M:58.69,n:2}, Cr:{rho:7.19,M:52.0,n:6}, Cu:{rho:8.96,M:63.55,n:2}, Au:{rho:19.32,M:197.0,n:1}, Ag:{rho:10.49,M:107.87,n:1}}; const m = METALS[metal_deposited]; E_eq_g_Ah = m.M / (m.n * 26.8)   // [g/A·h] Metal Electrochemical Equivalent (E_eq) — Faraday's Law Reference: Faraday's law: E_eq = M/(n·F/3600) = M/(n·26.8); F=96485 C/mol — ISO 2081 §3.7

const m2 = METALS[metal_deposited]; r_dep_um_min = E_eq_g_Ah * current_density_a_dm2 * cathode_efficiency_pct / 100 * 10000 / (m2.rho * 60)   // [µm/min] Deposition Rate at Given Current Density (r_dep) Reference: r = E_eq·i·η_c / (ρ·60) [µm/min]; i in A/dm², ρ in g/cm³ — Faraday deposition rate
t_plate_min = target_thickness_um / r_dep_um_min   // [min] Required Plating Time (t_plate) Reference: t = δ / r_dep — ASTM B633 §10; batch cycle time determination
I_rect_a = current_density_a_dm2 * part_surface_area_dm2   // [A] Total Rectifier Current Required (I_rect) Reference: I = i × A_cathode — total current for all parts in bath simultaneously

P_rect_kw = I_rect_a * bath_voltage_v / 1000; E_spec_kw_dm2 = P_rect_kw / part_surface_area_dm2   // [kW, kW/dm²] Rectifier Power & Specific Energy Consumption Reference: P = U_cell × I; specific energy = P/A — rectifier sizing basis per MIL-STD-171

const m3 = METALS[metal_deposited]; W_deposit_g = E_eq_g_Ah * I_rect_a * t_plate_min / 60 * cathode_efficiency_pct / 100; W_anode_g = W_deposit_g / (cathode_efficiency_pct / 100) * 1.05   // [g] Metal Mass Deposited per Batch & Anode Consumption Reference: W = E_eq·I·t·η_c; anode consumption ≈ W_deposit / η_c × 1.05 (5% drag-out and oxidation loss)

V1.error_msg: Cathode efficiency < 20% for non-chromium process: abnormally low — check bath chemistry pH, temperature, and additive concentrations

V2.error_msg: Current density > 60 A/dm² for Cr: hard chrome territory — bath temperature control critical (50–60°C); burning and pitting risk above CD limit

V3.error_msg: Plating time > 2 hours: very long cycle — evaluate higher current density, tank agitation, or split into two baths; bath conductivity and thermal management critical for extended runs

smart_warnings.message: Chromium cathode efficiency > 30%: unusually high for hexavalent Cr bath — verify input; trivalent Cr (Cr³⁺) baths achieve 80–95% efficiency but require different parameters
Rectifier current > 10,000 A: industrial-scale rectifier — specify thyristor or IGBT rectifier with ±1% voltage regulation; busbar cross-section critical for voltage uniformity
High power input at elevated bath temperature: Joule heating will raise bath temperature — specify bath cooling system capacity as Q_cool ≈ P_rect × 0.6 kW
Gold plating > 5 µm: very high material cost — verify specification requires full gold; consider gold flash (0.5 µm) over nickel undercoat for connector applications per MIL-G-45204
Low CD on large area: very long plating time — evaluate rack density, multiple simultaneous racks, or continuous plating line for high-volume parts

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cathode_efficiency_pct < 20 && metal_deposited !== 'Cr'
fmea.effect: Cathode efficiency < 20% for non-chromium process: abnormally low — check bath chemistry pH, temperature, and additive concentrations
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: current_density_a_dm2 > 60 && metal_deposited === 'Cr'
fmea.effect: Current density > 60 A/dm² for Cr: hard chrome territory — bath temperature control critical (50–60°C); burning and pitting risk above CD limit
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: t_plate_min > 120
fmea.effect: Plating time > 2 hours: very long cycle — evaluate higher current density, tank agitation, or split into two baths; bath conductivity and thermal management critical for extended runs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Chromium cathode efficiency > 30%: unusually high for hexavalent Cr bath — verify input; trivalent Cr (Cr³⁺) baths achieve 80–95% efficiency but require different parameters
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: metal_deposited === 'Cr' && cathode_efficiency_pct > 30

fmea.failureMode: Rectifier current > 10,000 A: industrial-scale rectifier — specify thyristor or IGBT rectifier with ±1% voltage regulation; busbar cross-section critical for voltage uniformity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: I_rect_a > 10000

fmea.failureMode: High power input at elevated bath temperature: Joule heating will raise bath temperature — specify bath cooling system capacity as Q_cool ≈ P_rect × 0.6 kW
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_rect_kw > 100 && bath_temperature_c > 50

fmea.failureMode: Gold plating > 5 µm: very high material cost — verify specification requires full gold; consider gold flash (0.5 µm) over nickel undercoat for connector applications per MIL-G-45204
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: metal_deposited === 'Au' && target_thickness_um > 5

fmea.failureMode: Low CD on large area: very long plating time — evaluate rack density, multiple simultaneous racks, or continuous plating line for high-volume parts
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: current_density_a_dm2 < 1 && part_surface_area_dm2 > 1000

tool_name: Chiller Plant Free-Cooling Economiser — Switchover Temperature, Annual Hours & Energy Saving

inputs.name: Chiller Full-Load COP (rated condition)
inputs.note: AHRI 551/591 rating at 7/12°C CHWS/R, 30/35°C CWS/R
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Cooling Tower / Dry Cooler Approach Temperature (ΔT_approach)
inputs.note: Wet cooling tower: approach = T_CW,out − T_wb; dry cooler: approach = T_fluid,out − T_db

inputs.name: Plate Heat Exchanger Temperature Approach (ΔT_HX)
inputs.note: Brazed plate HX: ΔT_HX = 1.5–3 K; gasketed frame: 2–5 K

inputs.name: Design Peak Cooling Load (Q_design)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Annual Full-Load Equivalent Cooling Hours (FLEH)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Site Climate Zone — Bin Hours Below Switchover
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Electricity Tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: T_wb_sw_c = chilled_water_supply_c - approach_temp_k - heat_exchanger_dT_k  // [°C] — Switchover Wet-Bulb Temperature (T_wb,sw) — Ref: T_wb,sw = T_CHWS − ΔT_approach − ΔT_HX — ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.1.1; below this outdoor WB, free-cooling is feasible
h_FC = Math.min(site_climate_zone, annual_cooling_hours)  // [h/yr] — Annual Free-Cooling Hours (h_FC) — Ref: Free-cooling hours = min(bin hours below T_wb,sw, total cooling hours) — CIBSE TM21 §4.2
F_FC = h_FC / annual_cooling_hours  // Free-Cooling Fraction (F_FC) — Ref: Fraction of annual cooling delivered without mechanical refrigeration

E_chiller_base_kwh = design_cooling_load_kw / chiller_COP * annual_cooling_hours  // [kWh/yr] — Annual Chiller Energy — Baseline (E_chiller_base) — Ref: Baseline: all cooling via chiller at full-load COP — ASHRAE 90.1 §11 baseline building

pump_fan_parasitic_kw = design_cooling_load_kw * 0.03; E_saving_kwh = (design_cooling_load_kw / chiller_COP - pump_fan_parasitic_kw) * h_FC  // [kWh/yr] — Annual Energy Saving with Free-Cooling (E_saving) — Ref: E_saving = (Q/COP − P_parasitic) × h_FC; parasitic = 3% of load for CT fan + HX pump — CIBSE TM21 §5

cost_saving_usd = E_saving_kwh * electricity_rate_usd_kwh; HX_cost_est_usd = design_cooling_load_kw * 40; payback_yr = HX_cost_est_usd / cost_saving_usd  // [USD/yr, years] — Annual Cost Saving & Simple Payback vs HX Capital — Ref: HX installed cost ≈ 40 USD/kW (brazed plate, 2024 estimate); payback = CAPEX / annual saving

rules.message: Switchover WB temperature below 0°C in warm climate: free-cooling hours will be negligible — evaluate dry cooler or adiabatic pre-cooling to raise T_wb,sw
Very low CHWS temp with tight HX approach: fouling will increase ΔT_HX rapidly — specify minimum HX surface oversize of 20% per TEMA C
Free-cooling fraction > 80%: chiller operates less than 20% of time — evaluate chiller downsizing or VFD for part-load optimisation; chiller may fall below minimum unload point

smart_warnings.message: Free-cooling payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritise HX installation in next capital cycle; consider controls upgrade for automatic switchover per ASHRAE 90.1 §6.5.1
Warm climate with few free-cooling hours: marginal economic case — evaluate night-time free-cooling for data centres where T_wb is lower overnight
High cooling tower approach (>6 K): tower fouled or undersized — cleaning or replacement can recover 2–3 K and extend free-cooling season significantly
Free-cooling saves >50% of baseline chiller energy: very favourable climate — evaluate tri-mode (full/partial/zero mechanical) controls for transitional hours
Low-efficiency chiller in cold climate: both free-cooling AND chiller replacement are warranted — combined ROI should be modelled jointly

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: T_wb_sw_c < 0 && site_climate_zone < 1000
fmea.effect: Switchover WB temperature below 0°C in warm climate: free-cooling hours will be negligible — evaluate dry cooler or adiabatic pre-cooling to raise T_wb,sw

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: chilled_water_supply_c < 6 && heat_exchanger_dT_k < 1.5
fmea.effect: Very low CHWS temp with tight HX approach: fouling will increase ΔT_HX rapidly — specify minimum HX surface oversize of 20% per TEMA C

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: F_FC > 0.8
fmea.effect: Free-cooling fraction > 80%: chiller operates less than 20% of time — evaluate chiller downsizing or VFD for part-load optimisation; chiller may fall below minimum unload point

fmea.failureMode: Free-cooling payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritise HX installation in next capital cycle; consider controls upgrade for automatic switchover per ASHRAE 90.1 §6.5.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_yr < 2

fmea.failureMode: Warm climate with few free-cooling hours: marginal economic case — evaluate night-time free-cooling for data centres where T_wb is lower overnight
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: site_climate_zone < 500

fmea.failureMode: High cooling tower approach (>6 K): tower fouled or undersized — cleaning or replacement can recover 2–3 K and extend free-cooling season significantly
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: approach_temp_k > 6

fmea.failureMode: Free-cooling saves >50% of baseline chiller energy: very favourable climate — evaluate tri-mode (full/partial/zero mechanical) controls for transitional hours
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_saving_kwh > E_chiller_base_kwh * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Low-efficiency chiller in cold climate: both free-cooling AND chiller replacement are warranted — combined ROI should be modelled jointly
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: chiller_COP < 4 && site_climate_zone > 2000

tool_name: Cathodic Protection Design — Sacrificial Anode Current Demand, Mass & Service Life

inputs.name: Bare (Uncoated) Steel Surface Area (A_s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Area: √2 × linear ≈ ±1.4% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: Coating Breakdown Factor at End of Life (f_c)
inputs.note: DNV-RP-B401 Table 10-1: well-coated new: 3–5%; aged coating: 10–25%; bare/uncoated: 100%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Design Current Density (i_c) — Mean Value
inputs.note: DNV-RP-B401 Table 10-3: North Sea seabed ≈ 20–25 mA/m²; tropical shallow water ≈ 50–80 mA/m²; soil ≈ 10–30 mA/m²
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Cathodic Protection Design Life (T_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sacrificial Anode Material

options.label: Zinc (seawater) — ε=780 Ah/kg, E=−1.05 V vs Ag/AgCl

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Anode Utilisation Factor (u)
inputs.note: DNV-RP-B401 §6.4: bracelet anodes u=0.80; flush-mounted u=0.90; stand-off u=0.85
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Target Protection Potential (E_prot)

options.label: −900 mV — Elevated temperature or H₂S service

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_eff_m2 = structure_area_m2 * coating_breakdown_pct / 100  // [m²] — Exposed (Uncoated) Effective Area (A_eff) — Ref: A_eff = A_s × f_c — DNV-RP-B401 §6.2; coating breakdown factor applied to bare area
Ic_mean_A = A_eff_m2 * current_density_mA_m2 / 1000  // [A] — Mean Current Demand (I_c,mean) — Ref: I_c = i_c × A_eff — DNV-RP-B401 §6.3.1 Eq.(6.1)
Q_T_Ah = Ic_mean_A * design_life_yr * 8760  // [A·h] — Total Charge Required Over Design Life (Q_T) — Ref: Q_T = I_c,mean × T_d × 8760 — total coulombs required over design life

const ECA = {Zn:780, AlIn:2000, Mg:1230}[anode_material]; Ma_kg = Q_T_Ah / (ECA * anode_utilisation_pct / 100)  // [kg] — Total Anode Net Mass Required (M_a) — Ref: M_a = Q_T / (ε × u) — DNV-RP-B401 §6.4 Eq.(6.3); ε = electrochemical capacity in Ah/kg

anode_std_mass_kg = (anode_material === 'Mg') ? 5 : 15; N_a = Math.ceil(Ma_kg / anode_std_mass_kg)  // [count] — Number of Standard Anodes Required (N_a) — Ref: N_a = M_a / m_std; standard bracelet anode: Al-In 15 kg; Mg 5 kg net mass — DNV Table 10-4

const E_anode = {Zn:-1050, AlIn:-1050, Mg:-1550}[anode_material]; dE_mv = E_anode - protection_potential_mv; resistance_est_ohm = 0.315 * Math.sqrt(A_eff_m2 / N_a) / 10; I_output_A = dE_mv / 1000 / resistance_est_ohm  // [mV, A] — Driving Voltage & Current Output Check — Ref: Driving voltage ΔE = E_anode − E_prot; current = ΔE/R_a; R_a from Dwight formula — DNV-RP-B401 §7

rules.message: Bare steel with low current density: verify environment — bare steel in stagnant seawater may require 50–100 mA/m² per DNV Table 10-3
Zinc anodes at high current density: driving voltage insufficient for tropical warm water — switch to Al-In alloy with higher electrochemical efficiency
Total anode mass > 100 tonnes: very large CP system — evaluate impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP) for structures > 20,000 m²; lower OPEX despite higher CAPEX

smart_warnings.message: Zinc anodes for 20+ year design life: mass penalty is severe — Al-In provides 2.6× more charge per kg at same potential; mandatory for deepwater and long-life structures per DNV §5.2
Driving voltage < 150 mV: anode current output will be marginal — verify polarisation resistance of structure; may need to increase anode count by 40% for adequate protection
Over-protection risk with Mg anodes (E < −900 mV): hydrogen embrittlement possible on high-strength steels (Fy > 550 MPa) — limit to −950 mV per NACE SP0169 §6.2.2
Large exposed area with few anodes: current distribution will be uneven — increase anode count and space evenly; maximum spacing = 3 m for bare surfaces per DNV §10.3
Low coating breakdown (<3%): CP demand is minimal — verify coating integrity with holiday detection per NACE SP0188; CP as backup protection only

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: coating_breakdown_pct === 100 && current_density_mA_m2 < 30
fmea.effect: Bare steel with low current density: verify environment — bare steel in stagnant seawater may require 50–100 mA/m² per DNV Table 10-3

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: anode_material === 'Zn' && current_density_mA_m2 > 50
fmea.effect: Zinc anodes at high current density: driving voltage insufficient for tropical warm water — switch to Al-In alloy with higher electrochemical efficiency

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Ma_kg > 100000
fmea.effect: Total anode mass > 100 tonnes: very large CP system — evaluate impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP) for structures > 20,000 m²; lower OPEX despite higher CAPEX

fmea.failureMode: Zinc anodes for 20+ year design life: mass penalty is severe — Al-In provides 2.6× more charge per kg at same potential; mandatory for deepwater and long-life structures per DNV §5.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_life_yr > 20 && anode_material === 'Zn'

fmea.failureMode: Driving voltage < 150 mV: anode current output will be marginal — verify polarisation resistance of structure; may need to increase anode count by 40% for adequate protection
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dE_mv < 150

fmea.failureMode: Over-protection risk with Mg anodes (E < −900 mV): hydrogen embrittlement possible on high-strength steels (Fy > 550 MPa) — limit to −950 mV per NACE SP0169 §6.2.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: protection_potential_mv < -900 && anode_material === 'Mg'

fmea.failureMode: Large exposed area with few anodes: current distribution will be uneven — increase anode count and space evenly; maximum spacing = 3 m for bare surfaces per DNV §10.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_eff_m2 > 5000 && N_a < 20

fmea.failureMode: Low coating breakdown (<3%): CP demand is minimal — verify coating integrity with holiday detection per NACE SP0188; CP as backup protection only
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: coating_breakdown_pct < 3

tool_name: Steam Boiler Thermal Efficiency — Indirect Loss Method, Flue Gas Dew Point & Stack Heat Loss

inputs.name: Fuel Type

options.label: Natural gas — LHV=47500 kJ/kg, C=75%, H=24%, S=0%
Fuel oil No.6 HFO — LHV=40600 kJ/kg, C=86%, H=11%, S=2.5%
LPG Propane — LHV=46350 kJ/kg, C=82%, H=18%, S=0%
Coal (bituminous) — LHV=26000 kJ/kg, C=73%, H=5%, S=1.5%

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Flue Gas O₂ Concentration (dry basis)
inputs.note: Measured by portable O₂ analyser; natural gas target: 2–3% O₂
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Flue Gas CO Concentration (dry basis)
inputs.note: < 100 ppm: good combustion; > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — EN 303-5 limit: 250 ppm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5725-2:2019 §7 — Analytical chemistry: reproducibility ±5%

inputs.name: Boiler Rated Output (Q_rated)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Surface Radiation & Convection Loss (% of fired duty)
inputs.note: ASME PTC 4 Appendix B: well-insulated gas boiler ≈ 0.3–0.8%; older/larger ≈ 0.5–1.5%
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

formulas: XS_air_pct = flue_gas_O2_pct * 100 / (21 - flue_gas_O2_pct)  // [%] — Excess Air Percentage (XS_air) — Ref: XS_air = O₂% × 100 / (21 − O₂%) — ASME PTC 4-2013 §5.3 Eq.(5.2)

const FC = {NG:{LHV:47500,C:0.75,H:0.24,S:0}, HFO:{LHV:40600,C:0.86,H:0.11,S:0.025}, LPG:{LHV:46350,C:0.82,H:0.18,S:0}, Coal:{LHV:26000,C:0.73,H:0.05,S:0.015}}; const fp = FC[fuel_type]; LHV_kj_kg=fp.LHV; C_frac=fp.C; H_frac=fp.H; S_frac=fp.S  // [kJ/kg] — Fuel Composition Parameters by Type — Ref: Fuel composition from EN 12952-15 Table A.1 / ASME PTC 4 Appendix C

m_air_stoich = 11.5 * C_frac + 34.5 * H_frac + 4.3 * S_frac; m_fg_kg_kg = m_air_stoich * (1 + XS_air_pct/100) + 1; cp_fg = 1.004 + 0.000254 * stack_temp_c; L_fg_pct = m_fg_kg_kg * cp_fg * (stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / LHV_kj_kg * 100  // [%] — Dry Flue Gas Heat Loss (L_fg) — Ref: L_DFG = m_fg × c_p,fg × ΔT / LHV × 100 — ASME PTC 4 §5.4 Eq.(5.11)

L_CO_pct = CO_stack_ppm * 3.5e-4 * (XS_air_pct + 100) / 100  // [%] — Incomplete Combustion Loss — CO (L_CO) — Ref: L_CO ≈ CO_ppm × 3.5×10⁻⁴ × (1 + XS/100) — simplified Ostwald formula; ASME PTC 4 §5.8
eta_th_pct = 100 - L_fg_pct - L_CO_pct - radiation_loss_pct  // [%] — Net Thermal Efficiency (η_th) — Ref: η = 100 − ΣLosses (indirect method) — EN 12952-15 §7.3; ASME PTC 4 §5.1

T_dew_c = (fuel_type === 'HFO' || fuel_type === 'Coal') ? 120 + 18 * Math.log10(S_frac * 1000) : 55  // [°C] — Flue Gas Acid Dew Point Temperature (T_dew) — Ref: Müller-Kirchenbauer dew point: T_dew ≈ 120 + 18·log(SO₃ ppm) for S-bearing fuels; H₂SO₄ formation — EN 15502-1 §B.4

rules.message: Boiler efficiency < 75%: critically low — stack temperature and/or excess air are excessive; immediate combustion optimisation and flue gas audit required
CO > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — adjust air-to-fuel ratio; CO > 1000 ppm indicates safety risk of CO accumulation in boiler room
Stack temperature within 10°C of acid dew point: sulphuric acid condensation risk — economiser and stack lining corrosion; maintain stack temperature ≥ T_dew + 20°C

smart_warnings.message: Excess air > 30%: major energy waste — each 10% excess air at 250°C stack raises stack loss ≈ 0.6%; install O₂ trim control for continuous optimisation
Stack temperature > 250°C on gas-fired boiler: economiser opportunity — recovering heat to 130°C typically saves 4–8% fuel; verify dew point before sizing
Natural gas boiler below 88% efficiency: condensing technology available — condensing boilers achieve 92–97% (NCV basis) by recovering latent heat of flue gas water vapour
Low O₂ + high CO: sub-stoichiometric combustion — immediate safety risk of CO poisoning and explosion; increase combustion air immediately and check burner condition
Stack loss > 15%: dominant efficiency penalty — target: natural gas boiler stack loss ≤ 10% at 230°C stack; prioritise air preheating or economiser installation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: eta_th_pct < 75
fmea.effect: Boiler efficiency < 75%: critically low — stack temperature and/or excess air are excessive; immediate combustion optimisation and flue gas audit required

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: CO_stack_ppm > 400
fmea.effect: CO > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — adjust air-to-fuel ratio; CO > 1000 ppm indicates safety risk of CO accumulation in boiler room

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: stack_temp_c < T_dew_c + 10
fmea.effect: Stack temperature within 10°C of acid dew point: sulphuric acid condensation risk — economiser and stack lining corrosion; maintain stack temperature ≥ T_dew + 20°C

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 30%: major energy waste — each 10% excess air at 250°C stack raises stack loss ≈ 0.6%; install O₂ trim control for continuous optimisation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: XS_air_pct > 30

fmea.failureMode: Stack temperature > 250°C on gas-fired boiler: economiser opportunity — recovering heat to 130°C typically saves 4–8% fuel; verify dew point before sizing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stack_temp_c > 250 && fuel_type === 'NG'

fmea.failureMode: Natural gas boiler below 88% efficiency: condensing technology available — condensing boilers achieve 92–97% (NCV basis) by recovering latent heat of flue gas water vapour
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_th_pct < 88 && fuel_type === 'NG'

fmea.failureMode: Low O₂ + high CO: sub-stoichiometric combustion — immediate safety risk of CO poisoning and explosion; increase combustion air immediately and check burner condition
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: flue_gas_O2_pct < 1.5 && CO_stack_ppm > 200

fmea.failureMode: Stack loss > 15%: dominant efficiency penalty — target: natural gas boiler stack loss ≤ 10% at 230°C stack; prioritise air preheating or economiser installation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_fg_pct > 15

tool_name: Cantilever Retaining Wall — Overturning, Sliding, Bearing & Global Stability Check

inputs.name: Total Retained Height (H)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Wall Base Width (B)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Backfill Friction Angle (φ_b)
inputs.note: Compacted granular fill ≈ 32–38°; gravel ≈ 35–42°; sandy silt ≈ 28–32°
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Backfill Unit Weight (γ_b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Uniform Surcharge on Retained Surface (q_s)
inputs.note: Highway: 10–20 kPa; railway: 20–50 kPa; construction traffic: 12 kPa per AASHTO
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Concrete Unit Weight (γ_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Base-Soil Friction Angle (φ_base)
inputs.note: Concrete on soil: δ = 2/3·φ_soil; rough concrete on gravel: δ ≈ φ_soil
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Allowable Bearing Pressure of Foundation Soil (q_allow)
inputs.note: From geotechnical investigation; sand ≈ 150–300 kPa; gravel ≈ 300–600 kPa; clay varies widely
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

formulas: phi_rad = phi_backfill_deg * Math.PI / 180; Ka = Math.pow(Math.tan(Math.PI/4 - phi_rad/2), 2)  // Rankine Active Earth Pressure Coefficient (Ka) — Ref: Ka = tan²(45 − φ/2) — Rankine (1857); valid for horizontal backfill with no wall friction

Pa_soil_kn_m = 0.5 * Ka * gamma_backfill_kn_m3 * Math.pow(wall_height_m, 2); Pa_surcharge_kn_m = Ka * surcharge_kpa * wall_height_m; Pa_total_kn_m = Pa_soil_kn_m + Pa_surcharge_kn_m  // [kN/m] — Total Active Thrust & Surcharge Force (per unit length) — Ref: P_a = ½·K_a·γ·H² + K_a·q_s·H — EN 1997-1 §9.5.2; forces per unit wall length

M_OT = Pa_soil_kn_m * wall_height_m/3 + Pa_surcharge_kn_m * wall_height_m/2; W_wall_kn_m = wall_concrete_kn_m3 * base_width_m * 0.3; W_soil_kn_m = gamma_backfill_kn_m3 * wall_height_m * (base_width_m * 0.6); M_R = (W_wall_kn_m + W_soil_kn_m) * base_width_m / 2  // [kN·m/m] — Overturning Moment & Restoring Moment (about toe) — Ref: Moments about toe; W_wall = γ_c × B × t_stem (t=0.3 B approx); M_R = W × B/2 — NAVFAC DM-7.2 §5.2
OTF = M_R / M_OT  // Overturning Safety Factor (OTF) — Ref: OTF ≥ 2.0 static (ASCE 7 / EC7 DA1-1); ≥ 1.5 seismic — required minimum varies by code

phi_base_rad = phi_base_deg * Math.PI / 180; W_total = W_wall_kn_m + W_soil_kn_m; F_resist = W_total * Math.tan(phi_base_rad); SSF = F_resist / Pa_total_kn_m  // Sliding Safety Factor (SSF) — Ref: SSF = W·tan(δ) / P_a — EN 1997-1 §6.5.3; minimum SSF ≥ 1.5 (static)

W_total_kn_m = W_wall_kn_m + W_soil_kn_m; e = base_width_m/2 - (M_R - M_OT)/W_total_kn_m; q_max_kpa = W_total_kn_m/base_width_m * (1 + 6*e/base_width_m)  // [kPa] — Maximum Bearing Pressure Under Footing (q_max) — Ref: q_max = N/B × (1 + 6e/B) — eccentric loading; e = eccentricity from centroid — EC7 §6.5.4

rules.message: Overturning safety factor < 1.5: wall will overturn — increase base width, add heel extension, or install ground anchor
Sliding safety factor < 1.2: wall will slide — add base key, increase base width, or install batter piles
Maximum bearing pressure exceeds allowable: foundation failure risk — widen base, reduce eccentricity, or improve ground by compaction or piling

smart_warnings.message: OTF < 2.0: EN 1997-1 DA1 requirement not met for permanent structures — increase base width to B ≥ 0.6H for typical granular backfill
Sliding SF < 1.5: marginal — NAVFAC DM-7.2 requires SSF ≥ 1.5; add shear key 300 mm deep at front third of base for significant resistance improvement
High surcharge on weak backfill: active pressure substantially elevated — verify drainage behind wall; waterlogged granular fill can reach K_0 = 1.0
High wall with marginal OTF: seismic loading will reduce OTF further — ASCE 7 §11 seismic lateral earth pressure adds 50–100% to static thrust; perform seismic analysis
Bearing pressure > 80% of allowable: settlement risk under sustained surcharge — monitor differential settlement; consider ground improvement if soft soil detected below

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: OTF < 1.5
fmea.effect: Overturning safety factor < 1.5: wall will overturn — increase base width, add heel extension, or install ground anchor
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SSF < 1.2
fmea.effect: Sliding safety factor < 1.2: wall will slide — add base key, increase base width, or install batter piles
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: q_max_kpa > q_allow_kpa
fmea.effect: Maximum bearing pressure exceeds allowable: foundation failure risk — widen base, reduce eccentricity, or improve ground by compaction or piling

fmea.failureMode: OTF < 2.0: EN 1997-1 DA1 requirement not met for permanent structures — increase base width to B ≥ 0.6H for typical granular backfill
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OTF < 2.0

fmea.failureMode: Sliding SF < 1.5: marginal — NAVFAC DM-7.2 requires SSF ≥ 1.5; add shear key 300 mm deep at front third of base for significant resistance improvement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SSF < 1.5

fmea.failureMode: High surcharge on weak backfill: active pressure substantially elevated — verify drainage behind wall; waterlogged granular fill can reach K_0 = 1.0
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: surcharge_kpa > 20 && phi_backfill_deg < 28

fmea.failureMode: High wall with marginal OTF: seismic loading will reduce OTF further — ASCE 7 §11 seismic lateral earth pressure adds 50–100% to static thrust; perform seismic analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wall_height_m > 6 && OTF < 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Bearing pressure > 80% of allowable: settlement risk under sustained surcharge — monitor differential settlement; consider ground improvement if soft soil detected below
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: q_max_kpa > q_allow_kpa * 0.8

tool_name: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating

inputs.name: Biomass Fuel Type

options.label: Wood pellets EN ISO 17225-2 — GCV_dry=19800 kJ/kg

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Fuel Moisture Content (M) — as-received basis
inputs.note: EN ISO 18134-3 gravimetric method; fresh wood: 40–55%; air-dried: 20–30%; pellets: 8–12%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Hydrogen Content of Dry Fuel (H_dry)
inputs.note: From ultimate analysis EN ISO 16948; woody biomass ≈ 5.8–6.2%; straw ≈ 5.5–6.0%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Required Boiler Rated Heat Output (Q_boiler)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Boiler Thermal Efficiency (η_boiler)
inputs.note: EN 303-5 Class 5 (pellets): ≥ 88%; wood chips direct: 75–85%; stoker-fired straw: 78–86%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Oxygen Content in Flue Gas (O₂, dry)
inputs.note: Biomass requires higher excess air than gas (6–12% O₂); grate combustion: 7–10%; fluidised bed: 3–6%
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Efficiency/ratio: CoV≈0.02

inputs.name: Fuel Bulk Density (ρ_bulk)
inputs.note: Wood chips G30 ≈ 200–300 kg/m³; pellets 6 mm ≈ 600–700 kg/m³; straw bales ≈ 130–180 kg/m³
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

formulas: const GCV_map = {wood_chips:19500, pellets:19800, straw:17500, miscanthus:18000, sawdust:19000}; GCV_dry_kj_kg = GCV_map[biomass_type]  // [kJ/kg] — Gross Calorific Value — Dry Basis (GCV_dry) — Ref: EN ISO 18125:2017 (calorific values); GCV on dry ash-free basis by fuel type

M_frac = moisture_content_pct / 100; NCV_ar_kj_kg = GCV_dry_kj_kg * (1 - M_frac) - 2440 * (M_frac + 0.09 * H_content_dry_pct/100 * (1 - M_frac))  // [kJ/kg] — Net Calorific Value — As-Received Basis (NCV_ar) — Ref: NCV_ar = GCV_dry × (1−M) − 2440 × [M + 0.09·H_dry·(1−M)] — EN ISO 18125 / Milne equation; 2440 kJ/kg = latent heat of water vapour

m_fuel_kg_h = boiler_output_kw * 3600 / (NCV_ar_kj_kg * boiler_efficiency_pct / 100)  // [kg/h] — Fuel Consumption Rate (ṁ_fuel) — Ref: ṁ_fuel = Q_boiler / (NCV_ar × η_boiler) — mass flow for steady-state heat balance

V_fuel_m3h = m_fuel_kg_h / bulk_density_kg_m3; V_fuel_daily_m3 = V_fuel_m3h * 24  // [m³/h, m³/day] — Volumetric Fuel Consumption (storage sizing) — Ref: V_fuel = ṁ_fuel / ρ_bulk — fuel store sizing: minimum 3 days' storage recommended per CIBSE Guide B1

XS_air_pct = O2_flue_pct * 100 / (21 - O2_flue_pct); m_air_stoich_kg_kg = 8.89 * (1 - moisture_content_pct/100); m_air_actual_kg_kg = m_air_stoich_kg_kg * (1 + XS_air_pct / 100)  // [%, kg air/kg fuel] — Excess Air & Stoichiometric Air Requirement — Ref: Stoichiometric air ≈ 8.89 kg/kg for wood C₁H₁.₄O₀.₆; XS_air = O₂/(21−O₂) × 100 — EN 303-5

Q_fan_m3h = m_fuel_kg_h * m_air_actual_kg_kg / 1.2  // [m³/h] — Combustion Air Fan Duty (Q_fan) — Ref: Q_fan = ṁ_fuel × m_air / ρ_air; ρ_air = 1.2 kg/m³ at 20°C — combustion air fan sizing per EN 303-5 §5.4

rules.message: NCV_ar < 5000 kJ/kg: very wet fuel — combustion may not self-sustain below 35% moisture without auxiliary burner support; dry fuel below 25% moisture for reliable combustion
Pellet moisture cannot exceed 50% — EN ISO 17225-2 Class A1 pellets: M ≤ 10%; M > 50% physically impossible for pellets; check input
O₂ < 4% for straw combustion: high CO and unburned carbon risk — straw requires O₂ ≥ 6% due to irregular particle shape; increase secondary air

smart_warnings.message: Moisture > 35%: NCV drops sharply and boiler efficiency falls significantly — each 10% moisture increase reduces NCV by ~15%; pre-dry fuel or select lower-moisture grade per EN ISO 17225
NCV_ar < 10 MJ/kg: low energy density — fuel consumption and storage volume approximately double vs dry fuel; evaluate chip drying or switch to pellets
Excess air > 80%: very high for biomass — stack losses will be excessive; target 40–60% excess air (O₂ 7–9%) for grate-fired systems per EN 303-5
Daily fuel volume > 50 m³: large biomass logistics requirement — size vehicle access, unloading equipment, and fire compartmentation per NFPA 664
Pellet boiler efficiency < 80%: below EN 303-5 Class 4 minimum for pellet appliances — verify combustion tuning, grate design, and heat exchanger fouling

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: NCV_ar_kj_kg < 5000
fmea.effect: NCV_ar < 5000 kJ/kg: very wet fuel — combustion may not self-sustain below 35% moisture without auxiliary burner support; dry fuel below 25% moisture for reliable combustion

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: moisture_content_pct > 50 && biomass_type === 'pellets'
fmea.effect: Pellet moisture cannot exceed 50% — EN ISO 17225-2 Class A1 pellets: M ≤ 10%; M > 50% physically impossible for pellets; check input
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: O2_flue_pct < 4 && biomass_type === 'straw'
fmea.effect: O₂ < 4% for straw combustion: high CO and unburned carbon risk — straw requires O₂ ≥ 6% due to irregular particle shape; increase secondary air

fmea.failureMode: Moisture > 35%: NCV drops sharply and boiler efficiency falls significantly — each 10% moisture increase reduces NCV by ~15%; pre-dry fuel or select lower-moisture grade per EN ISO 17225
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: moisture_content_pct > 35

fmea.failureMode: NCV_ar < 10 MJ/kg: low energy density — fuel consumption and storage volume approximately double vs dry fuel; evaluate chip drying or switch to pellets
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NCV_ar_kj_kg < 10000

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 80%: very high for biomass — stack losses will be excessive; target 40–60% excess air (O₂ 7–9%) for grate-fired systems per EN 303-5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: XS_air_pct > 80

fmea.failureMode: Daily fuel volume > 50 m³: large biomass logistics requirement — size vehicle access, unloading equipment, and fire compartmentation per NFPA 664
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: V_fuel_daily_m3 > 50

fmea.failureMode: Pellet boiler efficiency < 80%: below EN 303-5 Class 4 minimum for pellet appliances — verify combustion tuning, grate design, and heat exchanger fouling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: boiler_efficiency_pct < 80 && biomass_type === 'pellets'

tool_name: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3

inputs.name: Applied Shear Force on Weld Group (V_u / V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Applied Normal (Tension/Compression) Force on Weld (N_u / N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Effective Weld Length (L_eff) — excluding craters
inputs.note: AWS D1.1 §2.5: L_eff = L_total − 2×leg size (end craters); minimum L_eff ≥ 4×leg size
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Base Metal Yield Strength (F_y / f_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Filler Metal Classification Strength (F_EXX)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Thickness of Thicker Connected Part (t_max)
inputs.note: Controls minimum leg size per AWS D1.1 Table 8.8 and EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.3
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Design Standard
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: a_min_aws_mm = (thicker_part_mm <= 6) ? 3 : (thicker_part_mm <= 12) ? 5 : (thicker_part_mm <= 19) ? 6 : (thicker_part_mm <= 38) ? 8 : 10; a_min_ec3_mm = Math.max(Math.sqrt(thicker_part_mm) - 0.5, 3); a_min_mm = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? a_min_aws_mm : a_min_ec3_mm  // [mm] — Minimum Required Leg Size (a_min) by Base Plate Thickness — Ref: AWS D1.1-2020 Table 8.8 (prequalified minimum weld size); EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.3.1 — a_min = √t_max − 0.5 mm

F_w_mpa = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? 0.6 * filler_metal_fexx_mpa * 0.707 : (filler_metal_fexx_mpa / Math.sqrt(3)) / 1.25; demand_kn_mm = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(V_demand_kn, 2) + Math.pow(N_demand_kn, 2)) / weld_length_mm; a_req_mm = demand_kn_mm * 1000 / F_w_mpa  // [mm] — Required Minimum Leg Size from Demand (a_req from capacity) — Ref: AWS §2.4.1: φ·F_w = 0.75 × 0.6·F_EXX × 0.707; EC3 §4.5.3.3: F_w,Rd = f_u/(√3·β_w·γ_M2)

a_design_mm = Math.ceil(Math.max(a_min_mm, a_req_mm))  // [mm] — Design Leg Size (a_design) — Governing — Ref: Governing size = maximum of code minimum and calculated requirement; round up to nearest mm

a_eff_mm = a_design_mm * 0.707; A_w_mm2 = a_eff_mm * weld_length_mm  // [mm, mm²] — Effective Throat (a_eff) & Weld Area — Ref: Throat = leg × cos45° = leg × 0.707 — ISO 2553:2019 §3.6; theoretical throat for equal-leg fillet

F_w_mpa = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? 0.75 * 0.6 * filler_metal_fexx_mpa : filler_metal_fexx_mpa / (Math.sqrt(3) * 1.25); phi_Rn_kn = F_w_mpa * A_w_mm2 / 1000  // [kN] — Weld Capacity — Shear on Throat (φR_n / F_w,Rd) — Ref: AWS §2.4.1 φR_n = 0.75 × 0.6·F_EXX × A_w; EC3 §4.5.3.3 F_w,Rd = f_vw,d × A_w

V_resultant_kn = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(V_demand_kn, 2) + Math.pow(N_demand_kn, 2)); DCR_weld = V_resultant_kn / phi_Rn_kn  // [dimensionless, kN] — Resultant Demand & Utilisation Ratio — Ref: Resultant force vector check; DCR ≤ 1.0 required — AWS §2.4.1 / EC3 §4.5.3

rules.message: Weld group overstressed — increase leg size, add weld length, or select higher-strength filler metal
Design leg size below code minimum for plate thickness — minimum leg size per AWS Table 8.8 / EC3 §4.5.3 must be met regardless of calculated demand
Weld length < 4× leg size: weld too short relative to size — AWS D1.1 §2.5 minimum effective length requirement violated; increase length or reduce leg size

smart_warnings.message: Weld leg > 75% of plate thickness: excessive heat input risk — risk of burn-through and distortion; maximum weld leg = t − 1.5 mm for plates < 12 mm per AWS §2.5.2
Weld utilisation > 85%: limited reserve for dynamic or fatigue loading — increase leg size by minimum 2 mm or add return welds at ends
Undermatching filler metal: F_EXX < 1.2×F_y of base metal — acceptable for ductility in some cases, but verify per AWS D1.1 Annex I for high-strength steel
Small weld leg under high load: verify welder qualification — leg size < 5 mm requires special quality control per ISO 3834-2
Significant tension component: combined stress state — verify weld orientation; transverse welds to applied tension are 50% stronger than longitudinal per AWS Annex B

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR_weld > 1.0
fmea.effect: Weld group overstressed — increase leg size, add weld length, or select higher-strength filler metal
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: a_design_mm < a_min_mm
fmea.effect: Design leg size below code minimum for plate thickness — minimum leg size per AWS Table 8.8 / EC3 §4.5.3 must be met regardless of calculated demand
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: weld_length_mm < 4 * a_design_mm
fmea.effect: Weld length < 4× leg size: weld too short relative to size — AWS D1.1 §2.5 minimum effective length requirement violated; increase length or reduce leg size

fmea.failureMode: Weld leg > 75% of plate thickness: excessive heat input risk — risk of burn-through and distortion; maximum weld leg = t − 1.5 mm for plates < 12 mm per AWS §2.5.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_design_mm > thicker_part_mm * 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Weld utilisation > 85%: limited reserve for dynamic or fatigue loading — increase leg size by minimum 2 mm or add return welds at ends
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_weld > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Undermatching filler metal: F_EXX < 1.2×F_y of base metal — acceptable for ductility in some cases, but verify per AWS D1.1 Annex I for high-strength steel
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: filler_metal_fexx_mpa < base_metal_Fy_mpa * 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Small weld leg under high load: verify welder qualification — leg size < 5 mm requires special quality control per ISO 3834-2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_design_mm < 5 && V_demand_kn > 50

fmea.failureMode: Significant tension component: combined stress state — verify weld orientation; transverse welds to applied tension are 50% stronger than longitudinal per AWS Annex B
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_demand_kn > V_demand_kn * 0.5

tool_name: CNC Thread Milling & Tapping — Tap Drill Diameter, Helix Angle, Lead & Cutting Parameters

inputs.name: Thread Designation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Thread Tolerance Class (Internal)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Workpiece Material Group (ISO 513)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Required Thread Engagement Depth (L_eng)
inputs.note: ISO 898-1: minimum engagement = 1.0D for steel-in-steel; 1.5D for bolt into aluminium
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Cutting Speed for Tapping / Thread Milling (V_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Coolant / Lubrication Strategy
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: const TH = {M6:{D:6,p:1.0,d1:4.917,d2:5.350}, M8:{D:8,p:1.25,d1:6.647,d2:7.188}, M10:{D:10,p:1.5,d1:8.376,d2:9.026}, M12:{D:12,p:1.75,d1:10.106,d2:10.863}, M16:{D:16,p:2.0,d1:13.835,d2:14.701}, M20:{D:20,p:2.5,d1:17.294,d2:18.376}, M24:{D:24,p:3.0,d1:20.752,d2:22.051}, M30:{D:30,p:3.5,d1:26.211,d2:27.727}}; const t=TH[thread_designation]; nominal_D=t.D; pitch_mm=t.p; d1_mm=t.d1; d2_mm=t.d2; tap_drill_mm = d1_mm + (thread_tolerance_class==='7H' ? 0.2 : thread_tolerance_class==='5H' ? 0.0 : 0.1)  // [mm] — Thread Geometry — Pitch, Minor Diameter & Tap Drill Size — Ref: ISO 68-1:1998 / ISO 965-1:1998 — basic profile; tap drill = d1 + tolerance allowance per 6H/5H/7H

alpha_deg = Math.atan(pitch_mm / (Math.PI * d2_mm)) * 180 / Math.PI  // [°] — Helix Angle (α) at Thread Pitch Diameter — Ref: tan(α) = p / (π × d₂) — ISO 5408; helix angle at pitch cylinder; typical 2–6° for coarse metric

Vc_factor = {flood:1.0, MQL:0.80, dry:0.60}[coolant_type]; Vc_corrected = Vc_m_min * Vc_factor  // [m/min] — Corrected Cutting Speed for Coolant Strategy — Ref: Coolant reduction factors — Sandvik C-2920 §12.4; dry threading requires significant speed reduction
n_rpm = Vc_corrected * 1000 / (Math.PI * nominal_D)  // [rpm] — Spindle Speed for Tapping / Thread Milling (n) — Ref: n = V_c × 1000 / (π × D) — applied to nominal thread diameter
Vf_mm_min = n_rpm * pitch_mm  // [mm/min] — Synchronised Feed Rate (V_f) — Rigid Tapping — Ref: V_f = n × p — rigid tapping synchronisation; F_per_rev = p (exact); CNC G84 / G74 cycle

const Vc_mult = {P:1.0, M:1.8, K:0.7, N:0.4, S:2.5}[workpiece_material_group]; Mc_Nm = 0.00022 * Vc_mult * Math.pow(nominal_D, 2.6) * pitch_mm  // [N·m] — Cutting Torque Estimate (M_c) & Spindle Load Check — Ref: Approximate tapping torque: M_c = C_m × D^2.6 × p; C_m empirical per material group — Titex / Walter reference data

rules.message: Tap drill diameter > 95% of nominal: insufficient thread material — tap drill calculation error; verify thread pitch
Thread engagement < 0.8D in non-steel material: strip-out risk — increase engagement to minimum 1.5D for aluminium, 2.0D for plastics
High speed on large tap (D > 10 mm): cutting speed is aggressive — verify machine spindle torque rating at this RPM; tap breakage risk increases significantly

smart_warnings.message: Dry tapping in stainless steel: tap seizure imminent — stainless work-hardens rapidly; mandatory MQL or flood coolant; reduce Vc to ≤ 8 m/min
Cutting speed too high for superalloy threading: V_c > 6 m/min in Ni/Ti alloys causes rapid BUE and tap fracture — reduce to 3–5 m/min and use HSSE-PM or solid carbide tap
Very small helix angle (< 2°): fine pitch thread — chip evacuation critical; use spiral flute tap (right-hand helix) for blind holes to eject chips upward
High feed rate on small tap: synchronisation error of ±1 rpm causes proportionally large pitch error — verify CNC rigid tapping encoder resolution; use compensation chuck below M8
Deep blind hole threading: chip accumulation risk — use spiral flute tap and retract every 5 mm for chip evacuation; flood coolant at maximum pressure mandatory

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: tap_drill_mm > nominal_D * 0.95
fmea.effect: Tap drill diameter > 95% of nominal: insufficient thread material — tap drill calculation error; verify thread pitch
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: thread_depth_mm < nominal_D * 0.8 && workpiece_material_group !== 'P'
fmea.effect: Thread engagement < 0.8D in non-steel material: strip-out risk — increase engagement to minimum 1.5D for aluminium, 2.0D for plastics

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: n_rpm > 10000 && nominal_D > 10
fmea.effect: High speed on large tap (D > 10 mm): cutting speed is aggressive — verify machine spindle torque rating at this RPM; tap breakage risk increases significantly

fmea.failureMode: Dry tapping in stainless steel: tap seizure imminent — stainless work-hardens rapidly; mandatory MQL or flood coolant; reduce Vc to ≤ 8 m/min
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: workpiece_material_group === 'M' && coolant_type === 'dry'

fmea.failureMode: Cutting speed too high for superalloy threading: V_c > 6 m/min in Ni/Ti alloys causes rapid BUE and tap fracture — reduce to 3–5 m/min and use HSSE-PM or solid carbide tap
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: workpiece_material_group === 'S' && Vc_m_min > 6

fmea.failureMode: Very small helix angle (< 2°): fine pitch thread — chip evacuation critical; use spiral flute tap (right-hand helix) for blind holes to eject chips upward
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: alpha_deg < 2

fmea.failureMode: High feed rate on small tap: synchronisation error of ±1 rpm causes proportionally large pitch error — verify CNC rigid tapping encoder resolution; use compensation chuck below M8
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vf_mm_min > 500 && nominal_D < 8

fmea.failureMode: Deep blind hole threading: chip accumulation risk — use spiral flute tap and retract every 5 mm for chip evacuation; flood coolant at maximum pressure mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: thread_depth_mm > nominal_D * 2.5

tool_name: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency

inputs.name: Beam Clear Span (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Unfactored Dead Load (Uniformly Distributed) (g_k)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Unfactored Live / Imposed Load (UDL) (q_k)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Concentrated Point Load at Midspan (P_k)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Second Moment of Area About Strong Axis (I_x)
inputs.note: From section tables: UB 406×178×67 → I_x = 24330 cm⁴; IPE 360 → I_x = 16270 cm⁴
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Overall Beam Depth (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Beam End Support Condition
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Allowable Deflection Limit (L / n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: Cd_map = {SS: 5/384, FF: 1/384, FP: 1/185, cant: 1/8}; Cd = Cd_map[end_condition]  // Deflection Coefficient (C_δ) by End Condition — Ref: Beam deflection coefficients — Roark's Formulas for Stress & Strain 8th Ed. §8.1; EN 1993-1-1 §7

E_steel = 210000; I_m4 = Ix_cm4 * 1e-8; L_m = span_m; delta_DL_mm = Cd * w_dead_kn_m * 1000 * Math.pow(L_m, 4) / (E_steel * 1e6 * I_m4) * 1000  // [mm] — Dead Load Deflection (δ_DL) — Ref: δ = C_δ × w × L⁴ / (E × I); E = 210,000 MPa for steel — EN 1993-1-1 §3.2.6

delta_LL_mm = Cd * w_live_kn_m * 1000 * Math.pow(span_m, 4) / (210000e6 * Ix_cm4 * 1e-8) * 1000 + (P_point_kn > 0 ? 1/48 * P_point_kn * 1000 * Math.pow(span_m, 3) / (210000e6 * Ix_cm4 * 1e-8) * 1000 : 0)  // [mm] — Live Load Deflection (δ_LL) — Governs for Limits — Ref: Live load + point load deflections superimposed; midspan point load δ = PL³/(48EI) for SS beam

delta_total_mm = delta_DL_mm + delta_LL_mm; delta_allow_mm = span_m * 1000 / deflection_limit_ratio  // [mm] — Total Deflection (δ_total) & Allowable Limit — Ref: Allowable = L/n; total = δ_DL + δ_LL — EN 1993-1-1 §7.2 Table NA.3 / AISC DG3
Ld_ratio = span_m * 1000 / beam_depth_mm  // Span-to-Depth Ratio (L/d) — Efficiency Check — Ref: L/d efficiency: floors 18–24; purlins 20–30; crane beams 12–16 — SCI P355 §3.2 design guidance

camber_mm = (delta_DL_mm > 10) ? Math.round(delta_DL_mm * 0.75) : 0; deflection_util = delta_LL_mm / delta_allow_mm  // [mm, dimensionless] — Recommended Camber & Deflection Utilisation — Ref: Camber = 75% of dead load deflection per AISC Code of Standard Practice §6.1; δ_LL / δ_allow ≤ 1.0

rules.message: Live load deflection exceeds allowable limit — increase section size (I_x), reduce span with intermediate support, or accept lower deflection limit
Total deflection exceeds L/100: extreme deflection — verify input values; beam is severely undersized or span is unrealistic for this load
L/d > 30 with significant live load: shallow beam will deflect excessively — consider L/d ≤ 20 as starting point for preliminary sizing under office / industrial loads

smart_warnings.message: Camber specified on non-simply-supported beam: verify that end connection allows free rotation and that camber direction is consistent with actual dead load deflection shape
Deflection utilisation > 85%: marginal — consider one section deeper; increasing depth by 50 mm typically reduces δ by 15–20% for the same weight
Dead load deflection > L/400: significant long-term creep and ponding risk on flat roofs — EC3 recommends δ_DL + creep < L/300 for roofs per §7.2.1
Precision deflection limit on shallow beam: floor vibration may govern before deflection limit — check natural frequency per SCI P354 (fn > 4 Hz for office floors)
Long cantilever (> 3 m): deflection tip grows as L³; very sensitive to load position — verify back-span anchorage and connection rotation capacity; dynamic check mandatory per SCI P076

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: deflection_util > 1.0
fmea.effect: Live load deflection exceeds allowable limit — increase section size (I_x), reduce span with intermediate support, or accept lower deflection limit

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: delta_total_mm > span_m * 10
fmea.effect: Total deflection exceeds L/100: extreme deflection — verify input values; beam is severely undersized or span is unrealistic for this load

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Ld_ratio > 30 && w_live_kn_m > 5
fmea.effect: L/d > 30 with significant live load: shallow beam will deflect excessively — consider L/d ≤ 20 as starting point for preliminary sizing under office / industrial loads

fmea.failureMode: Camber specified on non-simply-supported beam: verify that end connection allows free rotation and that camber direction is consistent with actual dead load deflection shape
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: camber_mm > 0 && end_condition !== 'SS'

fmea.failureMode: Deflection utilisation > 85%: marginal — consider one section deeper; increasing depth by 50 mm typically reduces δ by 15–20% for the same weight
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: deflection_util > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Dead load deflection > L/400: significant long-term creep and ponding risk on flat roofs — EC3 recommends δ_DL + creep < L/300 for roofs per §7.2.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_DL_mm > span_m * 1000 / 400

fmea.failureMode: Precision deflection limit on shallow beam: floor vibration may govern before deflection limit — check natural frequency per SCI P354 (fn > 4 Hz for office floors)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: deflection_limit_ratio >= 500 && Ld_ratio > 20

fmea.failureMode: Long cantilever (> 3 m): deflection tip grows as L³; very sensitive to load position — verify back-span anchorage and connection rotation capacity; dynamic check mandatory per SCI P076
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: end_condition === 'cant' && span_m > 3

tool_name: Surface Roughness Conversion — Ra, Rz, Rq, Rt & Machining Process Capability

inputs.name: Arithmetic Mean Roughness (Ra)
inputs.note: ISO 4287 §4.2.1 — Ra: most universal specification parameter; measured by profilometer
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Machining / Finishing Process
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Evaluation Cut-off Length (λ_c)
inputs.note: ISO 4288:1996 Table 1 — standard cut-off selection based on expected Ra range
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: ISO Surface Finish N-Number (N1–N12)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Measurement Traverse Length (L_t)
inputs.note: ISO 4288: L_t = 5 × λ_c for standard measurement (5 sampling lengths)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: Rz_um = Ra_um * 7.2  // [µm] — Rz (Ten-Point Height / Maximum Height) from Ra — Ref: ISO 4287 — empirical ratio Rz ≈ 7.2 × Ra for turned and ground surfaces (range 4–10×); ISO 1302:2002 §4.3
Rq_um = Ra_um * 1.11  // [µm] — Rq (Root Mean Square Roughness) from Ra — Ref: Rq ≈ 1.11 × Ra for Gaussian height distribution — ISO 4287 §4.2.2; used in tribology and bearing contact analysis

Rt_um = Ra_um * 10  // [µm] — Rt (Total Profile Height — Maximum Peak to Valley) from Ra — Ref: Rt ≈ 10 × Ra (conservative estimate for machined surfaces) — Whitehouse 'Surfaces and their Measurement' §3
Ra_from_N_um = 0.025 * Math.pow(2, N_value - 1)  // [µm] — ISO N-Number Ra Equivalence (from N-number input) — Ref: ISO 1302:2002 Table 1 — Ra_N = 0.025 × 2^(N−1); N7 = 1.6 µm; N9 = 6.3 µm

Ra_microinch = Ra_um * 39.37; Rz_microinch = Rz_um * 39.37  // [µin] — ASME B46.1 CLA (Centreline Average) — US Microinch Conversion — Ref: 1 µm = 39.37 µin — ASME B46.1-2019 §1.1; CLA (US) = Ra (ISO)

const PR = {grinding:[0.1,1.6], turning_finish:[0.8,3.2], turning_rough:[3.2,12.5], milling_finish:[0.8,3.2], milling_rough:[3.2,12.5], honing:[0.05,0.4], lapping:[0.01,0.2], edm:[0.4,6.3], shot_blast:[6.3,25]}; const r=PR[machining_process]; Ra_min_proc=r[0]; Ra_max_proc=r[1]; in_range = Ra_um >= Ra_min_proc && Ra_um <= Ra_max_proc  // [µm, µm, boolean] — Achievable Ra Range & Process Capability Assessment — Ref: Process capability ranges — Kalpakjian Manufacturing Engineering 7th Ed. §26; ISO 286-2 surface finish correlation

rules.message: Specified Ra is outside the normal capability of the selected machining process — verify process selection or accept that special tooling, passes, and conditions are required
Ra < 0.05 µm with standard machining: only achievable by lapping, superfinishing, or polishing — specify correct process
Traverse length < 5 × cut-off length: ISO 4288 requires minimum 5 sampling lengths for reliable Ra measurement — increase traverse length

smart_warnings.message: Ra < 0.4 µm from EDM: requires mirror EDM with very low energy settings — expect 5–10× longer machining time vs standard EDM at Ra 1.6 µm
Ra inconsistency: specified Ra > 3.2 µm but N-value requests ≤ 1.6 µm — review which parameter is the actual requirement; Ra from profilometer takes precedence
High Rz on finish turning: check insert nose condition and cutting parameters — Rz > 25 µm suggests chatter or built-up edge; increase Vc or reduce feed
Very fine surface with long cut-off: ISO 4288 requires λ_c = 0.25 mm for Ra < 0.1 µm — using 0.8 mm cut-off will include waviness in Ra and give falsely high reading
