N-number and Ra values are inconsistent by more than 2×: specify one parameter and derive the other — do not independently specify both to avoid conflicting drawing requirements

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: !in_range
fmea.effect: Specified Ra is outside the normal capability of the selected machining process — verify process selection or accept that special tooling, passes, and conditions are required

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Ra_um < 0.05 && machining_process !== 'lapping' && machining_process !== 'honing'
fmea.effect: Ra < 0.05 µm with standard machining: only achievable by lapping, superfinishing, or polishing — specify correct process

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: measurement_traverse_mm < 5 * cutoff_lambda_c_mm
fmea.effect: Traverse length < 5 × cut-off length: ISO 4288 requires minimum 5 sampling lengths for reliable Ra measurement — increase traverse length

fmea.failureMode: Ra < 0.4 µm from EDM: requires mirror EDM with very low energy settings — expect 5–10× longer machining time vs standard EDM at Ra 1.6 µm
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um < 0.4 && machining_process === 'edm'

fmea.failureMode: Ra inconsistency: specified Ra > 3.2 µm but N-value requests ≤ 1.6 µm — review which parameter is the actual requirement; Ra from profilometer takes precedence
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um > 3.2 && N_value <= 7

fmea.failureMode: High Rz on finish turning: check insert nose condition and cutting parameters — Rz > 25 µm suggests chatter or built-up edge; increase Vc or reduce feed
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rz_um > 25 && machining_process === 'turning_finish'

fmea.failureMode: Very fine surface with long cut-off: ISO 4288 requires λ_c = 0.25 mm for Ra < 0.1 µm — using 0.8 mm cut-off will include waviness in Ra and give falsely high reading
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um < 0.2 && cutoff_lambda_c_mm > 0.8

fmea.failureMode: N-number and Ra values are inconsistent by more than 2×: specify one parameter and derive the other — do not independently specify both to avoid conflicting drawing requirements
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_from_N_um > Ra_um * 2 || Ra_from_N_um < Ra_um * 0.5

tool_name: Rebar Development & Lap Splice Length — Tension, Compression & Seismic Requirements

inputs.name: Rebar Nominal Diameter (d_b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Rebar Yield Strength (f_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Concrete Compressive Strength (f'_c / f_ck)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Concrete Cover to Bar Surface (c)
inputs.note: ACI 318 §20.6.1: columns/beams min 40 mm; slabs min 20 mm; exposure class XS: min 50 mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Clear Spacing Between Bars (c_s)
inputs.note: ACI §25.2.1: minimum clear spacing ≥ d_b, 25 mm, or 4/3 × aggregate size
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Rebar Loading Condition

options.label: Tension — seismic (Class SDC D/E/F or DCM/DCH)

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Transverse Reinforcement Along Splice

options.label: Ties/stirrups present meeting ACI §25.5.2 or EC2 §8.7

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: lambda = 1.0; psi_t = 1.0; psi_e = 1.0; psi_s = (bar_diameter_mm <= 19) ? 0.8 : 1.0; cb = Math.min(cover_mm + bar_diameter_mm/2, bar_spacing_mm/2 + bar_diameter_mm/2); Ktr = (transverse_reinforcement === 'ties') ? 40 * 0.00129 * bar_diameter_mm / bar_diameter_mm * 10 : 0; cb_plus_Ktr_db = Math.min((cb + Ktr) / bar_diameter_mm, 2.5); ld_ACI_mm = (3 * fy_mpa * psi_t * psi_e * psi_s * lambda / (40 * lambda * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * cb_plus_Ktr_db)) * bar_diameter_mm  // [mm] — ACI 318-19 Development Length in Tension (l_d) — Ref: ACI 318-19 §25.4.2.3 Eq.(25.4.2.3a) — l_d = (3f_y ψ_t ψ_e ψ_s λ)/(40λ√f'_c × (c_b+K_tr)/d_b) × d_b

fbd_mpa = 2.25 * 1.0 * 1.0 * 0.21 * Math.pow(fc_prime_mpa, 2/3); lb_req_mm = (bar_diameter_mm / 4) * (fy_mpa / (1.15 * fbd_mpa)); alpha1=1.0; alpha2=Math.max(1 - 0.15*(cover_mm - bar_diameter_mm)/bar_diameter_mm, 0.7); lbd_EC2_mm = Math.max(alpha1 * alpha2 * lb_req_mm, Math.max(0.3 * lb_req_mm, 10 * bar_diameter_mm, 100))  // [mm] — EC2 Design Anchorage Length (l_bd) — Ref: EN 1992-1-1:2004 §8.4.4 — f_bd = 2.25η₁η₂f_ctd; l_b,req = (d_b/4)·(σ_sd/f_bd); l_bd = α₁α₂...·l_b,req

splice_factor = (load_type === 'seismic') ? 1.7 : (transverse_reinforcement === 'none') ? 1.3 : 1.3; ld_governing = Math.max(ld_ACI_mm, lbd_EC2_mm); ls_mm = ld_governing * splice_factor  // [mm] — Tension Lap Splice Length (l_s) — Ref: ACI 318-19 §25.5.2 — Class B splice: 1.3 × l_d; seismic per §18.8.2.3: 1.7 × l_d in potential hinge zones

ldc_ACI_mm = (fy_mpa <= 420) ? Math.max(0.24 * fy_mpa / Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * bar_diameter_mm, 0.043 * fy_mpa * bar_diameter_mm) : Math.max((0.25 * fy_mpa - 24) / Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * bar_diameter_mm, 0.043 * fy_mpa * bar_diameter_mm); ldc_mm = Math.max(ldc_ACI_mm, 200)  // [mm] — Compression Development Length (l_dc) — Ref: ACI 318-19 §25.4.9 — compression development length; minimum 200 mm

l_governing_mm = (load_type === 'compression') ? ldc_mm : ls_mm; l_bars = Math.ceil(l_governing_mm / bar_diameter_mm)  // [mm, bar diameters] — Governing Development / Splice Length — Ref: Governing length expressed in bar diameters for field verification; field check: L_splice / d_b

fc_min_recommended_mpa = (bar_diameter_mm >= 32) ? 35 : (bar_diameter_mm >= 25) ? 28 : 20; fc_adequate = fc_prime_mpa >= fc_min_recommended_mpa  // [MPa, boolean] — Minimum Concrete Concrete Compressive Strength for Bar Diameter — Ref: ACI 318-19 Commentary §R25.4 — large bars in low-strength concrete require excessive development lengths; minimum f'_c recommendation

rules.message: Concrete cover less than bar diameter: ACI §20.6.1 minimum cover requirement violated — fire resistance, corrosion protection, and bond transfer all inadequate
Clear bar spacing less than bar diameter: ACI §25.2.1 minimum spacing violated — concrete cannot flow between bars; splitting failure and poor consolidation
Seismic splices in low-strength concrete: ACI 318 §18.12.2 recommends f'_c ≥ 25 MPa in special moment frames; low concrete strength requires significantly longer lap lengths

smart_warnings.message: Lap splice > 60 bar diameters: very long splice — consider mechanical couplers (ISO 15835-1) or welded splices to reduce congestion; check if staggering splices is feasible
Large bar (d_b > 28 mm) in seismic zone: ACI 318 §18.8.2.1 prohibits lap splices within beam-column joints and in potential plastic hinge zones — use mechanical couplers
No transverse reinforcement on tension splice: splitting failure risk — provide minimum ties per ACI §25.5.2; minimum 2 ties within splice length at third-points
Concrete strength below recommended for large bars: development length will be excessive and bond stress may cause splitting — upgrade to recommended f'_c
EC2 development length > 1.5× ACI value: significant code disparity — verify which standard governs for the project; EC2 tends to be more conservative for poor cover conditions

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cover_mm < bar_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Concrete cover less than bar diameter: ACI §20.6.1 minimum cover requirement violated — fire resistance, corrosion protection, and bond transfer all inadequate
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: bar_spacing_mm < bar_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Clear bar spacing less than bar diameter: ACI §25.2.1 minimum spacing violated — concrete cannot flow between bars; splitting failure and poor consolidation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: load_type === 'seismic' && fc_prime_mpa < 25
fmea.effect: Seismic splices in low-strength concrete: ACI 318 §18.12.2 recommends f'_c ≥ 25 MPa in special moment frames; low concrete strength requires significantly longer lap lengths

fmea.failureMode: Lap splice > 60 bar diameters: very long splice — consider mechanical couplers (ISO 15835-1) or welded splices to reduce congestion; check if staggering splices is feasible
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: l_governing_mm > 60 * bar_diameter_mm

fmea.failureMode: Large bar (d_b > 28 mm) in seismic zone: ACI 318 §18.8.2.1 prohibits lap splices within beam-column joints and in potential plastic hinge zones — use mechanical couplers
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: load_type === 'seismic' && bar_diameter_mm > 28

fmea.failureMode: No transverse reinforcement on tension splice: splitting failure risk — provide minimum ties per ACI §25.5.2; minimum 2 ties within splice length at third-points
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: transverse_reinforcement === 'none' && load_type === 'tension'

fmea.failureMode: Concrete strength below recommended for large bars: development length will be excessive and bond stress may cause splitting — upgrade to recommended f'_c
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: !fc_adequate && bar_diameter_mm >= 25

fmea.failureMode: EC2 development length > 1.5× ACI value: significant code disparity — verify which standard governs for the project; EC2 tends to be more conservative for poor cover conditions
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lbd_EC2_mm > ld_ACI_mm * 1.5

tool_name: Hollow Section (RHS/SHS/CHS) Beam-Column — Combined Axial + Bending Interaction Check

inputs.name: Hollow Section Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Factored Axial Load (N_Ed / P_u)
inputs.note: Positive = compression; negative = tension
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Factored Bending Moment (M_Ed / M_u)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Cross-Sectional Area (A)
inputs.note: From CIDECT/manufacturer tables: RHS 200×100×8 → A=4448 mm²; SHS 150×150×8 → A=4448 mm²
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Plastic Section Modulus About Bending Axis (W_pl,y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Second Moment of Area About Bending Axis (I_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (f_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Effective Buckling Length (L_cr)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Radius of Gyration About Bending Axis (i_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: NRd_kn = A_mm2 * fy_mpa / (1.0 * 1000)  // [kN] — Compression Resistance (N_Rd) — Ref: EC3 §6.2.4 — N_c,Rd = A·f_y/γ_M0; γ_M0=1.0; CHS/RHS Class 1/2 cross-section

lambda_bar = (L_eff_m * 1000 / i_y_mm) / (Math.PI * Math.sqrt(210000 / fy_mpa)); phi_buck = 0.5 * (1 + 0.21 * (lambda_bar - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar, 2)); chi = Math.min(1.0, 1 / (phi_buck + Math.sqrt(Math.pow(phi_buck, 2) - Math.pow(lambda_bar, 2))))  // Non-Dimensional Slenderness (λ̄) & Buckling Reduction (χ) — Ref: EC3 §6.3.1.2 — λ̄ = (L_cr/i)/(π√(E/f_y)); buckling curve 'a' (α=0.21) for hollow sections
NbRd_kn = chi * A_mm2 * fy_mpa / 1000  // [kN] — Flexural Buckling Resistance (N_b,Rd) — Ref: EC3 §6.3.1.1 — N_b,Rd = χ·A·f_y/γ_M1; γ_M1=1.0
MRd_knm = Wpl_y_cm3 * 1e3 * fy_mpa / 1e6  // [kN·m] — Moment Resistance (M_Rd) — Ref: EC3 §6.2.5 — M_pl,Rd = W_pl,y·f_y/γ_M0; Class 1/2 section

k_yy = 1 + (lambda_bar - 0.2) * N_Ed_kn / NbRd_kn; k_yy = Math.min(k_yy, 1.0 + 0.8 * N_Ed_kn / NbRd_kn); UC_6_61 = N_Ed_kn / NbRd_kn + k_yy * M_Ed_knm / MRd_knm  // EC3 Combined Interaction Check (Eq.6.61 / 6.62) — Ref: EC3 §6.3.3 Eq.(6.61) — N_Ed/N_b,Rd + k_yy·M_y,Ed/M_y,Rd ≤ 1.0
UC_cross = N_Ed_kn / NRd_kn + M_Ed_knm / MRd_knm  // Cross-Section Check (Eq.6.31) — Local Plasticity — Ref: EC3 §6.2.9.1 simplified: N_Ed/N_c,Rd + M_Ed/M_Rd ≤ 1.0 for Class 1/2; conservative

rules.message: Beam-column interaction check failed (EC3 Eq.6.61 > 1.0) — increase section size, reduce effective length, or reduce applied loads
Cross-section combined loading check failed — section yields locally; use plastic moment capacity only if section is Class 1
Non-dimensional slenderness > 2.0: highly slender — buckling governs heavily; N_b,Rd < 0.25·N_Rd; intermediate bracing strongly recommended

smart_warnings.message: Utilisation ratio > 90%: minimal safety reserve — verify load combinations include all accidental and wind/seismic actions; do not proceed without structural engineer sign-off
High axial + moderate moment: k_yy amplification significant — consider moment connection detail to reduce effective eccentricity
Slender CHS column: LTB does not apply for CHS — only flexural buckling governs; CHS advantage over open sections is fully realised here
Long column in S235: consider S355 to reduce section size — same χ at lower weight for slenderness-governed design
Buckling factor χ < 0.5: more than 50% of section capacity lost to buckling — verify effective length assumptions; pinned connections often provide partial restraint; consider intermediate bracing

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: UC_6_61 > 1.0
fmea.effect: Beam-column interaction check failed (EC3 Eq.6.61 > 1.0) — increase section size, reduce effective length, or reduce applied loads
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: UC_cross > 1.0
fmea.effect: Cross-section combined loading check failed — section yields locally; use plastic moment capacity only if section is Class 1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: lambda_bar > 2.0
fmea.effect: Non-dimensional slenderness > 2.0: highly slender — buckling governs heavily; N_b,Rd < 0.25·N_Rd; intermediate bracing strongly recommended

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation ratio > 90%: minimal safety reserve — verify load combinations include all accidental and wind/seismic actions; do not proceed without structural engineer sign-off
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: UC_6_61 > 0.90 || UC_cross > 0.90

fmea.failureMode: High axial + moderate moment: k_yy amplification significant — consider moment connection detail to reduce effective eccentricity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_Ed_kn > NbRd_kn * 0.7 && M_Ed_knm > MRd_knm * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Slender CHS column: LTB does not apply for CHS — only flexural buckling governs; CHS advantage over open sections is fully realised here
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lambda_bar > 1.5 && section_type === 'CHS'

fmea.failureMode: Long column in S235: consider S355 to reduce section size — same χ at lower weight for slenderness-governed design
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_eff_m > 8 && fy_mpa === 235

fmea.failureMode: Buckling factor χ < 0.5: more than 50% of section capacity lost to buckling — verify effective length assumptions; pinned connections often provide partial restraint; consider intermediate bracing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: chi < 0.5

tool_name: Precision Boring & Reaming — ISO Fit Selection, Tolerance Band & Achievable Surface Roughness

inputs.name: Nominal Bore Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: ISO Hole Tolerance Grade (IT Grade)

options.label: IT5 — Precision bearings, gauge bores (Ra ≤ 0.4 µm)
IT6 — General precision, ball bearings (Ra ≤ 0.8 µm)
IT8 — Medium precision, loose fits (Ra ≤ 3.2 µm)
IT9 — Coarse press fits, rough bores (Ra ≤ 6.3 µm)

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Hole Fundamental Deviation (Position Code)

options.label: H — Zero lower deviation (standard hole basis)

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Mating Shaft Designation (e.g. h6, f7, k6)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Boring / Reaming Operation

options.label: Honing after boring — IT5–IT6, Ra 0.05–0.4 µm

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Cutting Speed (V_c)
inputs.note: Reaming steel: 8–15 m/min; boring steel: 60–180 m/min; boring aluminium: 200–500 m/min
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Feed per Revolution (f_r)
inputs.note: Reaming: 0.1–0.5 mm/rev; fine boring: 0.03–0.15 mm/rev; rough boring: 0.2–1.0 mm/rev
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: D_mean = Math.sqrt(Math.max(1, nominal_diameter_mm - 3) * nominal_diameter_mm); i_micron = 0.45 * Math.pow(D_mean, 1/3) + 0.001 * D_mean; const IT_factor = {5:7, 6:10, 7:16, 8:25, 9:40}; IT_um = IT_factor[ISO_hole_tolerance] * i_micron  // [µm] — IT Tolerance Value (IT) for Nominal Diameter — Ref: ISO 286-1:2010 §4.2 — IT = c × i; i = 0.45·D_mean^(1/3) + 0.001·D_mean; IT7 factor = 16

EI_um = 0; ES_um = IT_um; bore_min_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + EI_um/1000; bore_max_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + ES_um/1000  // [mm] — Hole Limits (Upper and Lower Deviation for H-basis) — Ref: ISO 286-1 — H basis: EI = 0 (lower deviation); ES = +IT; bore Φ_min to Φ_max

const shaft_offsets = {h6:{ei:-IT_um*0.4, es:0}, h7:{ei:-IT_um*0.6, es:0}, f7:{ei:-IT_um*1.6, es:-IT_um}, g6:{ei:-IT_um*0.7, es:-IT_um*0.3}, k6:{ei:0, es:IT_um*0.5}, p6:{ei:IT_um*0.5, es:IT_um*1.2}, s6:{ei:IT_um*1.2, es:IT_um*2.0}}; const sf = shaft_offsets[shaft_designation]; shaft_min_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + sf.ei/1000; shaft_max_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + sf.es/1000  // [mm] — Typical Shaft Tolerance Band (for common fits) — Ref: ISO 286-1 Table 3 — shaft fundamental deviations; approximate for common designations

max_clearance_um = (bore_max_mm - shaft_min_mm) * 1000; min_clearance_um = (bore_min_mm - shaft_max_mm) * 1000  // [µm] — Fit Limits — Maximum Clearance & Maximum Interference — Ref: Clearance > 0 = clearance fit; < 0 = interference; between = transition — ISO 286-1 §3

n_rpm = Vc_m_min * 1000 / (Math.PI * nominal_diameter_mm); Vf_mm_min = n_rpm * feed_per_rev_mm  // [rpm, mm/min] — Spindle Speed & Table Feed Rate — Ref: n = V_c × 1000 / (π × D); V_f = n × f_r — machining parameter calculation

const Ra_cap = {fine_boring:[0.2,0.8], reaming:[0.4,1.6], rough_boring:[3.2,12.5], honing:[0.05,0.4]}; const rc = Ra_cap[boring_tool]; Ra_achievable_min=rc[0]; Ra_achievable_max=rc[1]; const IT_Ra_map = {5:0.4, 6:0.8, 7:1.6, 8:3.2, 9:6.3}; Ra_req_max = IT_Ra_map[ISO_hole_tolerance]; process_capable = Ra_achievable_max <= Ra_req_max  // [µm, µm, boolean] — Achievable Ra & Process Capability Check — Ref: ISO 2768 / Kalpakjian §26 — surface roughness vs IT grade correlation; process must achieve Ra ≤ Ra_req

rules.message: Selected boring/reaming process cannot achieve required Ra for specified IT grade — upgrade to finer process (e.g., add honing after boring for IT5/IT6)
Minimum clearance > 200 µm: very loose fit — verify shaft designation is correct; this may cause bearing misalignment or excessive play
Table feed > 500 mm/min for reaming: excessive — maximum feed for reaming is typically 150 mm/min; higher feeds cause chatter marks and dimensional errors

smart_warnings.message: Unexpected interference fit: verify shaft designation — transition fits (k6) can have interference but assembly may require press or thermal expansion method
IT tolerance < 10 µm required but rough boring selected: this process cannot hold ≤ IT5 — mandatory fine boring or honing; tool setup and thermal stabilisation critical
Large bore (D > 500 mm) with tight IT6 tolerance: thermal expansion alone can exceed IT value — workpiece must be measured at 20°C ±1°C per ISO 1 temperature standard
Reaming speed > 150 m/min: built-up edge and chatter risk — reaming is inherently a low-speed finishing process; reduce to 8–15 m/min for steel, 30–50 m/min for aluminium
Wide clearance band relative to IT: verify mating shaft tolerance is correctly specified — total system clearance variation may affect running accuracy or press-fit security

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: !process_capable
fmea.effect: Selected boring/reaming process cannot achieve required Ra for specified IT grade — upgrade to finer process (e.g., add honing after boring for IT5/IT6)

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: min_clearance_um > 200 && shaft_designation !== 'f7'
fmea.effect: Minimum clearance > 200 µm: very loose fit — verify shaft designation is correct; this may cause bearing misalignment or excessive play

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Vf_mm_min > 500 && boring_tool === 'reaming'
fmea.effect: Table feed > 500 mm/min for reaming: excessive — maximum feed for reaming is typically 150 mm/min; higher feeds cause chatter marks and dimensional errors

fmea.failureMode: Unexpected interference fit: verify shaft designation — transition fits (k6) can have interference but assembly may require press or thermal expansion method
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: min_clearance_um < -50 && shaft_designation !== 'p6' && shaft_designation !== 's6'

fmea.failureMode: IT tolerance < 10 µm required but rough boring selected: this process cannot hold ≤ IT5 — mandatory fine boring or honing; tool setup and thermal stabilisation critical
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: IT_um < 10 && boring_tool === 'rough_boring'

fmea.failureMode: Large bore (D > 500 mm) with tight IT6 tolerance: thermal expansion alone can exceed IT value — workpiece must be measured at 20°C ±1°C per ISO 1 temperature standard
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: nominal_diameter_mm > 500 && ISO_hole_tolerance <= 6

fmea.failureMode: Reaming speed > 150 m/min: built-up edge and chatter risk — reaming is inherently a low-speed finishing process; reduce to 8–15 m/min for steel, 30–50 m/min for aluminium
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vc_m_min > 150 && boring_tool === 'reaming'

fmea.failureMode: Wide clearance band relative to IT: verify mating shaft tolerance is correctly specified — total system clearance variation may affect running accuracy or press-fit security
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: max_clearance_um - min_clearance_um > IT_um * 3

tool_name: Welded T-Joint & Angle Bracket — Weld Group Moment, Shear & Torsion Capacity

inputs.name: Fillet Weld Leg Size (a_leg)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Weld Group Pattern
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Connection Plate Width (b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Connection Plate Height (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Applied Shear Force (V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Applied In-Plane Bending Moment (M_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Load Eccentricity from Weld Group Centroid (e)
inputs.note: Bracket with standoff distance: e = horizontal distance from load to weld group centroid
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Filler Metal Classification Strength (F_EXX)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: a_throat = weld_leg_mm * 0.707; const patterns = {three_side: {A: (2*plate_height_mm + plate_width_mm)*a_throat, Iy: (2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12 + plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm/2,2))*a_throat/1e6}, four_side: {A: 2*(plate_width_mm+plate_height_mm)*a_throat, Iy: (2*plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm/2,2) + 2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12)*a_throat/1e6}}; const wp = patterns['four_side']; Aw_mm2 = 2*(plate_width_mm + plate_height_mm) * a_throat; Iy_mm4 = (2*plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,2)/4 + 2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12) * a_throat  // [mm², mm⁴] — Weld Group Throat Area & Properties — Ref: Weld group elastic properties — Blodgett 'Design of Welded Structures' §7.4; AISC DG16 §3.2
f_w_allow_mpa = 0.75 * 0.6 * filler_fexx_mpa  // [MPa] — Allowable Weld Shear Stress (f_w,allow) — Ref: AWS D1.1-2020 §2.4.1 / AISC 360-22 §J2.4 — φ·f_w = 0.75 × 0.6·F_EXX on effective throat area
fv_mpa = V_Ed_kn * 1000 / Aw_mm2  // [MPa] — Direct Shear Stress in Weld (f_v) — Ref: f_v = V / A_w — direct shear on weld throat

M_total_Nmm = (M_Ed_knm + V_Ed_kn * eccentricity_mm / 1000) * 1e6; fb_mpa = M_total_Nmm * (plate_height_mm / 2) / Iy_mm4  // [MPa] — Bending Stress in Weld from In-Plane Moment (f_b) — Ref: f_b = M × c / I_y; c = d/2 (extreme fibre distance) — elastic weld group analysis per Blodgett §7.4

f_resultant_mpa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(fv_mpa, 2) + Math.pow(fb_mpa, 2)); UC_weld = f_resultant_mpa / f_w_allow_mpa  // [MPa, dimensionless] — Resultant Weld Stress & Utilisation (Vector Sum) — Ref: Vector sum: f_res = √(f_v² + f_b²); UC = f_res / f_allow — elastic vector method per AISC DG16
a_req_mm = Math.ceil(weld_leg_mm * UC_weld / 0.707 * 0.707)  // [mm] — Required Weld Leg for Demand (a_required) — Ref: Required leg = current leg × UC_weld (if UC > 1, scale up proportionally)

standards: AISC Design Guide 16 (Flush and Extended End-Plate Moment Connections)

rules.message: Weld group overstressed — increase weld leg size, extend weld pattern to 4-sided, or reduce applied loads
Small weld leg under significant moment: moment arm is short — minimum 6 mm leg recommended for moment connections; quality control of small welds is difficult
Large eccentricity relative to plate height: torsion-like loading — verify weld group polar moment of inertia; instantaneous centre of rotation method may be required for high eccentricity

smart_warnings.message: Weld utilisation > 85%: increase leg by 2 mm increments — note that a 25% increase in leg size (e.g., 8 → 10 mm) increases capacity by 25% for the same pattern
Bending stress dominates (>3× shear): verify that weld configuration is optimised — adding weld at extreme fibres (top/bottom) is most efficient for moment resistance
Two vertical welds under moment: very low horizontal polar moment — moment creates high horizontal force with no top/bottom horizontal weld to resist it; switch to 4-sided weld pattern
Large bracket eccentricity (> 200 mm) with high shear: significant eccentric moment — verify weld group using instantaneous centre method per AISC Manual Table 8-4 for accurate capacity
Required weld leg exceeds half the plate thickness: weld will be as thick as the plate — verify plate adequate strength and consider full-penetration butt weld instead

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: UC_weld > 1.0
fmea.effect: Weld group overstressed — increase weld leg size, extend weld pattern to 4-sided, or reduce applied loads
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: weld_leg_mm < 5 && M_Ed_knm > 5
fmea.effect: Small weld leg under significant moment: moment arm is short — minimum 6 mm leg recommended for moment connections; quality control of small welds is difficult

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: eccentricity_mm > plate_height_mm && V_Ed_kn > 20
fmea.effect: Large eccentricity relative to plate height: torsion-like loading — verify weld group polar moment of inertia; instantaneous centre of rotation method may be required for high eccentricity

fmea.failureMode: Weld utilisation > 85%: increase leg by 2 mm increments — note that a 25% increase in leg size (e.g., 8 → 10 mm) increases capacity by 25% for the same pattern
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: UC_weld > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Bending stress dominates (>3× shear): verify that weld configuration is optimised — adding weld at extreme fibres (top/bottom) is most efficient for moment resistance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fb_mpa > fv_mpa * 3

fmea.failureMode: Two vertical welds under moment: very low horizontal polar moment — moment creates high horizontal force with no top/bottom horizontal weld to resist it; switch to 4-sided weld pattern
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: weld_pattern === '2_vert' && M_Ed_knm > 0

fmea.failureMode: Large bracket eccentricity (> 200 mm) with high shear: significant eccentric moment — verify weld group using instantaneous centre method per AISC Manual Table 8-4 for accurate capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eccentricity_mm > 200 && V_Ed_kn > 50

fmea.failureMode: Required weld leg exceeds half the plate thickness: weld will be as thick as the plate — verify plate adequate strength and consider full-penetration butt weld instead
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_req_mm > plate_width_mm * 0.5 / 0.707

tool_name: Masonry Load-Bearing Wall — Axial Capacity, Slenderness Reduction & Eccentricity Check

inputs.name: Clear Wall Height (h)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Wall Thickness (t)
inputs.note: Standard: 100 mm block, 140 mm, 190 mm, 200 mm, 215 mm (brick), 250 mm, 300 mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Masonry Characteristic Compressive Strength (f_k)
inputs.note: EN 1996 Table NA.1: 100 mm dense block + M4 mortar → f_k ≈ 3.5–6 MPa; engineering brick + M12 → 8–15 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Partial Safety Factor for Masonry (γ_M)

options.label: γ_M = 1.5 — Category I units, controlled site
γ_M = 2.0 — Category II units, standard site

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Design Vertical Load per Unit Length of Wall (N_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Load Eccentricity at Mid-Height (e_mid)
inputs.note: EN 1996-1-1 §6.1.2.2: e_mid = e_i + e_hi; e_i = accidental eccentricity = h_eff/450; e_hi from horizontal loads
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Wall End / Support Restraint Condition
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: h_eff_m = wall_height_m * end_condition; lambda = h_eff_m * 1000 / wall_thickness_mm  // [m, dimensionless] — Effective Height & Slenderness Ratio (λ) — Ref: EN 1996-1-1:2005 §5.5.1.2 — h_eff = ρ_n × h; EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum λ = 27
fd_mpa = fk_mpa / gamma_M  // [MPa] — Design Compressive Strength (f_d) — Ref: EC6 §6.1.2.1 — f_d = f_k / γ_M; design strength accounting for workmanship and material class

e_rel = eccentricity_mm / wall_thickness_mm; A_phi = 1 - 2 * e_rel; Phi_m = Math.max(A_phi * (1 - (lambda/140)^2), 0.0)  // Capacity Reduction Factor for Slenderness (Φ_m) — Ref: EN 1996-1-1 §6.1.2.2 Eq.(G.2) — Φ_m = (1−2e/t)(1−(λ/140)²); Annex G simplified method
NRd_kn_m = Phi_m * wall_thickness_mm * fd_mpa  // [kN/m] — Design Vertical Load Resistance (N_Rd) — Ref: EC6 §6.1.2.1 — N_Rd = Φ_m × t × f_d (per unit length); t in mm, f_d in MPa → kN/m

DCR = N_Ed_kn_m / NRd_kn_m; e_limit_mm = wall_thickness_mm / 3  // [dimensionless, mm] — Demand-Capacity Ratio (DCR) & Utilisation — Ref: DCR ≤ 1.0 required; e_limit = t/3 per EC6 §6.1.2.2 — maximum allowable eccentricity
t_req_mm = Math.ceil(N_Ed_kn_m / (Phi_m * fd_mpa))  // [mm] — Minimum Required Wall Thickness for Load (t_req) — Ref: Rearranged from N_Rd: t_req = N_Ed / (Φ_m × f_d); round up to standard block size

rules.message: Wall load capacity exceeded — increase thickness, use higher-strength masonry units, or reduce applied load
Slenderness ratio > 27: EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum limit — wall is too slender; add pilasters, returns, or intermediate lateral supports
Eccentricity exceeds t/3: EC6 §6.1.2.2 limit — Φ_m approaches zero; review structural layout to reduce eccentricity; consider larger wall thickness

smart_warnings.message: High slenderness with pinned ends: Φ_m strongly reduced — adding simple floor/roof tie at top converts to fixed-pin (ρ=0.75), increasing N_Rd by ~25%
Low-strength masonry under significant load: specify minimum M4 mortar and Category I units with testing — avoid using generic f_k without material testing on site
Utilisation > 80%: marginal capacity for concentrated loads (beam bearings) — verify bearing stress at load introduction point per EC6 §6.1.3
Thin wall (< 100 mm) under significant vertical load: structural masonry requires minimum 100 mm; check if wall is load-bearing or partition — reclassify if non-structural
Combined high eccentricity + slenderness: double penalty on Φ_m — capacity is severely reduced; detailed second-order analysis required per EC6 Annex G

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Wall load capacity exceeded — increase thickness, use higher-strength masonry units, or reduce applied load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lambda > 27
fmea.effect: Slenderness ratio > 27: EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum limit — wall is too slender; add pilasters, returns, or intermediate lateral supports
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: eccentricity_mm > wall_thickness_mm / 3
fmea.effect: Eccentricity exceeds t/3: EC6 §6.1.2.2 limit — Φ_m approaches zero; review structural layout to reduce eccentricity; consider larger wall thickness

fmea.failureMode: High slenderness with pinned ends: Φ_m strongly reduced — adding simple floor/roof tie at top converts to fixed-pin (ρ=0.75), increasing N_Rd by ~25%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lambda > 20 && end_condition === 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength masonry under significant load: specify minimum M4 mortar and Category I units with testing — avoid using generic f_k without material testing on site
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fk_mpa < 3.5 && N_Ed_kn_m > 100

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 80%: marginal capacity for concentrated loads (beam bearings) — verify bearing stress at load introduction point per EC6 §6.1.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR > 0.80

fmea.failureMode: Thin wall (< 100 mm) under significant vertical load: structural masonry requires minimum 100 mm; check if wall is load-bearing or partition — reclassify if non-structural
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wall_thickness_mm < 100 && N_Ed_kn_m > 50

fmea.failureMode: Combined high eccentricity + slenderness: double penalty on Φ_m — capacity is severely reduced; detailed second-order analysis required per EC6 Annex G
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eccentricity_mm > wall_thickness_mm * 0.25 && lambda > 18

tool_name: CNC Cylindrical Grinding — Wheel Speed, Q-Prime MRR, G-Ratio & Grinding Burn Prevention

inputs.name: Grinding Wheel Outside Diameter (D_s)
inputs.note: Standard sizes: 200, 250, 300, 350, 400, 450, 500 mm for cylindrical OD grinding
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Wheel Rotational Speed (n_s)
inputs.note: Never exceed wheel's maximum RPM stamped on blotter — ANSI B7.1 §6.3 mandatory check
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Workpiece Diameter (D_w)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Workpiece Rotational Speed (n_w)
inputs.note: Typical OD grinding: 100–400 rpm; higher speeds increase temperature and heat generation
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Radial Infeed per Pass (a_e)
inputs.note: Roughing: 0.02–0.05 mm/pass; semi-finish: 0.005–0.02; finish: 0.001–0.005; sparking-out: 0
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Axial Traverse Feed per Workpiece Revolution (f_a)
inputs.note: f_a = fraction of wheel width B: roughing ≈ 0.5–0.8B; finishing ≈ 0.1–0.3B
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Wheel Grade / Hardness

options.label: F/G — Soft (vitrified, soft workpieces, heavy stock)
Q/R/S — Hard (fine finish, very hard materials)

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: Vs_m_s = Math.PI * wheel_diameter_mm / 1000 * wheel_speed_rpm / 60  // [m/s] — Wheel Peripheral Speed (V_s) — Ref: V_s = π × D_s × n_s / 60 — ANSI B7.1 §6.3; maximum speed MUST NOT exceed wheel marking
Vw_m_min = Math.PI * workpiece_diameter_mm / 1000 * workpiece_speed_rpm  // [m/min] — Workpiece Peripheral Speed (V_w) — Ref: V_w = π × D_w × n_w — workpiece surface speed

Qprime_mm3_mm_s = Vw_m_min / 60 * 1000 * infeed_mm_pass  // [mm³/(mm·s)] — Specific Material Removal Rate (Q'_w) — Ref: Q'_w = V_w × a_e — specific MRR per unit wheel width; key thermal indicator — Malkin §4.3

q_ratio = Vs_m_s / (Vw_m_min / 60); temp_index = Qprime_mm3_mm_s / Vs_m_s  // [dimensionless, mm²/(m)] — Speed Ratio (q) & Grinding Temperature Index — Ref: q = V_s/V_w — speed ratio; thermal index = Q'_w/V_s; Malkin-Shaw grinding burn criterion: temp_index < 0.04 for steel

const G_base = {soft:20, medium_soft:30, medium:50, medium_hard:80, hard:120}; G_est = G_base[wheel_grade_hardness] * (Vs_m_s / 35)  // [mm³_workpiece/mm³_wheel] — G-Ratio (Grinding Ratio) Estimate — Ref: G = V_w_removed / V_wheel_worn — empirical estimate by grade; G_base at V_s=35 m/s; Malkin §7

traverse_rate_mm_min = traverse_feed_mm_rev * workpiece_speed_rpm; t_pass_s = (workpiece_diameter_mm * Math.PI) / (Vw_m_min / 60 * 1000) * (traverse_feed_mm_rev / 1)  // [mm/min] — Axial Traverse Rate & Cycle Time per Pass — Ref: Axial traverse = f_a × n_w [mm/min]; traverse rate for CNC programme setup

rules.message: Wheel peripheral speed > 45 m/s: exceeds standard vitrified wheel limit — verify wheel is rated for high-speed grinding (HSG mark); operator safety risk of wheel burst per ANSI B7.1
Speed ratio q < 20: conventional grinding range too low — wheel may load and glaze; increase V_s or reduce V_w; target q = 60–150 for conventional grinding
Thermal index > 0.05: grinding burn risk on steel — reduce Q'_w by decreasing infeed or workpiece speed; verify coolant flow rate ≥ 20 L/min at wheel contact zone

smart_warnings.message: High wheel speed with soft grade: wheel structure opens rapidly — soft grades lose bonds faster at high speed; upgrade to medium-soft for V_s > 35 m/s applications
Large infeed on small workpiece: Q'_w very high relative to workpiece thermal mass — grinding burn and workpiece deflection imminent; reduce infeed to < 0.01 mm for D_w < 20 mm
Wide traverse pitch on fine infeed: likely chatter pattern will appear — finish pass traverse should be ≤ 1/3 of wheel width B for uniform surface without helical marks
Low G-ratio estimate (< 15): wheel wears rapidly — check if wheel grade is too soft for workpiece hardness; consider harder grade or CBN/diamond wheel for hardened steels (HRC > 55)
High workpiece speed on small diameter: dynamic balance and bearing precision critical — workpiece runout at high rpm causes chatter; verify workpiece mounting and centre alignment to < 0.002 mm TIR

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Vs_m_s > 45
fmea.effect: Wheel peripheral speed > 45 m/s: exceeds standard vitrified wheel limit — verify wheel is rated for high-speed grinding (HSG mark); operator safety risk of wheel burst per ANSI B7.1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: q_ratio < 20
fmea.effect: Speed ratio q < 20: conventional grinding range too low — wheel may load and glaze; increase V_s or reduce V_w; target q = 60–150 for conventional grinding

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: temp_index > 0.05
fmea.effect: Thermal index > 0.05: grinding burn risk on steel — reduce Q'_w by decreasing infeed or workpiece speed; verify coolant flow rate ≥ 20 L/min at wheel contact zone

fmea.failureMode: High wheel speed with soft grade: wheel structure opens rapidly — soft grades lose bonds faster at high speed; upgrade to medium-soft for V_s > 35 m/s applications
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vs_m_s > 35 && wheel_grade_hardness === 'soft'

fmea.failureMode: Large infeed on small workpiece: Q'_w very high relative to workpiece thermal mass — grinding burn and workpiece deflection imminent; reduce infeed to < 0.01 mm for D_w < 20 mm
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: infeed_mm_pass > 0.03 && workpiece_diameter_mm < 20

fmea.failureMode: Wide traverse pitch on fine infeed: likely chatter pattern will appear — finish pass traverse should be ≤ 1/3 of wheel width B for uniform surface without helical marks
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: traverse_feed_mm_rev > 5 && infeed_mm_pass < 0.005

fmea.failureMode: Low G-ratio estimate (< 15): wheel wears rapidly — check if wheel grade is too soft for workpiece hardness; consider harder grade or CBN/diamond wheel for hardened steels (HRC > 55)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: G_est < 15

fmea.failureMode: High workpiece speed on small diameter: dynamic balance and bearing precision critical — workpiece runout at high rpm causes chatter; verify workpiece mounting and centre alignment to < 0.002 mm TIR
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vw_m_min > 50 && workpiece_diameter_mm < 30

tool_name: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC

inputs.name: Bolt Nominal Diameter (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bolt Property Class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Bolts in Group (n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Number of Shear Planes (n_sp)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Factored Shear Force on Bolt Group (V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Thinnest Connected Plate Thickness (t)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Connected Plate Yield Strength (f_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: End Distance in Load Direction (e_1)
inputs.note: EC3 §3.5: minimum e_1 ≥ 1.2 × d_0 (hole diameter = d + 2 mm)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Bolt Pitch in Load Direction (p_1)
inputs.note: EC3 §3.5: minimum p_1 ≥ 2.2 × d_0; standard: 3d to 5d
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: const As_map = {12:84.3, 16:157, 20:245, 24:353, 27:459, 30:561}; As_mm2 = As_map[bolt_diameter_mm]; const fub_map = {'8.8':800, '10.9':1040, '12.9':1220}; fub_mpa = fub_map[property_class]  // [mm², MPa] — Bolt Tensile Stress Area & Ultimate Strength — Ref: ISO 898-1:2013 — tensile stress areas; ISO 4014:2011 structural bolt dimensions

gamma_M2 = 1.25; alpha_v = 0.6; Fv_Rd_kn = alpha_v * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * n_shear_planes / (gamma_M2 * 1000)  // [kN] — Single Bolt Shear Resistance (F_v,Rd) per EC3 — Ref: EC3 §3.6.1 Table 3.4 — F_v,Rd = α_v·f_ub·A_s·n_sp/γ_M2; α_v=0.6 for 8.8/10.9 (shear plane through threaded portion)

d0_mm = bolt_diameter_mm + 2; alpha_b = Math.min(e1_d0 = edge_dist_e1_mm / d0_mm / 3, p1_d0 = pitch_p1_mm / d0_mm / 3 - 0.25, fub_mpa / (plate_fy_mpa * 1.3), 1.0); k1 = Math.min(2.8 * 30 / d0_mm - 1.7, 2.5); Fb_Rd_kn = k1 * alpha_b * plate_fy_mpa * 1.3 * bolt_diameter_mm * connected_plate_t_mm / (gamma_M2 * 1000)  // [kN] — Bearing Resistance — Single Bolt on Thinnest Plate (F_b,Rd) — Ref: EC3 §3.6.1 Table 3.4 — F_b,Rd = k_1·α_b·f_u·d·t/γ_M2; f_u ≈ 1.3·f_y (conservative for S235–S460)

Fgov_single_kn = Math.min(Fv_Rd_kn, Fb_Rd_kn); phi_Rn_group_kn = n_bolts * Fgov_single_kn  // [kN] — Governing Single Bolt Resistance & Group Capacity — Ref: Group capacity = n × min(F_v,Rd, F_b,Rd) — EC3 §3.7 (no group reduction for n ≤ 6 in standard pitch)
DCR = V_Ed_kn / phi_Rn_group_kn  // Demand-Capacity Ratio (DCR) — Ref: DCR = V_Ed / R_group ≤ 1.0 required — EC3 §3.7 / AISC 360-22 §J3

n_req = Math.ceil(V_Ed_kn / Fgov_single_kn); slip_check_kn = 0.7 * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * 0.5 / (gamma_M2 * 1000) * n_bolts  // [count, kN] — Minimum Required Bolt Count for Demand — Ref: n_req = V_Ed / F_single; slip resistance (cat. B) = 0.7·f_ub·A_s·μ/γ_M3 — EC3 §3.9 (μ=0.5 class A surface)

rules.message: Bolt group shear/bearing capacity exceeded — increase bolt count, diameter, or property class
End distance e_1 < 1.2 × d_0: EC3 §3.5 minimum violated — bolt will tear out of plate
Bolt pitch p_1 < 2.2 × d_0: below EC3 minimum spacing — risk of plate splitting between holes

smart_warnings.message: Bearing governs at <60% of shear capacity: plate is thin or end/edge distance is short — increase plate thickness or end distance e_1 to balance resistances
Large bolt group in single shear (n > 8): EC3 §3.8 long joint reduction factor β_Lj applies when Lj > 15d — verify connection length does not trigger reduction
12.9 class bolt with thin plate: bearing governs severely — 12.9 bolts rarely needed in standard structural connections; verify whether 10.9 suffices
Utilisation > 85%: marginal for fatigue or seismic load — add one bolt or increase diameter; fatigue class detail per EC3-1-9 is independent of static capacity
Slip resistance insufficient for Category B (serviceability): connection will slip under service load — specify pretensioned bolts and improve faying surface to Class A (sand-blasted) μ=0.5

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Bolt group shear/bearing capacity exceeded — increase bolt count, diameter, or property class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: edge_dist_e1_mm < 1.2 * (bolt_diameter_mm + 2)
fmea.effect: End distance e_1 < 1.2 × d_0: EC3 §3.5 minimum violated — bolt will tear out of plate
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: pitch_p1_mm < 2.2 * (bolt_diameter_mm + 2)
fmea.effect: Bolt pitch p_1 < 2.2 × d_0: below EC3 minimum spacing — risk of plate splitting between holes

fmea.failureMode: Bearing governs at <60% of shear capacity: plate is thin or end/edge distance is short — increase plate thickness or end distance e_1 to balance resistances
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fb_Rd_kn < Fv_Rd_kn * 0.6

fmea.failureMode: Large bolt group in single shear (n > 8): EC3 §3.8 long joint reduction factor β_Lj applies when Lj > 15d — verify connection length does not trigger reduction
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_bolts > 8 && n_shear_planes === 1

fmea.failureMode: 12.9 class bolt with thin plate: bearing governs severely — 12.9 bolts rarely needed in standard structural connections; verify whether 10.9 suffices
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: property_class === '12.9' && connected_plate_t_mm < 15

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 85%: marginal for fatigue or seismic load — add one bolt or increase diameter; fatigue class detail per EC3-1-9 is independent of static capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Slip resistance insufficient for Category B (serviceability): connection will slip under service load — specify pretensioned bolts and improve faying surface to Class A (sand-blasted) μ=0.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: slip_check_kn < V_Ed_kn && n_shear_planes === 1

tool_name: Orthogonal Cutting Mechanics — Chip Thickness Ratio, Shear Angle, Cutting Forces & Tool Life

inputs.name: Uncut Chip Thickness (t_1 = feed per rev for orthogonal cut)
inputs.note: For orthogonal turning: t_1 = f (feed). Defines pre-cut chip geometry.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Measured Chip Thickness After Cutting (t_2)
inputs.note: Measure deformed chip with micrometer; always t_2 > t_1 for normal cutting
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Tool Rake Angle (γ)
inputs.note: Positive rake = sharper, lower force; negative rake = stronger edge, higher force; carbide inserts: -7° to +15°
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Tool-Chip Interface Friction Coefficient (μ)
inputs.note: Dry cutting steel on carbide: μ≈0.5–0.8; with flood coolant: μ≈0.3–0.5; can be measured via F_t/F_c ratio
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.2 — Empirical coefficient: ±5% reproducibility

inputs.name: Shear Yield Stress of Workpiece at Shear Plane (k_s)
inputs.note: k_s ≈ 0.6 × Rm (UTS); steel 0.45C: k_s≈350 MPa; Ti6Al4V: k_s≈550 MPa; Al7075: k_s≈200 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Width of Cut (b = depth of cut for orthogonal)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Cutting Speed (V_c)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 230-2:2014 §5 — CNC axis velocity: positioning ±5-25µm, ≈±2%

inputs.name: Taylor Constant C at Reference Conditions
inputs.note: From manufacturer or ISO 3685 tool life test; C = speed for 1-minute tool life
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Taylor Exponent (n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: r_c = uncut_chip_thickness_mm / chip_thickness_mm; gamma_rad = rake_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180; phi_rad = Math.atan(r_c * Math.cos(gamma_rad) / (1 - r_c * Math.sin(gamma_rad))); phi_deg = phi_rad * 180 / Math.PI  // [dimensionless, °] — Chip Thickness Ratio (r_c) & Shear Plane Angle (φ) — Ref: Merchant (1945) minimum energy: tan(φ) = r_c·cos(γ)/(1−r_c·sin(γ)) — CIRP Annals Vol.57; r_c < 1 always (chip thicker than uncut)

beta_rad = Math.atan(friction_coefficient_mu); beta_deg = beta_rad * 180 / Math.PI  // [°] — Friction Angle (β) on Tool-Chip Interface — Ref: β = arctan(μ) — Merchant's friction model; λ = β in Merchant circle

shear_area_mm2 = uncut_chip_thickness_mm * width_of_cut_mm / Math.sin(phi_rad); Fs_N = shear_stress_mpa * shear_area_mm2; Fn_N = Fs_N * Math.tan(phi_rad + beta_rad - gamma_rad)  // [N] — Shear Force on Shear Plane (F_s) & Normal Force (F_n) — Ref: F_s = k_s × A_s; A_s = t_1×b/sinφ — Merchant model; Fn via geometry of Merchant circle

Fc_N = Fs_N * Math.cos(beta_rad - gamma_rad) / Math.cos(phi_rad + beta_rad - gamma_rad); Ft_N = Fs_N * Math.sin(beta_rad - gamma_rad) / Math.cos(phi_rad + beta_rad - gamma_rad)  // [N] — Tangential (Cutting) Force F_c & Thrust Force F_t — Ref: Merchant circle: F_c = F_s·cos(β−γ)/cos(φ+β−γ); F_t = F_s·sin(β−γ)/cos(φ+β−γ) — Boothroyd §4

Pc_kw = Fc_N * Vc_m_min / 60000; u_specific_J_mm3 = Fc_N / (uncut_chip_thickness_mm * width_of_cut_mm * Vc_m_min / 60 * 1000) * Vc_m_min / 60  // [kW] — Net Cutting Power & Specific Energy — Ref: P_c = F_c × V_c / 60000; specific energy u = P_c / MRR

T_life_min = Math.pow(Taylor_C_ref / Vc_m_min, 1 / Taylor_n_exp); T_eco_min = Taylor_n_exp / (1 - Taylor_n_exp) * (2 + 0); V_eco_m_min = Taylor_C_ref * Math.pow(T_eco_min, -Taylor_n_exp)  // [min, m/min] — Tool Life Prediction — Extended Taylor (T_life) — Ref: Taylor: T = (C/V)^(1/n) — ISO 3685; economic speed V_eco (minimum cost) = C·(n·t_ct/(1−n))^(−n) simplified

rules.message: Chip thickness t_2 must exceed uncut thickness t_1: chip always thickens during cutting (r_c < 1). Verify measurement — thinner chip indicates measurement error or built-up edge
Shear angle < 5°: extreme chip compression — very high cutting forces and temperature; check if friction is excessive or rake angle too negative
Tool life < 5 minutes: uneconomical — reduce V_c by 15–20%; target T = 15–25 min for economic insert change interval per ISO 3685

smart_warnings.message: Chip thickness ratio < 0.2: severe chip compression — chip is 5× thicker than uncut; heat generation and tool wear will be extreme; increase rake angle or apply flood coolant
Shear angle > 50°: unusually large — verify chip thickness measurement accuracy; r_c > 0.8 may indicate built-up edge depositing on tool face
Cutting power > 50 kW: verify machine spindle rating; thermal loading at this level causes workpiece distortion; verify fixturing stiffness and coolant supply
Thrust force > 50% of cutting force: high passive/radial load — deflects slender workpieces; L/D > 4 requires steady rest; verify surface finish will be acceptable
Operating below economic tool life: running too fast — at current V_c, tool change cost dominates; reduce to V_eco for minimum cost per part

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: chip_thickness_mm < uncut_chip_thickness_mm
fmea.effect: Chip thickness t_2 must exceed uncut thickness t_1: chip always thickens during cutting (r_c < 1). Verify measurement — thinner chip indicates measurement error or built-up edge
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: phi_deg < 5
fmea.effect: Shear angle < 5°: extreme chip compression — very high cutting forces and temperature; check if friction is excessive or rake angle too negative

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: T_life_min < 5
fmea.effect: Tool life < 5 minutes: uneconomical — reduce V_c by 15–20%; target T = 15–25 min for economic insert change interval per ISO 3685

fmea.failureMode: Chip thickness ratio < 0.2: severe chip compression — chip is 5× thicker than uncut; heat generation and tool wear will be extreme; increase rake angle or apply flood coolant
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: r_c < 0.2

fmea.failureMode: Shear angle > 50°: unusually large — verify chip thickness measurement accuracy; r_c > 0.8 may indicate built-up edge depositing on tool face
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: phi_deg > 50

fmea.failureMode: Cutting power > 50 kW: verify machine spindle rating; thermal loading at this level causes workpiece distortion; verify fixturing stiffness and coolant supply
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pc_kw > 50

fmea.failureMode: Thrust force > 50% of cutting force: high passive/radial load — deflects slender workpieces; L/D > 4 requires steady rest; verify surface finish will be acceptable
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ft_N > Fc_N * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Operating below economic tool life: running too fast — at current V_c, tool change cost dominates; reduce to V_eco for minimum cost per part
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_life_min < T_eco_min * 0.5

tool_name: Single Pile Axial Capacity — Shaft Friction, Base Resistance & EC7 Design Verification

inputs.name: Pile Shaft Diameter (D)
inputs.note: Standard CFA: 300–900 mm; driven steel H: 250–360 mm; bored cast-in-situ: 300–1500 mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Pile Embedded Length (L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

inputs.name: Pile Construction Method

options.label: Driven steel H-pile — β method in sand

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Dominant Soil Profile
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Soil Strength Parameter — c_u (kPa) for clay OR N_60 for sand
inputs.note: Clay: c_u from UU triaxial or vane shear; Sand: N_60 from SPT (energy-corrected)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Shaft Adhesion / Friction Factor (α or β)
inputs.note: Clay α: 0.4–0.5 bored, 0.5–0.6 driven; Sand β: 0.2–0.4; EC7 Annex D / FHWA NHI-16-064
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Unit Base (End-Bearing) Resistance (q_b)
inputs.note: Clay: q_b = N_c × c_u = 9 × c_u; Dense sand: q_b = 3000–8000 kPa; Rock: 10,000–20,000 kPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Partial Resistance Factor for Pile (γ_R,t)

options.label: γ_R,t = 1.3 — Driven pile, model factor correlated

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: D_m = pile_diameter_mm / 1000; perimeter_m = Math.PI * D_m; Ab_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(D_m, 2)  // [m, m²] — Pile Shaft Perimeter & Base Area — Ref: Geometric properties — perimeter for shaft friction integration; base area for end bearing

fs_kpa = (soil_type === 'clay') ? alpha_adhesion * cu_or_N60_value : alpha_adhesion * cu_or_N60_value * 10; Qs_kn = fs_kpa * perimeter_m * pile_length_m  // [kN] — Total Shaft (Skin) Friction Resistance (Q_s) — Ref: Q_s = α·c_u·π·D·L (clay α-method) OR β·σ'_v·π·D·L (sand β-method) — EC7 Annex D; FHWA NHI-16-064 §3.3
Qb_kn = qb_kpa * Ab_m2  // [kN] — Base (End-Bearing) Resistance (Q_b) — Ref: Q_b = q_b × A_b — EC7 §7.6.2.3; q_b from correlation or field test (CPT/PLT)
Rck_kn = Qs_kn + Qb_kn  // [kN] — Characteristic Pile Resistance (R_c,k) — Ref: R_c,k = Q_s + Q_b — EC7 §7.6.2.1; characteristic value from calculation (model factor included in γ_R)

Rcd_kn = Rck_kn / gamma_R_t; mob_factor = (pile_type === 'bored') ? 0.9 : 1.0; Rcd_mob_kn = Rcd_kn * mob_factor  // [kN] — Design Pile Resistance (R_c,d) & Settlement-Limited Mobilisation — Ref: R_c,d = R_c,k / γ_R,t — EC7 §7.6.2.3; mobilisation factor 0.9 for bored piles (full shaft mobilisation at 1% D)

eta_group = (pile_type === 'clay') ? Math.min(1.0, 0.7 + 0.1 * Math.log10(pile_length_m / D_m)) : 1.0; R_group_kn = 4 * Rcd_mob_kn * eta_group  // [dimensionless, kN] — Pile Group Efficiency (for 2×2 group estimate) — Ref: Group efficiency η < 1 in clay (block failure): EC7 §7.6.2.5 / Converse-Labarre; sand groups η ≈ 1.0

rules.message: Design resistance is negative: check soil strength input — minimum c_u or N_60 must be positive and realistic
L/D < 8: stubby pile — shaft friction contribution is limited; this behaves more as a spread footing; verify end bearing dominates
Unit base resistance exceeds 15 × c_u: unrealistic for clay — theoretical maximum N_c = 9 for deep piles; cap q_b at 9 × c_u per Skempton (1951)

smart_warnings.message: End bearing > 70% of shaft: base-dominant pile — settlement at working load may be 30–50 mm before full base mobilisation; consider longer pile to increase shaft contribution
Bored pile in soft clay (c_u < 40 kPa): construction disturbance reduces α — use α = 0.4 maximum; verify bentonite support and concrete workability
Driven precast pile D > 900 mm: driving damage risk is severe for large diameters — switch to bored, CFA, or barrette; driving stresses per GRLWEAP analysis mandatory
Long bored pile with standard γ_R: consider pile testing (static or dynamic CAPWAP) to validate shaft friction distribution — testing permits γ_R reduction to 1.3 per EC7 §7.6.3.3
Group efficiency < 85% in clay: block failure may govern — calculate block capacity per EC7 §7.6.2.5; Qblock = c_u × (perimeter_block × L + Ablock × 9)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Rcd_mob_kn < 0
fmea.effect: Design resistance is negative: check soil strength input — minimum c_u or N_60 must be positive and realistic
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: pile_length_m / (pile_diameter_mm / 1000) < 8
fmea.effect: L/D < 8: stubby pile — shaft friction contribution is limited; this behaves more as a spread footing; verify end bearing dominates

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: qb_kpa > cu_or_N60_value * 15 && soil_type === 'clay'
fmea.effect: Unit base resistance exceeds 15 × c_u: unrealistic for clay — theoretical maximum N_c = 9 for deep piles; cap q_b at 9 × c_u per Skempton (1951)

fmea.failureMode: End bearing > 70% of shaft: base-dominant pile — settlement at working load may be 30–50 mm before full base mobilisation; consider longer pile to increase shaft contribution
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Qb_kn > Qs_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Bored pile in soft clay (c_u < 40 kPa): construction disturbance reduces α — use α = 0.4 maximum; verify bentonite support and concrete workability
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pile_type === 'bored' && cu_or_N60_value < 40

fmea.failureMode: Driven precast pile D > 900 mm: driving damage risk is severe for large diameters — switch to bored, CFA, or barrette; driving stresses per GRLWEAP analysis mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pile_diameter_mm > 900 && pile_type === 'driven_concrete'

fmea.failureMode: Long bored pile with standard γ_R: consider pile testing (static or dynamic CAPWAP) to validate shaft friction distribution — testing permits γ_R reduction to 1.3 per EC7 §7.6.3.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: gamma_R_t === 1.5 && pile_length_m > 30

fmea.failureMode: Group efficiency < 85% in clay: block failure may govern — calculate block capacity per EC7 §7.6.2.5; Qblock = c_u × (perimeter_block × L + Ablock × 9)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_group < 0.85 && soil_type === 'clay'

tool_name: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius

inputs.name: Sheet / Plate Thickness (t)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Bend Line Length (L) — into page
inputs.note: Length of the press brake punch; equals workpiece width perpendicular to bend direction
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width (V)
inputs.note: Rule of thumb: V = 8 × t for mild steel; V = 6 × t for aluminium; larger V = lower force, more springback
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Material Ultimate Tensile Strength (R_m)
inputs.note: Mild steel DC01: Rm=270–370 MPa; S355: Rm=470–630 MPa; SS304: Rm=515–720 MPa; Al5052: Rm=230 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Material Yield Strength (R_e / R_p0.2)
inputs.note: Springback depends on R_e/E ratio — high-strength materials spring back more
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Yield/ultimate strength (steel): CoV=0.07

inputs.name: Required Final (After Springback) Bend Angle (θ_final)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 3 — Angular tolerance class 'm': ±0°20' for >50mm side

inputs.name: Punch Nose Radius (r_p)
inputs.note: Minimum radius ≥ material thickness for most materials; sharp punches cause cracking
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

formulas: F_bend_kn = 1.33 * Rm_mpa * Math.pow(material_thickness_mm, 2) * bend_length_mm / (die_opening_mm * 1000)  // [kN] — Bending Force — Air Bending (Wiping / V-Die) — Ref: Air bending: F = 1.33·R_m·t²·L/V — DIN 6935 / SME Sheet Metal Handbook §14.2; factor 1.33 for 90° V-bending

E_mpa = 210000; elongation_pct = Math.max(5, (1 - Fy_mpa/Rm_mpa) * 60); r_min_mm = material_thickness_mm * (50 / elongation_pct - 1)  // [mm] — Minimum Bend Radius (r_min) to Avoid Cracking — Ref: r_min = t(50/A−1) — SME §14.3; A=elongation% from Rm/Re ratio; ISO 7438 §7.3 bend test criterion

r_neutral_mm = punch_radius_mm + material_thickness_mm / 2; springback_factor = 1 - Fy_mpa * r_neutral_mm / (E_mpa * material_thickness_mm); Theta_punch_deg = bend_angle_deg / springback_factor; delta_theta_deg = Theta_punch_deg - bend_angle_deg  // [°] — Springback Angle (Δθ) — Simplified Analytical — Ref: Springback: θ_punch/θ_final = 1/(1 − 3·R_e·r/(E·t)) — Hosford & Caddell §7; simplified elastic recovery

k_factor = (punch_radius_mm < 2 * material_thickness_mm) ? 0.33 : 0.41; BA_mm = (punch_radius_mm + k_factor * material_thickness_mm) * bend_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180  // [mm] — Flat Blank Length — Bend Allowance (BA) — Ref: BA = (r_p + K·t)·θ_rad — SME / Machinery's Handbook 32nd Ed.; K-factor: 0.33 (tight), 0.41 (standard), 0.50 (loose)

F_bottom_kn = F_bend_kn * 3  // [kN] — Punch Bottoming Force (for coining / bottom bending) — Ref: Bottom bending: F_bottom ≈ 3 × F_air_bending — SME §14.4; coining eliminates springback but requires 5–10× air-bending force

F_required_kn = F_bend_kn * 1.15; tonnage_kn = F_required_kn  // [kN (÷ 9.81 = tonnes-force)] — Press Brake Tonnage Required with Safety Factor — Ref: 15% safety factor on calculated bending force; select press brake rated ≥ F_required; 1 tonne-force = 9.81 kN

rules.message: Punch radius < minimum bend radius: cracking risk on outer surface — increase punch radius to r_min or pre-heat material; verify with trial bends
Die opening < 6t: excessive bending force and tool wear — minimum V = 6t for aluminium, 8t for mild steel; smaller die opening risks sheet damage and machine overload
Required bending force > 1000 tonnes: very heavy press brake needed — verify material and geometry; consider multi-pass incremental bending or rolling for thick plates

smart_warnings.message: Springback > 10°: significant overbend required — for high-strength steel and AHSS, springback can reach 15–25°; consider bottom bending or laser tracking closed-loop angle control on CNC press brake
High-strength material with large r_min: difficult-to-form sheet — consider hot-forming, pre-annealing, or laser scoring at bend line for UHSS > 700 MPa
Long bend + high force: press brake deflection significant — ram crowning compensation mandatory; deflection without crowning causes trapezoidal bends on long parts
Die opening > 16t: excessive — large V-opening reduces bending accuracy and increases springback; use V = 8–10t for best accuracy-to-force balance on structural steel
Large punch radius with shallow angle: wipe bending geometry — verify punch stroke and die sizing prevent material slip; consider pre-positioning stops

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: punch_radius_mm < r_min_mm
fmea.effect: Punch radius < minimum bend radius: cracking risk on outer surface — increase punch radius to r_min or pre-heat material; verify with trial bends

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: die_opening_mm < 6 * material_thickness_mm
fmea.effect: Die opening < 6t: excessive bending force and tool wear — minimum V = 6t for aluminium, 8t for mild steel; smaller die opening risks sheet damage and machine overload

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: F_required_kn > 10000
fmea.effect: Required bending force > 1000 tonnes: very heavy press brake needed — verify material and geometry; consider multi-pass incremental bending or rolling for thick plates

fmea.failureMode: Springback > 10°: significant overbend required — for high-strength steel and AHSS, springback can reach 15–25°; consider bottom bending or laser tracking closed-loop angle control on CNC press brake
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_theta_deg > 10

fmea.failureMode: High-strength material with large r_min: difficult-to-form sheet — consider hot-forming, pre-annealing, or laser scoring at bend line for UHSS > 700 MPa
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fy_mpa > 500 && r_min_mm > 3 * material_thickness_mm

fmea.failureMode: Long bend + high force: press brake deflection significant — ram crowning compensation mandatory; deflection without crowning causes trapezoidal bends on long parts
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bend_length_mm > 3000 && F_required_kn > 2000

fmea.failureMode: Die opening > 16t: excessive — large V-opening reduces bending accuracy and increases springback; use V = 8–10t for best accuracy-to-force balance on structural steel
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: die_opening_mm > 16 * material_thickness_mm

fmea.failureMode: Large punch radius with shallow angle: wipe bending geometry — verify punch stroke and die sizing prevent material slip; consider pre-positioning stops
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: punch_radius_mm > material_thickness_mm * 3 && bend_angle_deg < 30

tool_name: RC One-Way Slab — Flexural Design Steel Area, Depth Check & One-Way Shear Verification

inputs.name: Factored Bending Moment per Metre Width (M_u / M_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Factored Shear Force per Metre Width (V_u / V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Total Slab Thickness (h)
inputs.note: ACI 318 minimum one-way slab: L/20 (simply supported); L/28 (one end continuous)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT6 — Precision machining (thickness): tolerance 6-19µm

inputs.name: Clear Cover to Flexural Steel (c_c)
inputs.note: ACI §20.6.1: interior slab min 20 mm; exterior min 40 mm; marine: 50 mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.2mm for 6-30mm (≈1% typical)

inputs.name: Flexural Bar Diameter (d_b)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Concrete Compressive Strength (f'_c / f_ck)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Rebar Yield Strength (f_y / f_yk)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Slab Clear Span (l_n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

formulas: d_mm = slab_thickness_mm - cover_mm - bar_diameter_mm / 2; Rn_mpa = Mu_knm_m * 1e6 / (0.9 * 1000 * Math.pow(d_mm, 2))  // [mm, MPa] — Effective Depth (d) & Nominal Moment Coefficient (R_n) — Ref: d = h − c_c − d_b/2; R_n = M_u/(φ·b·d²); φ=0.9 tension-controlled — ACI 318-19 §21.2.2

rho_req = (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa / fy_mpa) * (1 - Math.sqrt(1 - 2 * Rn_mpa / (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa))); rho_min = Math.max(0.25 * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) / fy_mpa, 1.4 / fy_mpa); rho_max = 0.75 * 0.85 * 0.85 * fc_prime_mpa / fy_mpa * (600 / (600 + fy_mpa)); rho_design = Math.max(rho_req, rho_min)  // Required Steel Ratio (ρ) — ACI Parabolic Method — Ref: ρ = (0.85f'c/fy)(1−√(1−2Rn/0.85f'c)) — ACI 318-19 §22.2; ρ_min per §9.6.1.2; ρ_max = 0.75ρ_balanced

As_req_mm2_m = rho_design * 1000 * d_mm; bar_area_mm2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(bar_diameter_mm, 2); spacing_mm = Math.floor(bar_area_mm2 / As_req_mm2_m * 1000)  // [mm²/m, mm] — Required Steel Area & Bar Spacing — Ref: A_s = ρ·b·d; spacing s = a_bar/A_s × 1000 — ACI §25.2.1: max spacing ≤ min(3h, 450 mm)

s_max_mm = Math.min(3 * slab_thickness_mm, 450); s_ok = spacing_mm <= s_max_mm  // [mm, boolean] — Maximum Allowable Bar Spacing Check — Ref: ACI 318-19 §7.7.2.3 — maximum flexural bar spacing: min(3h, 450 mm)

lambda = 1.0; phi_Vc_kn_m = 0.75 * 0.17 * lambda * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * 1000 * d_mm / 1000  // [kN/m] — One-Way Shear Capacity (φV_c) — ACI Simplified — Ref: ACI 318-19 §22.5.5.1 — φV_c = 0.75×0.17λ√f'c×b_w×d; slabs without stirrups; SI units

shear_DCR = Vu_kn_m / phi_Vc_kn_m; L_d_ratio = span_m * 1000 / d_mm; L_d_limit = (fy_mpa === 420) ? 20 : 18  // Shear DCR & Span-to-Depth Check — Ref: Shear DCR ≤ 1.0 (no stirrups required); L/d deflection control per ACI §24.2 (approximate method)

rules.message: Required steel ratio exceeds 75% of balanced — slab is over-stressed; increase slab thickness by 20–30 mm or increase f'c; compression-controlled failure mode unacceptable
One-way shear demand exceeds concrete capacity: stirrups required in slab (unusual) — verify load/span; more practical to increase slab thickness until V_u < φV_c
Effective depth < 70% of total thickness: very large cover or bar size — verify cover is correct; large bars in thin slabs reduce d significantly

smart_warnings.message: Span-to-depth ratio exceeds ACI §24.2 deflection limit — computed deflections must be checked; long-term creep deflection (×2.0 for 5+ years) may damage partitions or finishes
Bar spacing < 75 mm: congestion risk — concrete aggregate cannot flow between bars; ACI §25.2.1 requires clear spacing ≥ bar diameter, 25 mm, or 4/3 × aggregate size
Bar spacing exceeds ACI maximum: cracks will be wide — use smaller diameter bars at closer spacing or redesign to meet s ≤ min(3h, 450 mm)
Minimum steel governs: slab is lightly loaded for its depth — consider reducing slab thickness to L/20 minimum per ACI Table 9.3.1.1 for efficiency
High moment coefficient (R_n > 30% of 0.85f'c): approaching over-reinforced zone — verify d is adequate; increase thickness before adding reinforcement

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: rho_req > rho_max
fmea.effect: Required steel ratio exceeds 75% of balanced — slab is over-stressed; increase slab thickness by 20–30 mm or increase f'c; compression-controlled failure mode unacceptable

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: shear_DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: One-way shear demand exceeds concrete capacity: stirrups required in slab (unusual) — verify load/span; more practical to increase slab thickness until V_u < φV_c

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: d_mm < slab_thickness_mm * 0.7
fmea.effect: Effective depth < 70% of total thickness: very large cover or bar size — verify cover is correct; large bars in thin slabs reduce d significantly

fmea.failureMode: Span-to-depth ratio exceeds ACI §24.2 deflection limit — computed deflections must be checked; long-term creep deflection (×2.0 for 5+ years) may damage partitions or finishes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_d_ratio > L_d_limit

fmea.failureMode: Bar spacing < 75 mm: congestion risk — concrete aggregate cannot flow between bars; ACI §25.2.1 requires clear spacing ≥ bar diameter, 25 mm, or 4/3 × aggregate size
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: spacing_mm < 75

fmea.failureMode: Bar spacing exceeds ACI maximum: cracks will be wide — use smaller diameter bars at closer spacing or redesign to meet s ≤ min(3h, 450 mm)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: !s_ok

fmea.failureMode: Minimum steel governs: slab is lightly loaded for its depth — consider reducing slab thickness to L/20 minimum per ACI Table 9.3.1.1 for efficiency
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rho_design < rho_min * 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High moment coefficient (R_n > 30% of 0.85f'c): approaching over-reinforced zone — verify d is adequate; increase thickness before adding reinforcement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rn_mpa > 0.85 * fc_prime_mpa * 0.3

tool_name: Roller Bearing L10h Life & Modified Lnm Life (ISO 281)

inputs.name: Bearing Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load Exponent (p)
inputs.hint: Ball bearing: 3.0 | Roller bearing: 10/3 = 3.333 — ISO 281
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Dynamic Load Rating (C)
inputs.hint: From bearing catalog (SKF/FAG/NSK)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Fatigue Load Limit (Pu)
inputs.hint: From bearing catalog — ISO 281 Annex A
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Equivalent Dynamic Load (P)
inputs.hint: P = X*Fr + Y*Fa (combined radial and axial force)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Radial Force (Fr)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Axial Force (Fa)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Operating Speed (n)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

inputs.name: Reliability Factor (a1)
inputs.hint: ISO 281 Table 1 — a1 reliability factor
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: a1 Numeric Value
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Viscosity Ratio (kappa = nu/nu1)
inputs.hint: kappa = actual viscosity / ISO 281 nominal reference viscosity. kappa < 1 indicates boundary lubrication
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Contamination Factor (eC)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: eC Numeric Value
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Target Bearing Life
inputs.hint: Typical: electric motors 20,000h | gearboxes 30,000h | heavy machinery 60,000h
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: L10_million_rev = POWER(dynamic_load_C_kN / equiv_load_P_kN, load_exponent_p)   // [10^6 rev] Basic L10 life — ISO 281 Clause 5
L10h = (L10_million_rev * 1000000) / (60 * rpm)   // [hours] L10 life in hours — 90% reliability
a_ISO_factor = 0.1 * POWER(MAX(0.001, 1 - (fatigue_limit_Pu_kN * contamination_ec_val / equiv_load_P_kN)), 1.5) * SQRT(viscosity_ratio_kappa)   // [—] ISO life factor — ISO 281:2007 Annex A
Lnm = reliability_a1_val * a_ISO_factor * L10h   // [hours] Modified bearing life (Lnm)
Life_Reduction_Pct = (1 - Lnm / L10h) * 100   // [%] Modified life reduction (due to contamination and viscosity)
Bearing_Speed_Limit_Check = rpm / (dynamic_load_C_kN * 1000 / 1000)   // [—] Speed-load ratio indicator
Load_Ratio = equiv_load_P_kN / dynamic_load_C_kN   // [—] P/C ratio — should be low (<0.5 is ideal)
Maintenance_Interval_h = Lnm * 0.1   // [hours] Recommended relubrication interval (10% of Lnm)

overload_check.error_msg: MECHANICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: P >= C. Operating load exceeds dynamic capacity. L10 is less than 1 million revolutions. Bearing will fail immediately; selection is incorrect.

rpm_minimum.error_msg: Speed cannot be zero — division by zero in L10h calculation.
