contamination_range.error_msg: eC contamination factor must be between 0 and 0.80 (ISO 281 Annex A Table A.4).

load_ratio_extreme.error_msg: P/C ratio > 0.8: Bearing is severely overloaded. Minimum L10 life is less than 2 million revolutions. Select a larger bearing series.

smart_warnings.message: CRITICAL LUBRICATION DEFICIENCY: kappa < 0.4. Rolling elements are in direct contact; EHD film does not form. Metal-on-metal wear initiated. Lnm calculation unreliable. Higher viscosity oil or lower speed is mandatory.
Insufficient oil film: kappa between 0.4 and 1.0. Partial EHD film. Fatigue life is significantly below theoretical values. Optimize oil selection or evaluate viscosity-enhancing additives.
High contamination: eC < 0.30. Abrasive particles are destroying the raceways. Upgrade filter class to ISO 4406 <= 16/14/11. Evaluate shielded/sealed bearing types.
INSUFFICIENT MODIFIED LIFE: Lnm is less than 50% of target life. Bearing will fail prematurely under current conditions. Larger series, better lubrication, or lower load is required.
P/C > 0.5: High load ratio. A larger bearing series should be evaluated. P/C <= 0.2 is ideal for long service life.
L10h < 20,000 hours: This is the industrial minimum for electric motors. Current design is insufficient. Increase bearing size or reduce load.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: equiv_load_P_kN >= dynamic_load_C_kN
fmea.effect: MECHANICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: P >= C. Operating load exceeds dynamic capacity. L10 is less than 1 million revolutions. Bearing will fail immediately; selection is incorrect.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: contamination_ec_val < 0 || contamination_ec_val > 0.80
fmea.effect: eC contamination factor must be between 0 and 0.80 (ISO 281 Annex A Table A.4).
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Load_Ratio > 0.8
fmea.effect: P/C ratio > 0.8: Bearing is severely overloaded. Minimum L10 life is less than 2 million revolutions. Select a larger bearing series.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL LUBRICATION DEFICIENCY: kappa < 0.4. Rolling elements are in direct contact; EHD film does not form. Metal-on-metal wear initiated. Lnm calculation unreliable. Higher viscosity oil or lower speed is mandatory.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: viscosity_ratio_kappa < 0.4

fmea.failureMode: Insufficient oil film: kappa between 0.4 and 1.0. Partial EHD film. Fatigue life is significantly below theoretical values. Optimize oil selection or evaluate viscosity-enhancing additives.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: viscosity_ratio_kappa >= 0.4 && viscosity_ratio_kappa < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: High contamination: eC < 0.30. Abrasive particles are destroying the raceways. Upgrade filter class to ISO 4406 <= 16/14/11. Evaluate shielded/sealed bearing types.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: contamination_ec_val < 0.30

fmea.failureMode: INSUFFICIENT MODIFIED LIFE: Lnm is less than 50% of target life. Bearing will fail prematurely under current conditions. Larger series, better lubrication, or lower load is required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lnm < target_life_hours * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: P/C > 0.5: High load ratio. A larger bearing series should be evaluated. P/C <= 0.2 is ideal for long service life.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Load_Ratio > 0.5

fmea.failureMode: L10h < 20,000 hours: This is the industrial minimum for electric motors. Current design is insufficient. Increase bearing size or reduce load.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L10h < 20000 && target_life_hours >= 20000

tool_name: Hollow Section RHS/SHS/CHS Beam-Column Combined Loading Check — EN 1993-1-1 / AISC 360-22 / CIDECT DG2
category: Steel Structure / Hollow Structural Sections / Beam-Column Stability

inputs.name: Design Standard
inputs.note: Select EC3 interaction method or AISC 360 Chapter H interaction method.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Hollow Section Type
inputs.note: RHS rectangular hollow section, SHS square hollow section, CHS circular hollow section.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Section Width
inputs.validation_rule: Required for RHS/SHS. For SHS, B = H. Must satisfy B > 2t + 2r.
inputs.note: RHS 200×100×8 → B=100 mm or 200 mm according to selected local y/z axis convention.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Section Depth
inputs.validation_rule: Required for RHS/SHS. For SHS, H = B. Must satisfy H > 2t + 2r.
inputs.note: Primary bending axis depth.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Outside Diameter
inputs.validation_rule: Required for CHS. Must satisfy D > 2t.
inputs.note: CHS outside diameter.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Wall Thickness
inputs.validation_rule: t > 0. For RHS/SHS: 2t + 2r < MIN(B,H). For CHS: 2t < D.
inputs.note: Nominal wall thickness. Corrosion allowance must be deducted before calculation.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: External Corner Radius
inputs.validation_rule: For RHS/SHS only. r >= 0 and r < MIN(B,H)/4.
inputs.note: If unknown, use lookup section properties instead of sharp-corner approximation.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Gross Cross-Sectional Area From Table
inputs.validation_rule: If provided, overrides geometric area calculation.
inputs.note: Preferred for certified RHS/SHS/CHS catalogue sections.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Second Moment of Area About y-axis From Table
inputs.validation_rule: If provided, overrides geometric Iy calculation.
inputs.note: Use certified manufacturer table for final design.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Second Moment of Area About z-axis From Table
inputs.validation_rule: If provided, overrides geometric Iz calculation.
inputs.note: Use certified manufacturer table for final design.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Elastic Section Modulus About y-axis From Table
inputs.validation_rule: If provided, overrides geometric Wy calculation.
inputs.note: Elastic modulus for EC3 Class 3 or conservative HSS check.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Elastic Section Modulus About z-axis From Table
inputs.validation_rule: If provided, overrides geometric Wz calculation.
inputs.note: Elastic modulus for EC3 Class 3 or conservative HSS check.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Yield Strength
inputs.validation_rule: fy > 0. Use nominal or test-certified yield strength.
inputs.note: S355 → fy=355 MPa.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Elastic Modulus
inputs.note: Structural steel default E=210000 MPa.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Elastic/shear modulus: CoV=0.03

inputs.name: EC3 Cross-Section Resistance Partial Factor (γ_M0)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Must follow national annex.
inputs.note: Default γM0=1.00 unless national annex overrides.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: EC3 Member Stability Partial Factor (γ_M1)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Must follow national annex.
inputs.note: Default γM1=1.00 unless national annex overrides.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: AISC Compression Resistance Factor (φ_c)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for AISC_360_22 LRFD.
inputs.note: AISC LRFD compression resistance factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: AISC Flexural Resistance Factor (φ_b)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for AISC_360_22 LRFD.
inputs.note: AISC LRFD flexural resistance factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Design Axial Force
inputs.validation_rule: Compression positive. Tension negative. Absolute value must be physically consistent with load combination.
inputs.note: Compression governs buckling interaction. Tension uses gross section axial yielding check.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Design Bending Moment About y-axis
inputs.validation_rule: M_y_Ed >= 0.
inputs.note: Major-axis bending if y-axis is selected as strong axis.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Design Bending Moment About z-axis
inputs.validation_rule: M_z_Ed >= 0.
inputs.note: Minor-axis bending for RHS. For CHS, y/z capacities are equal.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Design Shear Force Along y-axis
inputs.validation_rule: V_y_Ed >= 0.
inputs.note: Used for shear warning and high-shear moment reduction trigger.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Design Shear Force Along z-axis
inputs.validation_rule: V_z_Ed >= 0.
inputs.note: Used for shear warning and high-shear moment reduction trigger.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Effective Unbraced Length For Buckling About y-axis
inputs.note: Member length between effective restraints for y-axis buckling.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Effective Unbraced Length For Buckling About z-axis
inputs.note: Member length between effective restraints for z-axis buckling.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Effective Length Factor About y-axis
inputs.note: K=1.0 for pinned-pinned idealization.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Effective Length Factor About z-axis
inputs.note: K=1.0 for pinned-pinned idealization.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: EC3 Buckling Curve Imperfection Factor About y-axis (α_y)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Select from EC3 buckling curves a0/a/b/c/d.
inputs.note: Typical hot-finished hollow section α=0.21 for curve a; cold-formed may require higher curve.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: EC3 Buckling Curve Imperfection Factor About z-axis (α_z)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Select from EC3 buckling curves a0/a/b/c/d.
inputs.note: Typical hot-finished hollow section α=0.21 for curve a; cold-formed may require higher curve.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: EC3 Beam-Column Interaction Factor (k_yy)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Must be calculated according to selected EC3 interaction method.
inputs.note: Set by EC3 Annex A/B method or conservative project rule.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: EC3 Beam-Column Interaction Factor (k_yz)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Must be calculated according to selected EC3 interaction method.
inputs.note: Set by EC3 Annex A/B method or conservative project rule.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: EC3 Beam-Column Interaction Factor (k_zy)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Must be calculated according to selected EC3 interaction method.
inputs.note: Set by EC3 Annex A/B method or conservative project rule.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: EC3 Beam-Column Interaction Factor (k_zz)
inputs.validation_rule: Used only for EN_1993_1_1. Must be calculated according to selected EC3 interaction method.
inputs.note: Set by EC3 Annex A/B method or conservative project rule.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

formulas: A_rect_calc = section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS' ? (B * H - (B - 2 * t) * (H - 2 * t)) : null   // [mm²] RHS/SHS Gross Area Sharp-Corner Approximation Reference: CIDECT Design Guide 2; Assumptions: Sharp-corner approximation, Use certified lookup properties when corner radius is significant

A_chs_calc = section_type === 'CHS' ? Math.PI / 4 * (Math.pow(D, 2) - Math.pow(D - 2 * t, 2)) : null   // [mm²] CHS Gross Area Reference: CIDECT Design Guide 2; Assumptions: Uniform wall thickness, Circular section without ovality

A = A_lookup !== null && A_lookup !== undefined ? A_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? A_chs_calc : A_rect_calc)   // [mm²] Selected Gross Area Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2; AISC 360-22 Chapter B; Assumptions: Gross section check, No net-section bolt-hole reduction included

Iy_rect_calc = section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS' ? (B * Math.pow(H, 3) - (B - 2 * t) * Math.pow(H - 2 * t, 3)) / 12 : null   // [mm⁴] RHS/SHS Second Moment of Area About y-axis Reference: CIDECT Design Guide 2; Assumptions: Sharp-corner approximation, Axis y passes through centroid

Iz_rect_calc = section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS' ? (H * Math.pow(B, 3) - (H - 2 * t) * Math.pow(B - 2 * t, 3)) / 12 : null   // [mm⁴] RHS/SHS Second Moment of Area About z-axis Reference: CIDECT Design Guide 2; Assumptions: Sharp-corner approximation, Axis z passes through centroid

I_chs_calc = section_type === 'CHS' ? Math.PI / 64 * (Math.pow(D, 4) - Math.pow(D - 2 * t, 4)) : null   // [mm⁴] CHS Second Moment of Area Reference: CIDECT Design Guide 2; Assumptions: Circular hollow section, Iy = Iz

Iy = Iy_lookup !== null && Iy_lookup !== undefined ? Iy_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc : Iy_rect_calc)   // [mm⁴] Selected Second Moment of Area About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1; AISC 360-22 Chapter E; Assumptions: Gross elastic property, No local buckling reduction applied

Iz = Iz_lookup !== null && Iz_lookup !== undefined ? Iz_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc : Iz_rect_calc)   // [mm⁴] Selected Second Moment of Area About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1; AISC 360-22 Chapter E; Assumptions: Gross elastic property, No local buckling reduction applied

Wy = Wy_lookup !== null && Wy_lookup !== undefined ? Wy_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc / (D / 2) : Iy / (H / 2))   // [mm³] Elastic Section Modulus About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5; AISC 360-22 Chapter F; Assumptions: Elastic stress distribution, Conservative for compact plastic sections

Wz = Wz_lookup !== null && Wz_lookup !== undefined ? Wz_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc / (D / 2) : Iz / (B / 2))   // [mm³] Elastic Section Modulus About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5; AISC 360-22 Chapter F; Assumptions: Elastic stress distribution, Conservative for compact plastic sections
iy = Math.sqrt(Iy / A)   // [mm] Radius of Gyration About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1; AISC 360-22 Chapter E; Assumptions: Gross area, Elastic member stability
iz = Math.sqrt(Iz / A)   // [mm] Radius of Gyration About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1; AISC 360-22 Chapter E; Assumptions: Gross area, Elastic member stability
lambda_y_geom = K_y * L_y / iy   // [dimensionless] Effective Slenderness About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.3; AISC 360-22 E2; Assumptions: Prismatic member, Defined effective length
lambda_z_geom = K_z * L_z / iz   // [dimensionless] Effective Slenderness About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.3; AISC 360-22 E2; Assumptions: Prismatic member, Defined effective length

Ncr_y = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E * Iy / Math.pow(K_y * L_y, 2) / 1000   // [kN] Euler Critical Force About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.3; Euler buckling basis; Assumptions: Elastic buckling, Pinned-equivalent effective length

Ncr_z = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E * Iz / Math.pow(K_z * L_z, 2) / 1000   // [kN] Euler Critical Force About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.3; Euler buckling basis; Assumptions: Elastic buckling, Pinned-equivalent effective length

lambda_bar_y = Math.sqrt((A * fy / 1000) / Ncr_y)   // [dimensionless] EC3 Non-Dimensional Slenderness About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.3; Assumptions: Class 1/2/3 gross section, Compression member buckling check

lambda_bar_z = Math.sqrt((A * fy / 1000) / Ncr_z)   // [dimensionless] EC3 Non-Dimensional Slenderness About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.3; Assumptions: Class 1/2/3 gross section, Compression member buckling check

chi_y_raw = 1 / (0.5 * (1 + alpha_y * (lambda_bar_y - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_y, 2)) + Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.5 * (1 + alpha_y * (lambda_bar_y - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_y, 2)), 2) - Math.pow(lambda_bar_y, 2)))   // [dimensionless] EC3 Buckling Reduction Factor About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.2; Assumptions: EC3 buckling curve selected correctly, chi <= 1 applied downstream

chi_z_raw = 1 / (0.5 * (1 + alpha_z * (lambda_bar_z - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_z, 2)) + Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.5 * (1 + alpha_z * (lambda_bar_z - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_z, 2)), 2) - Math.pow(lambda_bar_z, 2)))   // [dimensionless] EC3 Buckling Reduction Factor About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.2; Assumptions: EC3 buckling curve selected correctly, chi <= 1 applied downstream

Nb_Rd_y = Math.min(chi_y_raw, 1) * A * fy / gamma_M1 / 1000   // [kN] EC3 Design Buckling Resistance About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.1; Assumptions: Uniform compression, No torsional buckling mode included

Nb_Rd_z = Math.min(chi_z_raw, 1) * A * fy / gamma_M1 / 1000   // [kN] EC3 Design Buckling Resistance About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.1.1; Assumptions: Uniform compression, No torsional buckling mode included
Npl_Rd = A * fy / gamma_M0 / 1000   // [kN] EC3 Cross-Section Axial Plastic Resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.4; Assumptions: Gross section yielding, No net-section fracture check

My_Rd = Wy * fy / gamma_M0 / 1000000   // [kN·m] EC3 Elastic Moment Resistance About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5; Assumptions: Elastic section modulus used, Local buckling class reduction not included

Mz_Rd = Wz * fy / gamma_M0 / 1000000   // [kN·m] EC3 Elastic Moment Resistance About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5; Assumptions: Elastic section modulus used, Local buckling class reduction not included

UC_EC3_y = N_Ed > 0 ? N_Ed / Nb_Rd_y + k_yy * M_y_Ed / My_Rd + k_yz * M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd : Math.abs(N_Ed) / Npl_Rd + M_y_Ed / My_Rd + M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd   // [dimensionless] EC3 Beam-Column Interaction Utilization About y-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.3; Assumptions: Interaction factors provided or calculated externally, Elastic moment resistance used conservatively

UC_EC3_z = N_Ed > 0 ? N_Ed / Nb_Rd_z + k_zy * M_y_Ed / My_Rd + k_zz * M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd : Math.abs(N_Ed) / Npl_Rd + M_y_Ed / My_Rd + M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd   // [dimensionless] EC3 Beam-Column Interaction Utilization About z-axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.3; Assumptions: Interaction factors provided or calculated externally, Elastic moment resistance used conservatively

Fe_y = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E / Math.pow(lambda_y_geom, 2)   // [MPa] AISC Elastic Critical Stress About y-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 E3; Assumptions: Flexural buckling, No torsional-flexural mode included

Fe_z = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E / Math.pow(lambda_z_geom, 2)   // [MPa] AISC Elastic Critical Stress About z-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 E3; Assumptions: Flexural buckling, No torsional-flexural mode included

Fcr_y = fy / Fe_y <= 2.25 ? Math.pow(0.658, fy / Fe_y) * fy : 0.877 * Fe_y   // [MPa] AISC Critical Stress About y-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 E3; Assumptions: Inelastic/elastic column curve, Compact/non-slender element reduction not included

Fcr_z = fy / Fe_z <= 2.25 ? Math.pow(0.658, fy / Fe_z) * fy : 0.877 * Fe_z   // [MPa] AISC Critical Stress About z-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 E3; Assumptions: Inelastic/elastic column curve, Compact/non-slender element reduction not included
Pc_y = phi_c * Fcr_y * A / 1000   // [kN] AISC Design Compressive Strength About y-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 E3; Assumptions: LRFD, Gross area, No slender element reduction
Pc_z = phi_c * Fcr_z * A / 1000   // [kN] AISC Design Compressive Strength About z-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 E3; Assumptions: LRFD, Gross area, No slender element reduction

Mc_y = phi_b * fy * Wy / 1000000   // [kN·m] AISC Design Flexural Strength About y-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 Chapter F; Assumptions: Elastic section modulus used conservatively, Local buckling and LTB not included

Mc_z = phi_b * fy * Wz / 1000000   // [kN·m] AISC Design Flexural Strength About z-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 Chapter F; Assumptions: Elastic section modulus used conservatively, Local buckling and LTB not included

UC_AISC_y = N_Ed > 0 ? (N_Ed / Pc_y >= 0.2 ? N_Ed / Pc_y + (8 / 9) * (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z) : N_Ed / (2 * Pc_y) + (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z)) : Math.abs(N_Ed) / (phi_c * fy * A / 1000) + M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z   // [dimensionless] AISC H1-1 Compression Flexure Utilization About y-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 H1.1; Assumptions: Doubly symmetric member, LRFD, Biaxial flexure interaction

UC_AISC_z = N_Ed > 0 ? (N_Ed / Pc_z >= 0.2 ? N_Ed / Pc_z + (8 / 9) * (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z) : N_Ed / (2 * Pc_z) + (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z)) : Math.abs(N_Ed) / (phi_c * fy * A / 1000) + M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z   // [dimensionless] AISC H1-1 Compression Flexure Utilization About z-axis Reference: AISC 360-22 H1.1; Assumptions: Doubly symmetric member, LRFD, Biaxial flexure interaction

UC = design_standard === 'EN_1993_1_1' ? Math.max(UC_EC3_y, UC_EC3_z) : Math.max(UC_AISC_y, UC_AISC_z)   // [dimensionless] Governing Utilization Ratio Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.3 / AISC 360-22 H1.1; Assumptions: UC <= 1.0 passes, UC > 1.0 fails
status = UC <= 1 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'   // [dimensionless] Result Status Reference: ISO 9001 §8.6; Assumptions: All validation rules passed, Input load combination is design-level

f_res = Math.sqrt(Math.pow((N_Ed * 1000) / A, 2) + Math.pow((M_y_Ed * 1000000) / Wy, 2) + Math.pow((M_z_Ed * 1000000) / Wz, 2))   // [MPa] Combined Stress Vector Resultant (diagnostic) Reference: SectorCalc engine rule; Assumptions: Elastic stress approximation, Used as diagnostic not replacement for code interaction

u_c_UC = UC * Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.005, 2) + Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.05, 2))   // [dimensionless] Combined Standard Uncertainty Of Governing Utilization Reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM; Assumptions: Independent uncertainty contributors, Sensitivity coefficients approximated by relative contribution

rules.message: CHS requires outside diameter D. CIDECT DG2 geometric definition incomplete.

rules.condition: (section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS') && (B === null || H === null || B === undefined || H === undefined || B === '' || H === '')
rules.message: RHS/SHS requires B and H. CIDECT DG2 geometric definition incomplete.
SHS requires B = H within dimensional tolerance.
Wall thickness must be positive. Fail closed.
Invalid CHS geometry: D must exceed 2t.
Invalid RHS/SHS geometry: B and H must exceed 2t.
Material properties must be positive.
Effective length and K-factors must be positive.
EC3 partial factors must not be below 1.00 unless project authority explicitly approves.
AISC LRFD resistance factors must satisfy 0 < phi <= 1.
Very slender compression member. Review serviceability, second-order effects, and erection stability.
Combined loading utilization exceeds 1.00. Member fails selected standard check.

smart_warnings.message: Calculated sharp-corner properties are preliminary. Use certified manufacturer table for final RHS/SHS/CHS design.
smart_warnings.condition: A_lookup === null || A_lookup === undefined || Iy_lookup === null || Iy_lookup === undefined || Iz_lookup === null || Iz_lookup === undefined || Wy_lookup === null || Wy_lookup === undefined || Wz_lookup === null || Wz_lookup === undefined

smart_warnings.message: Utilization exceeds 0.90. FMEA and independent design review required before release.
Compression plus bending activates beam-column stability interaction. Verify effective length, moment gradient, and second-order analysis.
High width-to-thickness ratio. Check local buckling and section classification before accepting gross-section resistance.
High diameter-to-thickness ratio. Check CHS local buckling limits before accepting gross-section resistance.
High shear demand detected. Apply shear-moment interaction or reduced moment resistance according to selected standard.
smart_warnings.condition: V_y_Ed > 0.5 * A * fy / Math.sqrt(3) / 1000 || V_z_Ed > 0.5 * A * fy / Math.sqrt(3) / 1000

smart_warnings.message: High axial force ratio. Check connection eccentricity, residual stress, load combination, and second-order effects.

engine_rules.error_propagation: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM Type A/B. Report u_c(UC), input uncertainty table, sensitivity contributors for A, Iy, Iz, fy, E, N_Ed, M_y_Ed, M_z_Ed.

fmea.failureMode: Local buckling (flange/web)
fmea.effect: Loss of section capacity, sudden collapse risk
fmea.control_measure: Verify width-to-thickness ratios per EN 1993-1-1 §5.5. Check stiffener spacing.

fmea.failureMode: Global buckling (y-y axis)
fmea.effect: Loss of member stability, lateral-torsional interaction
fmea.control_measure: Verify slenderness ratio L_y/i_y. Check effective length K_yL_y per buckling curve.

fmea.failureMode: Global buckling (z-z axis)
fmea.effect: Loss of member stability, reduced compression capacity
fmea.control_measure: Verify slenderness ratio L_z/i_z. Check effective length K_zL_z per buckling curve.

fmea.failureMode: Biaxial bending overload
fmea.effect: Combined stress exceeds yield, plastic hinge formation

fmea.failureMode: Cross-section geometry input error
fmea.effect: Wrong section properties (A, I_y, I_z, W_y, W_z), invalid results
fmea.control_measure: Verify section dimensions against manufacturer catalogue. Cross-check with A_lookup, Iy_lookup from standard tables.

fmea.failureMode: Material grade mismatch
fmea.effect: Incorrect yield strength fy, wrong capacity
fmea.control_measure: Verify steel grade (S235/S275/S355/S460) per EN 10025. Check fy value against grade specification.

fmea.failureMode: Missing effective length restraint
fmea.effect: Buckling length underestimation, unconservative design
fmea.control_measure: Verify K_y, K_z factors. Check lateral restraint conditions (pinned/fixed/free). Refer to EN 1993-1-1 Table 6.2.

reference_code.interface: interface HollowSectionBeamColumnInput { design_standard: 'EN_1993_1_1' | 'AISC_360_22'; section_type: 'RHS' | 'SHS' | 'CHS'; B?: number; H?: number; D?: number; t: number; r?: number; A_lookup?: number; Iy_lookup?: number; Iz_lookup?: number; Wy_lookup?: number; Wz_lookup?: number; fy: number; E: number; gamma_M0: number; gamma_M1: number; phi_c: number; phi_b: number; N_Ed: number; M_y_Ed: number; M_z_Ed: number; V_y_Ed?: number; V_z_Ed?: number; L_y: number; L_z: number; K_y: number; K_z: number; alpha_y: number; alpha_z: number; k_yy: number; k_yz: number; k_zy: number; k_zz: number; } interface HollowSectionBeamColumnOutput { A: number; Iy: number; Iz: number; Wy: number; Wz: number; iy: number; iz: number; lambda_y_geom: number; lambda_z_geom: number; Ncr_y: number; Ncr_z: number; UC: number; u_c_UC: number; f_res: number; status: 'PASS' | 'FAIL'; governing_standard: string; warnings: string[]; }
reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Throw on invalid geometry, missing standard-specific parameters, negative material properties, invalid load inputs, and UC > 1.0.
reference_code.testing: Jest. 5 cases: nominal RHS compression+bending PASS, SHS high axial FAIL, CHS geometry invalid BLOCK, zero thickness BLOCK, high slenderness WARNING.

tuv_checklist: Dimensional check completed for every formula
limitations: Local buckling effective width reduction not fully automated.
EC3 section class must be verified externally or added as a separate pre-check module.
AISC HSS slender-element reduction must be added for noncompact/slender HSS final design.
Connection eccentricity and weld/bolt connection resistance are outside scope.
Fire, fatigue, seismic, accidental load, and dynamic amplification checks are outside scope.

tool_name: RC Beam Shear & Flexure Calculator

inputs.name: Design Standard
inputs.note: Select the governing design code
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Beam Width (b_w)
inputs.validation_rule: b_w > 0
inputs.note: Rectangular beam web width
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8 — Electric machine: efficiency tolerance ±3%

inputs.name: Beam Height (h)
inputs.note: Total beam section depth
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Effective Depth (d)
inputs.validation_rule: d > 0 AND d < h
inputs.note: Distance from extreme compression fiber to centroid of tension reinforcement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Tension Reinforcement Area (A_s)
inputs.validation_rule: A_s > 0
inputs.note: Cross-sectional area of tension steel
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Shear Reinforcement Area (A_sv)
inputs.note: Area of shear reinforcement (both legs, 1m spacing). Optional if no stirrups.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Stirrup Spacing (s_v)
inputs.validation_rule: s_v > 0 WHEN A_sv > 0
inputs.note: Longitudinal spacing of shear reinforcement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Design Bending Moment (M_Ed)
inputs.validation_rule: M_Ed >= 0
inputs.note: Ultimate design moment at section
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Design Shear Force (V_Ed)
inputs.validation_rule: V_Ed >= 0
inputs.note: Ultimate design shear force at section
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Concrete Cylinder Strength (f_ck)
inputs.validation_rule: f_ck > 0 WHEN EN_1992_1_1
inputs.note: EN 1992-1-1: Characteristic concrete cylinder strength
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (f_yk)
inputs.note: EN 1992-1-1: Characteristic yield strength of reinforcement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Concrete Partial Factor (γ_c)
inputs.note: EN 1992-1-1 §2.4.2.4: Persistent/transient = 1.5
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Steel Partial Factor (γ_s)
inputs.note: EN 1992-1-1 §2.4.2.4: Persistent/transient = 1.15
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Long-term Factor (α_cc)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Strut Inclination (cot θ)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Axial Force (N_Ed)
inputs.note: EN 1992-1-1: Compression positive. Leave 0 for pure bending.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Concrete Compressive Strength (f'_c)
inputs.note: ACI 318: Specified concrete compressive strength
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (f_y)
inputs.note: ACI 318: Specified yield strength of reinforcement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Steel Elastic Modulus (E_s)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% (unclassified parameter)

inputs.name: Lightweight Factor (λ)
inputs.note: ACI 318 §19.2.1: 1.0 for NWC, 0.85 for sand-LW, 0.75 for all-LW
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Shear Reduction Factor (φ_v)
inputs.note: ACI 318 §21.2: 0.75 for shear/torsion
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: rho_l = A_s / (b_w * d)   // [-] Tension reinforcement ratio
f_cd = alpha_cc * f_ck / gamma_c   // [MPa] Design concrete strength
f_yd = f_yk / gamma_s   // [MPa] Design steel strength
lambda = f_ck <= 50 ? 0.8 : 0.8 - (f_ck - 50) / 400   // [-] Stress block factor
eta = f_ck <= 50 ? 1.0 : 1.0 - (f_ck - 50) / 200   // [-] Strength factor
x = A_s * f_yd / (eta * f_cd * b_w * lambda)   // [mm] Neutral axis depth
z = min(d - 0.5 * lambda * x, 0.95 * d)   // [mm] Lever arm
M_Rd = A_s * f_yd * z / 1e6   // [kN.m] Design flexural capacity
k = min(1 + sqrt(200 / d), 2.0)   // [-] Size factor
rho_l_limited = min(rho_l, 0.02)   // [-] Limited reinforcement ratio
V_Rdc = (0.18/gamma_c * k * (100 * rho_l_limited * f_ck)^(1/3)) * b_w * d / 1000   // [kN] Shear Capacity — Concrete
V_Rds = A_sv / s_v * z * f_yd * cot_theta / 1000   // [kN] Shear Capacity — Stirrups
v1 = f_ck <= 50 ? 0.6 : 0.6 * (1 - (f_ck - 50) / 200)   // [-] Strength reduction factor
V_Rdmax = b_w * z * v1 * f_cd / (cot_theta + 1/cot_theta) / 1000   // [kN] Shear Capacity — Strut Crushing
V_Rd = min(V_Rds, V_Rdmax)   // [kN] Governing shear capacity
UC_flexure = M_Ed / M_Rd   // [-] Flexural utilization ratio
UC_shear = V_Ed / V_Rd   // [-] Shear utilization ratio
UC = max(UC_flexure, UC_shear)   // [-] Governing utilization
beta1 = f_c_prime <= 28 ? 0.85 : max(0.85 - 0.05 * (f_c_prime - 28) / 7, 0.65)   // [-] Stress block factor
a = A_s * f_y / (0.85 * f_c_prime * b_w)   // [mm] Equivalent stress block depth
c = a / beta1   // [mm] Neutral axis depth (ACI)
epsilon_t = 0.003 * (d - c) / c   // [-] Net tensile strain
phi_f = epsilon_t >= 0.005 ? 0.9 : (epsilon_t >= 0.002 ? 0.65 + 0.25 * (epsilon_t - 0.002) / 0.003 : 0.65)   // [-] Flexural reduction factor
M_Rd_ACI = phi_f * A_s * f_y * (d - a / 2) / 1e6   // [kN.m] Design flexural capacity
V_c = 0.17 * lambda_concrete * sqrt(f_c_prime) * b_w * d / 1000   // [kN] Shear capacity — concrete
V_s = A_sv * f_y * d / s_v / 1000   // [kN] Shear capacity — steel
V_Rd_ACI = phi_v * (V_c + V_s)   // [kN] Design shear capacity
UC_flexure_ACI = M_Ed / M_Rd_ACI   // [-] Flexural utilization ratio
UC_shear_ACI = V_Ed / V_Rd_ACI   // [-] Shear utilization ratio
UC_ACI = max(UC_flexure_ACI, UC_shear_ACI)   // [-] Governing utilization

bw_check.error_msg: Beam width must be greater than zero.

h_check.error_msg: Beam height must be greater than zero.

d_check.error_msg: Effective depth must be greater than zero.

d_h_check.error_msg: Effective depth cannot exceed total beam height.

as_check.error_msg: Tension reinforcement area must be greater than zero.

v_ed_check.error_msg: Design shear force cannot be negative.

sv_missing.error_msg: Stirrup spacing required when shear reinforcement is provided.

fck_missing_ec2.error_msg: Concrete cylinder strength f_ck required for EN 1992-1-1.

fc_missing_aci.error_msg: Concrete compressive strength f'_c required for ACI 318.

uc_fail.error_msg: Section FAILS — utilization exceeds 1.0. Redesign required.

smart_warnings.message: UC = {UC} between 0.90–1.00. Engineering review required before acceptance.
UC = {UC} > 1.00. Section FAILS. Governing mode: {governingMode}. Section must be redesigned.
x/d = {xd} > 0.45. Neutral axis depth limit exceeded per EN 1992-1-1 §5.5(4).
ε_t = {epsilon_t} < 0.005. Section not tension-controlled. Ductility limited.
V_s exceeds V_s_max. Stirrup spacing too large or section insufficient.
Stirrup spacing s_v exceeds maximum permitted (~d/2). Review detailing rules.

fmea.failureMode: Flexural yielding
fmea.description: Tension steel yields before concrete crushes. Ductile failure mode if ρ_l ≤ ρ_bal.
fmea.effect: Large deflections but visible warning before collapse

fmea.failureMode: Compression-controlled brittle failure
fmea.description: Neutral axis too deep. Concrete crushes before steel yields. Low ductility.
fmea.effect: Sudden collapse without warning

fmea.failureMode: Diagonal shear failure
fmea.description: Diagonal tension crack propagates through web. Sudden failure without warning.
fmea.effect: Brittle shear failure

fmea.failureMode: Concrete strut crushing
fmea.description: Web crushing before stirrup yielding. Section too slender or stirrups too strong.
fmea.effect: Brittle web failure

fmea.failureMode: Stirrup yielding
fmea.description: Stirrups yield before concrete strut crushes. Large shear deformations.
fmea.effect: Excessive cracking before failure

fmea.failureMode: Anchorage failure
fmea.description: Insufficient development length. Bar pull-out at support.
fmea.effect: Loss of flexural capacity at support

fmea.failureMode: Bar placement error
fmea.description: Actual effective depth less than assumed due to placement tolerance or congestion.
fmea.effect: Reduced capacity vs. design

fmea.failureMode: Material strength mismatch
fmea.description: Actual concrete or steel strength below specified grade. Compliance failure.
fmea.effect: Non-compliance with design assumptions

tool_name: Centrifugal Pump Cavitation & NPSH Analysis (API 610)

inputs.name: Fluid Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Fluid Density (rho)
inputs.hint: Water 20°C: 998 | Water 80°C: 972 | Crude Oil: 870
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Tank / Atmospheric Surface Pressure (P0)
inputs.hint: Open tank: 1.013 bar(a) | Closed vessel: operating pressure
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Fluid Vapor Pressure (Pv)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Static Suction Head (Hs)
inputs.hint: Positive suction head: (+) | Suction lift (pump above liquid): (-)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Suction Pipe Friction Losses (hf)
inputs.hint: Sum of friction losses from pipe runs, valves, and fittings
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Pump Required NPSH (NPSHr)
inputs.hint: From pump performance curve (manufacturer datasheet)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Suction Pipe Inner Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Flow Rate (Q)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Volume: √3 × linear ≈ ±1.7% (conservatively ±2%)

inputs.name: API 610 Safety Margin
inputs.hint: API 610 recommendation: NPSHa >= NPSHr + 0.5m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Installation Altitude
inputs.hint: Sea level: 0m | Altitude decreases atmospheric pressure
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 class 'm': ±0.3mm for 30-120mm range

formulas: Atm_Correction = 1.013 * POWER(1 - 0.0000226 * elevation_m, 5.256)   // [bar] Altitude-corrected atmospheric pressure
Head_Tank = (tank_pressure_bar * 100000) / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81)   // [m] Tank pressure head in water column equivalent
Head_Vapor = (vapor_pressure_bar * 100000) / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81)   // [m] Vapor pressure head in water column equivalent
NPSHa = Head_Tank - Head_Vapor + static_suction_head_m - suction_pipe_losses_m   // [m] Net Positive Suction Head Available (NPSHa) — API 610 Clause 6.1
NPSH_Margin_m = NPSHa - npsh_required_m   // [m] NPSH safety margin
NPSH_Ratio = NPSHa / npsh_required_m   // [—] NPSH ratio — API 610: minimum 1.1
Flow_Velocity_m_s = (flow_rate_m3h / 3600) / (3.14159 * POWER(pipe_diameter_mm / 1000 / 2, 2))   // [m/s] Suction pipe flow velocity
Velocity_Head = POWER(Flow_Velocity_m_s, 2) / (2 * 9.81)   // [m] Velocity head
Max_Suction_Lift = Head_Tank - Head_Vapor - suction_pipe_losses_m - npsh_required_m - safety_margin_m   // [m] Theoretical maximum suction lift

vapor_exceeds_tank.error_msg: PHYSICAL ERROR: Vapor pressure is equal to or greater than tank pressure. Fluid is already at boiling point — pumping in liquid phase is impossible. Reduce temperature or increase tank pressure.

density_check.error_msg: Fluid density < 400 kg/m³: Not a realistic liquid (LNG is ~420, others are higher). Verify input value.

suction_lift_max.error_msg: Suction lift < -9m: Theoretical maximum vacuum lift for water is ~9m. This value exceeds physical limits. Revise pump position.

pipe_velocity_check.error_msg: Suction line velocity > 3 m/s: Friction losses will be excessive, reducing NPSHa and increasing cavitation risk. Hydraulic Institute recommendation: suction velocity < 1.5 m/s.

smart_warnings.message: CAVITATION INITIATED: NPSHa < NPSHr. Vapor bubbles are forming in the impeller eye. Micro-implosions are causing surface erosion. Stop the pump immediately and rectify suction conditions.

CAVITATION RISK: Does not meet API 610 requirement of NPSHa >= NPSHr + 10% safety margin. Cavitation will initiate immediately on flow surges or temperature rises. Reduce suction line friction losses.
Insufficient NPSH margin: NPSHa / NPSHr is between 1.1 and 1.3. Cavitation may trigger during process fluctuations (temperature, flow). Hydraulic Institute recommends a minimum ratio of 1.3.
Hot fluid + negative suction lift: Vapor pressure increases exponentially with temperature, rapidly reducing NPSHa. This combination poses critical cavitation risk. Locate pump below tank level.
Suction line friction losses > 2m: Directly subtracts from NPSHa. To reduce losses: increase pipe diameter, minimize valves, shorten pipe runs, and avoid tight elbows.
NPSHa < 1.0m: Operating conditions are outside normal centrifugal pump limits. Evaluate special low-NPSH pump models (with inducer) or submersible pumps.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: vapor_pressure_bar >= tank_pressure_bar
fmea.effect: PHYSICAL ERROR: Vapor pressure is equal to or greater than tank pressure. Fluid is already at boiling point — pumping in liquid phase is impossible. Reduce temperature or increase tank pressure.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fluid_density_kg_m3 < 400
fmea.effect: Fluid density < 400 kg/m³: Not a realistic liquid (LNG is ~420, others are higher). Verify input value.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: static_suction_head_m < -9.0
fmea.effect: Suction lift < -9m: Theoretical maximum vacuum lift for water is ~9m. This value exceeds physical limits. Revise pump position.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: pipe_diameter_mm > 0 && flow_rate_m3h > 0 && Flow_Velocity_m_s > 3.0
fmea.effect: Suction line velocity > 3 m/s: Friction losses will be excessive, reducing NPSHa and increasing cavitation risk. Hydraulic Institute recommendation: suction velocity < 1.5 m/s.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CAVITATION INITIATED: NPSHa < NPSHr. Vapor bubbles are forming in the impeller eye. Micro-implosions are causing surface erosion. Stop the pump immediately and rectify suction conditions.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: CAVITATION RISK: Does not meet API 610 requirement of NPSHa >= NPSHr + 10% safety margin. Cavitation will initiate immediately on flow surges or temperature rises. Reduce suction line friction losses.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio >= 1.0 && NPSH_Ratio < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: Insufficient NPSH margin: NPSHa / NPSHr is between 1.1 and 1.3. Cavitation may trigger during process fluctuations (temperature, flow). Hydraulic Institute recommends a minimum ratio of 1.3.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio >= 1.1 && NPSH_Ratio < 1.3

fmea.failureMode: Hot fluid + negative suction lift: Vapor pressure increases exponentially with temperature, rapidly reducing NPSHa. This combination poses critical cavitation risk. Locate pump below tank level.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_temp_c > 60 && static_suction_head_m < 0

fmea.failureMode: Suction line friction losses > 2m: Directly subtracts from NPSHa. To reduce losses: increase pipe diameter, minimize valves, shorten pipe runs, and avoid tight elbows.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: suction_pipe_losses_m > 2.0

fmea.failureMode: NPSHa < 1.0m: Operating conditions are outside normal centrifugal pump limits. Evaluate special low-NPSH pump models (with inducer) or submersible pumps.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSHa < 1.0

formulas: Load_Current_A = (load_kw * 1000 * demand_factor) / (SQRT(3) * voltage_v * power_factor)   // [A] Line current — 3-Phase load
Resistivity = 0.0175   // [ohm*mm2/m] Copper resistivity (20°C) — IEC 60228
Resistance_R_ohm = (Resistivity * cable_length_m) / cross_section_val   // [ohm] Phase conductor resistance (one-way)
Total_Loop_R = 2 * Resistance_R_ohm   // [ohm] Go and return path loop resistance
Reactance_X_ohm = 0.00008 * cable_length_m   // [ohm] Conductor inductive reactance
Sin_Phi = SQRT(1 - POWER(power_factor, 2))   // [—] sin phi
VD_Voltage = SQRT(3) * Load_Current_A * (Resistance_R_ohm * power_factor + Reactance_X_ohm * Sin_Phi)   // [V] Voltage drop — 3-Phase | Source: IEC 60364-5-52
VD_Percent = (VD_Voltage / voltage_v) * 100   // [%] Voltage drop percentage
Voltage_At_Load = voltage_v - VD_Voltage   // [V] Line voltage at load terminal
Power_Loss_W = 3 * POWER(Load_Current_A, 2) * Resistance_R_ohm   // [W] Cable heat dissipation loss (3-phase)
Power_Loss_Pct = (Power_Loss_W / (load_kw * 1000)) * 100   // [%] Power loss percentage
Recommended_Min_Section = (SQRT(3) * Load_Current_A * Resistivity * cable_length_m * power_factor) / (max_vd_pct / 100 * voltage_v)   // [mm2] Minimum required cross-section based on voltage drop

power_factor_range.error_msg: Power factor must be between 0.5 and 1.0. Typically 0.8-0.9 for inductive loads. Below 0.5 is not practical.

voltage_current_match.error_msg: Current > 1000A requires a minimum cross-section of 70mm² to prevent thermal overload.

section_minimum.error_msg: IEC 60228: Power circuit minimum cross-section is 1.5mm². Smaller sizes are non-standard.

length_resistance.error_msg: Long run + small cross-section: for lines >500m, voltage drop and reactance effects are critical. Evaluate cross-section increase or voltage level upgrade.

smart_warnings.message: VOLTAGE DROP LIMIT EXCEEDED: Motor will draw excessive current at this low voltage -> overheat -> melt insulation -> cause short circuit. Increase cross-section by at least one standard size.
Voltage drop close to limit: Within 80% of limit. Cross-section upgrade is recommended to prevent exceeding limit on load surges or hot weather.
Cable heat dissipation loss > 3%: Annual energy cost is significant. Investment in cross-section upgrade typically pays back in 2-3 years. Run economic sizing optimization (Kelvin's Law).
High ambient temperature: Cable ampacity derates. Apply correction factor (Ct) for ambient >40°C per IEC 60364. Standard catalog ratings assume 30°C.
Selected cross-section is insufficient: does not meet voltage drop limits. Calculated minimum cross-section is displayed. Choose next standard size up.
High current (>630A): Consider parallel cables or busbar duct system. Parallel cable routing can be complex; busduct is more reliable.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: power_factor < 0.5 || power_factor > 1.0
fmea.effect: Power factor must be between 0.5 and 1.0. Typically 0.8-0.9 for inductive loads. Below 0.5 is not practical.

fmea.failureMode: VOLTAGE DROP LIMIT EXCEEDED: Motor will draw excessive current at this low voltage -> overheat -> melt insulation -> cause short circuit. Increase cross-section by at least one standard size.

fmea.failureMode: Cable heat dissipation loss > 3%: Annual energy cost is significant. Investment in cross-section upgrade typically pays back in 2-3 years. Run economic sizing optimization (Kelvin's Law).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Power_Loss_Pct > 3

fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c > 40
